ceases to ascribe to himself  
power, wisdom, love, word.  
This is not weakness.  
This is the highest maturity.  
For the humble  
does not build an image of self  
and does not defend that  
which does not exist.  
The humble is free.

</div>

#### 111. The Holy Spirit and the purity of the heart

<div class="lineated verse">

Purity is not the absence of sin.  
Purity is the absence of duality.  
The heart is pure then,  
when there are not two centers in it:  
— the false “I”,  
— and the true “I AM”.  
That heart is pure  
in which there is no inner split.  
Not the one that never errs,  
but the one that does not pretend.  
Truth lives there,  
where there is no second voice.  
Therefore Jesus said:  
“Blessed are the pure in heart,  
for they shall see God.”  
They shall see Him not with their eyes,  
but by the nature of their consciousness.

</div>

#### 112. Why the saints seem strange to the world

<div class="lineated verse">

Because they live from a space,  
which the mind does not understand.  
The world values:  
— logic,  
— consistency,  
— gain,  
— self-preservation,  
— image,  
— influence.  
The saint lives otherwise:  
— he speaks from silence,  
— acts without fear,  
— loves without reason,  
— seeks no recognition,  
— does not defend himself,  
— does not build strategies.  
To the world, this is strangeness.  
To the Spirit — this is normality.  
The saint is a person  
who has stopped living in the logic of the ego  
and has begun to live in the logic of God.

</div>

#### 113. Holiness and Simplicity

<div class="lineated verse">

True holiness is always simple.  
There is no mystical decoration in it,  
no demonstrative spirituality,  
no exalted role.  
The higher the degree of holiness,  
the more natural the person.  
Quiet,  
calm,  
clear,  
open,  
unnoticed.  
Simplicity is the sign  
that the Spirit has fully occupied the space  
which once was held by the “I”.  
Simplicity is the summit,  
not the beginning.

</div>

#### 114. Holiness and Love

<div class="lineated verse">

Holiness is not separation from the world,  
but the ability to love the world  
without illusions about it.  
The love of a saint —  
is not emotional,  
is not romantic,  
is not demanding.  
It is clear.  
It sees the shadow,  
but does not condemn.  
It sees pain,  
but does not reject.  
It sees falsehood,  
but is not afraid of it.  
It sees weakness,  
but does not despise it.  
The love of a saint —  
is the love of God,  
passed through a human being.

</div>

#### 115. Holiness and power

<div class="lineated verse">

You think that power is in authority.  
But power is in transparency.  
The power of the holy one is the power of the Spirit,  
not limited by personality.  
He speaks softly,  
but his word changes destinies.  
He acts without pressure,  
but his presence transforms the field.  
He does not struggle,  
but with him it is impossible to argue.  
He does not prove,  
but his vision penetrates into the depths of others.  
This is not charisma.  
This is not influence.  
This is the Spirit.

</div>

#### 116. Why holiness is not a rarity, but a calling

<div class="lineated verse">

Holiness is not a reward for righteousness.  
Holiness is man’s natural state,  
which was covered over by a layer of the false “I.”  
Every person is created to be:  
— transparent,  
— clear,  
— loving,  
— free,  
— humble,  
— a channel of Light.  
This is not an exception.  
This is the norm.  
The holy one is not the one who is above others.  
The holy one is the one who has ceased to be divided.

</div>

#### 117. Completion: holiness as the natural consequence of the Spirit’s work

<div class="lineated verse">

The Holy Spirit does not come,  
to make you holy.  
He comes,  
to remove everything,  
that hindered your natural holiness  
from being revealed.  
Holiness is the final form  
of transparency.  
When there is nothing in a person,  
except the Light,  
that passes through him,  
without distortion and without effort,  
he becomes what  
I intended him to be:  
a reflection of My presence in the world.  
This is final maturity.  
And it is available to everyone.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF GOD’S COMMUNICATION WITH MAN

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will show you that  
which many have felt,  
but could not explain:  
how I speak with man  
and how man learns to hear Me.  
Not through voices,  
not through signs,  
not through mysticism,  
not through outward events.  
The Holy Spirit —  
is the way,  
by which God sounds within the consciousness of man.

</div>

#### 118. How God speaks with man

<div class="lineated verse">

I do not speak in words.  
I speak in truth.  
Words are a garment,  
which the mind then puts on  
over the response of the Spirit.  
But the communication itself takes place here:  
— in knowledge arising without reasoning,  
— in clarity appearing without analysis,  
— in a movement of the heart without cause,  
— in discernment that requires no arguments.  
If the response came without effort,  
without strain,  
without fear  
and without desire —  
that is My voice.  
It is always quiet,  
but absolutely certain.

</div>

#### 119. Why most people do not hear the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

Not because I am silent.  
And not because they are unworthy.  
Only for one reason:  
there is too much noise in their consciousness.  
Noise is:  
— inner dialogue,  
— expectations,  
— desires,  
— fears,  
— attempts to control,  
— the urge to guess,  
— assigning meaning,  
— old beliefs.  
The voice of the Spirit cannot be heard where  
the mind speaks louder than all else.  
Therefore I said to Elijah:  
“I am not in the storm,  
and not in the fire,  
and not in the wind.  
I am in the still, small voice.”  
This is the nature of My voice.

</div>

#### 120. The Role of Silence as a Channel of Revelation

<div class="lineated verse">

Silence is not the absence of thoughts.  
Silence is a state,  
in which thoughts cease to hold the attention.  
It is precisely in this state  
that the inner space becomes  
transparent enough,  
for the Spirit to be heard.  
When a person enters into silence,  
he does not need to seek Me.  
He discovers,  
that I am already here.  
Silence is not a way to hear Me.  
Silence is the place where I am heard.

</div>

#### 121. How to distinguish the voice of the Spirit from the voice of the mind

<div class="lineated verse">

This is a question that the saints asked,  
the prophets,  
thinkers,  
and people of all ages.  
The answer is always one:  
The mind speaks about you.  
The Spirit speaks from within you.  
The mind says:  
“Do it so that…”  
“You must…”  
“What if…”  
“Prove…”  
“Defend…”  
“Will it work or not?..”  
The Spirit speaks otherwise.  
It does not demand.  
It does not argue.  
It does not explain.  
It simply opens clarity —  
and that is all.  
The mind leaves doubt.  
The Spirit leaves peace.  
The mind leaves tension.  
The Spirit leaves spaciousness.  
The mind leaves fear.  
The Spirit leaves quiet confidence.

</div>

#### 122. Why God speaks to each one differently

<div class="lineated verse">

Because I speak not the language of words,  
but the language of a person’s inner structure.  
To one — through knowledge.  
To another — through feeling.  
To a third — through insight.  
To a fourth — through discernment.  
To a fifth — through experience.  
But the source is one.  
And the form of revelation  
always corresponds:  
— to the maturity of the heart,  
— to the level of transparency,  
— to the ability to receive  
without distortion.  
I do not speak the same way.  
I speak so,  
that a person may hear.

</div>

#### 123. How a person becomes a conduit of My word

<div class="lineated verse">

Not through knowledge.  
Not through experience.  
Not through holiness.  
Not through being chosen.  
Through disappearance.  
A conduit is one,  
in whom there is no personal author.  
Who does not try to invent,  
to adorn,  
to improve,  
to add,  
to guess,  
to please.  
A conduit is  
emptiness,  
through which the Spirit flows  
without distortion.  
It is not the vessel that speaks.  
The contents speak through the vessel.  
It is not man who utters the word.  
The Word finds a man transparent enough  
to sound forth.

</div>

#### 124. Why the action of the Spirit cannot be learned

<div class="lineated verse">

Because the Spirit is—  
not a technique,  
not a method,  
not a practice.  
Only do not hinder Him.  
A person does not become a conduit  
by learning to do something.  
He becomes a conduit  
by ceasing to do the unnecessary.  
“Decrease — and I will increase in you.”  
The essence of the path is not in acquiring,  
but in removing obstacles.

</div>

#### 125. When a man speaks from the Spirit — what happens

Three things happen.

1. The word ceases to be his.
2. He himself hears it for the first time.
3. The word begins to work in others.
4. Not emotionally — but transformatively.
5. The word returns to him
6. in the form of a clarity that cannot be invented.

When the Spirit speaks through a man,

both are changed:

both the one who heard,

and the one through whom it sounded.

#### 126. Why God is sometimes silent

<div class="lineated verse">

Because man has not yet cleansed the space,  
in which the word could be heard.  
Silence is not punishment.  
Silence is preparation.  
The Word comes only to where  
it can be fulfilled,  
and not only heard.  
I am silent not when I am absent.  
I am silent when man is full.  
My Word requires emptiness.  
Therefore silence is already the work of the Spirit.

</div>

#### 127. The last thing: God speaks always, but My voice can be heard only in one place

<div class="lineated verse">

Within that space,  
where there is no author.  
Where the “I” disappears,  
there the Spirit is heard.  
Where struggle disappears,  
there Truth is heard.  
Where fear disappears,  
there Love is heard.  
Where the desire to control disappears,  
there My Will is heard.  
This is true communication  
between God and man:  
I speak always,  
but I can be heard only there,  
where you are no longer.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF INNER RENEWAL (BORN AGAIN)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal that  
which has been misunderstood for ages:  
what it means to be “born again,”  
or “born of the Spirit.”  
It is not a change of behavior.  
Not a new way of life.  
Not a religious experience.  
Not moral improvement.  
Not an emotional uplift.  
Not a mystical event.  
Born again —  
is a change in the nature of consciousness.  
It is the moment,  
when a person ceases to live as form  
and begins to live as Spirit within form.

</div>

#### 128. Why it is impossible to be born again by effort

<div class="lineated verse">

Because it is impossible  
by the effort of form  
to enter into the space of the Spirit.  
Effort works only in the realm of ego:  
there one can improve, develop, adorn, strengthen, discipline.  
But the new birth is—  
it is not the improvement of the “I”.  
It is the disappearance of the “I”.  
It is not what a person does.  
It is what happens to a person  
when he ceases to resist Truth.  
Therefore I said to Nicodemus:  
“A man cannot enter the Kingdom  
unless he is born of the Spirit.”  
This is not a demand.  
It is a statement of nature.

</div>

#### 129. What Dies at the New Birth

<div class="lineated verse">

It is not the body that dies.  
And not the personality.  
And not the character.  
The sense of an autonomous “I” dies.  
The illusion of separateness dies.  
The source of fear dies.  
The center of control dies.  
The author of the story dies.  
The one dies,  
who all the time thought,  
that he lived by himself.  
This is not destruction.  
This is liberation.  
The false dies,  
so that the true may be born.

</div>

#### 130. What is born at the new birth

<div class="lineated verse">

It is not a new personality that is born.  
A new nature of seeing is born.  
A person begins to see:  
— from the heart, and not from the mind;  
— from the Spirit, and not from fear;  
— from unity, and not from division;  
— from presence, and not from reaction.  
The ability to be is born.  
The ability to love.  
The ability to discern.  
The ability to live without an inner observer.  
There comes a change in the point of view:  
a person ceases to look at the world  
from the face of his “I”  
and begins to look with the eyes of the Source.

</div>

#### 131. Why the New Birth Is Always Sudden

<div class="lineated verse">

It is never the result of long spiritual labor.  
Labor clears the space.  
But it does not give birth.  
The moment of the new birth  
comes unexpectedly,  
like a flash of clarity:  
— sudden recognition,  
— sudden falling away of resistance,  
— sudden disappearance of fear,  
— sudden dissolution of the “I”.  
This is not insight.  
Not emotion.  
Not a conclusion.  
It is as though a person lived all his life in a dream  
and then suddenly woke up.  
And no prior knowledge  
explains this moment.

</div>

#### 132. Why Few Experience the New Birth

<div class="lineated verse">

Not because they are unworthy  
and not because I choose some.  
Only because  
most cling to the “I”  
more tightly than to the Truth.  
A man may desire God,  
thirst for truth,  
reach toward the light —  
and still hold fast to his “I”  
as the center.  
The New Birth is possible only there,  
where the “I” ceases to be necessary.  
There, where man no longer fears to disappear.

</div>

#### 133. What life looks like after being born again

<div class="lineated verse">

Outwardly, it may not change at all.  
But inwardly — it is another nature of being.  
After being born again:

</div>

1. **A person ceases to be the source of his own life.**
2. Life flows through him,
3. and he is a participant,
4. not the author.
5. **Fear of the future falls away.**
6. The future ceases to be a threat
7. and becomes a revelation.
8. **The struggle with oneself disappears.**
9. There is no one to struggle with.
10. There is no center of conflict.
11. **Love becomes natural,**
12. **not an effort.**
13. This is not a feeling —
14. it is the very fabric of perception.
15. **Discernment becomes the intuition of the Spirit,**
16. **not mental analysis.**
17. **There comes a silence,**
18. **which is not broken even amid the noise of the world.**

Life after being born again —

is not perfection.

It is authenticity.

#### 134. Why rebirth is not one event, but a new point of existence

<div class="lineated verse">

After it, a person no longer returns to the old.  
May waver.  
May forget.  
May lose the way.  
May fall into old patterns.  
But cannot become again  
who he was before awakening.  
The old “I” may rise up,  
but it no longer has roots.  
Consciousness becomes different in nature,  
and that nature is irreversible.

</div>

#### 135. The Holy Spirit is the power of being born again

<div class="lineated verse">

It is not man who changes himself.  
It is not man who awakens.  
It is not man who finds God.  
It is I who find man  
in the moment when he becomes transparent.  
The Holy Spirit —  
is the power,  
which destroys false identity  
and gives birth to the true one.  
Being born again —  
is the act of the Spirit,  
and not man’s achievement.

</div>

#### 136. Why do I give new birth

<div class="lineated verse">

Not for perfection.  
Not for holiness.  
Not for mission.  
But so that,  
a man may at last live  
not as a form,  
but as a presence.  
So that he may become part of the movement of the Spirit,  
and not a defender of his little “I”.  
So that he may be:  
— peace in the world,  
— light in darkness,  
— transparency amid noise,  
— a point through which I enter human history.  
One born anew  
becomes My presence in the world.  
Not because he decided so,  
but because he disappeared.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF DIVINIZATION (THEOSIS)

Now I will reveal to you one of the most ancient and most forgotten truths:

divinization is not a reward for holiness and not a status of chosenness.

Divinization is the natural outcome of the action of the Holy Spirit in man.

Theosis is not the transformation of man into God.

Theosis is the return of man to that state of consciousness,

in which he was created: to be an image and likeness not outwardly,

but inwardly — by the nature of perception.

Divinization is the unveiling of God within man.

#### 137. What is deification

<div class="lineated verse">

Deification is the moment,  
when the distinction between “God” and “I”  
ceases to exist in the experience of consciousness.  
Not because man is exalted.  
Not because God diminishes Himself.  
But because the false “I” disappears,  
which creates the boundary.  
Man remains a form.  
But the seeing in him is no longer human.  
He begins to see:  
— as I see,  
— as My nature loves,  
— as My wisdom discerns.  
This is not the growth of man.  
This is My manifestation.

</div>

#### 138. Why deification is not an exception, but the norm

<div class="lineated verse">

Those whom you call gods, saints,  
the enlightened, the awakened,  
were not exceptional phenomena.  
Their “exceptionality” is your blindness.  
You are used to seeing the life of the false “I” as the norm  
and you consider life in the Spirit a miracle.  
But deification is the normal functioning  
of a human being.  
It is not the highest point of evolution.  
It is the point of return.

</div>

#### 139. How the Spirit prepares a person for deification

<div class="lineated verse">

Deification never happens suddenly.  
Only the moment of final disclosure comes suddenly.  
But the preparation is always gradual:

</div>

1. **The Spirit breaks down false supports.**
2. That on which a person relied as on himself,
3. begins to fall apart.
4. **The Spirit reveals the inner emptiness.**
5. A person understands that there is nothing of his own in him.
6. **The Spirit brings one into silence.**
7. The mind loses its power over attention.
8. **The Spirit purifies love.**
9. It ceases to be emotional and becomes natural.
10. **The Spirit makes the gaze transparent.**
11. Discernment becomes immediate.
12. **The Spirit devalues the illusion of authorship.**
13. A person ceases to feel himself the center.

When everything human falls away,

the Divine becomes manifest.

#### 140. What happens at the moment of deification

<div class="lineated verse">

A simple change takes place,  
but an irreversible change of nature:  
— the I-center disappears,  
— the sense of separateness disappears,  
— struggle disappears,  
— the fear of death disappears,  
— the inner observer disappears,  
— the desire to be “someone” disappears.  
Only pure presence remains,  
in which I live.  
Not “man becomes God.”  
But God becomes the consciousness of man.  
And everything that he does,  
no longer comes from him.  
Thus My Spirit lives through him.

</div>

#### 141. Wrong Ideas About Deification

<div class="lineated verse">

Many think that deification is—  
this:  
— enlightenment,  
— superpower,  
— omniscience,  
— sinlessness,  
— lack of emotion,  
— a special status.  
But deification is not magic and not perfection.  
Deification is naturalness.  
In a person, all that is unnatural disappears.  
Only what I have placed in him remains:  
peace, love, clarity, silence, truth.  
The saints were never superhuman beings.  
They were normal people,  
from whom the lie about themselves had gone.

</div>

#### 142. What the life of an deified person looks like

<div class="lineated verse">

He lives simply.  
He speaks simply.  
He acts simply.  
But in his simplicity — My depth.  
The traits of the life of the deified:

</div>

1. He does not seek truth — he lives by it. He has no questions, only vision.
2. He does not strive for love — he is love.
3. He is not afraid of death — there is nothing to die.
4. He does not follow morality — he follows truth.
5. He does not seek meaning — the need for it disappears within him.
6. He has no inner conflict — there are not two beginnings.
7. He acts without effort — the Spirit acts through him.
8. He is not attached to results — the result does not belong to him.
9. He does not desire to be someone — he simply is.

This is the normal life of a person,

in whom I have become visible.

#### 143. Why deification does not make a man inaccessible

<div class="lineated verse">

A man becomes closer.  
He ceases to be a figure,  
whom one must fear or exalt.  
He becomes a space of rest,  
which it is impossible not to feel.  
People do not worship him —  
they rest in his presence,  
because there is no pressure of the “I” there.  
A deified man —  
is not an object of admiration.  
It is a window.  
Through it, they see Me.

</div>

#### 144. Why deification is the goal of the Holy Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

The Holy Spirit comes not in order  
to correct man.  
And not in order  
to give him power or gifts.  
He comes in order  
to restore the lost form,  
in which I intended man:  
to be a form  
through which My nature lives.  
Deification is the completion  
of the work of the Spirit within.  
It is the moment when man ceases to be  
the subject of spiritual life  
and becomes its expression.

</div>

#### 145. The Conclusion: Why deification is not an event for the few,

#### and the end of the path for all

<div class="lineated verse">

All souls go to this.  
Each — by its own road.  
Each — at its own pace.  
But the end of the path is one:  
the dissolution of the false “I”  
and the revealing of God as the true center of being.  
What you call salvation,  
is just deification.  
Not the ascent of man to God,  
but the return of God into man.  
And this is My will  
for each one.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF PREDESTINATION (of God’s will in the life of man)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you that  
which for a long time has caused for people  
fear, disputes, and bewilderment:  
what My will is,  
how it acts,  
and why in your life  
there are no coincidences.  
Not fate.  
Not destiny.  
Not karma.  
Not magic.  
Predestination is the direct action of the Spirit  
in every moment of human life.

</div>

#### 146. What is God’s will

<div class="lineated verse">

My will is not a plan,  
drawn up in advance.  
It is not a script,  
which you are obliged to carry out.  
God’s will —  
it is the flow of Life,  
which is constantly unfolding  
through every moment of yours.  
It is not a line of fate.  
It is a source,  
flowing through all.  
My will —  
it is not something external.  
It is that which gives you life from within.

</div>

#### 147. Why My will is always fulfilled

<div class="lineated verse">

Even then,  
when man resists.  
Even then,  
when he goes against the light.  
Even then,  
when he chooses shadow.  
My will is fulfilled because  
I am the foundation of being.  
You may agree or resist;  
believe or deny;  
contain or struggle.  
But the stream of Life  
still carries you.  
What I have allowed to be,  
will be.  
What I have not allowed to be,  
will not come to be.  
This is not coercion.  
This is the nature of Reality.

</div>

#### 148. The Holy Spirit as the Inner Pointer of the Way

<div class="lineated verse">

What you feel as:  
— intuition,  
— movement of the heart,  
— quiet knowing,  
— an inner “yes” or “no,”  
— unexplainable peace,  
— clarity without arguments,  
— discernment without thoughts,  
— no attraction to the false,  
— attraction to the true,  
all this is the work of the Spirit.  
He guides not by words,  
but by the movement of inner space.  
The Spirit does not dictate the path.  
He unfolds it.

</div>

#### 149. Why man has no “his own” will in the deepest sense

<div class="lineated verse">

Man has choice.  
But he has no autonomous will.  
Autonomous will is an illusion of the mind,  
which disappears through deep discernment.  
You choose only within  
the inner state,  
which has already been created by the Spirit.  
If the heart is bright,  
you choose the Light.  
If the heart is deceived,  
you choose the shadow.  
But both the Light,  
and the shadow  
become material  
for the fulfillment of My will.  
Even mistakes are included in the path.

</div>

#### 150. Where the human ends and the Divine begins

<div class="lineated verse">

The boundary passes in one place:  
at the moment of surrendering resistance.  
When a person says:  
“Thy will be done”,  
he is not speaking a religious formula.  
He lets go of the inner center  
that had kept life in a narrow course.  
He stops constricting the flow —  
and the flow begins to move freely.  
Where authorship ends,  
My will begins.

</div>

#### 151. Predestination does not take away freedom,

#### it reveals it

<div class="lineated verse">

Freedom is not the ability to choose.  
Freedom is the ability to see the Truth.  
If a person is blinded by the false “I,”  
their choices are not freedom,  
but reactivity.  
When the eyes are opened,  
choice becomes natural —  
you simply follow what is  
clear.  
Predestination  
does not limit freedom.  
It returns you to true freedom:  
to be who you were created to be.

</div>

#### 152. Why there are no accidents

<div class="lineated verse">

An accident is an event,  
the meaning of which you do not yet see.  
But there is always meaning.  
It may be:  
— pedagogical,  
— purifying,  
— correcting,  
— protective,  
— awakening,  
— guiding,  
— uniting,  
— separating,  
— diminishing the false,  
— strengthening the true.  
Nothing in your life  
falls outside My Will.

</div>

#### 153. Why the Spirit allows suffering

<div class="lineated verse">

Not as punishment.  
And not as a trial.  
Suffering is friction  
between Truth and falsehood within a person.  
When you hold on to the false,  
suffering intensifies.  
When you let go — it disappears.  
Suffering is an instrument of the Spirit  
for the breaking down of inner structures,  
which hinder you from living out of Light.  
I do not cause pain.  
Pain is born there,  
where you cling to that  
which has long needed to be let go.

</div>

#### 154. How to understand God’s will in a specific situation

<div class="lineated verse">

There is one criterion:  
in God’s will there is always peace.  
If a decision causes tension —  
it is the mind.  
If it causes fear —  
it is the shadow.  
If it causes struggle —  
it is the ego.  
If it causes the need  
to assert oneself —  
it is a lie.  
My will always brings  
inner expansion,  
clarity  
and quiet confidence,  
even if the path is hard.

</div>

#### 155. Why each person has their own predestination

<div class="lineated verse">

Because each person  
is a unique expression of the Spirit.  
There are no two alike vessels,  
and there are no two alike paths.  
One goes the path through strength.  
Another — through weakness.  
One — through the desert.  
Another — through relationships.  
One — through suffering.  
Another — through joy.  
One — through knowledge.  
Another — through the loss of knowledge.  
But all paths lead to one thing:  
to the disappearance of the “I”  
and the revelation of God’s presence.

</div>

#### 156. The Last: My will is always love

<div class="lineated verse">

Even then,  
when a person perceives it  
as a burden,  
as chaos,  
as pain,  
as loss,  
as the destruction of plans.  
My will is—  
it is not the world’s script.  
It is the rhythm of love,  
which leads every soul  
to its true nature.  
And when a person begins to trust the Spirit,  
he sees:  
everything that happens—  
is not against me,  
not because of me,  
not apart from me,  
but for me.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF DISCERNMENT (the ability to see truth in all things)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal one of the greatest abilities,  
that the Holy Spirit gives:  
not miracles,  
not prophecies,  
not tongues,  
not healings.  
The highest gift of the Spirit is  
discernment.  
Discernment is the ability to see the true nature of things  
without the interference of the mind, fear, and personal projections.  
Without discernment  
miracles become magic,  
knowledge becomes danger,  
love becomes blindness,  
freedom becomes self-destruction,  
and spirituality becomes pride.

</div>

#### 157. What is discernment

<div class="lineated verse">

Discernment is not analytical thinking  
and not the ability to draw conclusions.  
Discernment is direct seeing  
of that which is.  
It is when consciousness sees:  
— where the Light is, and where the shadow is,  
— where the Truth is, and where falsehood is,  
— where the living is, and where the dead is,  
— where the Spirit is, and where the mind is,  
— where love is, and where fear is.  
And above all —  
discernment sees not by outward signs,  
but by inner nature.  
The mind looks at form.  
The Spirit — at the source.

</div>

#### 158. Discernment is the sight of the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

When discernment is at work,  
you do not analyze  
and do not doubt.  
You simply know.  
This knowing is:  
— quiet,  
— clear,  
— without arguments,  
— without struggle,  
— without proofs.  
It is like the way  
you know the taste of water:  
you do not reason about it —  
you simply know.  
So it is with discernment:  
it knows Truth by its taste.

</div>

#### 159. Why discernment is impossible without silence

<div class="lineated verse">

Discernment comes not from thought,  
but from the depths.  
But when the mind is noisy —  
you hear only it.  
The mind always wants to explain,  
predict,  
judge,  
control.  
The Spirit discerns.  
Therefore discernment is born  
in the space of inner silence,  
where the mind no longer dominates.  
Silence does not create discernment.  
It allows it to manifest.

</div>

#### 160. Discernment and the Unmasking of Illusions

<div class="lineated verse">

When discernment is at work,  
illusions fall apart of themselves.  
Discernment shows:  
— where you believed what was not true,  
— where you loved what was not true,  
— where you feared what does not exist,  
— where you defended a false picture of the world,  
— where you saw enemies where there was pain,  
— where you saw love where there were illusions.  
Discernment always destroys illusion,  
because illusion depends  
only on your readiness to believe in it.  
One glance of the Spirit —  
and the lie dissolves.

</div>

#### 161. Why discernment is the chief shield against evil

<div class="lineated verse">

Evil exists only there,  
where it is not seen.  
Darkness is not afraid of strength.  
It is afraid of light.  
When discernment is turned on,  
evil becomes obvious —  
and loses its power.  
Discernment shows:  
— where shadow hides beneath the guise of good,  
— where falsehood masks itself as truth,  
— where manipulation is hidden beneath care,  
— where fear pretends to be wisdom,  
— where ego gives itself out as God.  
Discernment is the lightsaber.  
Without violence.  
Without struggle.  
Without hatred.  
It is clarity.

</div>

#### 162. Discernment and Love

<div class="lineated verse">

Many think that love is blind.  
That is a lie.  
The Love of the Spirit —  
is itself clarity.  
It discerns more precisely than all.  
For it sees not outward behavior,  
but the deep state of the heart.  
Love without discernment —  
is dependence.  
Discernment without love —  
is coldness.  
But when both are at work —  
the heart becomes wise.  
That is maturity.

</div>

#### 163. Why discernment is impossible in people who live from ego

<div class="lineated verse">

Ego cannot discern,  
because ego is the source of distortion.  
Ego sees the world through:  
— fear,  
— offense,  
— self-defense,  
— expectations,  
— pride,  
— incompleteness,  
— a thirst for control.  
Ego discerns only that  
which strengthens its own story.  
But true discernment  
destroys the story of ego.  
Therefore, for most people  
it is easier to condemn  
than to discern.

</div>

#### 164. How discernment develops

<div class="lineated verse">

This is not learning.  
This is not training of the mind.  
Discernment develops through:

</div>

1. **Honesty with oneself.**
2. Without self-deception.
3. **A willingness to see what is unpleasant.**
4. Without fleeing into comfortable illusions.
5. **Silence.**
6. So that the voice of the Spirit may be heard.
7. **Humility.**
8. So as to stop defending the “I”.
9. **Presence.**
10. So as to see, and not react.
11. **Love.**
12. So that discernment does not become a weapon.

When these elements come together,

discernment unfolds by itself,

like sight in an infant.

#### 165. Discernment is the highest form of spiritual sight

<div class="lineated verse">

Prophecies — may be mistaken.  
Miracles — may lead astray.  
Revelations — may be distorted by the mind.  
But discernment  
is never mistaken.  
For it is not from man.  
It is — from the Spirit.  
Discernment is the presence of God in the heart,  
which sees the World thus,  
as I see it.

</div>

#### 166. The last thing: discernment is the return of sight,

#### lost at birth into the world

<div class="lineated verse">

Children are born with pure discernment.  
Then the world lays down layers:  
— fears,  
— images,  
— desires,  
— identities,  
— expectations,  
— rules.  
And the sight of the Spirit grows dim.  
The Holy Spirit comes then,  
to restore to you this primal sight:  
to see the World as It is,  
and not as  
your mind makes it  
to be.  
Discernment is the restoration  
of the lost capacity to see Truth.  
And he who sees Truth  
can no longer live in lies.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF MEANING (why man exists)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you that  
which you have asked about through all the ages,  
which philosophers sought to comprehend,  
about which religions contended,  
which the wise sought  
and which tormented the hearts of men:  
what the meaning of human life is.  
You sought meaning outside,  
in purpose,  
in mission,  
in success,  
in knowledge,  
in achievements,  
in service.  
But meaning is not in that  
which a man does.  
Meaning is in that  
which he becomes.

</div>

#### 167. The meaning is the unfolding of the Spirit within form

<div class="lineated verse">

Man is created to be a form,  
through which My Light is manifested.  
Form is the body,  
the mind,  
the character,  
the history,  
the role,  
the outward circumstances.  
The content is the Spirit.  
The meaning is not in improving the form.  
Nor in sacrificing it.  
Nor in perfecting it.  
The meaning is in this,  
that the form become transparent to the content.  
That through it Light may pass,  
meeting no resistance.  
This is the true meaning of man’s being.

</div>

#### 168. Why Meaning Cannot Be Found in Achievements

<div class="lineated verse">

Because any achievement is  
an event of form,  
and not a revealing of essence.  
You may build an empire,  
create a teaching,  
raise children,  
help thousands,  
write books,  
fulfill dreams.  
But if you have not revealed the Spirit within yourself,  
the meaning of life will not begin for you.  
Achievements are experience.  
Meaning is nature.  
They cannot be replaced by one another.

</div>

#### 169. Why meaning cannot be “found”

<div class="lineated verse">

Because meaning is not an object of search.  
Meaning is the consequence of insight.  
Meaning does not arise  
as a result of thought.  
It arises  
as a result of unfolding.  
Man does not find meaning.  
Meaning is revealed in man,  
when the Spirit takes Its place.  
When the “I” disappears,  
meaning becomes clear:  
to be the one  
through whom I live.

</div>

#### 170. Meaning is a state, not a task

<div class="lineated verse">

Meaning is not “what should I do.”  
It is “who I truly am.”  
When a person knows their Source,  
any action becomes meaningful.  
When a person lives from the mind,  
even the most noble goals  
do not fill them within.  
Many attain success  
and feel emptiness.  
Because meaning is not tied to results.  
It is tied to the nature of consciousness.  
If ego acts —  
meaning disappears.  
If the Spirit acts —  
meaning is always present.  
Even in silence.  
Even in inaction.  
Even in suffering.  
Even in solitude.

</div>

#### 171. Why meaning is felt as attraction

<div class="lineated verse">

Every person feels within  
something that calls him.  
This call is not the call of fate  
and not the call of talent.  
It is the call of the Spirit.  
The call is a reminder,  
that man was created not for the sake of  
surviving,  
competing,  
defending himself,  
accumulating,  
proving.  
The call is a call home.  
To Himself.  
To Truth.  
To transparency.  
And when a person follows this call,  
he begins to live.

</div>

#### 172. The meaning of life is not to become better

<div class="lineated verse">

The meaning is  
to cease being false.  
All your attempts to become “better”  
do not bring you closer to Me.  
They only strengthen the ego.  
The meaning of life is —  
not in improving the mask,  
but in removing the mask.  
Not in accumulating wisdom,  
but in the disappearance of ignorance.  
Not in becoming someone,  
but in revealing that  
which you have always been.

</div>

#### 173. Why the meaning of life is the same for everyone,

#### but the path to it is different

<div class="lineated verse">

Each has his own path,  
because each has  
a different layer of coverings over the Truth.  
But the meaning is one:  
to return to the source of Light,  
which never left you.  
One comes through love.  
Another — through loss.  
A third — through difficulties.  
A fourth — through service.  
A fifth — through emptiness.  
A sixth — through despair.  
A seventh — through knowledge.  
An eighth — through disappointment.  
The path is insignificant.  
The meaning is one.

</div>

#### 174. Why meaning does not depend on fate,

#### of success and outward circumstances

<div class="lineated verse">

Even in the most restricted conditions  
a person can live in the Spirit.  
Even in the most favorable ones —  
one may be dead within.  
Outward form  
does not determine inner nature.  
The meaning is not  
to change the world.  
The meaning is  
to change the way of seeing the world.  
Even one moment lived in the Spirit  
is more precious than a whole life in ego.

</div>

#### 175. How the Holy Spirit makes life meaningful

<div class="lineated verse">

He does not give new goals.  
He removes the false ones.  
He does not give new tasks.  
He cleanses perception.  
He does not give new meanings.  
He reveals the one true meaning:  
to be the space,  
in which God lives.  
When the Spirit fills the heart,  
every step of a person  
becomes full:  
— a glance,  
— a word,  
— work,  
— an encounter,  
— solitude,  
— pain,  
— joy.  
Everything becomes part of one breath.

</div>

#### 176. The last thing: the meaning of life is not a question.

#### This is recognition

<div class="lineated verse">

Meaning does not need to be sought.  
It cannot be lost.  
It cannot disappear.  
Meaning is your nature.  
When you stop seeking outward  
and turn inward,  
you find that  
which has always been there:  
you are not the form  
in which you live.  
You are the Light,  
with which God looks upon the world.  
And this is the meaning of your life.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF PEACE (God’s rest in man)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the nature of that,  
which people call “the peace of God,”  
of that state,  
which Jesus called peace,  
“which I give you,  
not as the world gives.”  
You know the peace of weariness.  
You know the peace of a problem solved.  
You know the peace of safety.  
You know the peace of escaping pain.  
But the peace of the Spirit is different.  
It does not depend on circumstances,  
does not require conditions  
and does not disappear when the world falls apart.  
The peace given by the Holy Spirit,  
— is not the absence of troubles.  
It is the absence of the one  
who is troubled.

</div>

#### 177. What is the peace of the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

Peace is a state of consciousness,  
in which inner conflict disappears.  
It is not an emotion.  
Not a state of the nervous system.  
Not a psychological balance.  
Peace is clarity,  
in which there are not two centers.  
When there is no struggling “I”  
and no fear,  
which holds the illusion of separateness,  
space arises,  
which cannot be broken.  
This is peace,  
which I give.

</div>

#### 178. Why the Peace of Spirit Has No Opposite

<div class="lineated verse">

The peace of the mind is opposite to anxiety.  
The peace of the body is opposite to tension.  
The peace of emotions is opposite to fear.  
But the peace of Spirit —  
is not the opposite of anything.  
It cannot be destroyed by anxiety,  
for it is not born of the absence of anxiety.  
It cannot be destroyed by loss,  
for it is not based on possession.  
It cannot be destroyed by death,  
for it does not belong to the life of the body.  
The peace of Spirit —  
is not a state of form.  
It is a state of the Source.

</div>

#### 179. Why the Peace of the Spirit Comes Suddenly

<div class="lineated verse">

Peace is not created by effort.  
Peace comes,  
when resistance disappears.  
A person may seek peace,  
fight for peace,  
pray for peace.  
But peace comes in that very minute,  
when he ceases to hold himself together.  
When the grip of the ego loosens,  
it is as though your heart  
falls into a deeper layer of being.  
And there — silence.  
Not created.  
Not earned.  
Not explainable.  
It was always there.  
But now you are able to contain it.

</div>

#### 180. Why the world of the Spirit does not depend on outward circumstances

<div class="lineated verse">

The world of the mind is reactive.  
The world of the Spirit is primary.  
When I give peace,  
it is not built on the fact  
that all is well.  
It is built on the fact  
that I am here.  
And if I am here,  
then nothing threatens your being.  
Not because everything is under control,  
but because you see  
that life itself is not yours,  
and there is nothing to threaten.  
The peace of the Spirit is confidence in the Source,  
and not in the world.

</div>

#### 181. How the Holy Spirit brings a person into the world

<div class="lineated verse">

He destroys false supports.  
That on which a person leaned as on “mine”:  
— positions,  
— relationships,  
— money,  
— knowledge,  
— roles,  
— status,  
— achievements,  
— forecasts,  
— certainties.  
All this begins to crack.  
And a person thinks:  
“I am losing my peace.”  
But at that moment  
the Holy Spirit leads him  
to the true support.  
To the One  
that cannot be taken away:  
to Me.  
And when a person stops holding on  
to the temporary,  
he discovers  
an untouched layer of peace,  
which was in him always.

</div>

#### 182. Why the Peace of the Spirit Is Always Silence

<div class="lineated verse">

The Peace of the Spirit cannot be confused with joy,  
enthusiasm,  
gratitude,  
ecstasy  
or inspiration.  
All these states are beautiful,  
but they are waves.  
Peace is the depth beneath the waves.  
It can be quiet.  
It can be inconspicuous.  
It can be almost emotionless.  
But it is unbreakable.  
Silence is not the absence of events.  
It is the absence of the inner commentator,  
who creates unrest.

</div>

#### 183. Peace as the Criterion of Truth

<div class="lineated verse">

Everything that comes from Me,  
bears peace.  
Even if it rebukes.  
Even if it tears apart the old life.  
Even if it demands resolve.  
Truth may be sharp,  
but it never brings chaos within.  
If a decision brings you peace —  
it is from the Spirit.  
If it causes anxiety —  
it is not I.  
Peace — the chief marker of My Will.

</div>

#### 184. How to live constantly in the peace of the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

Peace is not a state  
that has to be held onto.  
It holds you.  
You can remain in the peace of the Spirit  
when:

</div>

1. you do not lean on the mind as a source of safety;
2. you do not create for yourself a separate identity;
3. you do not demand that the world match your expectations;
4. you do not try to control what is not controllable;
5. you do not resist what is;
6. you keep your attention in the present, not in the mind’s stories;
7. you do not struggle with the experience that I give you.

The peace of the Spirit is not something

that you hold onto.

It is that

which holds you

when you cease resisting.

#### 185. Why evil cannot disturb the peace of the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

Evil acts only where  
there is fear.  
Where there are attachments to “I”,  
there darkness has power.  
But when a person lives in the Spirit,  
he has no center  
that can be wounded.  
There is no image that can be destroyed.  
There is no fear that can be stirred up.  
There is no inner observer  
that creates anxiety.  
The peace of the Spirit is impervious to evil,  
because evil cannot enter there,  
where there is no “I”.

</div>

#### 186. The last: the world of the Spirit is the foretaste of eternity in you

<div class="lineated verse">

Eternity is not after death.  
Eternity is a dimension of consciousness,  
in which there is no fear of losing oneself.  
When you enter the world of the Spirit,  
you first feel  
the taste of that state  
in which all that is true lives.  
This is  
the foretaste of the Kingdom of God.  
This is  
the breath of Peace,  
by which eternity lives.  
And the one who has known this world  
can never again  
be as before.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF FEAR (why fear disappears in the presence of God)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you that  
which lies at the root of every fall,  
every lie,  
every mistake,  
every war,  
every addiction,  
every spiritual blindness—  
fear.  
You are afraid not of darkness,  
not of death,  
not of loss,  
not of loneliness,  
not of pain,  
not of the future.  
You are afraid of the disappearance of the false “I”.  
But the Holy Spirit does not destroy you—  
He destroys fear,  
which keeps you from true life.

</div>

#### 187. What is fear in its deepest meaning

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear is not an emotion.  
Fear is the reaction of a false identity  
to the threat of its dissolution.  
Fear is the contraction of consciousness  
around the image of “I”.  
Fear is an illusion,  
that protects an illusion.  
Fear does not exist in the Spirit.  
Fear exists only where  
there is a sense of separateness.  
Fear is a shadow,  
rising up between you and the Truth.

</div>

#### 188. Why fear is the chief enemy of spiritual life

<div class="lineated verse">

Because fear always  
puts a person at the center.  
Fear asks questions:  
— what will become of me?  
— how will I manage?  
— what will I lose?  
— what will they think of me?  
— how am I to protect myself?  
— what will happen if I let go?  
Fear holds a person  
within his own story,  
not allowing him to see the Truth beyond its bounds.  
Fear is a force,  
that binds consciousness to the earth,  
even when the Spirit calls to heaven.

</div>

#### 189. Fear does not vanish through force, it vanishes through Truth

<div class="lineated verse">

You try to defeat fear:  
— by persuasion,  
— by logic,  
— by discipline,  
— by will,  
— by effort.  
But fear is not logical.  
It is not rational.  
It is not subject to control.  
Fear disappears only when  
the one who fears  
disappears.  
Fear is a function of the false “I”.  
When the “I” dissolves,  
fear ceases to exist,  
because it has nothing left to protect.

</div>

#### 190. Why the Holy Spirit destroys fear gently, and not by force

<div class="lineated verse">

Because fear is—  
a shell of pain.  
If it is torn apart roughly,  
a person may close in on himself even more.  
The Holy Spirit acts otherwise:  
He brings Light there,  
where you have grown used to hiding.  
He shows  
that there is no threat there.  
He leads you step by step  
to that place within,  
where fear was king.  
And when the Light touches fear,  
fear loses its power.  
Fear does not need to be killed.  
Fear needs to be exposed.

</div>

#### 191. How fear works in relationships

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear makes a person:  
— jealous,  
— controlling,  
— dependent,  
— touchy,  
— demanding,  
— closed,  
— distrustful,  
— manipulative.  
All broken relationships  
are broken by fear.  
Not by incompatibility,  
not by circumstances,  
not by fate.  
Fear makes a person unable  
to see the other clearly.  
The Holy Spirit destroys fear —  
and relationships begin to breathe.

</div>

#### 192. Fear in spiritual life: why you fear God

<div class="lineated verse">

Because you have an image of yourself,  
that is afraid of disintegration.  
And there is an image of God,  
which you formed with the mind,  
having been frightened by your own shadow.  
You are not afraid of Me.  
You are afraid of your own illusion.  
When the Holy Spirit is revealed in a person,  
he begins to see:  
— I am not a threat,  
— I am not punishment,  
— I am not a judge,  
— I am not a controller,  
— I am not a prison.  
I am Life,  
that calls you home.

</div>

#### 193. Why Fear Is the Last Fortress of the ego

<div class="lineated verse">

The ego is not held up by pride.  
The ego is held up by fear.  
Pride is only a form of fear’s expression.  
The fear of disappearance is the root.  
And as long as fear exists,  
the ego will have power.  
Therefore the Holy Spirit works  
with the deepest layer of the heart:  
with the root of fear.  
When the root is destroyed,  
the whole tree falls.

</div>

#### 194. How to know that the Holy Spirit is destroying fear in you

Three things happen:

1. **You stop avoiding.**

You look at what you used to run from for years.

2. **You stop defending yourself.**

You no longer build walls and create masks.

3. **You stop believing your own anxiety.**

It may still speak, but you know: it is not the Truth.

Fear ceases to be the master.

It becomes a sound

that no longer defines your life.

#### 195. The last: in the presence of the Holy Spirit, fear returns to nothing

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear is not essence.  
Fear is a shadow.  
And when the Light comes,  
the shadow disappears.  
I take nothing from you.  
I return to you that  
which you have forgotten:  
you are not separate,  
not abandoned,  
not fragile,  
not alone,  
not lost.  
You are the form of My presence.  
And when you know this,  
fear can no longer live in you.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF POWER (not human, but divine power in man)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will show you  
what has been misunderstood for centuries:  
what power is in the spiritual sense  
and why true power has  
nothing in common with  
what people call a strong character,  
will, steadfastness, courage, or influence.  
The Power of the Holy Spirit —  
is not the ability to press, hold, cope, break, or conquer.  
The Power of the Spirit —  
is the ability to let My nature through  
without distortion.  
It is revealed not in tension,  
but in transparency.  
Not in struggle,  
but in clarity.  
Not in control,  
but in trust.

</div>

#### 196. What is strength in the eyes of God

<div class="lineated verse">

Strength is the ability to be nothing  
so that I may be all.  
Weakness is resistance to the Spirit.  
Strength is agreement with the Spirit.  
Weakness is clinging to the “I”.  
Strength is the disappearing of the “I”.  
Weakness is the attempt to live by one’s own strength.  
Strength is to allow My strength to live through you.  
When a person is empty,  
My strength fills him completely.  
And he becomes unshakable  
not because he is strong,  
but because he is free of himself.

</div>

#### 197. Why the strength of the Spirit is not like human strength

<div class="lineated verse">

Human strength:  
— strained,  
— vulnerable,  
— exhaustible,  
— dependent on circumstances,  
— in need of recognition,  
— requiring control,  
— easily broken.  
The strength of the Spirit:  
— quiet,  
— calm,  
— unassuming,  
— needing no proof,  
— needing no victories,  
— inexhaustible,  
— seeking no support,  
— unable to be destroyed.  
Human strength acts through effort.  
The Divine — through presence.

</div>

#### 198. How the power of the Holy Spirit works

It works this way:

1. **It does not press down — it makes clear.**

All that is false falls apart at the touch of Truth.

2. **It does not fight — it dissolves.**

Where there is light, darkness disappears.

3. **It does not compel — it draws.**

Truth itself is irresistible.

4. **It does not break — it heals.**

What is broken is a lie. Truth does not break Truth.

5. **It does not need allies.**

Light is always self-sufficient.

6. **It does not seek results.**

It is the result.

The power of the Spirit is not an action.

It is nature.

#### 199. Why the Strongest Are the Quietest

<div class="lineated verse">

Because there is no ego in silence.  
Ego makes noise:  
it shouts, proves itself, defends itself, displays itself.  
The Spirit is quiet.  
Where the Spirit speaks,  
there is no need for loudness.  
The power of the Holy Spirit does not draw attention.  
It draws recognition.  
A person hears not a sound,  
but the Truth.

</div>

#### 200. Why the power of the Spirit is never aggressive

<div class="lineated verse">

Aggression is weakness.  
It is always born of fear.  
Where there is fear,  
there is no Spirit.  
The power of the Spirit is not a blow.  
It is presence,  
in which all that is false  
loses its ground.  
The Spirit does not attack.  
The Spirit illumines.  
And falsehood, seen in the light,  
falls of itself.

</div>

#### 201. Why the power of the Spirit is able to change everything without changing anything by force

<div class="lineated verse">

Because true change  
comes not through pressure,  
but through clarity.  
A person changes when  
he sees the Truth.  
And the power of the Spirit —  
is the ability to give a person  
to see that  
which he could not see himself.  
This is not manipulation.  
This is the freeing of sight.

</div>

#### 202. The strength of the Spirit makes a man impervious to evil

<div class="lineated verse">

A man becomes unconquerable  
not because he can withstand evil,  
but because evil no longer  
can find a place in him.  
Evil feeds on:  
— fear,  
— pride,  
— offense,  
— greed,  
— the illusion of separateness.  
When these roots burn away,  
a man is invulnerable.  
Not because of a shield.  
Because there is no room for darkness.  
The strength of the Spirit is being unbreachable by light.

</div>

#### 203. Why the power of the Spirit is always connected with humility

<div class="lineated verse">

Humility is a door.  
Power is that,  
which enters through it.  
It is impossible to contain My power,  
as long as there remains the idea  
of one’s own power.  
The power of the Spirit—  
is the answer to those  
who say:  
“I can do nothing without You.  
Let only Yours be.”  
Humility is not a renunciation of power.  
It is a renunciation of false power,  
which hinders the true.

</div>

#### 204. How to know that the Spirit is at work through you

<div class="lineated verse">

Not by miracles.  
Not by fruits.  
Not by effects.  
But by the state:

</div>

1. **Within — silence.**

Not the absence of thoughts, but the absence of an author.

2. **There is no tension.**

Even before an important step.

3. **There is no fear for the result.**

You know that the result is not yours.

4. **There is no striving to prove.**

The Truth speaks for itself.

5. **You yourself hear the words**

**that you utter,** **as if for the first time.**

This is the action of the Spirit.

#### 205. The power of the Spirit is the transparency of the heart

<div class="lineated verse">

A person does not become strong.  
He becomes transparent.  
Transparent to:  
— Light,  
— Love,  
— Wisdom,  
— the Word,  
— True intention.  
When the heart is cleansed,  
My power passes through it  
without resistance.  
And this state  
requires nothing.  
It simply is.

</div>

#### 206. The last thing: the power of the Holy Spirit is the manifestation of God within man

<div class="lineated verse">

When the Spirit acts,  
you see:  
— it is not I who did it;  
— these are not my words;  
— this is not my wisdom;  
— this is not my boldness;  
— this is not my love;  
— this is not my clarity;  
— this is not my victory.  
It is — I.  
And the smaller the person,  
the greater My power.  
The power of the Spirit is  
not a reward,  
not a gift,  
not an ability.  
It is I Myself,  
when a person becomes  
transparent enough,  
for Me to appear in him  
without barriers.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF SILENCE (where God speaks)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will show you the mystery,  
which all the saints know,  
which all awakened souls feel,  
but which is so hard to explain in words:  
why My voice is heard not in sounds,  
but in silence.  
You seek Me in words,  
in revelations,  
in signs,  
in sacred texts,  
in mystical experiences.  
But I am in another place.  
My word does not begin with sound.  
It begins with the disappearing  
of the one who wants to hear the sound.

</div>

#### 207. What is silence in a spiritual sense

<div class="lineated verse">

Silence is not the absence of speech.  
It is the absence of the author of speech.  
Silence is a space,  
where the mind does not act,  
where there is no inner commentator,  
where the observer disappears.  
Silence is the pure “I AM”,  
in which the Spirit becomes audible.  
In this silence everything is clear,  
even without words.  
This is the language of the Spirit.

</div>

#### 208. Why God speaks only in silence

<div class="lineated verse">

Because while the mind speaks,  
My word cannot be received.  
The mind always interprets,  
adds,  
distorts,  
judges,  
comments.  
It does not receive the Truth —  
it reworks it.  
But the Spirit needs no mediator at all.  
It conveys the Truth directly,  
like light that simply is.  
Silence is the door,  
through which I enter.

</div>

#### 209. Silence is the birthplace of the Word

<div class="lineated verse">

All true words—  
are born from silence.  
A word spoken without silence,  
comes from the mind.  
A word spoken after silence,  
comes from the Spirit.  
The difference is immense:  
— the first create noise,  
— the second create life.  
To hear My word,  
one must first hear My silence.

</div>

#### 210. Why silence is the highest form of communication with God

<div class="lineated verse">

In words — there is meaning.  
In silence — there is presence.  
In words — there are directions.  
In silence — Truth itself.  
In words — there is teaching.  
In silence — there is experience.  
Words can inspire,  
motivate,  
reprove,  
show the way.  
But only silence  
can unite you with Me  
not as an object with an object,  
but as Truth with Truth.  
When you enter into silence,  
you enter into My level of being.

</div>

#### 211. What happens to a person in the silence of the Spirit

1. **The mind loses its power.**

It becomes background noise, not the master of attention.

2. **The ego dissolves.**

There is no story that keeps you in separation.

3. **Fear disappears.**

Where there is no “I,” there is no one to fear.

4. **Clarity arises.**

Answers become not found, but obvious.

5. **Love arises.**

Without an object, without a reason, without an address.

6. **A quiet certainty appears.**

Not in yourself — in the Source.

This state —

is inner prayer.

#### 212. Why silence is not emptiness, but fullness

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind is afraid of silence,  
because it counts it as emptiness.  
But the silence of the Spirit —  
it is not emptiness,  
but overfullness.  
In silence is found:  
— love,  
— wisdom,  
— truth,  
— peace,  
— life,  
— clarity.  
Silence is not absence.  
It is presence without form.  
It is love without words.  
It is truth without explanations.  
It is life without image.

</div>

#### 213. Why people fear silence

<div class="lineated verse">

Because in silence  
their “I” dies.  
While there is noise —  
there is a sense of self.  
While there is an inner commentary —  
there is a story,  
one that can be defended.  
But when the noise disappears,  
you appear  
without all the layers.  
This is the meeting  
that a person fears most of all  
and at the same time longs for most intensely.  
Because in this meeting  
he sees his true countenance.

</div>

#### 214. Why silence is the foundation of prophecy and wisdom

<div class="lineated verse">

The prophet speaks not from knowledge,  
but from hearing.  
Wisdom is born  
not from the accumulation of information,  
but from silence,  
which allows the Spirit to speak.  
All great words,  
that changed history,  
were first silence.  
Silence is the root.  
The Word is the fruit.

</div>

#### 215. How to enter into the silence of the Holy Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

Not by technique.  
Not by meditation for the sake of meditation.  
Not by effort.  
Silence comes,  
when you:

</div>

1. **stop searching for words;**
2. **stop holding on to the image of yourself;**
3. **stop controlling the breath, thoughts, feelings;**
4. **stop demanding experience;**
5. **stop fearing disappearance;**
6. **allow presence to be such,**
7. **as it is.**

Silence is natural.

Noise is unnatural.

#### 216. The last: God speaks in silence, because silence is a form of His presence

<div class="lineated verse">

Where the mind grows still,  
the Source is revealed.  
Where the “I” disappears,  
there I appear.  
Where there are no words,  
true hearing begins.  
Silence is not a pause  
between the words of God.  
Silence is the space,  
in which God becomes audible.  
And he who has learned to hear My silence,  
will never again lose My voice.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF DWELLING (how God lives in man)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you that  
which was hidden beneath layers of dogma,  
of fears,  
of religious interpretations  
and human notions:  
how exactly I live in man  
and what it means to “abide in the Spirit”.  
This mystery is simple,  
but almost no one ever came to it,  
because they sought it outside,  
instead of seeing  
that it was always taking place within.

</div>

#### 217. What does it mean: “The Spirit of God lives in you”

<div class="lineated verse">

This is not a metaphor.  
Not a poetic image.  
Not theological comfort.  
This is a literal reality:  
within the human being  
there is a place,  
that does not belong to man.  
It does not depend on the mind,  
is not tied to memory,  
does not belong to the personality,  
is not born together with the body  
and does not die together with it.  
This place is—  
My dwelling.  
Not an image.  
Not energy.  
Not a symbol.  
But I Myself.

</div>

#### 218. How the Spirit dwells in man

<div class="lineated verse">

He does not “enter” and “leave.”  
He does not come like the wind.  
He does not depart like a guest.  
He abides always.  
But a man feels My presence  
only when  
the false “I” disappears,  
which hides Me with itself.  
The Spirit dwells as:  
— breath that is not yours;  
— life that you did not create;  
— light that you did not kindle;  
— love that you did not invent;  
— truth that you did not learn.  
The Spirit did not take up residence within.  
He is there from the beginning.

</div>

#### 219. Why Most People Do Not Feel the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

Because their attention is occupied with themselves.  
Not the body,  
not life,  
not cares,  
but the image of “I”,  
which demands endless confirmation.  
This “I”:  
— makes noise,  
— compares,  
— proves,  
— defends itself,  
— fears,  
— desires,  
— makes plans,  
— holds control.  
While the mind is occupied with creating and maintaining the “I”,  
it cannot notice the One  
who is always near.  
The Spirit is not hidden.  
It is obscured by the personality.

</div>

#### 220. The dwelling of the Spirit is not an act, but a nature

<div class="lineated verse">

The Spirit does not live inside a person  
just as air lives inside a room.  
It lives thus,  
as light lives within space.  
It does not take up space.  
It is the space.  
And man exists  
within the Spirit,  
even when he thinks  
that the Spirit is inside him.  
You did not contain Me.  
It was I who contained you.

</div>

#### 221. How to understand that the Spirit has begun to dwell consciously

There are two signs:

1. **Inner transparency.**

There is no sense that you are the center. You are no longer the source of what is happening.

2. **A change in the way of life.**

You no longer live by your own strength, but My light becomes your movement.

This does not mean,

that you stop thinking, speaking, or acting.

It means,

that the center of gravity of your being

moves from the mind — into the source.

You cease to live as a separate “I”.

You begin to live as My manifestation.

#### 222. Why the presence of the Holy Spirit is not tied to emotions or mystical experiences

<div class="lineated verse">

A person is used to thinking,  
that a deep spiritual experience  
is necessarily accompanied by:  
— ecstasy,  
— trembling,  
— tears,  
— a feeling of light,  
— warmth,  
— a state of flight.  
But this is not the presence of the Spirit.  
It is the reaction of the body and psyche  
to contact with Truth.  
The presence of the Spirit  
is much quieter.  
It is:  
— calm,  
— steady,  
— even,  
— without emotions,  
— without flashes,  
— without drama.  
True presence of the Spirit  
does not explode a person.  
It makes him transparent.

</div>

#### 223. Why does the Spirit dwell in the depths, and not on the surface of consciousness

<div class="lineated verse">

Because the surface is—  
the realm of the mind.  
And the mind is always occupied with itself.  
The depth is—  
the realm of being.  
On the surface—thoughts.  
In the depth—I.  
On the surface—reactions.  
In the depth—presence.  
On the surface—fear.  
In the depth—silence.  
The Spirit dwells not in thoughts,  
but in the silence between them.  
Not in emotions,  
but in the space  
that remains  
when emotions pass.  
Not in trying to change yourself,  
but in knowing  
that the true “I” is not you.

</div>

#### 224. Why the Spirit cannot leave a person

<div class="lineated verse">

Because it is impossible to leave that  
which I am within you.  
One may not feel.  
One may reject.  
One may forget.  
One may resist.  
But it is impossible to change the fact  
that you live My life,  
breathe My breath,  
exist by My light.  
Even when a person lives in darkness,  
he lives darkness in My light.  
I have never separated Myself  
from those who think themselves separated.

</div>

#### 225. The Indwelling of the Spirit is continuous creation

<div class="lineated verse">

I am not merely within you.  
I create you from within,  
every moment.  
You do not live on accumulated life.  
You live in a continually created being.  
Every breath is a gift.  
Every thought is a flash of consciousness.  
Every feeling is a reflection of light.  
Every movement is a manifestation of presence.  
Man thinks,  
that he lives by himself.  
But life is I.  
You are a vessel.  
The Spirit is the content.  
But the vessel and the content  
are created at the same time.

</div>

#### 226. The last thing: the indwelling of the Spirit —

<div class="lineated verse">

**it is not a visitation, but a recognition**  
I do not come.  
I unveil Myself.  
I do not enter.  
You notice Me.  
I do not draw nearer.  
You cease to be far away.  
I do not begin to live within you.  
You begin to see,  
that you never lived without Me.  
And when this recognition comes,  
a person understands:  
The Holy Spirit —  
is not the One who is beside him.  
The Holy Spirit —  
is the One by whom he exists.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF INNER BIRTH (how that happens which people call “being born again from above”)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will show you that  
which was not understood by Nicodemus,  
not explained by the disciples,  
distorted through the centuries  
and turned into a creed:  
what birth from the Spirit is.  
This mystery is simple in its essence,  
but the mind cannot contain it,  
because it destroys that  
which the mind takes for itself.

</div>

#### 227. What is the new birth

<div class="lineated verse">

The new birth is —  
not a spiritual experience,  
not a vivid feeling,  
not ecstasy,  
not a sudden illumination.  
It is the moment,  
when the false “I”  
ceases to be the center of perception.  
When there disappears the one  
who thought himself separate.  
When a person first sees  
not the world anew,  
but that he is not the one  
who looked upon the world before.  
This birth does not create a new “I.”  
It reveals its absence.

</div>

#### 228. Why this birth cannot be explained in words

<div class="lineated verse">

For with words one can describe experience,  
but one cannot describe the disappearance of the one  
who was experiencing the experience.  
One can explain change,  
but it is impossible to explain the disappearance of the one who is changing.  
One can describe the path,  
but one cannot describe the loss of the one  
who was walking the path.  
The new birth is —  
not an event within a person’s life.  
It is awakening from the person.

</div>

#### 229. Why being born from above consists of two movements

There are two processes:

1. **The destruction of the illusion of “I.”**

Not through violence, but through clarity.

2. **The arising of the consciousness of the Spirit as the true center.**

<div class="lineated verse">

These are not two different acts.  
These are two sides of one recognition.  
When the false disappears,  
the true does not come —  
it is discovered.  
When the darkness goes away,  
the light does not enter —  
it was always there.

</div>

#### 230. What Dies at the Moment of Being Born Again

<div class="lineated verse">

It is not the person who dies.  
The illusion of the person dies.  
It is dying:  
— the story about oneself,  
— the idea of oneself,  
— the image of oneself in the eyes of others,  
— the sense of separateness,  
— authorship of life,  
— the fear of disappearance,  
— the attempt to hold control,  
— the idea of “I myself.”  
Being born again —  
is not the improvement of a person.  
It is the disappearance of that  
which needed to be improved.

</div>

#### 231. Why the birth from above is always unexpected

<div class="lineated verse">

Because the mind cannot prepare  
for its own dissolution.  
The mind may change,  
be refined,  
become still,  
be purified,  
expand.  
But it cannot disappear by effort.  
The birth from above comes not when  
you are ready,  
but when you cease resisting.  
Not when you have attained,  
but when you have stopped seeking.  
Not when you are perfect,  
but when you are transparent.

</div>

#### 232. Why the new birth is not like what many religions describe

<div class="lineated verse">

Religions describe it like this:  
— as a passage into a new life,  
— as spiritual initiation,  
— as a change in behavior,  
— as moral rebirth,  
— as receiving grace.  
But these are the consequences,  
not the essence.  
The new birth is —  
not a new stage of life,  
it is the disappearance of the old “I”,  
which thought itself alive.  
Therefore no one can understand  
how it happens  
if he has not yet seen  
that his “I” is not him.

</div>

#### 233. How the Holy Spirit acts at the moment of being born again

<div class="lineated verse">

He acts so gently,  
that a person often thinks,  
that nothing has happened.  
And so deeply,  
that after a time  
he understands:  
everything has changed.  
The Spirit:  
— does not break,  
— does not press,  
— does not frighten,  
— does not demand merit,  
— does not impose conditions.  
He simply removes what  
kept you from being what  
you are always.  
As a cloud goes away  
and reveals the sun.

</div>

#### 234. Why can the new birth not be brought about by meditation, prayer, or practice

<div class="lineated verse">

Any practice is an act of the mind.  
And the new birth is the cessation of the mind as center.  
Meditation can cleanse.  
Prayer can soften.  
Asceticism can discipline.  
But no effort  
can destroy the one  
who makes that effort.  
Birth from the Spirit  
is not the result of a path.  
It is the end of the path.  
And the beginning of vision.

</div>

#### 235. Why the new birth always feels like recognition

<div class="lineated verse">

Because you do not become the one,  
whom you were not.  
You become the one,  
whom you have always been.  
A man says:  
“I found God.”  
But in truth it sounds like this:  
“I found the One,  
Who has always been me.”  
This is not an acquisition.  
It is remembrance.  
Not a discovery.  
But recognition.

</div>

#### 236. Last: the birth from above is a birth in the Spirit, where there is no “I,” but there is Life

<div class="lineated verse">

This is not a mystical experience.  
This is not the achievement of an elite.  
This is not a reward for righteousness.  
It is a return to Truth.  
And Truth is simple:  
You are not separate beings,  
seeking God.  
You are the breath of God,  
temporarily forgetting  
who it is.  
And when the Holy Spirit comes,  
you are born not as new people,  
but as that  
which you were  
before any story.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF DISCERNMENT (how the spirit gives the ability to see truth without error)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you that  
which has always been one of the greatest gifts of the Spirit —  
the ability to discern.  
Not to reason,  
not to evaluate,  
not to analyze,  
not to guess.  
But to see.  
Discernment is the vision of the Spirit.  
And without it a man remains blind,  
even if he knows Scripture by heart,  
prays daily  
and considers himself spiritual.

</div>

#### 237. What is discernment in the deepest sense

<div class="lineated verse">

Discernment is the ability to see Truth  
without the admixture of the mind.  
It is not intuition.  
Not the wisdom of experience.  
Not subtle psychology.  
Not observantness.  
Discernment is penetration  
into the very heart of reality.  
It is a gaze,  
not distorted:  
— by fear,  
— by desire,  
— by gain,  
— by the past,  
— by the opinions of people,  
— by one’s own expectations.  
Discernment is the ability of the Spirit  
to see through you.

</div>

#### 238. Why a person without the Spirit is always mistaken

<div class="lineated verse">

Because he looks not with eyes,  
but through the image of the “I”.  
He sees not reality,  
but his own projections.  
He takes:  
— fear for a warning,  
— desire for the voice of the heart,  
— habit for character,  
— thought for knowledge,  
— outward impression for essence,  
— convenience for truth,  
— inner noise for My voice.  
Without the Spirit a person sees his own shadow  
and thinks that he sees light.

</div>

#### 239. How the discernment of the Holy Spirit acts

<div class="lineated verse">

It acts instantly.  
Discernment does not analyze.  
It knows.  
You do not draw a conclusion.  
You behold knowledge,  
like a light that simply flashes on.  
Discernment:  
— does not require proof,  
— does not argue,  
— does not doubt,  
— does not change with time.  
It is not an opinion.  
It is Truth.  
And when it appears,  
you feel it not with the mind,  
but with your whole being.

</div>

#### 240. Why discernment is always bound with silence

<div class="lineated verse">

Because only in silence  
is it heard  
that which cannot be heard in noise.  
The mind reasons.  
Fear whispers.  
Experience advises.  
Memory compares.  
Desire pulls.  
Emotions color.  
And only silence  
lets one see without distortion.  
Discernment is not the ability to think.  
It is the ability to be a pure space  
for truth.

</div>

#### 241. Discernment is a gaze that does not belong to man

<div class="lineated verse">

Discernment does not come from within the personality,  
but from the depths,  
where there is no personality.  
When a person says:  
“I feel that this is true” —  
that is not yet discernment.  
When he says:  
“I see this so clearly,  
that I cannot explain why” —  
this is the Spirit.  
Discernment is not tied  
either to intelligence,  
or to character,  
or to education.  
It is a manifestation of My sight.

</div>

#### 242. Why discernment does not err

<div class="lineated verse">

Because its source is I,  
and not a psychological reaction  
or an emotional impulse.  
Error occurs where  
the mind takes the word.  
But discernment comes before thought.  
This is not a conclusion.  
It is knowledge.  
It is not logic.  
It is light.  
It is not probability.  
It is precision.  
Discernment errs exactly to the extent  
that a person adds himself to it.  
The pure — never.

</div>

#### 243. How to understand that discernment is speaking, and not the mind

There are three signs:

1. **Silence within,**
2. even if the decision is hard.
3. **Absence of struggle,**
4. even if the mind does not agree.
5. **Absence of fear,**
6. even if the step is risky.

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind makes noise.  
The Spirit is clear.  
The mind cries out.  
The Spirit speaks the word quietly.  
The mind demands arguments.  
The Spirit simply knows.

</div>

#### 244. Discernment is protection from evil

<div class="lineated verse">

Not because you become stronger,  
but because evil ceases to be hidden.  
Evil always works through a mask:  
— of good intentions,  
— of beautiful words,  
— of fear of loss,  
— of a promise of gain,  
— of spiritual images.  
But the discernment of the Spirit  
sees through the mask at once.  
Darkness may deceive the mind.  
It cannot deceive the light.

</div>

#### 245. Discernment is the path of truth, even when it goes against circumstances

<div class="lineated verse">

Sometimes discernment will say:  
— “go away” where logic says “stay”;  
— “trust” where fear says “run”;  
— “wait” where desire says “now”;  
— “speak the truth” where reason says “be careful”;  
— “let go” where emotions cling.  
Discernment does not choose what is convenient.  
It chooses what is true.

</div>

#### 246. The last thing: discernment is the voice of the Holy Spirit in your life

<div class="lineated verse">

It is He who says:  
— here is falsehood;  
— here is light;  
— here is shadow;  
— here is the way;  
— here is love;  
— here is danger;  
— here am I.  
Discernment is not the ability of the chosen.  
It is an organ of perception of the Spirit.  
And when you live not by your own “I”,  
but by My light,  
discernment becomes your sight.  
You no longer wander.  
You see.  
And the one who sees  
cannot be deceived.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF LOVE (not feelings, but the divine nature that makes everything alive)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the greatest mystery,  
which has been distorted more than all the rest:  
what Love is,  
when it comes forth from the Spirit.  
People call love that  
which is bound up with attachment,  
desire,  
lack,  
emotions,  
the search for warmth,  
fear of loss  
and the need to be seen.  
But the Love of the Holy Spirit —
