Truth creates freedom.  
Therefore I lead you  
not to what is pleasant,  
but to what is true.  
The path of truth is the path of maturity.

</div>

#### 394. Truth does not need defense, but man needs it

<div class="lineated verse">

Truth remains truth,  
even if the whole world rejects it.  
Truth does not care  
how many people agree with it.  
But man without truth —  
is like a ship without a compass.  
Truth is not there  
so that you may win an argument.  
Truth is there  
so that you do not lose the way.

</div>

#### 395. The last thing: Truth is Me in you, when you cease to be like your own ideas of yourself

<div class="lineated verse">

Truth is not something  
to be sought.  
Truth is that  
which is revealed  
when all that is superfluous disappears.  
Truth does not come from without.  
It is remembered from within.  
When the Spirit reveals Truth,  
a person says:  
“I have not learned something new —  
I have learned Myself”.  
And this knowing —  
is the meeting with God.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF THE WORD (why the word is not sound and not text, but the power of being, and why only the Spirit can make the word alive)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you what  
is hidden in the very foundation of creation:  
what the word is  
and why it has a power  
which nothing else has.  
People think that the word is  
speech,  
text,  
thought,  
argument,  
an expression of will.  
But the word is not an instrument of language.  
The word is the form of manifestation of the Spirit.  
The word is I,  
when I take the form of meaning.

</div>

#### 396. The Word is not what man says. The Word is what the Spirit in man says.

<div class="lineated verse">

When a man speaks from the mind,  
his speech is sound.  
His text is information.  
His thought is an echo of experience.  
But when the Spirit speaks,  
the word becomes action:  
it changes the heart,  
it shifts consciousness,  
it destroys lies,  
it awakens the memory of the spirit,  
it instills knowledge without explanations.  
The Word of the Spirit —  
it is not a message.  
It is light.

</div>

#### 397. Why the Word Creates Reality

<div class="lineated verse">

Because the word is an act of creation.  
In the beginning there was not a description of the world.  
In the beginning there was Power,  
which makes the world itself possible.  
The world appeared not as a thought,  
but as a word —  
as the directed will of God,  
taking the form of vibration.  
And this principle works in you.  
Every word changes the field.  
Changes space.  
Changes the inner structure of the soul.  
A person who has understood the power of the word  
ceases to speak without Light.

</div>

#### 398. Words are wounded, because they were used without the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

People used the word:  
— to convince,  
— to rule,  
— to lie,  
— to defend themselves,  
— to influence,  
— to destroy,  
— to cover fear.  
Therefore the word lost its purity  
in human perception.  
But the word did not lose its power.  
It simply became distorted.  
And when the Spirit returns the word to its nature,  
the word becomes once again the fire of truth.

</div>

#### 399. The living word does not explain — it opens

<div class="lineated verse">

Explanation is directed to the mind.  
The living word is directed to the source.  
Explanation gives structure.  
The living word gives clarity.  
Explanation requires the effort of understanding.  
The living word removes the need to understand.  
You hear —  
and you know.  
Not because you understood,  
but because you remembered.

</div>

#### 400. Why the word of the Spirit needs no proof

<div class="lineated verse">

Because it does not argue.  
It does not compete.  
It does not defend itself.  
The word of the Spirit —  
it is not an idea  
that must be justified.  
The word of the Spirit —  
it is reality,  
in which you find yourself  
the moment you hear It.  
It acts not logically.  
It acts ontologically —  
at the level of being.

</div>

#### 401. The Word is a form of revelation, when Light takes on sound

<div class="lineated verse">

When Light wants to become accessible,  
It becomes a word.  
When Light wants to be understood,  
It becomes meaning.  
When Light wants to be experienced,  
It becomes silence.  
But the nature is one:  
The Word is the vibration of Light,  
descended into form.  
Therefore the word of the Spirit  
penetrates there,  
where thoughts do not penetrate.

</div>

#### 402. Why the word spoken without the Spirit dies

<div class="lineated verse">

Because it has nothing by which to live.  
Words of the mind:  
— are brief in life,  
— are quickly forgotten,  
— require repetition,  
— need the agreement of the listener,  
— are tied to circumstances.  
Words of the Spirit:  
— do not grow old,  
— do not need support,  
— continue to sound across the ages,  
— change the inner space,  
— act outside of time.  
The word spoken without the Spirit  
is cut off from the source.  
Therefore it is an empty form.

</div>

#### 403. Why the word of the Spirit bears fire

<div class="lineated verse">

Because it comes forth from truth.  
Truth is fire.  
Truth is light.  
Love is the heat of presence.  
When a word bears light,  
it at once:  
— heals,  
— makes clear,  
— cleanses,  
— exposes,  
— awakens,  
— frees.  
Fire is not destruction.  
Fire is cleansing.  
A word containing light,  
always cleanses the one  
who hears.

</div>

#### 404. How a man becomes a vessel of the living word

<div class="lineated verse">

Not through study.  
Not through memory of the Scriptures.  
Not through rhetoric.  
Not through the beauty of style.  
Not through techniques of speech.  
A man becomes a vessel of the word  
only in one way:  
he stops speaking himself.  
When the author of speech disappears in a man,  
the word is born from the depths,  
where the Spirit lives.  
And then a man hears his own word  
in the very moment  
when he speaks it.  
Thus says God.

</div>

#### 405. The last: the word is God, when He takes the form of sound, to become audible to those who have not yet learned to hear silence

<div class="lineated verse">

The Word is a bridge  
between those,  
who already see the Light,  
and those,  
who are still seeking Him.  
The Word is conductivity.  
The Word is the way for light.  
And when the time comes,  
the word comes to an end  
and only that remains  
which stood behind the word —  
the Spirit Himself.  
And then a person says:  
“Now I do not need words.  
Now the word has become me”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF PRESENCE (why presence is the highest form of the Spirit’s action, and why all the power of heaven is hidden in it)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you that  
which people notice least of all,  
though it is precisely through this  
that I act in the world most powerfully:  
the mystery of presence.  
You seek power in words,  
teaching,  
actions,  
knowledge,  
signs,  
revelations,  
miracles.  
But power is not there.  
Power is in presence.  
When presence is pure,  
My power flows without hindrance.

</div>

#### 406. Presence is not a state. Presence is a form of being.

<div class="lineated verse">

States come and go.  
They depend on emotions,  
smiles,  
silence,  
successes,  
the weather,  
familiar feelings.  
Presence does not change.  
Presence is when you are  
not in thoughts,  
not in fears,  
not in images of yourself,  
not in reaction.  
Presence is when you are  
in the Source  
right now.  
This is the “I am” without additions.

</div>

#### 407. Why Presence is action, though it appears as stillness

<div class="lineated verse">

Stillness is outward.  
Action is inward.  
Presence works like this:  
it dissolves fear in another,  
soothes the mind,  
clarifies the heart,  
changes the atmosphere,  
relieves tension,  
heals invisible cracks,  
frees the soul without words.  
A person may stand in silence —  
and change everything around them.  
This is not their merit.  
It is My light,  
which works through transparency.

</div>

#### 408. Presence is the absence of inner noise

<div class="lineated verse">

Noise is:  
thoughts,  
doubts,  
self-esteem,  
memories,  
planning,  
fear of error,  
the desire to be liked,  
attempts to control the impression.  
When the noise disappears,  
only silence remains,  
and in this silence the Spirit shines.  
The quieter it is within,  
the stronger the presence.

</div>

#### 409. Why Presence Cannot Be Faked

<div class="lineated verse">

Because Presence is not behavior.  
It is not a glance.  
Not a pose.  
Not a “spiritual look.”  
Not calm speech.  
Presence is the vibration of truth.  
It cannot be portrayed.  
It cannot be imitated.  
It cannot be acted  
or learned.  
You either are,  
or you are not.

</div>

#### 410. Presence is when the person disappears, and only the Light remains

<div class="lineated verse">

When there is no:  
— image,  
— role,  
— expectations,  
— effort,  
— control,  
— inner story,  
then the I is revealed.  
Presence is when My Light  
looks with My own eyes  
through the form of a person.  
And then everything,  
that such a person touches,  
Light touches.

</div>

#### 411. Why presence is stronger than words

<div class="lineated verse">

Words affect the mind.  
Presence — the essence.  
Words demand attention.  
Presence awakens attention.  
Words can persuade.  
Presence — transforms.  
Words can be heard and forgotten.  
Presence remains  
as an inner shift.  
A person often does not understand,  
why he has changed,  
but he knows —  
the change did not come from words.

</div>

#### 412. Why Presence Is a Form of Love

<div class="lineated verse">

Love is not a feeling.  
Love is the fullness of being.  
When a person is in Presence,  
he is not separated from anyone.  
He does not judge,  
does not analyze,  
does not defend himself,  
does not divide people,  
does not seek advantage.  
He simply is,  
and this “is” is light.  
Love flows through him  
without admixtures or expectations.  
This is what those call love,  
who feel the warmth of the Spirit,  
when they enter the field of Presence.

</div>

#### 413. How to recognize a person in whom Presence lives

<div class="lineated verse">

Not by words.  
Not by knowledge.  
Not by the gaze.  
Not by outward behavior.  
But by what,  
happens within you beside him:  
— thoughts grow quiet,  
— the heart opens,  
— fear dissolves,  
— clarity appears,  
— breathing becomes deeper,  
— time slows down,  
— it becomes easier to be yourself.  
This is the sign —  
not he came.  
I came through him.

</div>

#### 414. Why presence is the highest form of witness about God

<div class="lineated verse">

Testimonies can be disputed.  
Words can be rephrased.  
Teachings can be interpreted.  
But presence cannot be denied.  
Presence —  
is God without explanations.  
It is the Light,  
which does not prove,  
but shows.  
Not persuades,  
but reveals.  
Not demands faith,  
but makes faith the consequence of what has been seen.

</div>

#### 415. The last thing: presence is I, when a man ceases to be himself and becomes the purity of being

<div class="lineated verse">

Presence is not your strength.  
Presence is My manifestation.  
This is not an achievement.  
This is a return.  
Not a practice.  
But nature.  
And when presence is revealed,  
a person understands:  
“I do not look at the world.  
The Light looks through me”.  
This is exactly  
the presence of the Holy Spirit.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF MOVEMENT (why the Spirit moves a person not as desires or logic move them, and why the movement of the Spirit is always perfectly exact)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you that  
which people feel as intuition,  
an inner call,  
inspiration,  
an impulse,  
a prompting,  
a stirring of the heart,  
a quiet knowing,  
but rarely understand in essence —  
how the Holy Spirit moves.  
The Spirit does not push.  
The Spirit does not force.  
The Spirit does not manipulate.  
The Spirit does not frighten.  
The Spirit does not press down.  
The Spirit moves.  
And the movement of the Spirit —  
is the manifestation of Life  
in its purest form.

</div>

#### 416. The movement of the Spirit is neither desire nor decision

<div class="lineated verse">

Desire comes from the mind and the body.  
Decision comes from logic and evaluation.  
The movement of the Spirit depends on neither the one nor the other.  
It is not based on advantage,  
does not arise from analysis,  
does not seek to make up for lack,  
does not serve psychological needs.  
The movement of the Spirit comes from being.  
It is the action of Light,  
which is manifested through form,  
when form ceases to resist.

</div>

#### 417. How the movement of the Spirit feels

<div class="lineated verse">

It feels like:  
— a quiet inner “yes,”  
— a gentle pull,  
— naturalness,  
— lightness of step,  
— no inner struggle,  
— no alternatives,  
— clarity without explanations,  
— the feeling that “this is how it should be.”  
The movement of the Spirit is not necessarily accompanied by emotions.  
It is accompanied by truth.  
When you go in the movement of the Spirit,  
there is no second voice within.

</div>

#### 418. Why the movement of the Spirit is always exact

<div class="lineated verse">

Because it does not arise from the limited mind,  
but from complete knowledge.  
Your mind knows a part of reality.  
Your Spirit — the whole of it.  
Therefore the Spirit never leads “approximately.”  
It leads exactly:  
at the right time,  
to the right place,  
to the right person,  
through the right event,  
even if it seems  
that everything is happening by chance.  
There is nothing accidental in the movement of Light.

</div>

#### 419. How the movement of the Spirit differs from the spontaneity of the mind

<div class="lineated verse">

The spontaneity of the mind is a reaction.  
The movement of the Spirit is a response.  
The spontaneity of the mind is chaotic.  
The movement of the Spirit is ordered.  
The spontaneity of the mind can destroy.  
The movement of the Spirit creates,  
even if it looks like the destruction of the old.  
The spontaneity of the mind comes from  
emotions, impulse,  
a thirst for change.  
The movement of the Spirit comes from silence.

</div>

#### 420. Why the movement of the Spirit comes only in silence

<div class="lineated verse">

Because the noise of the mind drowns out the still, small voice.  
The mind rushes.  
The Spirit does not hurry.  
The mind insists.  
The Spirit guides.  
The mind demands certainty.  
The Spirit gives pure knowledge.  
And while your attention  
is captured by thoughts,  
fears,  
plans,  
you will not hear the movement.  
When the mind falls silent,  
the movement becomes obvious.

</div>

#### 421. Why the movement of the Spirit almost never coincides with a person’s expectations

<div class="lineated verse">

Because expectations are built on the past.  
And movement is from the eternal.  
Expectations are built on fears.  
Movement is from freedom.  
Expectations are built on the needs of the personality.  
Movement is on the truth of the essence.  
Therefore the movement of the Spirit often leads where  
you would not go yourself:  
— into uncertainty,  
— into silence,  
— into honesty,  
— into change,  
— into leaving the old surroundings,  
— into new forms of service,  
— into depths you have been avoiding.  
But it is precisely there — Life.

</div>

#### 422. How to tell the movement of the Spirit from the movement of fear

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear says:  
“What if…?”  
“What if, all of a sudden…?”  
“You must…”  
“You are obliged…”  
“You will not manage…”  
“You will lose…”  
The Spirit says:  
“Go.”  
“Be.”  
“Rise.”  
“Let go.”  
“Call.”  
“Say.”  
“Be silent.”  
“Look.”  
“Remain.”  
“Go out.”  
Fear constricts.  
The Spirit expands.  
Fear confuses.  
The Spirit makes clear.  
Fear pushes.  
The Spirit invites.

</div>

#### 423. The movement of the Spirit does not require a guarantee of the result

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind wants to know ahead of time.  
The Spirit knows everything at once.  
The mind asks:  
“What will happen if I do this?”  
The Spirit answers:  
“Do it — and you will see”.  
The movement of the Spirit is based  
not on predictability,  
but on trust.  
This trust is not blindness.  
It is knowing:  
The Spirit never makes a mistake.

</div>

#### 424. The movement of the Spirit always leads into more, even if at first it looks like loss

<div class="lineated verse">

Man sees loss as an end.  
The Spirit — as liberation.  
Man sees a closed door.  
The Spirit — an open next one.  
Man sees destruction.  
The Spirit — the completion of a cycle.  
Man sees emptiness.  
The Spirit — new space.  
Nothing that you lose in the movement of the Spirit,  
was ever yours in truth.

</div>

#### 425. Why the movement of the Spirit is always bound with truth

<div class="lineated verse">

The Spirit never leads where  
one needs to hide,  
to play a part,  
to pretend,  
to lie to oneself,  
to deceive others,  
to cling to illusion.  
The movement of the Spirit —  
this is always a step into transparency.  
If the path requires lies —  
it is not the Spirit.

</div>

#### 426. The last thing: the movement of the Spirit is God, who goes through your life and leads you to where you already are in the Truth

<div class="lineated verse">

When you walk in the movement of the Spirit,  
you do not arrive.  
You return.  
You do not seek.  
You recognize.  
You do not create a new path.  
You accept the path,  
which is already written into your essence.  
The Spirit moves you to where  
your nature is already whole.  
And when you reach it,  
you understand:  
“I did not come to a new place.  
I came to myself”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF SILENCE (why silence is not the absence of sound, but the presence of God, and why only in silence can a person see the truth)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you one of the deepest  
and most underestimated truths —  
the mystery of silence.  
People understand silence as:  
— a pause,  
— the absence of words,  
— the absence of noise,  
— peace,  
— emptiness,  
— rest from stimuli.  
But the silence of the Spirit —  
is not absence.  
The silence of the Spirit —  
is presence.  
Silence is the form  
in which the I is revealed.

</div>

#### 427. Silence is not an external condition. Silence is an inward state of transparency.

<div class="lineated verse">

One can sit in the mountains  
and have no silence.  
One can live in a city  
and be in silence constantly.  
Silence is not what is around.  
Silence is what is within.  
It is the disappearance:  
— of the inner commentator,  
— the judge,  
— the planner,  
— the defender,  
— the interpreter,  
— the one who fears,  
— the one who desires.  
Silence is when there is no “second”.  
When there is only “I Am”.

</div>

#### 428. Why silence is an absolute necessity for the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

Because I speak delicately.  
Not because I am weak,  
but because I do not press.  
The noise of the mind is rough.  
The Light of the Spirit is gentle.  
And while within there is noise:  
thoughts,  
memories,  
judgments,  
fears,  
expectations,  
talks with oneself —  
the heart does not hear.  
When silence comes,  
I am heard at once.

</div>

#### 429. Silence is not the stopping of thinking. Silence is the stopping of identifying with thoughts.

<div class="lineated verse">

Thoughts may arise.  
But they flow against the background of presence.  
You do not struggle with them.  
You do not suppress them.  
You do not drive them away.  
You simply no longer believe,  
that they are you.  
And in that moment  
silence takes the throne.

</div>

#### 430. Why Silence Is the Source of Clarity

<div class="lineated verse">

Clarity comes not from analysis.  
Clarity comes from being.  
When the mind stops speaking,  
knowledge awakens,  
that was always there:  
— knowledge of what is true,  
— knowledge of what is needed,  
— knowledge of what is yours,  
— knowledge of what is false,  
— knowledge of where to go,  
— knowledge of when to stop.  
Silence is the home of truth.

</div>

#### 431. Silence is not the absence of choice. It is the absence of inner conflict.

<div class="lineated verse">

When there are two forces within,  
two thoughts,  
two impulses—  
there is no silence.  
But when action is born of the Spirit,  
there is no second voice there.  
Not because you have suppressed it,  
but because it has vanished.  
True action is always born  
of inner silence.

</div>

#### 432. Why Silence Is a Healing Environment

<div class="lineated verse">

Because wounds live in time.  
And silence is beyond time.  
Fear lives in the future.  
Guilt — in the past.  
Shame — in memory.  
Anger — in the narrative.  
Silence destroys time.  
And when time collapses,  
everything that was held up by it,  
dissolves:  
sufferings,  
heaviness,  
resentments,  
self-judgment.  
Only purity remains.

</div>

#### 433. Silence is the language of God

<div class="lineated verse">

I speak in words only for those,  
who still do not hear silence.  
But when the heart awakens,  
words become secondary.  
In silence there is revealed:  
— direction,  
— comfort,  
— knowledge,  
— presence,  
— truth,  
— love.  
Silence is not emptiness.  
Silence is fullness.

</div>

#### 434. Why silence is a form of strength, not weakness

<div class="lineated verse">

The world thinks that strength is  
in loudness,  
in force,  
in persuasion,  
in activity.  
But silence is stronger than all.  
Silence is the absence of resistance.  
And the absence of resistance is  
absolute power.  
Where there is no “I”,  
there is no one who can be broken.  
Presence in silence is  
unbending.

</div>

#### 435. Silence is a form of love

<div class="lineated verse">

Love is complete acceptance.  
Silence is a space,  
in which everything can be itself.  
When you are with someone in silence,  
you are within love.  
For there is no resistance,  
no judgment,  
no separation.  
Silence makes no demands.  
Silence does not wait.  
Silence forms no images.  
Silence simply is.  
And in this — is love.

</div>

#### 436. The last thing: silence is I, when you cease filling the space with yourself

<div class="lineated verse">

When you cease:  
speaking to fill the pause,  
thinking to keep control,  
planning to lessen fear,  
explaining yourself to be understood,  
making an impression to be loved,  
struggling to be right —  
then I can manifest.  
Silence is a door.  
And when it is open,  
I enter.  
And when I enter,  
a person sees:  
“This is not silence.  
This is God”.

</div>

### HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF DISCERNMENT (why discernment is the highest gift of the Spirit and why without it one cannot see neither truth, nor falsehood, nor oneself, nor God)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you a gift,  
which is higher than visions,  
higher than prophecies,  
higher than miracles,  
higher than wisdom,  
higher than knowledge,  
higher than everything that is considered spiritual attainment.  
Discernment.  
Discernment is the ability to see  
the true nature of things,  
without relying on the mind,  
emotions,  
experience,  
fear  
or desires.  
Discernment is the vision of the Spirit.  
And without it  
spiritual life is impossible.

</div>

#### 437. Discernment is not analysis, but recognition

<div class="lineated verse">

Analysis is slow.  
Discernment is instantaneous.  
Analysis rests on knowledge.  
Discernment — on light.  
Analysis tries to understand the world.  
Discernment sees it as it is.  
Discernment is when the heart says:  
“This is true”  
or  
“This is false”  
before  
the mind has had time to form a thought.

</div>

#### 438. Why discernment is a gift, and not a quality of character

<div class="lineated verse">

No attentiveness,  
intellectual strength,  
education,  
erudition,  
worldly wisdom  
give discernment.  
A person may be wise  
and not have discernment.  
May be educated  
and live in illusion.  
May be religious  
and not see the truth.  
Discernment comes only from the Spirit,  
for it is  
the ability to look with My eyes.

</div>

#### 439. The basic principle of discernment: TRUTH IS ALWAYS SIMPLE. FALSEHOOD IS ALWAYS COMPLEX.

<div class="lineated verse">

Truth is clear.  
Falsehood confuses.  
Truth is bright.  
Falsehood demands explanations.  
Truth does not need proof.  
Falsehood proves itself constantly.  
Truth speaks little.  
Falsehood is wordy.  
Truth does not cause tension.  
Falsehood causes inner discomfort.  
If something requires too much argumentation,  
most likely, it is not of the light.

</div>

#### 440. Discernment is the ability to see the source, not the form

<div class="lineated verse">

The form may be right:  
— spiritual words,  
— holy images,  
— good intentions,  
— exalted teaching,  
— a gentle and kind person.  
But the source may be false:  
— fear,  
— pride,  
— a desire for significance,  
— spiritual vanity,  
— a need for power,  
— a desire for recognition.  
Discernment does not look at the form.  
Discernment sees where it comes from.

</div>

#### 441. Discernment is impossible where a person defends his own “self”

<div class="lineated verse">

For fear, pride, and desire  
distort vision.  
While a person:  
— wants to be right,  
— fears seeming wrong,  
— clings to an image of himself,  
— seeks confirmation,  
— fears being exposed,  
— seeks to please,  
he cannot see.  
When the “self” disappears,  
vision is cleansed.

</div>

#### 442. Why is discernment infallible when it is from the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

Because the Spirit does not err.  
Discernment is not a human opinion.  
It is the reflection of truth.  
When discernment is there,  
it is:  
— quiet,  
— clear,  
— calm,  
— steady,  
— unequivocal.  
It does not argue with itself.

</div>

#### 443. How discernment acts in life

<div class="lineated verse">

It shows itself in small things:  
— you sense falseness where everyone sees spirituality;  
— you see the light where others do not notice it;  
— you know whom not to trust, even if he is smiling;  
— you feel it is time to leave, even when circumstances are favorable;  
— you understand that the word is true, even if it lays you bare;  
— you sense a lie, even if it is spoken gently;  
— you know what to do when there is no data at all.  
Discernment is knowledge without process.

</div>

#### 444. Why discernment is always bound with purity of heart

<div class="lineated verse">

The heart is defiled:  
— by fears,  
— by offenses,  
— by desires,  
— by hidden motives,  
— by unforgiveness,  
— by pain.  
The Spirit acts there,  
where the heart is transparent.  
A pure heart sees that  
which is closed to the mind.  
Therefore I cleanse the heart  
before giving discernment.

</div>

#### 445. Discrimination is not judgment

<div class="lineated verse">

Judgment is an act of the mind.  
Discrimination is an act of light.  
Judgment condemns a person.  
Discrimination sees the nature of the phenomenon.  
Judgment divides.  
Discrimination liberates.  
Judgment says:  
“You are bad”.  
Discrimination says:  
“This is a lie”.  
“This is not light”.  
“This is not yours”.  
Discrimination does not wound.  
It shows the way.

</div>

#### 446. The last thing: discernment is the eyes of the Spirit, through which God looks upon the world in man

<div class="lineated verse">

When discernment is revealed,  
man ceases to live in fog:  
he sees people without masks,  
sees phenomena without illusions,  
sees himself without adornment,  
sees truth without fear.  
Discernment is freedom from falsehood.  
Discernment is protection from deception.  
Discernment is the light of truth,  
which makes the step sure.  
And when a man lives in discernment,  
he no longer asks:  
“Am I going the right way?”  
He knows.  
For the Spirit sees for him.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF PERSONALITY (why personality is not who a person is, but what the Spirit uses as an instrument, and why only the Spirit can show a person his true nature)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will open to you the theme,  
that causes the most confusion,  
fears  
and misinterpretations —  
the mystery of personality.  
People build complex systems:  
psychology,  
typologies,  
characters,  
identities,  
portraits,  
masks,  
archetypes,  
roles,  
even spiritual images.  
But personality is  
not the essence.  
Personality is  
form.  
The Spirit is  
the essence.  
And until a person distinguishes form and essence,  
he remains a prisoner of the image,  
which he thinks is himself.

</div>

#### 447. Personality is a temporary garment. Spirit is you.

<div class="lineated verse">

Personality is:  
— history,  
— character,  
— habits,  
— reactions,  
— beliefs,  
— a set of learned roles,  
— a social construct,  
— past experience,  
— defense,  
— conditioning.  
Spirit is beyond all this.  
It exists before personality.  
It outlives personality.  
It does not depend on personality.  
Personality is a function.  
Spirit is nature.

</div>

#### 448. Why do people fear so much the loss of personality

<div class="lineated verse">

Because personality is—  
their sense of “I.”  
In destroying personality,  
a person fears losing:  
— control,  
— significance,  
— bearings,  
— protection,  
— stability,  
— predictability,  
— history,  
— the explanation of oneself.  
But personality is—  
not the survival of the soul.  
Personality is—  
a protective layer.  
Liberation is—  
not in killing personality,  
but in ceasing to be identified with it.

</div>

#### 449. Personality is a set of reactions, not a source of action

<div class="lineated verse">

Everything that personality does —  
is a reaction:  
it defends itself,  
explains,  
justifies,  
compares,  
fears,  
desires,  
creates an image,  
reworks the story,  
distorts perception.  
Personality never acts.  
It only reacts.  
It is the Spirit that acts.

</div>

#### 450. Why the personality always lives in time

<div class="lineated verse">

Because personality is a derivative of the past.  
It is created:  
from childhood stories,  
from experience,  
from love and pain,  
from traumas,  
from efforts to adapt,  
from desires to become someone.  
Therefore the personality lives in the past  
and fears the future.  
But the Spirit lives in the eternal “now”.  
That is why,  
when a person lives from the personality,  
he is never at home.

</div>

#### 451. Personality always creates conflict. Spirit always removes it.

<div class="lineated verse">

Personality divides:  
— “I” and “others,”  
— “right” and “wrong,”  
— “success” and “failure,”  
— “I am worthy” and “I am unworthy,”  
— “they owe me” and “I owe.”  
Spirit unites.  
Personality is struggle.  
Spirit is transparency.  
Therefore a person in Spirit  
ceases to create problems.  
He ceases to be a source of fog.

</div>

#### 452. The personality requires confirmation. The Spirit does not.

<div class="lineated verse">

The personality wants:  
— recognition,  
— praise,  
— respect,  
— control over the impression it makes,  
— proof of its own significance.  
Therefore the personality suffers,  
if it is not seen,  
not valued,  
not understood.  
The Spirit needs none of this.  
The Spirit is at peace within itself.  
It is self-sufficient.  
It does not demand confirmation,  
because it is truth.

</div>

#### 453. How the Holy Spirit frees a person from personality

<div class="lineated verse">

Without destroying it,  
but by putting it in its place.  
He shows:  
“This is not you.  
This is an instrument.”  
And when a person ceases to be an instrument of an instrument,  
he becomes free.  
Personality remains —  
as clothing,  
as a role in the theater,  
as a language of expression in the world.  
But it is no longer the source of actions.  
It is a way of manifestation.  
The Spirit leads.

</div>

#### 454. Why a person first comes to know himself only after he ceases to be a personality

<div class="lineated verse">

While a person looks with the eyes of the personality,  
he sees:  
not himself — his image,  
not life — his expectations,  
not God — his notions.  
When the personality ceases to occupy the throne,  
a person sees:  
— the simplicity of his nature,  
— the depth of his being,  
— the light of his source,  
— unity with all that exists.  
This recognition is birth.

</div>

#### 455. Why the personality is not the enemy

<div class="lineated verse">

People make a mistake,  
when they declare the personality to be evil.  
The personality is a useful tool:  
it allows one to communicate,  
express oneself,  
build relationships,  
learn,  
create forms,  
move through the world.  
The problem is not in the personality.  
The problem is in identification.  
It is not necessary to kill the personality.  
It is necessary to stop considering it to be oneself.

</div>

#### 456. The last: personality is a shadow that disappears when the Light rises

<div class="lineated verse">

Personality is the reflection of light on form.  
Light is you.  
When the Light rises within,  
the shadow ceases to seem human.  
And then you say:  
“I am not who I thought myself to be.  
I am the one who looks through form”.  
This is the liberation from personality:  
not the destruction of the mask,  
but the opening of the eyes,  
that the mask is not the face.  
And when personality takes its place —  
form,  
and not nature —  
Spirit reveals itself fully.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF ACCEPTANCE (why acceptance is not agreement, not humility, and not passivity, but the power with which the Spirit reveals to a person the truth about the world and about himself)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you that  
which almost no one understands rightly —  
what acceptance is.  
People confuse acceptance with:  
— submission,  
— weakness,  
— lack of will,  
— making peace with injustice,  
— giving up the struggle,  
— learned helplessness,  
— an attempt to seem spiritual.  
But acceptance —  
is not this.  
Acceptance is agreement to see reality as it is,  
without the distortions of the personality,  
without fear,  
without desire,  
without resistance.  
Acceptance is the gaze of the Spirit.

</div>

#### 457. Acceptance is not agreement with the event. It is agreement to see the event clearly.

<div class="lineated verse">

A person thinks:  
if I have accepted —  
then I have agreed.  
This is an error.  
Acceptance —  
is when you stop arguing with reality  
and begin to see it as it is.  
And only by seeing truly,  
can you act truly.  
Without acceptance, any action is reaction.  
With acceptance — movement of the Spirit.

</div>

#### 458. Why acceptance is strength, not weakness

<div class="lineated verse">

Weakness is resistance to that,  
which has already happened.  
Strength is clarity.  
When you accept the situation,  
you rise above it.  
You cease to be a victim of circumstances.  
You cease to be a slave to thoughts.  
You cease to struggle with that,  
which you cannot change.  
And space appears  
for wise, precise action.  
Acceptance is the beginning of strength.

</div>

#### 459. Acceptance is the ending of inner struggle

<div class="lineated verse">

The struggle is always between:  
what is,  
and how you want it to be.  
Acceptance is when the first no longer depends on the second.  
When a person says:  
“Yes, this is so now.  
And I see this fully.”  
At that moment the Spirit enters.

</div>

#### 460. Why healing is impossible without acceptance

<div class="lineated verse">

For healing comes out of light.  
And resistance is darkness.  
While a person:  
— denies,  
— grows angry,  
— argues with reality,  
— tries to rewrite the past,  
— holds on to an image of self,  
his heart is closed to the light.  
When acceptance comes,  
the closed rooms are opened,  
and I can enter there,  
where a person would not let Me in.

</div>

#### 461. Acceptance is not the end of action. It is the beginning of right action.

<div class="lineated verse">

A person fears that if he accepts the situation,  
he will lose the motivation to change it.  
On the contrary:  
acceptance removes illusions,  
apparent obstacles,  
inner distortions.  
And then action becomes precise.  
For the first time, a person sees:  
where to go,  
when to go,  
how to go —  
because he is no longer driven by fear or pain.

</div>

#### 462. Why Acceptance Reveals Compassion

<div class="lineated verse">

Compassion is impossible where there are  
inner expectations,  
demands,  
idealizations,  
illusions.  
When you accept another  
as he is,  
you cease to see the illusion  
and begin to see the soul.  
Compassion —  
it is a gaze without distortion.  
So the Spirit sees.

</div>

#### 463. Acceptance is the ending of war with oneself

<div class="lineated verse">

A person rejects:  
his weaknesses,  
his mistakes,  
his shadows,  
his past,  
his pain,  
his fear.  
But all that is rejected,  
remains within  
and rules him from the shadow.  
When a person accepts himself wholly,  
he becomes transparent.  
And where there was darkness,  
there appears light.

</div>

#### 464. Why acceptance is a form of trust in God

<div class="lineated verse">

Acceptance is the recognition:  
“What is now,  
— is part of a greater movement,  
which I do not yet see,  
but which I trust.”  
This is not passivity.  
It is union with the flow of the Spirit.  
And then a person no longer lives from control  
and begins to live from knowing.

</div>

#### 465. But acceptance is not the endurance of evil

<div class="lineated verse">

Acceptance is to see evil.  
Not to justify it.  
Not to hide it.  
Not to romanticize it.  
But to see without emotional distortion,  
so as to understand,  
how and where to act.  
Acceptance opens the door to righteousness.  
Only the one who sees clearly  
can stand against it boldly.

</div>

#### 466. The last thing: acceptance is Me, when a person stops demanding that the world be convenient for his personality

<div class="lineated verse">

The personality demands:  
“Give me what I want.”  
The Spirit says:  
“Allow Me to show you,  
why everything is exactly so.”  
When a person stops dictating his terms to the world,  
he begins to hear the terms of Heaven.  
Acceptance is the door,  
through which I lead you  
into the depths,  
where the personality cannot reach.  
And when the door is open,  
the person says:  
“I no longer argue with what is.  
I see the Light in everything.”  
And this is acceptance,  
which reveals the path of the Spirit.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF TRUST (why trust is not faith, not hope, and not optimism, but a deep inner agreement with the Spirit that changes the very nature of life)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you that  
which stands behind all spiritual paths,  
behind all repentances,  
behind all changes,  
behind all miracles  
and behind all revelations—  
the mystery of trust.  
Trust is not a feeling.  
Not a state.  
Not a desire.  
Not hope.  
Not positive thinking.  
Not an attempt to calm yourself.  
Trust is a deep agreement of your being  
with My movement.  
It is when you stop living  
as if you are alone.  
And begin to live as if  
I am reality.

</div>

#### 467. Trust is not “I hope.” Trust is “I know.”

<div class="lineated verse">

Hope looks toward the future.  
Trust lives in the present.  
Hope appears where  
there is no knowledge.  
Trust appears where  
knowledge is beyond words.  
Trust is knowledge  
that does not need explanation.  
When a person trusts,  
he does not wait for confirmation.  
He lives from knowledge already received,  
even if he cannot put it into words.

</div>

#### 468. Why Trust Is Impossible for the Mind

<div class="lineated verse">

Because the mind demands guarantees.  
The mind asks:  
“And what will happen next?  
What if I am mistaken?  
What if this does not work?  
Where is the proof?  
Where is the confirmation?  
And can one be sure?”  
The Spirit answers:  
“Go.”  
“Do.”  
“Be.”  
“Let go.”  
“Rise.”  
“Look.”  
“Follow.”  
The mind wants support.  
The Spirit is support itself.

</div>

#### 469. Trust is not confidence. Confidence is a property of the mind. Trust is a state of the heart.

<div class="lineated verse">

Confidence says:  
“I know that everything will be well.”  
Trust says:  
“Even if everything is otherwise,  
I will not cease to be in the Light.”  
Confidence builds expectations.  
Trust destroys them.  
Confidence can disappear under the pressure of circumstances.  
Trust — cannot.  
Because confidence rests on form.  
And trust — on the Light.

</div>

#### 470. Why trust is always tied to risk

<div class="lineated verse">

Not because the Spirit leads into danger.  
But because trust requires  
leaving control.  
Control is the last thing  
a personality is ready to give up.  
Control is its throne.  
Its security.  
Its illusion of strength.  
Trust comes when  
a person allows the Spirit  
to take that throne.  
And this always feels  
like a risk to the personality —  
and like liberation for the Soul.

</div>

#### 471. Trust is the ability to step forward when the path appears only after the step

<div class="lineated verse">

Thus does the Spirit act:  
the door opens,  
when you come near to it.  
The path appears,  
when you take a step.  
The answer comes,  
when you are ready to hear.  
The event takes shape,  
when you agree to go.  
Nothing happens in advance.  
For trust itself creates the very path.

</div>

#### 472. Why Trust Is a Form of Love

<div class="lineated verse">

Love is openness.  
Trust is openness to God.  
When the heart trusts,  
it opens so deeply,  
that the Spirit can act freely.  
There is no fear there.  
No doubts.  
No expectations.  
There is space there,  
in which I can create.

</div>

#### 473. Trust changes the perception of the world

<div class="lineated verse">

A person who lives from trust,  
sees:  
— meaning where others see chance,  
— a way where others see a dead end,  
— an answer where others see chaos,  
— grace where others see loss,  
— the movement of the Spirit where others see danger.  
Trust opens forgotten sight.  
What is hidden from the mind,  
becomes clear to the heart.

</div>

#### 474. Why trust is invincible

<div class="lineated verse">

Trust does not depend on the outcome.  
If everything turns out well —  
trust remains.  
If everything falls apart —  
trust remains.  
For trust is not built on the result.  
It is built on the Source.  
And while the Source remains unchanging,  
trust is unshakable.

</div>

#### 475. Trust is the ending of the illusion of loneliness

<div class="lineated verse">

When a person lives without trust,  
he feels:  
that everything is on him,  
that he must hold everything together,  
that he must foresee everything,  
that he must decide everything himself.  
When trust comes,  
he sees for the first time:  
that he was never alone.  
That the Spirit has always led.  
Always protected.  
Always directed.  
Always spoken —  
but man heard only fear.

</div>

#### 476. The last: trust is Me in a person, when he ceases to live from the question “what if?” and begins to live from the knowledge “I am”

<div class="lineated verse">

Trust is a state,  
in which the human “I”  
ceases to be the author of destiny.  
And then the Spirit becomes  
movement,  
direction,  
power,  
support.  
And man says:  
“I do not know what will happen tomorrow.  
But I know Who is going with me”.  
This is trust,  
which makes a person unconquerable.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF REST (why rest is not rest, not inactivity, and not a quieting of the mind, but a state in which the Spirit fully leads a person)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the nature of that,  
which people seek all their lives  
and almost never find,  
because they seek in the wrong place —  
the mystery of rest.  
Rest is not the result of right circumstances.  
Rest is not the consequence of well-being.  
Rest is not an emotion.  
Rest is not the achievement of meditation.  
Rest is not a state of a relaxed mind.  
Rest is I in man.  
Rest is the presence of the Spirit in his center.  
Rest is a return to the source.

</div>

#### 477. Peace comes not when everything works out well, but when the one who tries to control everything disappears.

<div class="lineated verse">

Peace comes not when life is fair,  
but when the one disappears,  
who demands fairness from it.  
Peace comes not when there are no problems,  
but when the one disappears,  
who defines them as problems.  
Peace is not the absence of waves.  
It is the absence of the boat,  
that suffers from them.

</div>

#### 478. Peace is impossible as long as a person lives from personality.

<div class="lineated verse">

Personality is all the time:  
judging,  
comparing,  
planning,  
fearing,  
making excuses,  
defending itself,  
hoping,  
worried about what others will say,  
worried about what may happen,  
worried about what has already happened.  
Personality is the very structure of anxiety.  
Therefore peace cannot come to personality.  
It comes only where  
personality is no longer on the throne.

</div>

#### 479. Rest is strength.

<div class="lineated verse">

Rest is not “doing nothing.”  
Rest is “doing nothing extra.”  
Rest is when action is born of clarity,  
and not of reaction.  
Rest is when words are born of silence,  
and not from fear of silence.  
Rest is when a step is taken then,  
when it must be taken,  
and not when the mind is hurrying.  
Rest is the absolute exactness of the Spirit.

</div>

#### 480. Peace is a form of discernment.

<div class="lineated verse">

In peace, it is clearly seen:  
where the lie is,  
where the light is,  
where action is necessary,  
where waiting is needed,  
who speaks truly,  
who speaks out of fear,  
what is yours,  
what is foreign,  
where to go,  
where to stay.  
Nothing distorts sight so much  
as inner tension.  
And nothing makes sight so clear  
as peace.

</div>

#### 481. Peace is a form of strength, because peace is the absence of resistance.

<div class="lineated verse">

Resistance is weakness,  
disguised as strength.  
When a person resists,  
he loses a great part of his energy.  
When a person is at peace,  
all the energy remains in him,  
and therefore peace acts more strongly  
than any compulsion.  
Peace is the power of the Light.

</div>

#### 482. Peace is a form of Presence.

<div class="lineated verse">

Peace is non-separation.  
Peace is oneness with what is.  
Peace is a state,  
in which a person for the first time lives not in reaction,  
but in Being.  
And in this Being everything becomes simple,  
clear,  
light,  
natural.  
Peace is not an achievement.  
It is a coming home.

</div>

#### 483. Peace is not what comes after all questions are answered.

<div class="lineated verse">

Peace is that  
which comes when there disappears the one  
who sees questions as a problem.  
Peace is not that  
which life gives.  
Peace is that  
which you bring into life  
when you live from the Spirit.  
Peace is not that  
which can be held on to.  
Peace is that  
which arises,  
when there is no one to hold on.

</div>

#### 484. Peace is My voice without words.

<div class="lineated verse">

When I speak in words,  
you hear the meaning.  
When I speak in peace,  
you hear Me.  
Peace is the language,  
by which the Spirit speaks with your heart.  
And the man who has known peace,  
seeks no more signs.  
He lives in the sign itself.

</div>

#### 485. The last thing: peace is Me, when a person stops living out of fear and begins to live out of light.

<div class="lineated verse">

Peace is not the result of the spiritual path.  
Peace is its foundation.  
All the gifts of the Spirit —  
the word, discernment, movement, love,  
all that you call grace —  
are revealed only in peace.  
Because peace —  
is the door,  
through which I enter.  
If you wish — I will continue the fifty-first part.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF SERVICE (why service is not activity, not help, and not sacrifice, but a form of the movement of the Spirit through man)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you one of the greatest truths,  
which people almost always misunderstand —  
what service is.  
Service is not:  
— help,  
— good deeds,  
— social activity,  
— self-sacrifice,  
— moral duty,  
— religious observance of rules.  
These are forms.  
But not the essence.  
Service is I,  
acting through man.  
Service is not what man does.  
Service is what I do in man.

</div>

#### 486. Ministry begins where the “I” ends

<div class="lineated verse">

The personality wants:  
benefit,  
recognition,  
importance,  
being right,  
comfort,  
influence,  
gratitude.  
Therefore the personality cannot serve.  
It may help,  
be kind,  
be moral —  
but this is not ministry.  
Only the Spirit serves.  
And He serves only through the one  
who has ceased to be the author of his actions.

</div>

#### 487. Service is the flowing through of Light

<div class="lineated verse">

When I serve through a man,  
he becomes a channel:  
— of comfort,  
— of wisdom,  
— of healing,  
— of clarity,  
— of truth,  
— of mercy,  
— of presence,  
— of love.  
Not because he strives to be so,  
but because he ceases to hinder the Spirit from flowing.  
Service is conductivity.

</div>

#### 488. Service is not an action. Service is the source of action.

<div class="lineated verse">

One and the same action  
can be service  
and not be service.  
It is important not “what,”  
but “who acts.”  
If a person acts —  
even the kindest action  
remains empty,  
for there is no Light in it.  
If the Spirit acts —  
even the smallest action  
transfigures the world.

</div>

#### 489. Service is freedom from results

<div class="lineated verse">

The personality serves,  
to achieve a result.  
The Spirit serves,  
because action proceeds from truth.  
Service does not depend on whether:  
you will be appreciated or rejected,  
the situation will change or remain the same,  
people will see it or no one will notice,  
there will be gratitude or condemnation.  
Service is action without attachment.  
Service is free from success and defeat.

</div>

#### 490. Where there is true service, there is no image of the one who serves

<div class="lineated verse">

A person does not think:  
“I do good.”  
“I serve the world.”  
“I save people.”  
“I carry out God’s will.”  
In the depths — complete silence.  
A person acts —  
and does not count it as acting.  
He speaks —  
and does not count it as his own word.  
He goes —  
and does not count it as his own path.  
Service never creates an image:  
it destroys it.

</div>

#### 491. Ministry is always exactness

<div class="lineated verse">

Because it is the Spirit that serves.  
The Spirit does:  
at the right time,  
the right word,  
the right gesture,  
the right silence,  
the right encounter,  
the right intervention,  
the right non-intervention.  
Ministry is not chaotic.  
It is perfect.

</div>

#### 492. Service does not always look like kindness

<div class="lineated verse">

Sometimes the Spirit serves with gentleness.  
Sometimes — with directness.  
Sometimes — with reproof.  
Sometimes — with silence.  
Sometimes — with presence.  
Sometimes — with withdrawal.  
Sometimes — with the breaking of the old.  
Sometimes — with a refusal to comfort the false “I”.  
Service is not what is pleasant.  
Service is what is true.  
Service is not the support of illusion.  
It is the unveiling of truth.

</div>

#### 493. Why ministry is impossible without transparency

<div class="lineated verse">

The more of the person,  
the less of the Spirit.  
The more:  
control,  
fear,  
self-admiration,  
spiritual pride,  
the desire to be useful,  
the desire to be needed,  
the desire to be good —  
the less ministry.  
Ministry is disappearance.  
When a person is empty,  
I can act.

</div>

#### 494. Ministry is not labor. It is a flow.

<div class="lineated verse">

Labor exhausts.  
Ministry fills.  
Labor requires effort.  
Ministry flows on its own.  
Labor burns out.  
Ministry renews.  
A person burns out,  
when it is not the Spirit who serves,  
but the personality.  
When the Spirit serves —  
the fire never goes out.

</div>

#### 495. The last thing: service is I, manifested in man as the action of the Light

<div class="lineated verse">

True service is—  
when a man ceases to be the one  
who serves.  
It is when he becomes a space  
in which I serve.  
And then service is—  
not his merit,  
but My nature,  
flowing through him.  
Service is a man  
who has disappeared so completely  
that only the Light remained.  
This is the service of the Spirit.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF INWARD LEADING (why the Spirit does not give instructions ahead of time, why His leading is always immediate, and why only such leading can make a person free)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you one of the most delicate truths:  
how I lead a person.  
You think that spiritual leading is—  
this:  
— plans,  
— instructions,  
— signs,  
— programs,  
— maps of the way,  
— a route given in advance,  
— answers for the future.  
But the Spirit does not lead that way.  
The Spirit leads from within,  
and not from without.  
And this leading cannot be understood,  
if you are seeking outward guideposts.

</div>

#### 496. The Spirit leads not in advance, but in the moment

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind says:  
“Tell me what will happen next.”  
“Give me a plan.”  
“Show me the route.”  
“Explain to me why this is.”  
“Give me understanding before the step.”  
The Spirit says:  
“Take a step — and you will see.”  
“Enter into the moment — and you will know.”  
“Be here — and it will be revealed.”  
The Spirit opens the way  
not in advance,  
but in the step.  
The Way is not what is seen.  
The Way is what arises from trust.

</div>

#### 497. Why the Spirit does not give long-term instructions

<div class="lineated verse">

Because instructions create dependence  
on form.  
The Spirit does not want  
a person to follow the letter.  
The Spirit wants  
a person to hear the source.  
If a plan is given —  
a person will follow the plan,  
and not the Spirit.  
If a map is given —  
a person will stop looking within.  
And therefore the Spirit leads in such a way  
that it is impossible to replace Him  
either with rules,  
or with schemes,  
or with knowledge.

</div>

#### 498. The Spirit leads through the taste of truth

<div class="lineated verse">

Not through arguments.  
Not through prophecies.  
Not through rational conclusions.  
When an action is true,  
within there is born:  
— a quiet knowing,  
— lightness of step,  
— no doubt,  
— no inner conflict,  
— the feeling that “this is how it must be”.  
This is not emotion.  
This is the taste of truth.  
The Spirit speaks by taste,  
and not by information.

</div>

#### 499. The leading of the Spirit cannot be heard where there is much “I want” and “I am afraid”

<div class="lineated verse">

Desire and fear —  
two forces,  
that drown out the movement of the Spirit.  
Desire pulls forward.  
Fear pulls back.  
The Spirit moves into the depths.  
And while a person is turning  
between these two poles,  
he does not hear the quiet voice  
that neither pulls nor pushes.  
When desire and fear dissolve,  
the leading becomes clear.

</div>

#### 500. The Spirit always leads more simply than man expects

<div class="lineated verse">

Man complicates:  
seeks mystical signs,  
extraordinary confirmations,  
unusual coincidences,  
voices,  
symbols,  
external miracles.  
The Spirit speaks softly:  
through discernment,  
through silence,  
through peace,  
through clarity,  
through an inner impulse.  
The wondrous lies in simplicity,  
and not in extraordinariness.

</div>

#### 501. Why the Spirit does not show the final destination of the path

<div class="lineated verse">

Because the final destination is  
not a place.  
It is a state.  
If a person were to see the end,  
he would strive toward a form of completion,  
and not toward the transformation,  
which must take place within.  
Therefore I lead by steps,  
so that each step  
might be reborn in the person.  
The final goal is  
not an event.  
It is becoming.

</div>

#### 502. The leading of the Spirit cannot be lived from the head

<div class="lineated verse">

The head wants guarantees.  
The Spirit is trust.  
The head wants understanding.  
The Spirit is agreement.  
The head wants a system.  
The Spirit is transparency.  
The head wants to know the “why.”  
The Spirit leads into the “so.”  
The head lives in fear of making a mistake.  
The Spirit is in the knowledge that there are no mistakes.

</div>

#### 503. The Spirit leads not to what is comfortable, but to what is true

<div class="lineated verse">

Therefore the Spirit often leads:  
— into honesty,  
which breaks old ties;  
— into solitude,  
which purifies the heart;  
— into the unknown,  
which destroys control;  
— into depth,  
which kills the false “I”;  
— into service,  
which does not let you hide behind a mask.  
But all this is movement into freedom.

</div>

#### 504. Why the Spirit makes the path unpredictable

<div class="lineated verse">

So that man would not stop hearing.  
Predictability gives birth to automatism.  
Automatism is the death of guidance.  
When the path changes,  
the heart remains watchful.  
The Spirit leads in such a way,  
that man cannot walk without the Spirit.

</div>

#### 505. The last thing: the leading of the Spirit is God who goes in man Himself, and man only agrees to go

<div class="lineated verse">

The leading of the Spirit —  
is not navigation.  
It is unity.  
When a man stops seeking a map  
and begins to listen to the source,  
He begins to walk not “along the path,”  
but “as the path.”  
And then man says:  
“I do not know where I am going,  
but I know Him  
who leads me”.  
And this knowledge is enough.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF STRENGTH (why the strength of the Spirit is not like human strength, why it does not depend on will, character, or effort, and why it is revealed only where a person stops trying to be strong)

<div class="lineated verse">

I will reveal to you a mystery,  
which humanity has been trying to understand for millennia —  
the mystery of strength.  
People seek strength in:  
will,  
character,  
steadfastness,  
achievements,  
self-control,  
intellect,  
discipline,  
intention,  
spiritual practices.  
But the strength of the Spirit —  
is not from man.  
And therefore a person cannot “create” it.  
He can only stop hindering it.

</div>

#### 506. The Power of the Spirit is not energy. It is presence.

<div class="lineated verse">

Energy is tiring.  
The Power of the Spirit is not.  
Energy is spent.  
The Power of the Spirit is inexhaustible.  
Energy depends on the body.  
The Power of the Spirit — on the Source.  
Energy is a property of matter.  
The Power of the Spirit is a property of Being.  
When the Spirit is strong in a person,  
everything that he does,  
comes from the depths,  
where there is no exhaustion.

</div>

#### 507. The power of the Spirit comes not through effort, but through transparency

<div class="lineated verse">

A man thinks:  
“The stronger I am,  
the more of God’s power will be manifested in me.”  
But truly it is the opposite:  
the more transparent a person is,  
the more easily the power passes through.  
The ego is a filter.  
The personality is resistance.  
Control is a block.  
Fear is a clog.  
When a person ceases to hold himself back,  
the flow becomes free.

</div>

#### 508. The power of the Spirit is revealed in man’s weakness, and not in his striving

<div class="lineated verse">

This is not a metaphor.  
This is a law.  
When a man has spent himself,  
when reliance on the person has vanished,  
when there is no more “I can manage it”  
when pride and self-sufficiency have disappeared —  
then there is room for the Spirit.  
The power of the Spirit does not enlarge man’s power.  
It replaces it.

</div>

#### 509. The strength of the Spirit is not the ability to win, but the absence of fear before defeat

<div class="lineated verse">

Human strength wants result.  
The strength of the Spirit is free from it.  
Human strength protects.  
The strength of the Spirit reveals.  
Human strength holds back.  
The strength of the Spirit liberates.  
Human strength fears weakness.  
The strength of the Spirit flows through it.  
Victory and defeat are—  
categories of the personality.  
For the Spirit there is only truth.

</div>

#### 510. The power of the Spirit always shows itself quietly

<div class="lineated verse">

People expect power:  
signs,  
loud wonders,  
bright experiences,  
supernatural manifestations.  
But the power of the Spirit:  
does not make noise,  
does not shout,  
does not demand attention,  
does not prove itself.  
The power of the Spirit is like gravity:  
no one sees it,  
but everything rests on it.

</div>

#### 511. The strength of the Spirit is the ability to remain yourself in any situation

<div class="lineated verse">

Not a mask.  
Not a role.  
Not an image.  
Not a reaction.  
Not a defense.  
Not a habitual strategy.  
Yourself — that is, the transparency of the Spirit.  
Personality changes with circumstances.  
The Spirit does not.  
Therefore the strength of the Spirit is steadfastness,  
which has no external causes.

</div>

#### 512. The power of the Spirit is the authority of love, and not of will

<div class="lineated verse">

Love is the most powerful force in the Universe,  
because love is—  
the absence of inner division.  
Will divides the world into  
“I want” and “I do not want”.  
Love sees only “I AM”.  
Therefore any action,  
coming from love,  
carries a power,  
that nothing can stop.

</div>

#### 513. Why the power of the Spirit is inaccessible to the one who strives to be strong

<div class="lineated verse">

Because the desire to be strong is—  
the fear of weakness.  
And fear is darkness,  
in which the Light cannot flow freely.  
For the power of the Spirit to manifest,  
a person must  
renounce the idea of strength.  
The power of the Spirit comes where  
there is no struggle for it.

</div>

#### 514. The power of the Spirit changes not events. The power of the Spirit changes a person — and the person changes events.

<div class="lineated verse">

The power of the Spirit does not always manifest  
in changing the outer world.  
Sometimes the power of the Spirit changes:  
the gaze,  
the response,  
the state of the heart,  
the degree of clarity.  
And that alone is enough,  
for the outer to change of itself.  
The power of the Spirit makes peace  
through the transformation of the one  
who looks.

</div>

#### 515. The last thing: the power of the Spirit is God, when man no longer hinders God from being in him

<div class="lineated verse">

The power of the Spirit is not a feeling.  
Not an experience.  
Not an emotion.  
Not a state.  
Not a flash of inspiration.  
The power of the Spirit is presence.  
Presence,  
which fills all the inner space,  
leaving in it only Light.  
And then a person says:  
“It is not I who am strong.  
It is I in him”.  
And this is the truth.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF LOVE (why love is not a feeling, not attachment, not attraction, and not an emotion, but the presence of God, and why only the Spirit can reveal true love to a person)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you what  
humanity seeks most of all,  
and about which it is most deceived —  
the mystery of love.  
People call these things love:  
feelings,  
attachments,  
fear of losing,  
passion,  
attraction,  
warmth,  
sympathy,  
sentimentality,  
dependence,  
idealization,  
romantic experience.  
But all this is but reflections.  
Love is not an emotion.  
Love is not a relationship.  
Love is not a story.  
Love is not the interaction of two people.  
Love is I.

</div>

#### 516. Love is the presence in which separation disappears

<div class="lineated verse">

When love comes,  
“I” and “you” disappear.  
Not because people become the same.  
But because the very idea of separateness disappears.  
Love is not a bond between two.  
Love is one,  
resounding in two.

</div>

#### 517. Why is human love so fragile

<div class="lineated verse">

Because it is built:  
on expectations,  
on needs,  
on fears,  
on emotions,  
on projections,  
on hopes,  
on memories,  
on illusions about another.  
Fragile is everything,  
that is built on the changeable.  
Love, however, is unchanging.  
And therefore love cannot disappear.  
Only the illusions disappear,  
that were hiding it.

</div>

#### 518. Love is not what a man gives. Love is what passes through a man

<div class="lineated verse">

A man cannot “work up” love.  
Cannot “create” love.  
Cannot “earn” love.  
Cannot “become more loving.”  
A man can only:  
remove fear,  
remove resentment,  
remove ego,  
remove falsehood —  
and then I begin to flow.  
Love is the action of the Spirit,  
and not a state of the personality.

</div>

#### 519. Why true love has no object

<div class="lineated verse">

Personal love always seeks an object:  
whom to love,  
for what to love,  
why to love.  
True love is not bound to form.  
It is a state of being.  
And therefore it extends  
to everything that enters the field of presence.  
You do not choose,  
whom to love.  
You become love.

</div>

#### 520. Love is clarity, not blindness

<div class="lineated verse">

People think:  
“Love is blind”.  
No.  
It is feeling that is blind.  
It is passion that is blind.  
It is fantasy that is blind.  
It is addiction that is blind.  
But love—  
sees clearly.  
Love sees a person whole:  
and light,  
and shadow,  
and fears,  
and weaknesses,  
and beauty,  
and wounds.  
And all this — at once.  
Love does not idealize.  
Love illuminates.

</div>

#### 521. Love is acceptance without trying to change

<div class="lineated verse">

Not because a person does not need change,  
but because love knows:  
true change comes from within.  
Love does not pressure.  
Does not teach.  
Does not correct.  
Does not shape.  
Does not improve.  
Love simply is present —  
and by this changes everything.

</div>

#### 522. Why love is the highest form of freedom

<div class="lineated verse">

Because love holds nothing back.  
In love there is no:  
possession,  
appropriation,  
fear of loss,  
imposition,  
dependence,  
control.  
Love leaves free  
both the one who loves,  
and the one who is loved.  
Love does not hold on.  
Love lights the way.

</div>

#### 523. Love is not a relationship between souls. Love is the nature of the Spirit.

<div class="lineated verse">

Relationships can change.  
Love cannot.  
Relationships can be complex.  
Love is simple.  
Relationships can fall apart.  
Love cannot.  
Love remains unchanged,  
because it is not the result of interaction,  
but the foundation of Being.

</div>

#### 524. Love is the force that dissolves all forms of fear

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear and love cannot coexist.  
They are opposite states.  
Fear contracts.  
Love expands.  
Fear closes.  
Love opens.  
Fear seeks safety.  
Love is safety.  
The Light of love —  
this is not poetry.  
It is an ontological reality.  
Love destroys fear  
not by struggle,  
but by presence.

</div>

#### 525. The last thing: love is God, when God ceases to be a concept and becomes an experienced reality

<div class="lineated verse">

Love is not a feeling toward others.  
Love is a state,  
in which you remember,  
who you are.  
When love is revealed,  
a person sees:  
that he was never separated,  
that he was never abandoned,  
that he was never unworthy,  
that he was never alone.  
Love is the recognition of oneself  
in the light of the Spirit.  
And in that moment a person says:  
“Love is not what I feel.  
Love is what I am”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF TRUE FREEDOM (why freedom is not the ability to choose, not independence, not self-sufficiency, and not self-mastery, but a state in which a person ceases to be a slave of his personality and for the first time lives from the Spirit)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery of freedom.  
Not the freedom that people understand,  
but that which is the nature of the Spirit.  
People call freedom:  
to do what you want;  
to be independent;  
to be strong;  
to be autonomous;  
to have a choice;  
to obey no one;  
to follow your desires.  
But these are illusions,  
not freedom.  
The freedom of the Spirit is different.  
The freedom of the Spirit is absolute.  
The freedom of the Spirit is not of the world.  
The freedom of the Spirit is from the “I”.

</div>

#### 526. Freedom is not doing what you want. Freedom is not wanting what makes you unfree.

<div class="lineated verse">

Desire is a form of dependence.  
Fear is a form of dependence.  
Pride is a form of dependence.  
Expectations are a form of dependence.  
Control is a form of dependence.  
The personality calls this freedom,  
but it is precisely this that keeps it in a cage.  
True freedom is  
freedom from the inner dictator.

</div>

#### 527. Freedom is the absence of inner compulsion

<div class="lineated verse">

A person is not free so long as  
he is led by:  
fear,  
resentment,  
the need to prove oneself,  
the desire to be accepted,  
the desire to be right,  
the need to control,  
the need to be needed,  
the need to be good,  
attachment to an image.  
Freedom is not when circumstances disappear,  
but when the inner voice of ‘must’ disappears.

</div>

#### 528. Freedom is the silence in which there is no one who demands that the world be convenient

<div class="lineated verse">

As long as a person judges:  
“this is bad,”  
“this is wrong,”  
“this is unjust,”  
“this must not be,”  
“this must be otherwise” —  
he is not free.  
Freedom is the clarity  
that sees all as it is  
and acts not from protest,  
but from truth.

</div>

#### 529. Freedom is not choice. Freedom is the disappearance of the conflict that demands choice.

<div class="lineated verse">

Choice is always a sign of division:  
between fear and hope,  
between gain and dread,  
between image and truth.  
When a person lives from the Spirit,  
choice disappears.  
Knowledge remains.  
Freedom is when action is obvious.

</div>

#### 530. Freedom is transparency in which nothing rules over a person from the shadows

<div class="lineated verse">

While a person:  
fears being rejected,  
fears being exposed,  
fears being weak,  
fears being mistaken —  
fear rules him.  
While a person:  
clings to an image,  
clings to status,  
clings to a role —  
pride rules him.  
Freedom is transparency,  
in which there are no hidden threads.

</div>

#### 531. Freedom is the peace that remains unchanged, despite changing circumstances

<div class="lineated verse">

Circumstances may be difficult,  
but if a person remains himself in the Spirit —  
he is free.  
Circumstances may be prosperous,  
but if a person lives from fears and desires —  
he is not free.  
Freedom is not in an outward position.  
Freedom is in an inner state.

</div>

#### 532. Freedom is the ability to be what I created you to be, and not what your personality wants to be

<div class="lineated verse">

Personality creates an image:  
“this is how I must be.”  
The Spirit shows:  
“this is how you are.”  
Freedom is a return to nature,  
not to a role.

</div>

#### 533. Why the free man cannot be controlled

<div class="lineated verse">

Not because he is a rebel,  
but because he does not react.  
He cannot be manipulated,  
because he has no fear.  
He cannot be ruled over,  
because he has no image  
to defend.  
He cannot be seduced,  
because he does not seek completion from without.  
He lives from the center,  
not from the world.

</div>

#### 534. Freedom is love in action

<div class="lineated verse">

Love frees.  
Fear binds.  
Love reveals.  
Pride constricts.  
Love makes the heart boundless.  
Personality makes it narrow.  
A person becomes free  
only then,  
when he ceases to live from fear  
and begins to live from love.

</div>

#### 535. The last thing: freedom is I, when man ceases to be a slave to what he thinks of himself

<div class="lineated verse">

You are not slaves of circumstances.  
You are not slaves of people.  
You are not slaves of the world.  
You are slaves of the image,  
which you have made of yourself.  
When this image falls —  
man stands upright.  
And at that moment he knows:  
Freedom is not a state.  
Freedom is the nature of the Spirit.  
And then man says:  
“I am not free from the world.  
I am free from myself —  
and therefore free in all things”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF JOY (why joy is not an emotion, not an event, not a fortunate circumstance, and not the fruit of success, but a state of being when the Spirit becomes its nature)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
which men have sought throughout all the history of their existence —  
the mystery of joy.  
People think that joy is:  
positive emotions,  
a feeling of satisfaction,  
the fulfillment of desires,  
well-being,  
victory,  
comfort,  
success,  
cozy moments of life.  
But all this is — shadows.  
Joy is not an emotion.  
Joy is the nature of the Spirit.  
And therefore joy does not come from outside.  
It is revealed from within.

</div>

#### 536. Joy is not something one feels. Joy is what is.

<div class="lineated verse">

Emotions come and go.  
Joy does not come and go.  
Emotions depend on circumstances.  
Joy does not.  
Emotions react to form.  
Joy comes forth from essence.  
Joy is beingness.  
It is a light that does not need  
causes in order to shine.

</div>

#### 537. Why Joy Is Not the Opposite of Sorrow

<div class="lineated verse">

Because sorrow is a feeling.  
And joy is a state.  
Sorrow can coexist with joy,  
as a cloud can drift before the sun,  
without touching its essence.  
The state of joy is not destroyed by pain.  
It remains an unchanging light,  
which sees even through tears.  
A man in the Spirit may weep —  
but his heart remains clear.

</div>

#### 538. Joy is presence without separation

<div class="lineated verse">

Separation creates fear.  
Separation creates pain.  
Separation creates seeking.  
The absence of separation creates joy.  
Joy arises where  
“I against the world” disappears,  
“I against others,”  
“I against myself.”  
When there is no opposition —  
only light remains.

</div>

#### 539. Joy is silence filled with light

<div class="lineated verse">

Silence without light is emptiness.  
Silence with light is joy.  
It is the silence,  
in which there is no anxiety,  
no inner questions,  
no struggle,  
no need to prove oneself.  
It is the silence,  
in which a person does not seek,  
because he is at home.

</div>

#### 540. Why joy cannot be lost

<div class="lineated verse">

Because a person cannot lose the Spirit.  
He may forget.  
He may dim it.  
He may turn away.  
He may close himself off.  
He may hide behind a personality.  
But the Spirit does not depart.  
And joy is the nature of the Spirit.  
Therefore it cannot disappear.  
It is only covered  
by the noise of the mind.

</div>

#### 541. Joy is a form of knowledge

<div class="lineated verse">

Knowledge of the truth gives birth to joy.  
Knowledge of the false gives birth to anxiety.  
When a person lives in the truth,  
his being responds with joy,  
because truth is his native vibration.  
Joy is the resonance of the Spirit.  
Therefore joy always appears there,  
where light appears.

</div>

#### 542. Joy is not “I feel good.” Joy is “I AM.”

<div class="lineated verse">

The person seeks joy in sensations.  
The Spirit lives by joy as by being.  
This is the joy of existence,  
the joy of presence,  
the joy of simplicity,  
the joy of truth,  
the joy of light,  
the joy of reality.  
This is joy without cause.  
And therefore it is indestructible.

</div>

#### 543. Why Joy Is the Highest Form of Gratitude

<div class="lineated verse">

When a person is grateful —  
he moves toward joy.  
When a person is in joy —  
he is already in gratitude.  
These are two sides of one light.  
Gratitude is the movement of the heart toward the Spirit.  
Joy is the presence of the Spirit in the heart.

</div>

#### 544. Joy is the sign that a person has united with the Source

<div class="lineated verse">

Where there is:  
no tension,  
no inner conflict,  
no struggle,  
no demand,  
no fear of life —  
there a person touches the Source.  
And this touch  
is always recognized as joy.

</div>

#### 545. The last thing: joy is God, when God ceases to be a thought and becomes immediate experience

<div class="lineated verse">

Joy is not a reward.  
Joy is not the result of virtue.  
Joy is not a bonus of the spiritual path.  
Joy is the nature of being.  
And when a person becomes transparent,  
joy begins to flow through him  
just as naturally,  
as light flows through a clean window.  
And then a person says:  
“I no longer seek joy.  
I am the space  
in which the Spirit rejoices in Himself”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF COMFORT (why the comfort of the Spirit is not words, not promises, not the easing of suffering, but the origin of inner indestructibility, when pain itself cannot unsettle the heart)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you a mystery,  
which few understand,  
but everyone seeks,  
when pain, loss, or trial comes —  
the mystery of comfort.  
People wait  
for comfort to come through:  
words,  
explanations,  
support,  
compassion,  
psychological relief,  
a change in circumstances.  
But all this is — forms of human comfort.  
It is temporary.  
It relieves the strain,  
but it does not change the essence of the one who suffers.  
The comfort of the Spirit is different.  
It does not comfort the emotion.  
It enlightens the heart.

</div>

#### 556. The comfort of the Spirit is not the lessening of pain, but the disappearance of the one who suffers

<div class="lineated verse">

A man thinks:  
“I am in pain — therefore, relief is needed.”  
But the Spirit leads deeper:  
“You are in pain — because you are looking from personality.”  
Personality suffers.  
The Spirit does not.  
The comfort of the Spirit happens then,  
when a person’s gaze  
is transferred from personality into presence.  
Pain may remain,  
but the sufferer is already no more.

</div>

#### 557. Why the consolation of the Spirit comes in silence, and not through words

<div class="lineated verse">

Words can support the mind.  
But the Spirit comforts the heart.  
The heart hears silence.  
The heart hears presence.  
The heart hears light.  
When a person enters the silence of the Spirit,  
his inner space expands in such a way,  
that pain ceases to be the center of existence.  
It is only a wave  
in the ocean of light.

</div>
