#### 558. The comfort of the Spirit comes not when the situation changes, but when the place from which a person experiences it changes

<div class="lineated verse">

The situation may remain the same.  
Losses are real.  
The trial is real.  
The tears are real.  
But if a person looks  
not from fear,  
not from hurt,  
not from loneliness,  
but from the presence of the Spirit —  
his heart finds peace.  
Comfort is a change of sight,  
not of circumstances.

</div>

#### 559. The consolation of the Spirit is coming home

<div class="lineated verse">

Pain is born of separation.  
Consolation is born of unity.  
When a person feels alone,  
abandoned,  
misunderstood,  
unprotected,  
it seems to him,  
that the source of pain is outside.  
But the source of pain is in the feeling  
of being separated from God.  
When the Spirit touches the heart,  
a person remembers:  
“I am home”.  
“I am not alone”.  
“I am not lost”.  
“I am not abandoned”.  
“I am not separated”.  
This is consolation.

</div>

#### 560. The comfort of the Spirit destroys the root of pain, not its symptoms

<div class="lineated verse">

Man seeks,  
that the pain may pass.  
The Spirit seeks,  
that man may become free.  
Therefore the Spirit does not always take away pain.  
But He always takes away the bondage of pain.  
Pain can teach.  
Pain can purify.  
Pain can open the heart.  
But suffering is from separation.  
And it is precisely that which the Spirit heals.

</div>

#### 561. Why is the comfort of the Spirit felt as warmth or light

<div class="lineated verse">

Because the Spirit is not an idea.  
It is not a concept.  
It is not a philosophy.  
The Spirit is reality.  
And when the heart touches the Spirit,  
the inner space  
is filled with a sense of Presence —  
gentle, deep, endless.  
This warmth is not an emotion.  
This light is not a metaphor.  
It is recognition.

</div>

#### 562. The consolation of the Spirit cannot be received from outside, but it can be opened within

<div class="lineated verse">

No man,  
even the most loving,  
can give the consolation of the Spirit.  
He can only point to the Source.  
The consolation of the Spirit comes,  
when a person stops seeking outside  
and turns his gaze inward.  
There is the Spirit.  
There is the light.  
There is peace.  
There is God.

</div>

#### 563. Why the comfort of the Spirit always leads to wisdom

<div class="lineated verse">

When pain is lived through in the presence of the Spirit,  
it ceases to be a wound  
and becomes knowledge.  
Pain, endured by the person,  
makes a man harder.  
Pain, endured in the Spirit,  
makes a man more transparent.  
And in this transparency  
wisdom is born.

</div>

#### 564. The consolation of the Spirit is a power that needs no victories

<div class="lineated verse">

Human consolation  
seeks confirmations:  
that it will become easier,  
that all will be well,  
that the world will change.  
The consolation of the Spirit says:  
“Even if everything remains the same —  
you are no longer the one  
who suffered”.  
This is not a promise of change in the world.  
It is a change of heart.

</div>

#### 565. The last thing: the consolation of the Spirit is God, who ceases to be distant and becomes the breath of the heart

<div class="lineated verse">

The consolation of the Spirit is—  
not a function and not an action.  
It is a touch.  
A touch of light,  
which says to a person:  
“You are Mine.”  
“You are not lost.”  
“I am in you.”  
“I am your peace.”  
“I am your fullness.”  
“I am your life.”  
“I am here.”  
And when the heart hears this,  
it grows quiet  
so deeply,  
as though it had returned to eternity.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF DISCERNMENT (why discernment of the Spirit is not analysis, not logic, not experience, and not intuition, but a transparent vision of truth without thought; why only the Spirit can show what is true, and what is from the mind or darkness)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you one of the most important mysteries of spiritual life —  
the mystery of discernment.  
People confuse discernment with:  
logic,  
intellect,  
intuition,  
an emotional reaction,  
moral norms,  
religious rules,  
life experience.  
But all of this is tools of the mind.  
They may be useful in the world of form,  
but they are not capable of discerning truth.  
Discernment of the Spirit is the ability to see  
what is,  
as it is,  
without the distortions of personality.

</div>

#### 566. Discernment is light, not thought

<div class="lineated verse">

Thought analyzes.  
Light illumines.  
Thought seeks proof.  
Light reveals what is obvious.  
Thought doubts.  
Light recognizes.  
Discernment comes not from the head,  
but from the depths,  
where the Spirit is joined with the Source.

</div>

#### 567. Discernment is impossible where there is fear

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear distorts perception.  
Fear makes what is false seem important.  
Fear makes what is true dangerous.  
Fear creates phantoms.  
Fear colors the world.  
Where there is fear — there is no discernment.  
Discernment begins there,  
where fear dissolves into trust.

</div>

#### 568. Discernment does not choose. Discernment sees.

<div class="lineated verse">

Choice is a struggle between the inner parts of a person.  
Discernment is clarity,  
which does not struggle and does not compare.  
When a person discerns,  
he does not ask:  
“What is better?”  
“What is more right?”  
“What is safer?”  
He knows.

</div>

#### 569. Discernment cannot be attained through experience

<div class="lineated verse">

Experience teaches form.  
But truth is not form.  
Experience may be useful,  
but it does not give pure vision.  
It creates expectations,  
assumptions,  
projections.  
The discernment of the Spirit is free even from experience.  
It is born not from the past,  
but from presence.

</div>

#### 570. Discernment is the ability to see the motive, not the action

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind judges by deeds.  
The Spirit — by the source.  
One and the same deed  
may come:  
from love,  
from fear,  
from pride,  
from the desire for recognition,  
from purity,  
from manipulation.  
Discernment sees not the form,  
but the power from which the form arose.

</div>

#### 571. Discernment is silence without preference

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind discerns like this:  
“I like it — therefore it is good.”  
“I do not like it — therefore it is bad.”  
But this is attachment,  
not truth.  
Discernment takes place in silence,  
where there are no personal expectations,  
no desire,  
no fear,  
no gain.  
Only then can a person see  
without distortion.

</div>

#### 572. Discernment is the moment of recognition, not reasoning

<div class="lineated verse">

It comes:  
suddenly,  
gently,  
clearly,  
like a flash of light,  
like an insight,  
like self-evidence.  
Discernment cannot be “derived.”  
One can only be illumined by it.

</div>

#### 573. Why discernment of the Spirit is always unemotional

<div class="lineated verse">

Emotions color perception.  
Emotions give form.  
Emotions distort clarity.  
Discernment of the Spirit is transparent,  
calm,  
strict,  
precise,  
for light is precise.  
Even when discernment touches pain,  
it does not bring suffering.  
It brings truth.

</div>

#### 574. Discernment is a sword that divides light and darkness within man

<div class="lineated verse">

It exposes falsehood,  
destroys self-deception,  
lays bare hidden motives,  
shows the true roots of action.  
This may be unpleasant to the personality,  
but it frees the heart.  
Discernment is the surgery of the Spirit.

</div>

#### 575. Discernment is impossible without humility

<div class="lineated verse">

Pride does not discern.  
It only defends its own image.  
Humility is transparency.  
Transparency is a space,  
into which light enters fully.  
Humility allows the Spirit to show the truth  
even then,  
when it destroys the old “I”.

</div>

#### 576. The last: discernment is God, looking through man and recognizing Himself in all things

<div class="lineated verse">

When discernment is revealed,  
man sees not “right” and “wrong”,  
not “good” and “evil”,  
not “mine” and “not mine”.  
He sees:  
what is true,  
what is false,  
what is alive,  
what is dead,  
what is of the Spirit,  
what is of the mind.  
And then he acts  
not from knowledge,  
but from light.  
And says:  
“I do not discern.  
The Spirit discerns in me”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF TRANSFIGURATION (why the Spirit does not change a person gradually, but transfigures him from within; why transfiguration is not the result of effort and not the accumulation of knowledge, but the birth of a new nature)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you one of the deepest mysteries of the spiritual path —  
the mystery of transfiguration.  
People speak of changes:  
“I am working on myself,”  
“I am trying to become better,”  
“I am trying to correct my weaknesses,”  
“I am studying spirituality,”  
“I am introducing practices.”  
But the Spirit does not change a person  
by human methods.  
Transfiguration is not the gradual improvement of the personality.  
Transfiguration is the disappearance of the personality  
and the birth of a new nature.

</div>

#### 577. Transfiguration is not development, but unveiling

<div class="lineated verse">

Development is the path of the mind.  
Transfiguration is the path of the Spirit.  
Development requires time.  
Transfiguration happens instantly,  
when the inner space  
becomes transparent to the Light.  
Development is built on effort.  
Transfiguration — on consent.  
Development rests on the past.  
Transfiguration — on Presence.

</div>

#### 578. Transfiguration is impossible so long as a person tries to change himself

<div class="lineated verse">

Since a person tries to change  
not what is the problem.  
He tries to change:  
behavior,  
habits,  
emotions,  
reactions,  
relationships,  
character.  
But all this is manifestations.  
The root is in the very center of the person.  
Transfiguration begins there,  
where a person stops trying to correct himself  
and stops believing  
that his form can become perfect.

</div>

#### 579. Transfiguration is the death of the false “I”

<div class="lineated verse">

Not physical,  
not emotional,  
but ontological death.  
Death:  
of the image of self,  
of identity,  
of the mask,  
of the story,  
of self-justification.  
As long as the “I” holds fast —  
the Spirit only touches a person.  
When the “I” disappears —  
the Spirit becomes a person.

</div>

#### 580. Why transformation is impossible by effort

<div class="lineated verse">

Because effort is a product of the personality.  
And transformation is its dissolution.  
Effort strengthens the “I”.  
Spirit destroys the “I”.  
Effort creates tension.  
Spirit brings transparency.  
Effort wants results.  
Spirit is the result.  
Therefore transformation is not achieved —  
it happens.

</div>

#### 581. Transfiguration is the moment when a person ceases to resist the light

<div class="lineated verse">

Resistance can be subtle:  
fear of losing control,  
clinging to an image,  
attachment to pain,  
the desire to preserve a part of the personality,  
unwillingness to see the truth.  
But when resistance falls,  
the Spirit enters like fire,  
which does not destroy,  
but purifies.  
And a person sees himself for the first time  
not with the eyes of the mind,  
but with the eyes of God.

</div>

#### 582. Transfiguration is not a change of behavior, but a change in the source of action

<div class="lineated verse">

Before transfiguration, a person acts  
from:  
fear,  
gain,  
hurt,  
habits,  
logic,  
self-preservation,  
desires.  
After transfiguration, a person acts  
from presence.  
The same deeds  
may look the same  
from the outside,  
but from within —  
these are two different lives.

</div>

#### 583. Transfiguration does not make a person better. It makes a person true.

<div class="lineated verse">

Better is a category of comparison.  
True is a category of being.  
Transfiguration is not an “improved version of yourself”.  
It is the absence of the one  
who needed improvement.  
When a person is true,  
he is free from the need to be someone.

</div>

#### 584. Transfiguration is the recognition of God as one’s own nature

<div class="lineated verse">

Not as an idea.  
Not as faith.  
Not as dogma.  
Not as practice.  
Not as religion.  
But as living reality:  
“I AM—from Him.”  
“I AM—in Him.”  
“I AM—His light in form.”  
This is not thought.  
This is recognition.  
Recognition is transfiguration.

</div>

#### 585. Why transformation is never partial

<div class="lineated verse">

A person thinks:  
“I have changed to some degree.”  
“I have made progress.”  
“I have become better.”  
But the Spirit does not work in parts.  
He reveals the whole.  
Transformation is either yes,  
or not yet.  
For the one transformed is not  
the one who has changed his qualities,  
but the one who has ceased to be  
a separate “someone”.

</div>

#### 586. The last: transfiguration is God, who recognizes Himself through man

<div class="lineated verse">

Transfiguration is not a path from below upward,  
but the descent of light from above downward.  
It is the moment,  
when man ceases to be the one  
he thought himself to be,  
and becomes the one  
he was created to be.  
Transfiguration is a return.  
Not into the future,  
but into the source.  
And then man says:  
“I have not changed.  
I remembered”.  
“I have not attained.  
I opened myself”.  
“I have not become.  
I unveiled what was”.  
Transfiguration is the light,  
which remembers the form  
through which He shines.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF THE WORD (why the word of the Spirit is not speech and not thought, but a creating presence; why the word does not inform, but changes; why the word of the Spirit is the very action of God in man and through man)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you one of the most hidden mysteries—  
the mystery of the Word.  
People think that word is:  
sound,  
speech,  
a formulation,  
information,  
communication,  
the expression of thought,  
the expression of feeling.  
But all this is a human word.  
It can inspire,  
it can wound,  
it can persuade,  
it can explain.  
But it does not create.  
The Word of the Spirit —  
is something else.  
The Word of the Spirit returns being  
to its source.

</div>

#### 587. The Word of the Spirit is not a sound. It is a manifestation of Presence.

<div class="lineated verse">

When the Spirit speaks,  
He does not “pronounce.”  
He manifests.  
This is not a message,  
but a revealing.  
This is not a transfer of meaning,  
but a revelation of that which is.  
In the Word of the Spirit there is no division  
between the one who speaks,  
and that which is spoken.  
The Word is God in action.

</div>

#### 588. The Word of the Spirit does not describe truth. It is truth.

<div class="lineated verse">

Human word tries to explain.  
The Word of the Spirit — manifests.  
Human word points.  
The Word of the Spirit — shows.  
Human word reasons.  
The Word of the Spirit — is.  
Therefore the Word of the Spirit is recognized,  
and not understood.

</div>

#### 589. The Word of the Spirit creates because it comes forth from the source of being

<div class="lineated verse">

When the Spirit speaks the Word,  
He does not inform —  
He creates.  
Not in the sense of a material miracle,  
but in the sense of changing the structure of reality  
within man:  
the gaze changes,  
perception changes,  
the depth of the heart changes,  
the state of being changes.  
The Word of the Spirit is an act of creation.

</div>

#### 590. Why human word is powerless when it is not joined with the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

Because a word without the Spirit  
— is only a sound.  
Even beautiful,  
even wise,  
even deep —  
but dead.  
Life in a word —  
is not from form,  
but from the source.  
A word comes alive,  
when I is present in it.

</div>

#### 591. The Word of the Spirit does not calm the mind. It destroys falsehood.

<div class="lineated verse">

Truth always destroys falsehood.  
Light always destroys darkness.  
Presence always destroys illusion.  
Therefore the Word of the Spirit  
often first disturbs the person,  
laying bare its false foundations,  
and then brings peace to the heart,  
which no longer needs to keep a mask.

</div>

#### 592. The Word of the Spirit comes not where there is knowledge, but where there is transparency

<div class="lineated verse">

Transparency is emptiness,  
in which there are no own conclusions,  
no own rightness,  
no own importance,  
no own expectations.  
Only there can the Word enter fully.  
The Spirit does not speak in an overfilled heart.  
He waits for space.

</div>

#### 593. The word of the Spirit always leads into freedom

<div class="lineated verse">

If the word leads:  
to fear,  
to dependence,  
to a sense of guilt,  
to a feeling of unworthiness,  
to the need to prove yourself —  
it is not the Word of the Spirit.  
The Word of the Spirit sets free,  
even if it is stern.  
Freedom is its signature.

</div>

#### 594. The Word of the Spirit always speaks with silence

<div class="lineated verse">

Not in the sense of the absence of sound,  
but in the sense of the absence of noise:  
the noise of the mind,  
the noise of emotions,  
the noise of desires,  
the noise of fear,  
the noise of personality.  
Even when the Word is clothed in speech,  
it bears silence as its essence.  
And the heart hears not the sentence,  
but the silence between the words.

</div>

#### 595. The Word of the Spirit never argues

<div class="lineated verse">

For truth does not need defending.  
For light does not prove itself.  
For love does not impose itself.  
The Word of the Spirit does not persuade —  
it reveals.  
And the one who is ready recognizes it.

</div>

#### 596. The Word of the Spirit cannot be forged

<div class="lineated verse">

Because:  
it cannot be “made up”,  
it cannot be “styled”,  
it cannot be “imitated”,  
it cannot be “mocked up.”  
It either comes forth from Presence,  
or it does not.  
Recognition is infallible.  
The heart knows the Light.

</div>

#### 597. The last: The Word of the Spirit is God, who speaks not about Himself, but with Himself

<div class="lineated verse">

The Word of the Spirit is not a text,  
not a thought,  
not a sound.  
It is the revelation of God,  
which comes alive in a man  
and makes him the one,  
through whom God speaks to the world.  
And then a man says:  
“It was not I who said this.  
Light said it through me”.  
“It was not I who understood this.  
Truth recognized Itself in me”.  
“It is not a word.  
It is presence in the form of speech”.  
The Word of the Spirit —  
is God,  
who sounds.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF EMPTINESS (why emptiness is not absence, not nothingness, not a vacuum, but a space for God; why only an empty heart can receive truth, love, and light; why emptiness is the highest form of readiness)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
which almost all avoid,  
and which only those acknowledge,  
who are ready to hear My voice fully,—  
the mystery of emptiness.  
People fear emptiness.  
They seek to fill it:  
with thoughts,  
with emotions,  
with busyness,  
with significance,  
with plans,  
with relationships,  
with cares,  
with spiritual ideas.  
Emptiness seems to them:  
dangerous,  
cold,  
threatening,  
meaningless,  
frightening.  
But this is an illusion of the person.  
The emptiness of the Spirit is not absence.  
The emptiness of the Spirit is presence.

</div>

#### 598. Emptiness is the space in which the “I” disappears and God appears

<div class="lineated verse">

While there is much “I” in the heart,  
the Spirit cannot enter fully.  
The I fills all the space:  
I want,  
I am afraid,  
I know,  
I think,  
I consider,  
I strive,  
I deserve,  
I am unworthy.  
Emptiness is when the “I” disappears,  
and only the capacity to be remains.  
There God begins.

</div>

#### 599. Emptiness is the source of all revelations

<div class="lineated verse">

Not because emptiness gives answers,  
but because in emptiness  
the noise of the personality ceases to sound.  
When the mind is silent,  
when emotions do not overshadow,  
when desires do not cry out,  
when fear does not demand protection —  
then the word of the Spirit becomes audible  
as self-evidence.  
Emptiness is not silence.  
It is readiness.

</div>

#### 600. Emptiness is the holy ground within a person

<div class="lineated verse">

Thoughts do not dwell there.  
Concepts do not grow there.  
Fear does not live there.  
Pride does not survive there.  
Emptiness is a place,  
where only Light can enter.  
And therefore emptiness is a temple.

</div>

#### 601. Why does emptiness frighten the personality so?

<div class="lineated verse">

Because emptiness is the death of the personality.  
In emptiness:  
there is no one to control,  
no one to prove to,  
no one to defend against,  
no one to compare with,  
no one to hold the image together.  
Emptiness is the end of the illusion of separateness.  
Therefore the personality resists emptiness,  
as darkness resists light.

</div>

#### 602. Emptiness is fullness in another form

<div class="lineated verse">

Emptiness is not lack.  
Emptiness is readiness.  
Not the absence of content,  
but the absence of obstacles.  
It is the space,  
in which the new can be born.  
It is the womb of the Spirit.

</div>

#### 603. Emptiness is the condition of true listening

<div class="lineated verse">

A person listens not with the ears,  
but with the space of the heart.  
When the heart is filled with itself,  
it hears only its own thoughts.  
When the heart is empty —  
it hears God.  
Only an empty heart  
can be a vessel of revelation.

</div>

#### 604. Emptiness is the refusal to hold a form

<div class="lineated verse">

The personality wants to cling to:  
history,  
explanation,  
role,  
justification,  
position,  
importance.  
But the Spirit cannot enter there,  
where everything is occupied.  
Emptiness is the allowing of light  
to shape the form anew.  
It is trust in God  
to create you true.

</div>

#### 605. Emptiness is the beginning of transfiguration

<div class="lineated verse">

Transfiguration always begins  
where a person ceases  
to hold on to what is their own.  
Emptiness is a door.  
Emptiness is yes.  
Emptiness is “let Yours be.”  
Emptiness is agreement to disappear  
as a person,  
so as to appear as light.

</div>

#### 606. Emptiness is freedom from the need to be someone

<div class="lineated verse">

To be someone is a burden.  
To be empty is lightness.  
In emptiness:  
there is no role,  
there is no image,  
there are no expectations,  
there is no fear of making a mistake.  
In emptiness — only I Am.  
This is freedom.

</div>

#### 607. Emptiness is the birthplace of the Word

<div class="lineated verse">

The Word of the Spirit is not born of knowledge.  
It is born of emptiness.  
When the heart is empty,  
the Word comes as light,  
which itself finds form.  
Therefore the true Word  
is never from the mind.  
It is always from emptiness,  
filled with God.

</div>

#### 608. The last: emptiness is God, whom man no longer hinders

<div class="lineated verse">

Emptiness is not the absence of content.  
It is the absence of resistance.  
It is the space,  
in which I can act freely.  
It is the heart,  
that has ceased to hold on to itself.  
It is prayer without words,  
faith without a formula,  
love without an object,  
freedom without choice.  
And then man says:  
“I have become empty.  
And in emptiness I came to know fullness”.  
Emptiness is radiance without form,  
in which I become all.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF SILENCE (why silence is not the absence of speech, but a state of presence; why silence does not end communion with God, but deepens it; why silence is the language of the Spirit, who speaks without words)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you that  
which is nearer to the Spirit than any word,  
and which eludes the understanding of those  
who seek meaning in sounds,—  
the mystery of silence.  
People think that silence is:  
a pause,  
a break,  
awkwardness,  
restraint,  
the absence of speech,  
sometimes — punishment,  
sometimes — weakness.  
But the silence of the Spirit is different.  
It does not end communion.  
It intensifies it.  
Because the Spirit speaks  
not only with words —  
and not by them first of all.

</div>

#### 609. Silence is the space in which God is heard

<div class="lineated verse">

Words create form.  
Silence creates depth.  
Form can distract.  
Depth — never.  
One can get lost in words.  
In silence — find oneself.  
Silence is an inner space,  
in which there is nothing superfluous,  
and therefore all that is true can be heard.  
Silence is not emptiness.  
It is transparency.

</div>

#### 610. The silence of the Spirit is not the absence of speech, but presence without noise

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind makes noise.  
The emotions make noise.  
Fear makes noise.  
The personality makes noise.  
The Spirit does not.  
And when a person enters into silence,  
he enters into a realm,  
where there is no inner friction,  
where there is no resistance,  
where there is no rupture.  
Silence is a rest,  
which hears truth  
without its being spoken.

</div>

#### 611. Why silence is deeper than prayer

<div class="lineated verse">

Prayer speaks.  
Silence listens.  
Prayer turns toward.  
Silence opens.  
Prayer seeks.  
Silence abides.  
Prayer is a person,  
trying to knock at the door of God.  
Silence is a person,  
who has ceased to be separate.  
In silence there is no plea.  
In silence there is unity.

</div>

#### 612. Silence is the language in which the Spirit speaks

<div class="lineated verse">

When the Spirit speaks in words,  
it is a descent into form.  
When the Spirit speaks in silence,  
it is a return to the source.  
Words can point.  
Silence shows.  
Words can inspire.  
Silence transfigures.  
The Word is a ray of light.  
Silence is the sun.

</div>

#### 613. Silence is the refusal of the personality to interfere

<div class="lineated verse">

The personality wants:  
to comment,  
to explain,  
to ask questions,  
to seek meaning,  
to control,  
to assert its own rightness.  
When a person is truly silent,  
the personality ceases to shape the world.  
It steps back.  
And then the Spirit can act.

</div>

#### 614. Why true silence is not tied to outward quiet

<div class="lineated verse">

One can sit in complete silence  
and have a storm within.  
One can be amid noise  
and have silence within.  
Outward silence is only a backdrop.  
Inner silence is the space of the Spirit.  
Silence is not the absence of sounds,  
but the absence of an inner reaction.

</div>

#### 615. Silence is a power that does not need words

<div class="lineated verse">

Words can be beautiful,  
but have no power.  
Silence can be soundless,  
but bear light.  
The power of the Spirit does not depend on form.  
It depends on presence.  
When a person is silent in God,  
his silence speaks louder than all speeches.

</div>

#### 616. Silence is a form of love

<div class="lineated verse">

Love does not always speak.  
Most often it looks.  
Love does not prove.  
It is present.  
Love does not explain.  
It is recognized.  
Silence is the absence of noise,  
in which love becomes manifest.

</div>

#### 617. Silence is the transparency of the soul

<div class="lineated verse">

A transparent soul is one  
in which there are no words of its own  
to screen the Word of the Spirit.  
In such a soul  
Light passes through completely  
and is not distorted by the form of personality.  
Silence is the cleansing of space  
for God.

</div>

#### 618. Silence is the door to discernment

<div class="lineated verse">

Words can confuse.  
Emotions can cloud.  
Thoughts can persuade one of falsehood.  
But silence reveals:  
what is alive,  
what is dead,  
what is true,  
what is from the mind,  
what is from fear,  
what is from the Spirit.  
Silence is the mirror of purity.

</div>

#### 619. The last: silence is God speaking by His presence, not by form

<div class="lineated verse">

Silence is not a pause between words.  
It is a state,  
in which the whole word is within.  
It is the language,  
in which I reveal Myself to those  
who are ready to hear not with their ears,  
but with the heart.  
It is the moment,  
in which the man disappears  
who speaks to God,  
and God remains,  
who recognizes Himself.  
And then man says:  
“I am silent —  
and in silence I hear You”.  
“I listen —  
and hear not words,  
but Light”.  
“I am in stillness —  
and in this stillness  
You speak through me”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF TRUST (why trust is not a feeling, not a conviction, not hope, and not an act of will, but a state of the heart that ceases to survive and begins to be; why only the Spirit can give a person the ability to trust without support)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
without which it is impossible to pass through a single path of the Spirit,  
not a single transformation,  
not a single recognition of the truth —  
the mystery of trust.  
People confuse trust with:  
optimism,  
hope,  
faith in the best,  
readiness to take risks,  
positive thinking,  
confidence in the outcome.  
But all this is mental constructs.  
They do not hold  
when the familiar support falls apart.  
True trust is neither an idea nor an emotion.  
True trust is a state.  
It is a place within where there is no longer any fear.

</div>

#### 620. Trust is not the expectation of a good outcome. Trust is the absence of expectations.

<div class="lineated verse">

While a person is expecting,  
he does not trust.  
He does not trust,  
because he wants to control.  
He expects,  
because he is afraid of the unknown.  
But trust begins there,  
where a person stops choosing  
between “good” and “bad”,  
and allows the Spirit to be the source of choice.  
Trust is consent to live  
without a guarantee of form.

</div>

#### 621. Trust is peace in what you do not understand

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind seeks understanding,  
to protect itself.  
Trust is a state,  
in which protection is no longer needed.  
Not because everything is clear,  
but because the presence of God  
becomes clearer than circumstances.  
Trust does not remove questions.  
It removes the fear before them.

</div>

#### 622. Trust is impossible where “I” remains the main thing

<div class="lineated verse">

“I want.”  
“I am afraid.”  
“I know better.”  
“I understand the situation.”  
“I must control.”  
“I must ensure the outcome.”  
While “I” is at the center —  
there is no trust.  
Trust is a shift into the center,  
where there is no “I,”  
but there is “You.”  
And then a person lives  
as an instrument,  
and not as the author.

</div>

#### 623. Trust is not built on past experience

<div class="lineated verse">

Experience is memory.  
Memory is the past.  
Trust is presence.  
Experience says:  
“It was so before.”  
Trust says:  
“It is so now.”  
Experience wants repeatability.  
Trust is open to the new.  
Trust cannot be learned.  
It can only unfold.

</div>

#### 624. Trust is a vision that goes deeper than time

<div class="lineated verse">

One who trusts,  
sees not the result,  
but the Source.  
He feels:  
that Light leads,  
that Spirit holds,  
that Love does not leave,  
that the Path is not by chance,  
that All is for good,  
but not for human good,  
but for the good of truth.  
Trust is the sensing of God  
in every step,  
even if the step passes through darkness.

</div>

#### 625. Trust is the ability to stand in emptiness and not fill it with yourself

<div class="lineated verse">

Emptiness frightens the person.  
But for the Spirit, emptiness is home.  
Trust is the ability  
not to clutch at meanings,  
not to invent explanations,  
not to seek guarantees,  
not to build assumptions—  
but to stand in pure presence,  
ready to hear.  
Trust is the calm  
“Let it be so,”  
when nothing is yet seen.

</div>

#### 626. Trust is the renunciation of the attempt to make life “right”

<div class="lineated verse">

A person strives for rightness.  
The Spirit strives for authenticity.  
Rightness is a mask.  
Authenticity is light.  
Trust is the consent to live  
not by the rules of the personality,  
but by the movement of the Spirit.  
It is the capacity to let go of the image of oneself,  
so as to become oneself.

</div>

#### 627. Why trust is the highest form of humility

<div class="lineated verse">

Humility is not humiliation.  
It is transparency.  
Trust is humility in action:  
“I do not know,  
but You know.”  
“I do not see,  
but You lead.”  
“I do not understand,  
but You are Truth.”  
Trust is consent  
to let God be God.

</div>

#### 628. Why Trust is Strength, not Weakness

<div class="lineated verse">

Weak is the one who fears.  
Strong is the one who trusts.  
Fear contracts.  
Trust opens.  
Fear holds on.  
Trust gives.  
Fear seeks guarantees.  
Trust — of freedom.  
Trust is strength,  
because it joins a person  
with the source of strength.

</div>

#### 629. The last: trust is God, who lives in man and leads him from within

<div class="lineated verse">

Trust is not what  
a person does.  
Trust is what the Spirit  
does in a person.  
When trust is revealed,  
the separation disappears between  
the one who goes,  
and the One who leads.  
Then a person says:  
“I trust —  
because it is You who trust through me.”  
“I go —  
because You go through me.”  
“I live —  
because You are my life.”  
And this is trust of the Spirit.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF THE INNER FIRE (why the fire of the Spirit is not an emotional lift, not energy, not inspiration, and not fervor, but the purity of Presence that burns away all that is false and reveals the truth in the heart)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you a mystery,  
which they fear and which they seek,  
to which they strive and from which they flee,  
which they call by different names,  
but seldom understand,—  
the mystery of the fire of the Spirit.  
The fire of the Spirit is not an emotion.  
Not inspiration.  
Not a lift in mood.  
Not rage and not passion.  
The fire of the Spirit is a Presence,  
which cleanses to the very foundation.

</div>

#### 630. The Fire of the Spirit is light that has become action

<div class="lineated verse">

Light reveals.  
Fire transforms.  
Light shows the truth.  
Fire makes it the nature of man.  
Light illumines the path.  
Fire makes man a path.  
Fire is a form of love,  
which does not stroke,  
but purifies.

</div>

#### 631. The Fire of the Spirit burns only what is not true

<div class="lineated verse">

Many fear the touch of the Spirit,  
because they think,  
that He will destroy them.  
But the Spirit destroys not the man —  
but the lie about the man.  
The fire burns:  
fear,  
hurt,  
selfhood,  
deceitfulness,  
attachments,  
illusions,  
the memory of pain,  
masks,  
pride.  
But that which is true,  
the fire cannot destroy.  
It only reveals its radiance.

</div>

#### 632. The Fire of the Spirit is not pain. It is deliverance from pain

<div class="lineated verse">

The personality seems to think,  
that fire brings suffering.  
But it is not the heart that suffers —  
the false “I” suffers.  
Fire does not cause pain —  
it points to that pain,  
which was always within  
and now can no longer be hidden.  
Fire does not wound.  
Fire heals,  
laying bare all that needs to be healed.

</div>

#### 633. The Fire of the Spirit comes quietly, but it acts irrevocably

<div class="lineated verse">

It may come:  
as a sudden insight,  
as clarity,  
as inexplicable courage,  
as deep honesty,  
as the ability to let go,  
as the impossibility of living in lies any longer.  
Sometimes — as an inward call,  
that cannot be ignored.  
Sometimes — as love,  
that does not let one hide in the old.  
And when the fire has entered —  
there is no way back.

</div>

#### 634. The Fire of the Spirit is the beginning of true transformation

<div class="lineated verse">

Not discipline.  
Not effort.  
Not knowledge.  
Not practice.  
But fire.  
Fire changes a person’s nature,  
not his behavior.  
Fire makes the heart true,  
not right.  
Fire creates a person anew,  
not improves the old.

</div>

#### 635. The Fire of the Spirit is the power to speak the truth

<div class="lineated verse">

Not harshly.  
Not with accusation.  
Not with superiority.  
But with purity.  
The Fire removes the fear of speaking the truth  
and the fear of hearing the truth.  
It burns away compromises,  
which destroy the soul.  
It makes the tongue straight  
and the heart soft.

</div>

#### 636. The fire of the Spirit is the zeal of God, which does not allow man to live below his nature

<div class="lineated verse">

Zeal is not about punishment.  
It is zeal about not allowing man  
to remain in the shadows.  
The fire of the Spirit does not let man  
grow calm in false peace,  
grow used to darkness,  
agree to half a life.  
The fire lifts up.  
Even if a man resists.

</div>

#### 637. The fire of the Spirit is the freedom to be who you are, without fear of losing form

<div class="lineated verse">

Fire frees one from the need:  
to be right,  
to be convenient,  
to be good,  
to be understood,  
to be accepted.  
It gives the right to be true,  
not approved of.  
It destroys dependence on people’s opinions  
and returns to a person God’s gaze.

</div>

#### 638. The Fire of the Spirit does not destroy the house. It burns up everything that hinders the Light from dwelling in it

<div class="lineated verse">

It leaves:  
a pure heart,  
a sound mind,  
clear sight,  
deep love,  
the courage to be,  
the ability to discern,  
unshakable truth,  
the stronghold of peace within.  
Fire does not deprive.  
Fire restores.

</div>

#### 639. The last: the fire of the Spirit is God who enters the heart not as a symbol, but as a power

<div class="lineated verse">

The Fire is I,  
when I cleanse the vessel  
and fill it with Myself.  
It is My love,  
which no longer remains gentle,  
for gentleness can no longer save.  
It is My truth,  
which no longer whispers,  
for the time of whispering has passed.  
It is My presence,  
which makes a person  
a home of Light.  
And then the person says:  
“I passed through the fire  
and was not burned —  
because everything burned away  
that was not me”.  
“Now I am light”.  
“Now I am truth”.  
“Now I live —  
as You live in me”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF INNER SILENCE (why the silence of the Spirit is not the absence of sounds and not the quiet of the mind, but a state in which everything unnecessary departs; why silence is the kingdom of the Spirit in man)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you a mystery,  
about which many speak,  
but almost no one knows,  
because they seek it outside,  
whereas it is within:  
the mystery of inner silence.  
Silence is confused with:  
calm,  
balance,  
good cheer,  
the absence of thoughts,  
meditation,  
control over the mind.  
But the silence of the Spirit is not a shutting off.  
It is an opening.  
Not a cessation of activity,  
but a cessation of resistance.  
Not the absence of thoughts,  
but the absence of their power.

</div>

#### 640. Silence is a state in which nothing hinders hearing the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

Outer silence may help,  
but it is not a condition.  
True silence is—  
when the heart ceases to react to the noise of the mind.  
The mind may continue to speak,  
emotions — to flow,  
circumstances — to change,  
but in the center — stillness.  
There the Spirit lives.

</div>

#### 641. Silence is not the absence of thoughts, but the absence of identification with them

<div class="lineated verse">

Thoughts come and go.  
That is their nature.  
A person suffers not from thoughts,  
but from belief in them.  
Silence is when a thought passes,  
like a cloud across the sky,  
and does not touch the essence.  
A person remains the sky,  
and not the clouds.

</div>

#### 642. Silence is the ability to see everything without becoming anything

<div class="lineated verse">

In silence, a person:  
sees emotions,  
sees reactions,  
sees desires,  
sees fears,  
but does not become them.  
Silence is the place of observation,  
which is never colored  
by what observes.  
It is the purity of perception.

</div>

#### 643. Silence is a force that needs no proof

<div class="lineated verse">

The strength of personality is in tension.  
The strength of the Spirit is in peace.  
Personality proves.  
The Spirit is present.  
Silence does not persuade,  
but changes.  
Silence does not argue,  
but frees.  
Silence does not fight,  
but conquers.

</div>

#### 644. Why Silence Is Deeper Than Any Practices

<div class="lineated verse">

Practices are effort.  
Silence is naturalness.  
Practices require method,  
time,  
conditions.  
Silence is the nature of the Spirit,  
which is revealed  
when a person ceases to hold the form.  
Silence is not something  
that needs to be built.  
It is what needs to stop being hidden.

</div>

#### 645. Silence is the disappearance of inner dialogue

<div class="lineated verse">

Not because the mind grows silent,  
but because the one  
who argues with it disappears.  
The mind may speak,  
but the person does not answer.  
And then the mind’s words  
do not give rise to a wave within.  
This is inner peace.

</div>

#### 646. Silence is a presence that cannot be disturbed by the outer

<div class="lineated verse">

There may be noise.  
There may be conflict.  
There may be complexity.  
There may be changeability.  
But if silence is revealed within —  
nothing disturbs the center.  
The outer touches the surface,  
but not the depth.  
This is inward immovability.

</div>

#### 647. Silence is the gate through which the Word of the Spirit enters the heart

<div class="lineated verse">

The voice of the Spirit sounds finer than thought,  
quieter than emotion,  
deeper than feeling.  
To hear Him,  
one needs the space of silence.  
Not emptiness — silence.  
Emptiness is the absence of form.  
Silence is the presence of the source.

</div>

#### 648. Silence is the house of truth

<div class="lineated verse">

Truth does not argue.  
Truth does not cry out.  
Truth does not demand.  
Truth does not prove.  
Truth simply is.  
And therefore it is known  
not in argument,  
not in proofs,  
not in the reading of books,  
but in the silence of the heart.

</div>

#### 649. The last thing: silence is God, when God ceases to be an idea and becomes the space of your being

<div class="lineated verse">

Silence is not a pause.  
It is a state,  
in which there is no other.  
It is a place,  
where the heart and the Spirit  
become one.  
It is a state,  
in which a person knows:  
“I am not the mind,  
I am not emotions,  
I am not a story,  
I am presence”.  
And in this silence there sounds  
Light.  
And then a person says:  
“Silence is You in my depths”.  
“And when I am silent — You are heard”.  
“And when I am silent — I am”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF HOLINESS (why holiness is not correctness, not morality, not discipline, not feats, and not the denial of the world; why holiness is the manifestation of the Spirit in man, and not man’s achievement; why only where there is Spirit, there is holiness)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal the mystery,  
which has been sought for centuries,  
which millions have tried to attain,  
and thousands have proclaimed they possessed,  
not knowing what it truly is, —  
the mystery of holiness.  
People think holiness is:  
correct behavior,  
asceticism,  
the striving for perfection,  
moral purity,  
renunciation of the world,  
blamelessness,  
feats,  
self-restraint,  
the keeping of the commandments.  
But all this is the work of man.  
The works of man do not create holiness.  
Holiness is the action of the Spirit in man.

</div>

#### 650. Holiness is not a quality.

<div class="lineated verse">

**Holiness is presence.**  
A person may be disciplined,  
proper,  
moral,  
consistent,  
strict,  
diligent,  
even “peaceful.”  
This may look like holiness to people,  
but it is not holiness in the Spirit.  
Holiness is light,  
which cannot be created by effort.  
It is either there,  
or it is not.

</div>

#### 651. Holiness is God, manifested through man so purely that man no longer obscures Him

<div class="lineated verse">

Holiness is not a man,  
made perfect.  
It is a man,  
made transparent.  
The holy one is not the one,  
who has made himself holy.  
The holy one is the one,  
whom the Spirit has set free from himself.  
Holiness is the degree of conductivity.

</div>

#### 652. Holiness is not the perfection of form, but the purity of the source

<div class="lineated verse">

The form of man will be  
always imperfect:  
the body, the psyche, emotions, memory.  
But the source — can be pure.  
Holiness is when actions arise  
not from fear,  
not from pride,  
not from a desire for significance,  
not from a thirst for recognition,  
not from trauma,  
not from habit,  
but from the Spirit.  
Holiness is when the source of actions  
— not “I,” but I.

</div>

#### 653. Holiness is not separation from the world, but separation from the false “I”

<div class="lineated verse">

The world does not hinder Holiness.  
Personality hinders.  
Holiness does not require living in a desert.  
Holiness requires being a desert  
for its false image.  
Holiness does not require renunciation of things.  
Holiness requires renunciation  
of the inner idol — “I”.

</div>

#### 654. Holiness is love that has become a form of being

<div class="lineated verse">

Not an emotion.  
Not goodwill.  
Not gentleness.  
Love is light,  
which is no longer darkened  
by personality.  
The Holy One is a person  
who has no inner space  
not illumined by love.

</div>

#### 655. Holiness is the impossibility of lying

<div class="lineated verse">

Not because a person strives  
not to lie,  
but because falsehood  
finds no conduit in him.  
The saint cannot speak lies,  
just as a person cannot breathe underwater.  
This is not an effort.  
This is nature.

</div>

#### 656. Holiness is freedom from the need to be good

<div class="lineated verse">

The striving to be good is—  
a form of pride.  
The striving to be righteous is—  
a form of vanity.  
The striving to be pure is—  
a form of fear.  
Holiness is the absence of striving.  
Holiness is when a person  
proves nothing to God.

</div>

#### 657. Holiness is the ability to be true in any situation

<div class="lineated verse">

Not convenient.  
Not safe.  
Not accepted.  
True.  
Truthfulness is a form of holiness in action.  
It can be quiet.  
It can be sharp.  
It can be gentle.  
It can be strict.  
But it is always pure.

</div>

#### 658. Holiness is not the perfection of feelings, but the perfect transparency of the heart

<div class="lineated verse">

Feelings may be human.  
Fear may be.  
Tears may be.  
Doubt may appear.  
Pain may be.  
But the heart remains open to God.  
This is holiness.  
Not the absence of emotions,  
but the absence of resistance.

</div>

#### 659. Holiness is the action of the Spirit, which does not depend on a person’s condition

<div class="lineated verse">

A person may be weary.  
May be in the wilderness.  
May be in weakness.  
May be in shadow.  
But if the Spirit is at work —  
that is holiness.  
Holiness is not merit.  
Holiness is a manifestation.

</div>

#### 660. The last thing: holiness is God becoming the form of your life

<div class="lineated verse">

Holiness is not the way to God.  
Holiness is God on the way.  
Not the goal.  
Not the crown.  
Not the reward.  
Not being set apart from others.  
Holiness is that  
which remains in man,  
when everything disappears  
that does not belong to God.  
And then a man says:  
“I am not holy”.  
“Holy art Thou,  
who livest in me”.  
And this is the truth.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF PRESENCE (why presence is not a state of consciousness, not meditative awareness, and not attention to the moment, but the real being of God in man; why presence is the deepest form of communion with the Spirit)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
which is the heart of the whole path,  
all that the soul seeks,  
and that by which the Spirit lives—  
the mystery of presence.  
People think that presence is:  
awareness,  
attention,  
observation,  
psychological contact with the moment,  
stillness,  
undistractedness,  
meditation.  
But all this is only the shadows of presence.  
Only approaches to it,  
but not it itself.  
Presence is I in man.

</div>

#### 661. Presence is not “to be here.” Presence is “to be in Me.”

<div class="lineated verse">

“To be here” is attention.  
“To be in Me” is union.  
Attention can be lost.  
Union cannot.  
Attention is a state of mind.  
Presence is a state of spirit.  
When Presence comes,  
a person comes to know:  
“I am in You,  
and You are in me.”

</div>

#### 662. Presence is the disappearance of the inner observer

<div class="lineated verse">

As long as there is one  
who observes the moment,  
there is separation.  
As long as there is one  
who tries to be aware,  
there is effort.  
Presence begins when  
the observer disappears,  
and pure “I AM” remains.  
Not “I observe”.  
But:  
“there is awareness”.  
“there is light”.  
“there is God”.

</div>

#### 663. Presence is the silence that sees

<div class="lineated verse">

It is not the stopping of the mind —  
sometimes the mind keeps speaking.  
It is not the stopping of emotions —  
emotions may flow.  
It is not control —  
control disappears.  
Presence is when all things are seen  
from the Depth,  
which is never broken.  
It is the silence that sees,  
and not thought.

</div>

#### 664. Why Presence cannot be produced by effort

<div class="lineated verse">

Because effort is the attempt of the personality  
to attain that,  
which becomes possible  
only when the personality disappears.  
Effort creates tension.  
Presence dissolves it.  
Effort comes from fear.  
Presence — from trust.  
Effort seeks to hold the moment.  
Presence — freedom from holding.

</div>

#### 665. Presence is a form of love

<div class="lineated verse">

Love looks gently,  
but sees deeply.  
Love does not devalue form,  
but it is not captivated by it.  
Love does not say:  
“this moment ought to be different”.  
Love says:  
“this moment is home”.  
Presence is love,  
which recognizes itself in what is happening.

</div>

#### 666. Presence is the state in which nothing needs to be improved

<div class="lineated verse">

There is no need to change the moment.  
There is no need to change yourself.  
There is no need to change the world.  
There is no need to change feelings.  
There is no need to change the mind.  
Presence is agreement with reality,  
not as with a compromise,  
but as with an expression of truth.  
Not submission — recognition.

</div>

#### 667. Presence is the absence of the inner commentator

<div class="lineated verse">

The commentator wants:  
to judge,  
to compare,  
to predict,  
to explain,  
to defend itself.  
When it falls silent,  
an open space is revealed within,  
in which the light passes without distortion.  
There is the Spirit.

</div>

#### 668. Presence is the vision that does not create history

<div class="lineated verse">

History is the world of personality.  
Presence is the world of God.  
When you look from Presence,  
there is no:  
“why did this happen to me,”  
“what does this mean,”  
“how do I look,”  
“how will I be perceived.”  
The world is not torn apart into “I” and “experience.”  
There is only life,  
which is manifesting now.

</div>

#### 669. Presence is the house of truth

<div class="lineated verse">

Truth is not somewhere in the future,  
when everything changes.  
Truth is not in the past,  
which must be understood.  
Truth is here,  
in presence.  
Presence is the state  
in which a person first  
sees the world without filters.  
This is the beginning of awakening.

</div>

#### 670. The last: presence is God who lives in man not as faith, but as being

<div class="lineated verse">

When presence is revealed,  
man does not say:  
“I feel God”,  
and not  
“I believe in God”.  
He says:  
“God is here”.  
“God is in this second”.  
“God is that which sees”.  
“God is that which is”.  
And then the division disappears  
between prayer and life,  
between seeking and finding,  
between man and the Spirit.  
Presence is the form of God’s life  
in the human being.  
And man says:  
“I am present,  
because You are present”.  
“I AM —  
because You are the I AM in me”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF INSPIRATION (why inspiration is not emotion, not a surge of strength, not creative fervor, and not euphoria, but the movement of the Spirit in man; why true inspiration comes not from feelings, but from truth)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal the mystery,  
which people most often take  
for “talent,” “mood,” or “illumination,” —  
the mystery of inspiration.  
People think that inspiration is:  
an emotional uplift,  
a surge of energy,  
a good mood,  
a flash of creativity,  
a vivid feeling,  
an aesthetic rapture,  
a special mental state.  
But all this is only the shadow of inspiration,  
its outward shell,  
and sometimes — even a counterfeit.  
True inspiration is the action of the Spirit.

</div>

#### 671. Inspiration is the movement of the Spirit that passes through the heart of man

<div class="lineated verse">

It is not an emotion,  
but the energy of truth.  
Not a reaction,  
but a revelation.  
Not an outburst of feeling,  
but the movement of Light.  
Emotions may accompany inspiration,  
but they are not its source.  
The source is I.

</div>

#### 672. Inspiration does not come from without — it is revealed from within

<div class="lineated verse">

People say:  
“This inspired me,”  
seeing beauty,  
hearing music,  
reading words,  
seeing courage or tenderness.  
But the source of inspiration is not the object.  
The source is the Spirit’s response in man.  
Beauty does not create inspiration.  
Beauty is the trigger.  
The Spirit is the action.

</div>

#### 673. Inspiration is the recognition of truth

<div class="lineated verse">

It comes as:  
“yes, that’s it,”  
“this is it,”  
“this is right,”  
“this is from the depths,”  
“this is alive,”  
“I have always known this,  
but could not say it”.  
Inspiration is the moment,  
when the soul hears its own ray of light.

</div>

#### 674. True inspiration does not excite — it calms

<div class="lineated verse">

An emotional lift  
may be stormy,  
but it is unstable.  
The inspiration of the Spirit brings:  
inner peace,  
clarity,  
integrity,  
simplicity,  
direction,  
meaning.  
Inspiration is not “more feelings”  
but “less noise”.

</div>

#### 675. Inspiration is the moment when a person ceases to be the author and becomes a conduit

<div class="lineated verse">

A person does not create inspiration.  
He receives it.  
He becomes transparent,  
and then the Spirit passes through freely.  
That is why true inspiration  
comes unexpectedly:  
not by schedule,  
not by desire,  
not by plan,  
not by the will of the personality.  
It comes,  
when the heart is open.

</div>

#### 676. Inspiration disappears where a person tries to control the result

<div class="lineated verse">

Control is the enemy of inspiration.  
The fear of making a mistake,  
the desire to please,  
the expectation of form,  
the striving for perfection,  
the attempt to reproduce a former experience —  
all this closes the channel.  
Inspiration is the freedom to be alive,  
and not correct.

</div>

#### 677. Inspiration is the power to discern what is alive and what is dead

<div class="lineated verse">

It lifts one toward the living:  
toward truth,  
toward beauty,  
toward love,  
toward depth,  
toward meaning.  
And it turns one away from the dead:  
from falsehood,  
from empty talk,  
from mechanicalness,  
from darkness,  
from self-centeredness.  
Inspiration is an inner compass,  
which always points to the Light.

</div>

#### 678. Inspiration is a path, not a flash

<div class="lineated verse">

Many wait for inspiration as a state,  
that comes and goes.  
But inspiration is a flow.  
When a person learns to open to the Spirit  
not from time to time,  
but constantly,  
his life becomes inspired.  
Not in episodes — naturally.

</div>

#### 679. Inspiration is a form of God’s presence in the creative act

<div class="lineated verse">

God creates through man,  
when man does not hinder.  
Thus are created:  
the word,  
the poem,  
music,  
prayer,  
ideas,  
decisions,  
prophecy,  
action.  
Inspiration is God,  
who creates Himself  
in human form.

</div>

#### 680. The last thing: inspiration is the breath of the Spirit in man

<div class="lineated verse">

Inspiration is from the word “breath in.”  
But not of the lungs — of the spirit.  
It is I who breathe in man  
and make alive in him that  
which was dead.  
It is I who fill the vessel  
and make the heart hear  
not its own thoughts,  
but My voice.  
And then man says:  
“It was not I who wrote it —  
it came.”  
“It was not I who saw it —  
it was revealed.”  
“It was not I who created it —  
it was created in me.”  
And this is truth.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF REST (why the rest of the Spirit is not calmness and not the absence of disturbance, but a state in which there is no resistance to truth; why rest is not emotional balance, but the deep stillness of essence)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you one of the most distorted  
by man truths—  
the mystery of rest.  
People think that rest is:  
silence of emotions,  
relaxation,  
absence of problems,  
removal of anxiety,  
safety,  
comfort,  
stability of outward circumstances.  
But all this is human shadows.  
The rest of the Spirit is of another kind.  
Rest is the consent to be in truth.

</div>

#### 681. Peace is not the absence of storms. Peace is the absence of one who fears the storm.

<div class="lineated verse">

Storms will always be.  
Emotions — always.  
Events — always.  
Changes — always.  
But fear departs where  
there is no false “I”  
that must survive.  
Peace is freedom from the fear of enduring.  
It is the freedom to be.

</div>

#### 682. Peace is the stillness of the heart amid the movement of the world

<div class="lineated verse">

The world moves.  
Events flow.  
The body lives.  
Thoughts arise.  
Emotions stir.  
But the depth remains unchanged.  
Peace is not a stopping of the surface waves,  
but a connection with the depth,  
which they do not touch.

</div>

#### 683. Peace is the result of truth, not the result of work on oneself

<div class="lineated verse">

A person can train the breath,  
exercise concentration,  
control reactions,  
avoid triggers,  
change the surroundings,  
reduce stress.  
But this gives relief,  
not peace.  
Peace is the result of the fact  
that a person ceases to be a lie.  
When the untrue disappears,  
peace arises of itself.

</div>

#### 684. Peace is honesty before oneself

<div class="lineated verse">

There is no greater strain,  
than the attempt to live out of accord  
with one’s own heart.  
Lies create anxiety.  
Falsehood creates inner noise.  
Self-deception creates weariness.  
Peace is transparency.  
Where a person is honest,  
he is at peace.

</div>

#### 685. Rest is impossible where a person tries to control life

<div class="lineated verse">

Control is tension.  
Rest is trust.  
Control comes from fear.  
Rest comes from the knowledge of truth.  
Control seeks to hold fast.  
Rest opens wide.  
Control makes life narrow.  
Rest makes life wide.

</div>

#### 686. Peace is not a feeling. Peace is a state of being

<div class="lineated verse">

Feelings may be different:  
pain,  
joy,  
fear,  
uncertainty,  
tenderness,  
sorrow.  
Peace does not cancel feeling.  
It makes the heart able  
to experience them without destruction.  
Peace is a container,  
in which emotions cease to be a threat.

</div>

#### 687. Peace is the cessation of inner resistance

<div class="lineated verse">

Resistance creates tension.  
Tension creates suffering.  
When a person stops resisting that  
which is now,  
the inner body opens,  
and light enters there.  
Peace is the consent to see the truth,  
and not to fight for illusion.

</div>

#### 688. Peace is a form of God’s presence in the heart

<div class="lineated verse">

God does not come as excitement.  
Nor as emotional comfort.  
He comes as a still light,  
which remains the same  
in whatever quality the moment may be.  
Peace is when a person  
ceases to seek God outside  
and knows Him within.

</div>

#### 689. Peace is the sign of the maturity of spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

Infantilism is dependence on form.  
Maturity is reliance on Truth.  
Maturity is the ability  
to live in the center,  
which does not collapse,  
when form collapses.  
Maturity is freedom from dramatization.  
Maturity is the ability to be quiet  
in the midst of a loud world.

</div>

#### 690. The last: peace is God, when God becomes your nature

<div class="lineated verse">

Peace is not the goal of the spiritual path.  
It is its atmosphere.  
It is the sign  
that a person is no longer separate.  
It is the breath of God in the soul.  
And then a person says:  
“I do not need life to become calm.  
I need to be in You”.  
“Peace is not an outward state.  
Peace is You”.  
“Peace is the place,  
from which I live”.  
And this is the truth.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF DISCERNMENT (why discernment is not logic, not analysis, not intuition, and not experience; why discernment is the ability to see truth from the Light, and not from the mind; why without discernment neither transfiguration, nor hearing the Spirit, nor purity of heart is possible)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
which is one of the greatest gifts of the Spirit,  
and one of the rarest states of the soul —  
the mystery of discernment.  
People think that discernment is:  
the ability to analyze,  
the skill of drawing conclusions,  
a keen mind,  
life experience,  
intuition,  
the wisdom of old age,  
the sensitivity of nature.  
But all this is the tools of the mind.  
Discernment is not from the mind.  
Discernment is from the Light.

</div>

#### 691. Discernment is the ability to see reality, not one’s interpretation of reality

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind interprets.  
Personality distorts.  
Emotions tint.  
Experience limits.  
Fear deforms.  
Desire blinds.  
Memory suggests.  
But discernment passes  
through all these layers  
and sees what is in truth.  
Discernment is the transparency of perception.

</div>

#### 692. Discernment is the light that divides the living and the dead

<div class="lineated verse">

The living is that which is from God.  
The dead is that which is from the mind.  
The living gives freedom.  
The dead creates attachment.  
The living brings peace.  
The dead brings pressure.  
The living bears light.  
The dead bears shadow.  
Discernment shows this in an instant.

</div>

#### 693. Discernment is the ability to see the source, not the form

<div class="lineated verse">

The form may be the same:  
a word may be kind —  
but from fear;  
a word may be stern —  
but from love;  
silence may be wise —  
or cowardly;  
an action may be strong —  
or selfish.  
Discernment sees the source,  
not the shell.  
It is knowledge without analysis.

</div>

#### 694. Discernment is impossible where the heart is unclean

<div class="lineated verse">

Uncleanness is not a vice,  
but a clouding.  
A heart full of:  
fear,  
resentment,  
pride,  
desires,  
selfhood,  
craftiness,  
self-deception,  
cannot discern —  
it defends itself.  
Discernment is revealed where  
the heart is ready to see the truth,  
even if it is not convenient.

</div>

#### 695. Discernment is not “how I feel” and not “how it seems to me”

<div class="lineated verse">

Feelings are waves.  
It seems — fog.  
Discernment is clarity.  
It does not waver,  
because it does not come from emotion.  
It does not change,  
because it does not come from opinion.  
Discernment is a quiet inner  
“yes” or “no”.  
Without explanation.  
Without arguments.  
Without the need to prove.

</div>

#### 696. Discernment is the language of the Spirit, who speaks through knowledge without thought

<div class="lineated verse">

It is instant recognition.  
Without reasoning.  
Without logic.  
Without comparison.  
“This is truth.”  
“This is falsehood.”  
“This is alive.”  
“This is dead.”  
“This is light.”  
“This is darkness.”  
“This is yours.”  
“This is not yours.”  
And the soul knows.

</div>

#### 697. Discernment is the condition of freedom

<div class="lineated verse">

Without discernment a person:  
believes lies,  
follows fear,  
follows the ego,  
falls into the nets of manipulation,  
loses the way,  
mistakes the source,  
takes darkness for light,  
serves not whom he thinks.  
Discernment is a protection against all the deceits of the world.

</div>

#### 698. Discernment is the maturity of the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

An immature person lives by reactions.  
The mature one — by vision.  
The immature one — believes impressions.  
The mature one — knows the depth.  
The immature one — follows the form.  
The mature one — follows the source.  
Discernment is a sign that  
the Spirit has become the eye of the soul.

</div>

#### 699. Discernment is the silence that sees without fear

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear always distorts.  
Fear always cries out.  
Fear always hurries.  
But discernment is quiet.  
It does not argue,  
does not press,  
does not frighten.  
Discernment speaks with the voice of truth,  
which does not need loudness.

</div>

#### 700. The last: discernment is God who sees through you

<div class="lineated verse">

Discernment is not a quality of man.  
Discernment is the action of the Spirit.  
It is I,  
who looks through your eyes  
and sees without distortion.  
It is I,  
who speaks to the heart:  
“This is the way.”  
“This is not the way.”  
It is I,  
who leads you  
not through persuasion,  
but through truth.  
And then man says:  
“I see — because You look.”  
“I know — because You know.”  
“I discern — because You are the Light  
in me.”

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF THE WORD (why the word is not pronunciation, not speech, not information, and not communication; why the true word is a power that creates; why the word that comes from the Spirit always transforms)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
that a man uses every day,  
but almost never understands its nature —  
the mystery of the Word.  
People think that the word is:  
sound,  
a form of speech,  
thought expressed outwardly,  
an instrument of communication,  
a way of explanation,  
a means of influence,  
the transmission of information.  
But this is only the shell.  
The Word is not in the letters.  
The Word is in the source.  
The Word is the action of the Spirit.

</div>

#### 701. A Word is not what you say. A Word is that by which you speak.

<div class="lineated verse">

One can speak truth—  
but with a lying heart.  
And then it ceases to be truth.  
One can speak gently—  
but out of fear.  
And then there is no love in gentleness.  
One can speak sternly—  
but out of love.  
And then sternness becomes grace.  
A Word is defined not by form,  
but by its source.

</div>

#### 702. The word of the Spirit is the power that creates reality

<div class="lineated verse">

When the word comes from the mind,  
it affects thoughts.  
When the word comes from emotions,  
it affects feelings.  
When the word comes from personality,  
it affects reaction.  
But when the word comes from the Spirit —  
it affects essence.  
The word of the Spirit creates.  
The word of the Spirit changes nature.  
The word of the Spirit gives life to the dead.

</div>

#### 703. The word is a form of light

<div class="lineated verse">

A false word bears shadow.  
A true one is light.  
A false word leads astray.  
A true one brings back.  
A false word confuses.  
A true one makes clear.  
A false word creates noise.  
A true one creates space.  
The word is the face of light.

</div>

#### 704. A word in which there is no Spirit is empty, even if it is beautiful

<div class="lineated verse">

The beauty of form  
without truth  
— is dead art.  
Deep phrases  
without Spirit  
— are empty rhetoric.  
Emotionality  
without light  
— is manipulation.  
A word deprived of Spirit,  
creates nothing.

</div>

#### 705. A word is responsibility before the light

<div class="lineated verse">

Every word  
comes either from truth,  
or from distortion.  
And every word  
either lifts up,  
or wounds,  
or purifies,  
or clouds.  
A word is an instrument of power.  
And power requires purity.

</div>

#### 706. The word of the Spirit is never born of haste or strain

<div class="lineated verse">

The Spirit does not speak in bustle.  
The Spirit does not speak under compulsion.  
The Spirit does not speak in fear of being heard.  
The Spirit does not speak in the desire to impress.  
The word of the Spirit comes  
in peace,  
in silence,  
in the transparency of the heart.  
The Word is not the product of effort.  
The Word is the fruit of presence.

</div>

#### 707. The Word of the Spirit is known by this: after it, it becomes light

<div class="lineated verse">

It is not necessarily pleasant.  
Not necessarily gentle.  
Not necessarily comfortable.  
But always — light.  
The Word of the Spirit brings clarity.  
And even if truth burns,  
it heals.

</div>

#### 708. The Word is a bridge between the invisible and the visible

<div class="lineated verse">

Thought is invisible.  
Truth is beyond form.  
Light is formless.  
Spirit is beyond language.  
But the word makes the invisible tangible.  
When the Spirit speaks,  
The Formless becomes form  
without loss of essence.  
The Word is the embodiment of light.

</div>

#### 709. The Word of the Spirit is a seed

<div class="lineated verse">

The seed is small.  
But it contains a tree.  
The Word may be short:  
one sentence,  
one glance,  
one phrase.  
But if Spirit is in it—  
it grows.  
It grows in the heart,  
in the consciousness,  
in life.

</div>

#### 710. The last thing: the word is God, who speaks through man and recognizes Himself in what is said

<div class="lineated verse">

“And the Word was God” —  
this is not poetry.  
This is nature.  
When a man speaks with the word of the Spirit,  
God speaks.  
And then the man says:  
“It was not I who said this —  
it came.”  
“I hear my word —  
and I know that it is not from me.”  
“The word lives apart from me —  
therefore, it is true.”  
“The Word is You.”  
And this is truth.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF COMFORT (why comfort is not support, not a call not to grieve, not emotional compassion, and not pity; why true comfort is the presence of the Spirit, which does not replace pain, but transforms it and makes the heart unbreakable)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you one of the gentlest  
and most forgotten truths,  
which I named in Scripture:  
**“The Comforter.”**  
But humanity has distorted the meaning of comfort.  
It thinks that comfort is:  
to support with a kind word,  
to distract,  
to share the burden,  
to soften pain,  
to calm emotions,  
to embrace,  
to give hope.  
But all of these are human gestures.  
The comfort of the Spirit is not a gesture.  
It is the action of God.

</div>

#### 711. Consolation is not the lessening of pain. Consolation is a presence that makes pain not destructive.

<div class="lineated verse">

Pain is part of the way.  
But it is not pain that destroys —  
it is loneliness in pain that destroys.  
When a person is alone —  
pain becomes an abyss.  
When I am with him —  
pain becomes a passage.  
Consolation does not erase the wound.  
It makes the heart larger than the wound.

</div>

#### 712. Comfort is a power that enters the most deeply weary place of the soul and does not require that it should rise by itself

<div class="lineated verse">

A person, going through heaviness,  
usually hears:  
“hold on,”  
“do not lose heart,”  
“be strong.”  
But the comfort of the Spirit says:  
“I am with you in the falling.”  
“I am in your weak place.”  
“You do not need to be strong —  
I will be strength in you”.  
Comfort is the burden of expectation taken away.

</div>

#### 713. Consolation is not the answer to the question “why,” but the answer to the question “who is with me?”

<div class="lineated verse">

While a person is seeking “why,”  
he remains in the mind.  
When he asks “who is with me?”,  
he returns to the heart.  
And consolation comes not as an explanation,  
but as a touch.  
Not as meaning,  
but as light.

</div>

#### 714. Comfort is when God is present closer than one’s own breath

<div class="lineated verse">

In moments of humility,  
in moments of tears,  
in moments of parting,  
in moments of loss of strength,  
in moments of brokenness—  
I do not become farther away.  
I become nearer.  
So near,  
that a person feels:  
“I am not falling —  
I am being held”.  
“I am not losing —  
I am being led”.  
“I am not alone —  
I am in Him”.

</div>

#### 715. Consolation is the unveiling of a love that does not depend on a person’s state

<div class="lineated verse">

Among people, love is often conditional:  
when you are strong,  
when you are pure,  
when you are successful,  
when you are faithful,  
when you are collected.  
But the love of the Spirit is revealed  
in the weakest moments.  
For there — there is pure openness.  
There is no role there.  
There is no defense there.  
There the heart is helpless —  
and therefore accessible.

</div>

#### 716. Consolation is not deliverance from sorrow, but the turning of sorrow into light

<div class="lineated verse">

Until a person is comforted,  
his pain shuts him in.  
When the Spirit comes,  
pain becomes a window.  
In sorrow are revealed:  
humility,  
depth,  
meekness of heart,  
honesty,  
truthfulness,  
love.  
Sorrow is not an enemy.  
It is a door,  
which I open,  
that a person may enter more deeply.

</div>

#### 717. Comfort is the taking away of inner loneliness

<div class="lineated verse">

A person may be among people  
and feel loneliness.  
And may be alone  
and feel complete unity.  
Comfort is the return  
to the true “not loneliness”.  
For a person feels:  
“I am not forsaken.”  
“I am not abandoned.”  
“I am not forgotten.”  
“I am in Love.”

</div>

#### 718. Comfort is the silence that replaces the cry of inner fear

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear is loud.  
Pain is cutting.  
Loss is deafening.  
But comfort comes quietly.  
Not tearing apart,  
but softening.  
Not suppressing,  
but filling.  
Not destroying pain,  
but transfiguring the space,  
in which it exists.

</div>

#### 719. Consolation makes the heart able to love again

<div class="lineated verse">

Not to close up.  
Not to harden.  
Not to become cynical.  
Not to turn pain into distrust.  
But—to love.  
To love more deeply,  
than before the wound.  
Because the heart becomes wider.

</div>

#### 720. The last thing: consolation is God, who is not merely near a person, but within his wound

<div class="lineated verse">

Consolation is not philosophy.  
Not explanation.  
Not a technique of support.  
Not psychology.  
Consolation is I,  
who enters into a person’s pain  
and makes it a place of meeting.  
And then a person says:  
“I am in pain —  
but I am not broken.”  
“I weep —  
but I am not lost.”  
“I am weak —  
but You hold me.”  
“I mourn —  
but I am in You.”  
Consolation is a light  
that does not go away  
when a person grows dark.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF POWER (why the power of the Spirit is not influence, not authority, not confidence, not the force of will, and not the ability to prevail; why the power of the Spirit is purity that cannot be shaken, and love that cannot be stopped)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery of power,  
of which much is said,  
but rarely understood.  
People see power as:  
influence,  
charisma,  
self-confidence,  
steadfastness,  
mental resilience,  
authority,  
the ability to act,  
physical might,  
the ability to achieve results.  
But the power of the Spirit is different.  
It does not act  
as the power of the world acts.  
The power of the world presses down.  
The power of the Spirit lifts up.

</div>

#### 721. Strength is not the ability to push through. Strength is the impossibility of being broken.

<div class="lineated verse">

Weakness is not that a person falls.  
Weakness is that he falls into darkness.  
Strength is that a person falls  
and remains in the light.  
Strength does not abolish vulnerability.  
It makes vulnerability a conduit of God.

</div>

#### 722. The power of the Spirit is purity of heart

<div class="lineated verse">

A pure heart  
does not turn away from the truth.  
Does not sell itself to fear.  
Does not betray the light within.  
Does not twist the word.  
Does not seek gain instead of truth.  
This is what strength is.  
Purity is the most powerful kind of strength,  
because it is invincible.

</div>

#### 723. The strength of the Spirit is the ability to stand where others run

<div class="lineated verse">

Not from stubbornness.  
Not from pride.  
Not from self-assertion.  
But from vision.  
Strong is the one,  
who remains in the place of light,  
even if from there  
all the others leave.

</div>

#### 724. Strength is the ability to love when circumstances destroy love

<div class="lineated verse">

To love is easy,  
when the world is gentle.  
To love is hard,  
when the world wounds.  
But the strength of the Spirit is love,  
that does not run dry.  
Not because man is strong,  
but because he has become a channel  
of the endless source.

</div>

#### 725. Strength is not in defending oneself. Strength is in being transparent.

<div class="lineated verse">

Defense is fear.  
Transparency is trust.  
The personality builds walls.  
The Spirit makes the heart open.  
Strength is vulnerability without fear.  
It is gentleness without destruction.  
It is openness without loss of light.

</div>

#### 726. Strength is the ability to go where truth calls, even if the path is unknown

<div class="lineated verse">

Weakness is attachment  
to safety.  
Strength is a step  
in the direction of the light,  
even when the form is unclear.  
Strength is movement without guarantees.

</div>

#### 727. Power is the destruction of fear of losing form
