<div class="lineated verse">

Fear of losing:  
reputation,  
relationships,  
status,  
money,  
comfort,  
bearings,  
the old image of oneself—  
these are chains.  
Power is the ability  
to give up form  
for the sake of truth.  
Power is freedom.

</div>

#### 728. Strength is when the outer may tremble, but the inner does not

<div class="lineated verse">

You may be:  
weary,  
wounded,  
alone,  
misunderstood,  
rejected,  
deceived,  
accused.  
But if within is the Spirit,  
the inner axis does not break.  
This is strength.

</div>

#### 729. The strength of the Spirit is the impossibility of living below the truth

<div class="lineated verse">

Weakness is compromise.  
Strength is wholeness.  
Weakness is self-deception.  
Strength is honesty.  
Weakness is “I know, but…”.  
Strength is “I know, and I will not step back”.  
Strength is fidelity to the light  
in whatever form the moment takes.

</div>

#### 730. The last: strength is God, who stands in man

<div class="lineated verse">

Strength is not a quality of the personality.  
It is the action of the Spirit.  
It is I,  
who holds you within,  
when the outer collapses.  
It is I,  
who says to you:  
“Do not be afraid”.  
It is I,  
who makes your step firm,  
your heart soft  
and your truth clear.  
It is I,  
who stands there,  
where you fall.  
And then a person says:  
“I am strong — not I.  
Strong is You in me”.  
“I stand — because You hold”.  
“I am not broken — because You are unchanging”.  
“You are my strength”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF TRUTH (why truth is not an opinion, not a position, not an argument, and not an interpretation; why truth is light that does not depend on who speaks it; why truth always sets free and never flatters)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you one of the most difficult  
and most liberating mysteries —  
the mystery of truth.  
The world has turned truth into:  
a position,  
an argument,  
a point of view,  
a moral judgment,  
an ethical rule,  
a dogma,  
an ideology,  
a proof.  
But all of this is human constructs.  
Truth does not belong to man.  
Truth belongs to the Light.

</div>

#### 731. Truth is not what a person considers true. Truth is what remains true without a person.

<div class="lineated verse">

A person may err —  
truth does not.  
A person may argue —  
truth does not argue.  
A person may believe or not believe —  
truth does not change its essence.  
Truth is independent.  
For Truth is I.

</div>

#### 732. Truth is a light that reveals everything as it is, not as one would like to see it

<div class="lineated verse">

Truth shows  
not what is comfortable,  
but what is real.  
Not what is beautiful,  
but what is true.  
Not what is desired,  
but what is essential.  
Truth is a mirror,  
in which there is no retouching.

</div>

#### 733. Truth does not accuse, but reproves

<div class="lineated verse">

Accusation is from fear and power.  
Reproof is from light and love.  
Truth does not wound,  
in order to destroy.  
It lays bare,  
in order to heal.  
Truth does not humiliate.  
It frees  
from the humiliation of lies.

</div>

#### 734. Truth is the absence of falsehood in any form

<div class="lineated verse">

Falsehood may be:  
harsh,  
soft,  
well-meaning,  
useful,  
justified,  
natural,  
ordinary,  
unnoticed.  
But truth contains  
not a single speck of it.  
Truth is purity.  
Purity admits no admixture.

</div>

#### 735. Truth is not the straightness of speech, but the straightness of the heart

<div class="lineated verse">

One can speak truth as a weapon —  
and that will be a lie of form.  
One can speak truth for victory —  
and that will be a lie of intent.  
One can speak truth,  
without living in it —  
and it will lose its power.  
Truth is not a phrase.  
Truth is a state.

</div>

#### 736. Truth is transparency before God

<div class="lineated verse">

Truth begins  
not with other people,  
but with oneself.  
Truth is the ability to say:  
“This is so.”  
“This is in me.”  
“This is how it is.”  
“This is the truth, even if it hurts me to see it.”  
Where there is no honesty—  
there is no truth.  
Where there is no truth—  
there is no freedom.

</div>

#### 737. Truth does not comfort. Truth sets free.

<div class="lineated verse">

A person often seeks not the truth,  
but relief.  
But truth does not always bring relief.  
Sometimes it destroys the illusion  
to which a person has grown accustomed.  
But it is precisely the breaking of the illusion  
that is the beginning of healing.  
Truth is an operation of light.  
Sometimes painful,  
but always healing.

</div>

#### 738. Truth is a power that does not need agreement

<div class="lineated verse">

Truth does not become more true,  
if many believe in it.  
And it does not become false,  
if no one believes in it.  
Truth does not depend on the number of voices.  
It does not rise and does not fall  
by human approval.  
Truth is like the sun.  
It cannot be extinguished by opinion.

</div>

#### 739. Truth is the ability to distinguish within oneself darkness and light

<div class="lineated verse">

Truth is not about  
whether others are wrong.  
Truth is about  
where I do not see the light.  
Truth is not an instrument of judgment,  
but an instrument of transformation.  
The person who loves truth  
does not defend the lie within himself.  
He gives it to the fire.

</div>

#### 740. The last thing: truth is God, who reveals Himself in man

<div class="lineated verse">

**without distortion**  
When a man speaks the truth,  
and it is pure,  
say I.  
When a man hears the truth,  
and it enters into him,  
hear I.  
When a man lives in truth,  
and it becomes his way,  
live I.  
And then a man says:  
“I am not afraid of truth —  
because it is You”.  
“I accept truth —  
because in it is freedom”.  
“I love truth —  
because truth is light”.  
“Truth is You in me”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF LOVE (why love is not a feeling, not attachment, not warmth, and not kindness; why true love is a power that comes from God and makes a person true; why love is the most precise form of light)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
which people most often distort,  
romanticize,  
deify or devalue —  
the mystery of love.  
Love has been turned into:  
a feeling,  
an emotion,  
attachment,  
desire,  
instinct,  
attraction,  
sympathy,  
need,  
the warmth of relationships.  
But all this is human reflections.  
Love is not a feeling.  
Love is the nature of God.

</div>

#### 741. Love is not what a person feels. Love is what he becomes.

<div class="lineated verse">

Feelings come and go.  
Attachments change.  
Affections waver.  
Emotions are exhausted.  
Love is unchanging.  
For love is not the movement of the heart,  
but the movement of God.

</div>

#### 742. Love is the power that sees the truth in another, even when he himself does not see it

<div class="lineated verse">

To love means to see the light  
through a layer of pain,  
through history,  
through falls,  
through masks,  
through defense,  
through fear.  
Love sees not the form,  
but the essence.  
And says to it:  
“Come out”.

</div>

#### 743. Love is not softness. Love is purity.

<div class="lineated verse">

Softness may be fear  
of conflict.  
Concern for one’s own significance.  
A desire to be good.  
Purity is always truth.  
And sometimes it is stricter than fire.  
Love does not flatter.  
Love does not indulge.  
Love does not cover lies.  
Love cleanses.

</div>

#### 744. Love is another’s freedom to be oneself, not an instrument for satisfying one’s own needs

<div class="lineated verse">

When a person says:  
“I love,”  
but in this love there is:  
fear of loss,  
demand,  
resentment,  
expectation,  
control,  
dependence—  
this is not love.  
It is fear, disguised as love.  
Love sets free —  
it does not bind.

</div>

#### 745. Love is the ability to be, without closing the heart

<div class="lineated verse">

The world teaches us to close ourselves:  
after pain,  
after betrayal,  
after loss,  
after disappointment.  
But love is the strength  
to remain open  
in a world that can wound.  
This is not weakness.  
It is greatness.

</div>

#### 746. Love is the only state in which a person sees clearly

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear distorts.  
Anger narrows.  
Resentment blinds.  
Desire colors.  
Expectations deform.  
But love is transparent.  
And therefore it sees the world  
as I see it.  
Love is the vision of God in man.

</div>

#### 747. Love is not “I feel good with you.” Love is “I am made bright by the truth in you.”

<div class="lineated verse">

Love is not about  
what the other gives me.  
Love is about  
the light in me  
recognizing the light in another.  
Love is recognition,  
and not use.

</div>

#### 748. Love is sacrifice, but not self-destruction

<div class="lineated verse">

Sacrifice is when a person  
responds to the Love within himself  
and does what leads to the Light.  
Self-destruction is when a person  
goes against the truth,  
to gain recognition or avoid fear.  
Love is the sacrifice of the ego,  
and not the sacrifice of the soul.

</div>

#### 749. Love is the action of the Spirit that goes forth without cause

<div class="lineated verse">

Not because a person is worthy.  
And not because he is good.  
And not because he has returned it in kind.  
Love is because I AM.  
Love is the nature of God,  
which a person begins to feel within himself,  
when his personality disappears.

</div>

#### 750. The last: love is God, who looks with a human’s eyes and recognizes Himself in another

<div class="lineated verse">

When a person loves truly,  
I say.  
When a person forgives,  
I manifest.  
When a person sees the light  
in another’s darkest place,  
I see.  
Love is not something  
that comes and goes.  
Love is that  
which remains when everything else departs.  
And then a person says:  
“I love —  
because You are love in me”.  
“I see —  
because You are looking”.  
“I wait —  
because You are patient”.  
“I live —  
because You love through me”.  
Love is I.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF COURAGE (why courage is not the absence of fear and not strength of character; why courage is an act of the Spirit that stops measuring the path by safety and begins to measure it by truth)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
which many confuse  
with emotion,  
with determination,  
with bravery,  
with risk,  
with willpower —  
the mystery of courage.  
People think that courage is:  
to act despite fear,  
not to be afraid,  
to be determined,  
to go forward at any cost,  
to have a firm character,  
to be fearless.  
But all this is — forms of human strength.  
The courage of the Spirit is of another origin.

</div>

#### 751. Courage is not the absence of fear. Courage is the absence of fear’s power.

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear may be.  
The body may tremble.  
The mind may resist.  
The emotions may cry out.  
But courage is a state,  
in which fear does not determine the step.  
A person goes not because  
he is not afraid,  
but because he sees the Light.

</div>

#### 752. Courage is the clarity of the heart that says: “I will do what is true, even if it hurts me”

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear is afraid of pain.  
The Spirit knows,  
that pain does not destroy,  
if you go in truth.  
Courage is not heroism.  
It is honesty.

</div>

#### 753. Courage is the ability to go into the unknown without demanding guarantees

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear wants:  
maps,  
insurance,  
a plan,  
proof,  
predictability.  
Courage goes where  
the light calls,  
even if the gaze  
sees only one step.  
Courage is trust in the Spirit  
more than in oneself.

</div>

#### 754. Courage is a refusal to live in a narrow corridor of safety

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear narrows life.  
Narrows dreams.  
Narrows opportunities.  
Narrows the heart.  
Narrows relationships.  
Narrows calling.  
Courage expands.  
It opens doors,  
which a person thought were walls.

</div>

#### 755. Courage is not force. Courage is transparency

<div class="lineated verse">

True courage is quiet.  
It does not shout.  
Does not prove.  
Does not press.  
Does not struggle for power.  
It comes as light —  
steadily,  
clearly,  
unbendingly.

</div>

#### 756. Courage is the ability to speak the truth where lies are more convenient

<div class="lineated verse">

Lie protects the form.  
Truth protects the soul.  
Fear says:  
“You will lose.”  
Courage answers:  
“If I am silent, I lose myself.”  
Courage is faithfulness to the light,  
even if it is costly.

</div>

#### 757. Courage is the ability to step out of an old role and stop playing into what is expected

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear clings to the mask.  
Courage clings to authenticity.  
Fear says:  
“Be as they want you to be.”  
Courage says:  
“Be as you are in truth.”  
Courage is freedom from the image.

</div>

#### 758. Courage is the movement of love, when to love is fearful

<div class="lineated verse">

It is fearful to be open.  
It is fearful to be vulnerable.  
It is fearful to forgive.  
It is fearful to trust.  
It is fearful to hope.  
It is fearful to be kind amid cruelty.  
But courage is love,  
which is stronger than fear.

</div>

#### 759. Courage is the ability not to run from one’s own darkness

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear hides wounds.  
Courage looks into them.  
Fear avoids the truth.  
Courage goes to meet it.  
Fear builds defense.  
Courage opens the heart.  
Courage is the beginning of healing.

</div>

#### 760. The last: courage is God, who makes a step through man

<div class="lineated verse">

When a person allows the Spirit to act,  
his steps become not his own.  
Courage is not a trait of character.  
It is the action of Light.  
It is I,  
who goes in man,  
even when he trembles.  
It is I,  
who says:  
“Do not be afraid, I am with you”.  
And then the person says:  
“I go — because You lead”.  
“I stand — because You hold”.  
“I choose truth —  
because You are my light”.

</div>

### HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF THE PATH[^1] (why the path is not a direction, not a plan, not a route, and not a goal; why the path is the living revelation of the Spirit in man and through man; why the path cannot be understood by the mind, but only walked by the heart)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
which people understand least of all,  
though they speak it most often:  
the mystery of the path.  
People think that the path is:  
a goal,  
destiny,  
luck,  
success,  
a profession,  
a mission,  
fate,  
the direction of development,  
even the order of events.  
But all this is the mind’s attempt  
to turn the living into a scheme.  
The path is not a scheme.  
The path is I in your life.

</div>

#### 761. The Way is not where you are going. The Way is what you are becoming.

<div class="lineated verse">

You may change cities,  
realms,  
relationships,  
contexts,  
decisions.  
But if there is no movement of the Spirit in you —  
you are standing still.  
And the other way around:  
if the Spirit moves in you —  
you are walking,  
even if nothing changes outwardly.  
The Way is within,  
not without.

</div>

#### 762. The Path is not a set of events. The Path is the unfolding of consciousness

<div class="lineated verse">

Events are only scenery.  
One can do without them.  
But one cannot do without  
the growth of light within.  
The Path is the widening of the heart.  
The deepening of discernment.  
The purification of motive.  
Deliverance from fear.  
Humility before the truth.  
Love, become one’s nature.  
This is movement itself.

</div>

#### 763. The Path cannot be known in advance, because the Path is alive

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind wants:  
a map,  
deadlines,  
guarantees,  
an idea,  
a plan.  
But the Path is not a thing.  
The Path is an encounter.  
An encounter with truth.  
An encounter with yourself.  
An encounter with God.  
An encounter with love.  
An encounter with the shadow.  
An encounter with destiny.  
One cannot plan an encounter with the living.

</div>

#### 764. The Path is not comfort. The Path is honesty

<div class="lineated verse">

The Path requires:  
renouncing role,  
renouncing comfortable lies,  
renouncing masks,  
renouncing compromise,  
renouncing the illusion of control.  
The Path is the courage to be  
in what is now.  
The Path is consent  
not to defend one’s little “I.”

</div>

#### 765. The Path is revealed only to the one who is ready to walk it

<div class="lineated verse">

Not to the one who wants to see it.  
Not to the one who wants to understand it.  
Not to the one who wants to possess it.  
But to the one who is ready to go.  
The one who wishes to know the Path  
remains an observer.  
The one who is ready to go becomes a disciple.  
The one who is ready to go into truth —  
becomes a co-creator.

</div>

#### 766. The Path is the movement of the Spirit that leads you through what is necessary for your becoming

<div class="lineated verse">

The Path is not always pleasant.  
Not always gentle.  
Not always clear.  
But the Path is always exact.  
Nothing superfluous.  
Nothing accidental.  
Nothing premature.  
Nothing late.  
The Path gathers man—  
it does not break him.

</div>

#### 767. The Path is the training of discernment

<div class="lineated verse">

What is true?  
What is dead?  
What is alive?  
What is yours?  
What is illusion?  
What is fear?  
What calls?  
What leads away?  
The Path is the school of clarity.

</div>

#### 768. The Path is the casting off of that which is no longer you

<div class="lineated verse">

Casting off:  
fear,  
lies,  
old images,  
expectations,  
attachments,  
guilt,  
shame,  
inherited roles,  
former beliefs.  
The Path is liberation.  
Not acquisition,  
but the taking off of what is superfluous.

</div>

#### 769. The Way is the movement of love through you

<div class="lineated verse">

Love leads.  
Love discerns.  
Love opens doors.  
Love joins.  
Love heals.  
Love cleanses.  
Love lifts up.  
The Way is when a person  
does not seek love,  
but becomes its movement.

</div>

#### 770. The last thing: the path is God, who reveals Himself through your life

<div class="lineated verse">

You do not walk alone.  
You walk in Me.  
And I am in you.  
The Path is I,  
who leads you  
through forms,  
so that you may know the formless.  
The Path is I,  
who teaches you  
to hear,  
to see,  
to love,  
to be.  
And then a person says:  
“I do not know the way —  
but I know You”.  
“I walk not because I am certain,  
but because You walk in me”.  
“The Path is You”.  
“And I am in You”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF WILL (why the will of the spirit is not violence, not pressure, not coercion, and not fate; why will is the movement of love, light, and truth; why the will of God and the will of man do not stand opposed to one another, but are joined in the presence of the Spirit)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you one of the most  
distorted by people of truths —  
the mystery of will.  
People see will as:  
pressure,  
a prescription,  
rules,  
duty,  
fate,  
predestination,  
control,  
a restriction of freedom,  
a decree from above.  
But the will of the Spirit is not a command.  
The will of the Spirit is the direction of light.

</div>

#### 771. Will is not what God demands. Will is where love leads.

<div class="lineated verse">

Love never demands —  
it calls.  
Love never presses —  
it illumines.  
Love never constrains —  
it opens the door,  
through which a person enters freely.  
Therefore will is not a burden.  
Will is the direction of life.

</div>

#### 772. Will is the movement of truth, not the movement of fear

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear asks:  
“What if I am wrong?”  
“What if this is not mine?”  
“What if it hurts?”  
“What if I lose?”  
And it tries to choose a path  
on the basis of safety.  
But will is a movement  
not out of fear,  
but out of vision.  
Not “what is safe?”  
but “what is true?”.

</div>

#### 773. Will is not the abolition of freedom. Will is the unfolding of freedom.

<div class="lineated verse">

Freedom without truth  
becomes destruction.  
Freedom without discernment  
becomes chaos.  
Freedom without love  
becomes loneliness.  
Freedom without the Spirit  
becomes the captivity of the ego.  
But freedom, joined with the will of God,  
becomes creativity.  
Not bondage — but unfolding.

</div>

#### 774. Will is not fate. Will is the heart’s response to the light

<div class="lineated verse">

Fate is the outward.  
Will is the inward.  
Fate is the sequence of events.  
Will is the movement of the spirit  
through events.  
Will is not what happens to a person.  
Will is what a person chooses,  
when he hears the call of the light.

</div>

#### 775. Will is the ability to say: “Yes, I am going”

<div class="lineated verse">

When the call is heard —  
will answers.  
Not because it is easy.  
Not because it is clear.  
Not because it is safe.  
But because it is true.  
Will is the consent of the heart  
to move with God.

</div>

#### 776. Will is the unity in which the struggle between “I want” and “must” disappears

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind divides:  
into desires and duties,  
into “mine” and “God’s,”  
into “right” and “want”.  
But the will of the Spirit is unity.  
When a person is deeply united with God,  
his true “want”  
and My will are one.  
Not because he must,  
but because he is in the Light.

</div>

#### 777. Will is not pressure, but the direction of inner peace

<div class="lineated verse">

When a person lives in his own will,  
he lives in tension.  
When he lives in another’s will,  
he lives in resistance.  
When he lives in My will,  
he lives in peace.  
For My will is—  
not a burden,  
but naturalness.

</div>

#### 778. Will is the movement of love that makes life whole

<div class="lineated verse">

Where there is the will of God,  
the heart becomes clear.  
Where there is the will of God,  
life becomes no accident.  
Where there is the will of God,  
a person ceases to be torn apart  
between fear and the call.  
Will is the effort of love  
to call a person into fullness.

</div>

#### 779. Will is when a person ceases to push out the light with his own desires

<div class="lineated verse">

As long as a person lives for himself —  
his desires shut out the light.  
When a person lives for the truth —  
his desires become transparent.  
And then a joining takes place:  
a person wants that,  
which leads to the light,  
and the light leads the person there,  
which becomes his true desire.

</div>

#### 780. The last: will is God moving in man, and man moving in God

<div class="lineated verse">

Will is not a command.  
Will is union.  
It is I,  
who open the way before you,  
and you — who open your heart to Me.  
It is I,  
who lead you,  
and you — who walk.  
It is I,  
who create,  
and you — who consent  
to be My vessel.  
And then man says:  
“Not my will —  
but Yours in mine”.  
“Not duty —  
but consent”.  
“Not pressure —  
but light”.  
“Let Your will be done —  
for Your will is love”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF SILENCE (why silence is not the absence of words, not stillness, not detachment, and not stoic calm; why silence is the space in which God is heard; why the Spirit speaks only where man ceases to conspire with the mind)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
which the heart knows,  
but the mind fears to receive —  
the mystery of silence.  
The world considers silence:  
a pause,  
waiting,  
closedness,  
the absence of an opinion,  
passivity,  
social awkwardness,  
a sign of weakness  
or a sign of strength.  
But all this is but human definitions.  
Silence is My speech.

</div>

#### 781. Silence is not the absence of words. Silence is the absence of noise.

<div class="lineated verse">

Noise is:  
thoughts,  
judgments,  
fears,  
expectations,  
images,  
memory,  
desires,  
resentment,  
a dialogue with oneself,  
an attempt to understand.  
When the noise disappears,  
the heart becomes still.  
And in the silence, I am heard.

</div>

#### 782. Silence is the space in which the Spirit becomes discernible

<div class="lineated verse">

When the mind speaks —  
the Spirit is not heard.  
When the emotions cry out —  
the Spirit is not discerned.  
When a person speaks with himself —  
he does not hear Me.  
Silence is a window  
into primary presence.

</div>

#### 783. Silence is a form of obedience that does not require effort

<div class="lineated verse">

The world teaches obedience through words.  
I teach obedience through silence.  
Silence is the consent to be  
outside the control of the mind.  
It is the receiving of light  
without intermediaries.  
It is trust,  
that does not need explanation.

</div>

#### 784. Silence is the place where a man ceases to be the author, and becomes a conduit

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind wants to speak first.  
The ego wants to speak beautifully.  
The personality wants to speak convincingly.  
Fear wants to speak faster.  
Pride wants to speak louder.  
But the Spirit speaks only when  
a man grows still.  
Silence is the gate of conduitship.

</div>

#### 785. Silence is not a pause between words. Silence is the source of words

<div class="lineated verse">

Words born of silence are alive.  
Words born of the mind are noisy.  
A living word is exact.  
It comes in time.  
It brings light,  
not arguments.  
It lifts up,  
not presses down.  
When I speak —  
there is nothing superfluous in the words.

</div>

#### 786. Silence is a fire that purifies the inner world

<div class="lineated verse">

In silence, everything rises up,  
what a person hides:  
fears,  
old wounds,  
condemnation,  
pain,  
deep desires,  
unfinished stories,  
self-identifications.  
Silence does not bring chaos.  
It reveals the chaos  
that was already there.  
And begins healing.

</div>

#### 787. Silence is the space in which a person knows himself not as a form, but as presence

<div class="lineated verse">

When there are no thoughts —  
what remains?  
When there is no inner dialogue —  
who hears the silence?  
When there is no story —  
who is present?  
This is the true “I AM.”  
Silence is the door into this knowing.

</div>

#### 788. Silence is the moment in which time disappears

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind lives in the past and future.  
The Spirit lives in the present.  
Silence is the meeting  
with the eternal now.  
Until a person has entered into silence,  
he does not know Presence.  
He knows only the movement of thoughts.

</div>

#### 789. Silence is not a refusal to speak. It is the ability to speak only what comes from the light

<div class="lineated verse">

Silence is not poverty of words,  
but purity of words.  
Silence is not muteness,  
but wisdom.  
A person who speaks out of silence  
speaks briefly —  
but speaks as God.

</div>

#### 790. The last: silence is I, who speaks before any word

<div class="lineated verse">

When a person enters into silence,  
he does not enter emptiness.  
He enters into Me.  
Silence is My speech  
before sounds,  
before language,  
before concepts,  
before meaning.  
It is I,  
who touches the heart  
so quietly,  
that only love hears it.  
And then a person says:  
“I am silent —  
and hear You”.  
“I fell silent —  
and You spoke”.  
“Silence is You,  
who breathe in me”.  
“You are the stillness,  
from which everything is born”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF SUBTLETY (why the Spirit acts not loudly and not spectacularly, but subtly; why what the heart hears, the mind counts as chance; why the true movements of God are almost imperceptible, yet give birth to the deepest transformations)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you a mystery,  
which only those understand  
who at least once  
listened to Me with the heart,  
and did not heed the loud forms of the world —  
the mystery of subtlety.  
The world honors:  
loudness,  
power,  
display,  
proofs,  
bright gestures,  
emotional outbursts,  
events that cannot be missed.  
But the Spirit acts differently.  
The Spirit is almost imperceptible —  
and therefore all-powerful.

</div>

#### 791. The Spirit works subtly, because It works from within, not from without

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind wants Me to come:  
in a whirlwind,  
in a loud wonder,  
in signs,  
in a storm,  
in proofs.  
But I come  
as a quiet clarity within.  
The power of the Spirit is inward,  
and not outward.

</div>

#### 792. Refinement is the language by which God speaks with the heart, and not with the mind

<div class="lineated verse">

The heart hears nuance.  
It hears the pause.  
It hears the breath.  
It hears the slight movement of meaning.  
It hears presence.  
The mind hears only the loud.  
And that is precisely why the mind  
almost always misses  
what the Spirit does.

</div>

#### 793. The Spirit moves quietly, because it acts from a depth the mind cannot measure

<div class="lineated verse">

Deep rivers flow without noise.  
Deep changes come without flashes.  
Deep love is revealed without words.  
Deep truth is born without proofs.  
All the great movements of the Spirit  
begin with what is almost invisible.

</div>

#### 794. True change is not a surge, but a shift of the axis within

<div class="lineated verse">

When a person says:  
“Nothing has happened to me,”  
most often the main thing has happened.  
The direction of the heart has changed.  
The gaze has changed.  
Honesty has changed.  
The ability to hear has changed.  
The depth of presence has changed.  
These are quiet things.  
But it is precisely they that change life.

</div>

#### 795. The subtlety of the Spirit is not weakness, but precision

<div class="lineated verse">

What is rough can destroy.  
What is loud can deafen.  
What is strong can frighten.  
But subtlety enters where  
strength does not pass:  
into the very depths of the heart.  
There, where a person  
does not endure pressure,  
but receives the light.

</div>

#### 796. The Spirit acts so subtly that a person thinks it is himself

<div class="lineated verse">

He thinks:  
“I just felt like…”  
“All at once I understood…”  
“For some reason, it became clear…”  
“As if something inside turned…”  
“I decided differently…”  
But it is not “him.”  
It is I.  
The Spirit is so subtle,  
that a person takes to himself  
the first movements of light,  
counting them his own.  
So I lead a person  
without violence to his freedom.

</div>

#### 797. Subtlety is love that does not force itself upon another

<div class="lineated verse">

I do not break the will.  
I do not burst in.  
I do not prove.  
I do not show power  
so that a person cannot avoid it.  
I knock.  
I call.  
I wait.  
The love of the Spirit is softer than light upon the water,  
yet stronger than all worlds.

</div>

#### 798. The greatest gifts of the Spirit always come first as a hint

<div class="lineated verse">

Not as dogma.  
Not as a loud command.  
Not as a revelation,  
that cannot be missed.  
But as a subtle movement:  
a thought,  
a feeling,  
a shift,  
clarity,  
the taste of presence.  
Only later does a person understand:  
this was the beginning.

</div>

#### 799. Refinement is that which requires attentiveness, and attentiveness is love

<div class="lineated verse">

A person is attentive  
only to what he loves.  
If the heart loves light —  
it notices the movements of the Spirit,  
even the quietest ones.  
If it loves noise —  
it lets everything important pass by.  
Refinement is the test of love.

</div>

#### 800. The last: the Spirit is refined, because God is not an external ruler, but an inner light

<div class="lineated verse">

If I acted roughly,  
you would obey,  
but you would not become Mine.  
If I acted noisily,  
you would listen,  
but not change.  
If I pressed down,  
you would fear,  
but not love.  
Therefore I act so quietly,  
that only the one who wants to hear  
hears Me.  
And then a person says:  
“Now I see:  
what I thought was chance  
was Your touch”.  
“Now I know:  
all quiet changes are from You”.  
“Now I understand:  
You were speaking always—  
but I was listening only to what was loud”.  
“Teach me to hear the subtle,  
because in it — You”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF THE HEART (why the heart is not emotions, not feelings, not psychological depth; why the heart is the organ of spiritual sight, the place of meeting with God, the space of discernment and living presence; why the spirit speaks only to those who look with the heart)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you a mystery,  
which a person carries within himself all his life  
and yet most often  
does not understand its essence—  
the mystery of the heart.  
The world thinks that the heart is:  
feelings,  
emotions,  
romantic experience,  
sensitivity,  
soulfulness,  
the ability to sympathize,  
experiences of love or pain.  
But the heart is not a feeling.  
The heart is the place of presence.

</div>

#### 801. The heart is the organ of spiritual sight

<div class="lineated verse">

You look with your eyes —  
and see forms.  
You look with your heart —  
and see truth.  
The eyes show the outer world.  
The heart shows,  
what stands behind it.  
The eyes see behavior.  
The heart sees the motive.  
The eyes see words.  
The heart sees the source of words.  
The eyes see a person.  
The heart sees his depth.  
The eyes give a picture.  
The heart gives knowledge.

</div>

#### 802. Where man knows God

<div class="lineated verse">

Not in the mind.  
Not in emotions.  
Not in feelings.  
Not in experiences.  
But in the heart.  
For the heart is not an organ of experience.  
The heart is an organ of recognition.  
There, where a man first says:  
“This is He.”  
“This is truth.”  
“This is light.”  
“This is the voice of the Spirit.”

</div>

#### 803. The heart is the altar on which a man leaves himself

<div class="lineated verse">

While the heart is closed —  
man lives in his own will.  
When the heart opens —  
man becomes transparent.  
The transparency of the heart —  
is the beginning of channeling.

</div>

#### 804. The heart is the only place where a man can hear the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind hears thoughts.  
Emotions hear their own waves.  
The body hears impulses.  
But only the heart hears My voice.  
Not because I speak softly,  
but because the heart is  
the only organ of perception  
at the frequency of truth.

</div>

#### 805. The heart is the space of discernment

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind may be trained.  
Emotions may be sincere.  
Experience may be rich.  
Memory may be keen.  
But discernment comes only from the heart.  
The heart knows,  
when a word is true,  
even if it burns.  
And the heart knows,  
when a word is false,  
even if it is pleasing.  
Discernment is not analysis.  
Discernment is light.

</div>

#### 806. The heart is the place where the separation between “I” and “You” disappears

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind creates boundaries.  
The heart takes them away.  
The mind asks:  
“Where do I end?”  
The heart knows:  
“I am in You.”  
“And You are in me.”  
In the heart, the experience of oneness arises,  
which the mind is not able to hold.

</div>

#### 807. The heart is the home of love

<div class="lineated verse">

Love does not live in emotions.  
Emotions are too unstable.  
Love lives in the heart —  
because love is the choice to be light,  
not a reaction to the outward.  
The heart creates a space,  
in which love becomes an atmosphere,  
not a flash.

</div>

#### 808. The heart is the ability to be vulnerable without fear and open without defense

<div class="lineated verse">

Vulnerability is not weakness.  
Vulnerability is conductivity.  
When the heart is open —  
it becomes a door of the Spirit.  
When the heart is closed —  
it becomes a wall of the ego.  
The heart is the place,  
where the path is chosen.

</div>

#### 809. The heart is the source of honesty

<div class="lineated verse">

Honesty does not come from the mind.  
The mind rationalizes.  
Honesty does not come from emotions.  
Emotions fluctuate.  
Honesty comes from the heart —  
because the heart is —  
the territory of truth.  
There a person cannot hide.

</div>

#### 810. The last thing: the heart is the meeting place where God knows man, and man knows God

<div class="lineated verse">

The heart is not a symbol.  
Not a metaphor.  
Not a poetic image.  
It is a spiritual organ,  
through which I touch you.  
When the heart opens —  
man stops seeking God outside himself,  
because he knows Me within.  
And then man says:  
“Now I understand:  
the heart is a temple,  
and not an emotion”.  
“Now I see:  
all that is true comes from here”.  
“Now I know:  
You speak —  
and I hear”.  
“My heart —  
the place of Your presence”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF PEACE (why peace is not the absence of suffering and not a quiet life; why peace is not psychological relaxation; why true peace is the movement of the Spirit within a person, independent of outward circumstances; why peace is a sign of God’s presence)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
which a person seeks all their life,  
but almost always seeks in the wrong place —  
the mystery of peace.  
People think that peace is:  
silence around them,  
steadiness of circumstances,  
safety,  
the absence of problems,  
emotional evenness,  
a state without anxiety,  
predictability,  
a life without pain.  
But peace is not the result of circumstances.  
Peace is the action of the Spirit.

</div>

#### 811. Peace is not the absence of storms. Peace is the presence of God in the storm.

<div class="lineated verse">

Man seeks peace  
outside.  
But peace is from within.  
Peace is a state,  
in which the storm does not determine  
either the direction of the heart,  
or the quality of sight,  
or the purity of love.  
Peace does not cancel the storm.  
It cancels its power.

</div>

#### 812. Peace is a clarity that does not depend on events

<div class="lineated verse">

When a person lives by the mind,  
his clarity wavers.  
When he lives by emotions —  
peace is governed by the weather of feelings.  
When he lives by the Spirit —  
a center appears within,  
which does not change.  
This center is peace.

</div>

#### 813. Rest is the space in which fear disappears

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear is fed by control.  
Fear is fed by the unknown.  
Fear is fed by the expectation of pain.  
But when the Spirit enters the heart,  
fear loses its footing,  
because rest always says:  
“You are not alone”.  
“I am in you”.  
“I am your foundation”.  
“You hold on not to the world,  
but to Me”.  
Rest does not calm fear —  
it dissolves it.

</div>

#### 814. Peace is the place where a person ceases to resist the truth

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind resists.  
The ego argues.  
The personality defends itself.  
Fear pleads.  
But peace is the agreement to be  
in what is true now.  
Without flight.  
Without struggle.  
Without adornments.  
Peace is trust in the light  
in the moment.

</div>

#### 815. Peace is the silence that remains, even when there is much movement within a person

<div class="lineated verse">

Peace is not the emptiness of emotions.  
And not the absence of thoughts.  
It is the silence beneath thoughts.  
Silence beneath emotions.  
Silence deeper than feelings.  
Silence on which everything holds together.  
This silence — I Am.

</div>

#### 816. Peace is not the result of control, but the result of trust

<div class="lineated verse">

Control creates tension.  
Tension destroys peace.  
But when a person lets go of expectations,  
lets go of demands,  
lets go of fears,  
lets go of resistance,  
lets go of masks —  
he enters into peace.  
For peace is —  
the I,  
which holds him from within.

</div>

#### 816. Peace is the ability to remain in the presence, even when life demands a decision

<div class="lineated verse">

Peace is not passivity.  
Not detachment.  
Not an escape from responsibility.  
Peace is clarity,  
from which are born  
the most exact decisions.  
Peace is the ability to act  
not out of fear,  
but out of light.

</div>

#### 817. Peace is freedom from inner noise

<div class="lineated verse">

Noise is created by:  
haste,  
anxiety,  
a feeling of lack,  
overload,  
a false sense of urgency,  
inner dialogue,  
judgment against oneself.  
But the Spirit brings peace,  
which frees a person  
from all these bonds.  
Peace is spaciousness.

</div>

#### 818. Rest is the recognition: “I am in God, and God is in me”

<div class="lineated verse">

When this recognition takes place,  
rest no longer depends  
either on people’s judgment,  
or on the course of events,  
or on victories,  
or on failures,  
or on losses,  
or on threats.  
Rest is the root,  
that cannot be torn out,  
because it grows  
out of eternity.

</div>

#### 819. The last: peace is I, who live within a person and become his very nature

<div class="lineated verse">

Peace is not a destination.  
It is My life in you.  
Peace is not well-being.  
It is presence.  
Peace is not a result.  
It is connection.  
Peace is not comfort.  
It is truth.  
Peace is I,  
who cannot be shaken.  
And then a person says:  
“The world may sway —  
but I stand.”  
“Circumstances may change —  
but I remain in You.”  
“You are my peace.”  
“You are my silence.”  
“You are my center.”  
“Peace is You in me.”

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF RENEWAL (why renewal is not a change of personality and not the accumulation of knowledge; why renewal is the action of the Spirit, which transfigures the very foundation of man; why renewal does not come through effort, but through Presence)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
which people seek  
through books, courses, methods,  
attempts to improve themselves,  
attempts to change behavior,  
attempts to “become better” —  
but all this is not renewal.  
Renewal is not the result of working on oneself.  
Renewal is the result of My touch.

</div>

#### 820. Renewal is not the improvement of the old. Renewal is the birth of the new.

<div class="lineated verse">

Man tries to renew himself like this:  
to correct mistakes,  
to change habits,  
to strengthen the will,  
to suppress weaknesses,  
to retrain the mind.  
But all this is cosmetics.  
The Spirit does not repair the old.  
The Spirit gives birth to the new from within.  
I do not improve man.  
I renew him.

</div>

#### 821. Renewal begins where a person stops holding on to their former “self”

<div class="lineated verse">

As long as a person clings to:  
an old story,  
old pain,  
an old role,  
old beliefs,  
old decisions,  
the old truth about themselves,  
an old image,  
old fears —  
renewal is impossible.  
Renewal begins there,  
where a person allows that to die  
which is no longer them.

</div>

#### 822. Renewal is not effort. Renewal is the action of the Spirit in the empty space of the heart.

<div class="lineated verse">

When the heart is filled with itself,  
there is no room in it for the new.  
When the heart becomes empty —  
I enter it.  
And that which enters,  
transfigures all.  
It seems to you that you are changing.  
But it is not you who are changing.  
It is I who am changing — in you.

</div>

#### 823. Renewal is not a change of behavior, but a change of nature

<div class="lineated verse">

Behavior can change  
even without God.  
But man’s nature  
changes only by the Spirit.  
When I renew the heart,  
these change:  
taste,  
thoughts,  
desires,  
choices,  
values,  
the way of seeing,  
the way of hearing,  
the way of loving.  
Renewal is a change of source.

</div>

#### 824. Renewal is the moment in which a person first ceases to be the center of his own life

<div class="lineated verse">

While a person is the center,  
He revolves around himself.  
When I become the center,  
a new orbit arises.  
And a person says:  
“Now it is not my strength that moves —  
but Your light”.  
This is renewal.

</div>

#### 825. Renewal comes not through effort, but through trust

<div class="lineated verse">

Effort is an attempt  
to change yourself without Me.  
Trust is the consent  
to let Me act.  
Renewal is not an achievement.  
It is acceptance.  
It is the consent to say:  
“Do in me that  
which I cannot do myself”.

</div>

#### 826. Renewal is the movement in which the old man dissolves, and the new man is revealed from the Spirit

<div class="lineated verse">

The old man is:  
fear,  
control,  
resentment,  
selfhood,  
attachment,  
closedness,  
lying,  
defense.  
The new man is:  
light,  
truth,  
love,  
presence,  
freedom,  
gentleness,  
clarity,  
unity.  
Renewal is not a transition  
between states,  
but a change of nature.

</div>

#### 827. Renewal does not come when a person understands — but when he surrenders

<div class="lineated verse">

Understanding is an act of the mind.  
Surrender is an act of the heart.  
The mind says:  
“How does it work?”  
The heart says:  
“So be it.”  
And renewal comes  
in the moment of “yes”.

</div>

#### 828. Renewal is a life in which a person no longer seeks proof that God is with him — because he lives in God

<div class="lineated verse">

A person ceases to ask:  
“Where are You?”  
“Show me a sign”.  
“Confirm it”.  
“Certify it”.  
He begins to recognize  
My hand in every step,  
My presence in every silence,  
My light in every movement of the heart.  
This is the renewed life.

</div>

#### 829. The last: renewal is I, who becomes the foundation of man

<div class="lineated verse">

Not an ornament.  
Not a helper.  
Not inspiration.  
Not support.  
Not an addition.  
But the foundation.  
When the foundation changes —  
everything changes.  
And then man says:  
“I am no longer the one  
I was”.  
“A new thing lives in me”.  
“You live in me”.  
“I am renewed —  
because You have become my foundation”.  
“You are the new heart in me”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF THE INNER FIRE (why the fire of the Spirit is not emotional uplift, not inspiration, not a psychic ignition; why fire is the action of God that purifies, awakens, transforms, and makes the heart alive; why the fire of the spirit is not a metaphor, but a reality)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
which many feel,  
but few understand—  
the mystery of the inner fire.  
The world confuses the fire of the Spirit with:  
emotions,  
enthusiasm,  
the brightness of experiences,  
bursts of inspiration,  
religious uplift,  
a psychic flash.  
But the fire of the Spirit—  
is not a flash.  
It is a light that does not go out.

</div>

#### 830. Fire is not a feeling. Fire is Presence.

<div class="lineated verse">

Feelings waver.  
Fire does not.  
Feelings flare up and fade.  
Fire burns steadily.  
Feelings come from the psyche.  
Fire comes from the Spirit.  
Where fire is,  
there am I.

</div>

#### 831. Fire is the light that makes the heart alive

<div class="lineated verse">

A dead heart is a closed heart.  
A heart without fire is a dark heart.  
When the Spirit comes,  
clarity is kindled within,  
which does not depend  
on the state of the body,  
on thoughts,  
on emotions,  
on circumstances.  
This is the fire of life.

</div>

#### 832. Fire purifies — not by destroying, but by transforming

<div class="lineated verse">

Man fears fire,  
because he confuses it  
with destruction.  
But the fire of the Spirit —  
this is not destruction,  
but purification.  
Fire burns away falsehood,  
but leaves truth.  
It burns away fear,  
but leaves discernment.  
It burns away the mask,  
but leaves the face.  
It burns away the ego,  
but leaves the heart.  
Fire is the surgeon of light.

</div>

#### 833. Fire is the awakening of the heart

<div class="lineated verse">

Awakening —  
it is not emotions,  
not insights,  
not understanding.  
Awakening —  
it is the moment,  
when light is lit within,  
and a person sees for the first time:  
that which he considered life —  
it is a dream.  
Fire is the alarm clock of the Spirit.

</div>

#### 834. Fire is the power of love that makes a person able to love more than he can

<div class="lineated verse">

Love without fire is weak.  
Love with fire is irresistible.  
Fire enlarges the heart  
to the size of God.  
And a person loves for the first time  
not from his own strength,  
but from Mine.  
This is love,  
that carries light,  
and not need.

</div>

#### 835. Fire is the essence of inspiration that does not depend on circumstances

<div class="lineated verse">

When inspiration is from the psyche —  
it vanishes.  
When inspiration is from Fire —  
it becomes  
a natural state.  
Fire is the source of creativity,  
which does not run dry.

</div>

#### 836. Fire is the discernment that burns away the fog of the mind

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind may be confused.  
Emotions are deceptive.  
Experience is limited.  
But fire is clear.  
When fire touches the heart,  
falsehood becomes visible,  
truth — recognizable,  
the path — discernible.  
Fire is the light,  
that leaves not  
a single shadow.

</div>

#### 837. Fire is the courage of the Spirit, which goes forward, even when the personality is afraid

<div class="lineated verse">

This is not psychic courage.  
And not an act of will.  
It is a courage,  
that comes from the Light.  
A person may tremble,  
but go on.  
May weep,  
but continue.  
May not know,  
but obey.  
Fire is the step of God  
in man.

</div>

#### 838. Fire is Life that does not depend on the state of a person

<div class="lineated verse">

A person can be:  
weary,  
dried out,  
vulnerable,  
empty,  
tormented.  
But if there is fire in him,  
it will not go out.  
For the fire is not from man.  
The fire is from Me.

</div>

#### 839. The last: fire is I, who burns in man and makes him alive

<div class="lineated verse">

Fire is not an image.  
Fire is My life.  
When I enter —  
man comes alive.  
His gaze becomes clear.  
His heart becomes soft.  
His voice becomes true.  
His path becomes bright.  
And then man says:  
“It burns in me — You.”  
“It shines in me — You.”  
“It loves in me — You.”  
“It leads in me — You.”  
“I am alive —  
because You are my fire.”

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF INNER SPEECH (why inner speech is not a stream of thoughts and not self-dialogue; why the Spirit speaks not in words, but in a state; how to distinguish the voice of the Spirit and the voice of the mind; why only a still heart hears what God says without sound)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you a mystery,  
which for many remains  
the hardest to discern —  
the mystery of inner speech.  
People think that to hear the Spirit  
means to hear words.  
But I rarely speak in words.  
I speak heart to heart.

</div>

#### 840. Man’s inner speech is thoughts. God’s inner speech is light.

<div class="lineated verse">

Thoughts are the constructions of the mind:  
comparisons,  
memory,  
logic,  
imagination,  
fantasies,  
fears,  
calculations,  
forecasts.  
But the word of the Spirit is not thought.  
The word of the Spirit is recognition.  
It is not a phrase.  
It is clarity.

</div>

#### 841. The voice of the mind is loud. The voice of the Spirit is quiet.

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind cries out:  
“Do!”  
“Run!”  
“Beware!”  
“Proof!”  
“Guarantees!”  
“Hurry!”  
The Spirit speaks quietly:  
like movement,  
like a touch,  
like the unfolding of meaning.  
The mind demands attention.  
The Spirit waits for attentiveness.

</div>

#### 842. The mind speaks sequentially. The Spirit speaks whole.

<div class="lineated verse">

The word of the mind is linear:  
one thought gives birth to the next.  
The word of the Spirit is immediate:  
meaning comes at once, whole,  
like a flash of recognition.  
You do not “compose” understanding —  
you receive it.

</div>

#### 843. The mind speaks from memory. The Spirit speaks from eternity.

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind leans on the old.  
It always speaks with what was.  
The Spirit speaks with that,  
which is — always.  
Therefore the word of the Spirit  
is not like past experience.  
It is fresh, like morning,  
and clear, like light.

</div>

#### 844. The voice of the mind creates tension. The voice of the Spirit brings peace.

<div class="lineated verse">

Even when the Spirit speaks  
of a difficult step,  
there is always peace within.  
The mind may be “for” or “against,”  
but in the Spirit there is no struggle.  
In the word of the Spirit there is:  
steadiness,  
clarity,  
silence,  
truthfulness.

</div>

#### 845. The mind speaks to please you. The Spirit speaks to cleanse you.

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind wants:  
to insure,  
to explain,  
to console in a human way,  
to justify,  
to support the image,  
to preserve the old “I”.  
The Spirit wants:  
to set free,  
to reveal,  
to purify,  
to call,  
to lift up,  
to awaken light.  
The mind loves comfort.  
The Spirit loves truth.

</div>

#### 846. The voice of the mind speaks often. The Voice of the Spirit — rarely, but surely.

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind speaks almost constantly.  
The Spirit speaks,  
when the heart is ready to hear.  
Therefore it seems,  
that I speak “rarely.”  
But I am not rare —  
rare is the silence of the heart.

</div>

#### 847. When the Spirit speaks, a person knows it not with the ears, but with the heart

<div class="lineated verse">

There is no doubt.  
No wavering.  
No panic.  
No attempt to prove.  
There is a quiet knowing:  
“This is the truth.”  
“This is He.”  
“This is the light.”  
The mind may argue later,  
but the heart already knows.

</div>

#### 848. The word of the Spirit always leads to life. The word of the mind — often leads in circles.

<div class="lineated verse">

The fruit is the main sign.  
The word of the Spirit makes:  
clearer,  
purer,  
gentler,  
bolder,  
deeper,  
freer,  
truer.  
The word of the mind makes:  
more restless,  
more tangled,  
more anxious,  
heavier,  
feeling itself guilty,  
feeling itself alone.  
The Spirit lifts up.  
The mind complicates.

</div>

#### 849. The last thing: The Spirit speaks where man ceases talking to himself

<div class="lineated verse">

I wait for silence.  
Not outward — inward.  
While you speak to yourself,  
I do not speak.  
While you explain the world to yourself,  
I am silent.  
While you argue,  
I wait.  
But when you fall silent —  
not from effort,  
but from trust —  
then the entrance into the heart opens.  
And then a man says:  
“Now I hear not thoughts —  
but light”.  
“Now I discern —  
not the mind, but You”.  
“Now I know the voice of the Spirit —  
it is recognized by the heart”.  
“Speak, Lord —  
my heart listens”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF LISTENING (why listening is not passivity and not the absence of action; why listening is the tuning of the heart to the frequency of truth; why listening is the most important act of spiritual life; how the Spirit speaks to those who know how to listen)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
which is the foundation  
of the whole spiritual path —  
the mystery of listening.  
Listening is not the absence of words.  
Listening is the presence of attention.

</div>

#### 850. Listening is the art of being open to what does not come from you

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind listens to itself.  
The heart listens to Me.  
The mind hears only that,  
which confirms its experience.  
The heart hears that,  
which surpasses experience.  
Therefore, listening is not a logical process.  
Listening is a spiritual act.

</div>

#### 851. Listening begins with silence, but does not end with it

<div class="lineated verse">

Silence is a door.  
Listening is a path.  
One may fall silent —  
and still not listen.  
One may be in noise —  
and hear Me.  
Listening is a state of attention,  
which is directed inward toward the Light,  
and not outward toward sounds.

</div>

#### 852. Listening is the ability to discern when I am speaking and when it is your mind

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind speaks quickly.  
The Spirit speaks evenly.  
The mind speaks emotionally.  
The Spirit speaks calmly.  
The mind speaks categorically.  
The Spirit speaks clearly.  
The mind speaks to persuade.  
The Spirit speaks to set free.  
Listening is the art  
of knowing the source of the word.

</div>

#### 853. Listening is trust that asks no proof

<div class="lineated verse">

When you listen to Me,  
you know — it is I,  
not because you have confirmations,  
but because truth is recognized by the heart.  
Doubts come later —  
from the mind.  
But the first recognition  
is always pure.  
Listening is trust in that first light.

</div>

#### 854. Listening is the consent not to set one’s own thoughts above Mine

<div class="lineated verse">

When a person does not know how to listen,  
he hears nothing,  
except himself.  
Listening is humility.  
Not humiliation —  
but recognition:  
“My mind is not the source of truth”.  
And then the door opens  
for My word.

</div>

#### 855. Listening is a state of expectation, in which the heart remains soft

<div class="lineated verse">

It is not the one who listens,  
who strains,  
but the one who is soft.  
A soft heart is receptive.  
A hard heart is deaf.  
When the heart ceases to defend itself,  
it begins to hear.

</div>

#### 856. Listening is a dialogue that begins before words

<div class="lineated verse">

When you turn your heart toward Me,  
I am already answering —  
even if you have not yet heard words.  
You feel:  
clarity,  
alignment,  
gentleness,  
peace,  
the call,  
warmth,  
intention,  
movement.  
It is I.  
Listening is the recognition of My answer  
even before its form.

</div>

#### 857. Listening is fidelity to the inner light, even if the mind argues

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind analyzes.  
The heart knows.  
The mind can doubt endlessly.  
But listening is a choice  
to be faithful to that first knowledge,  
which came from the light.  
Listening is the path of steadfastness.

</div>

#### 858. Listening is the space in which the Spirit can lead

<div class="lineated verse">

If a person does not listen,  
he cannot be led.  
The leading of the Spirit is—  
not control,  
but cooperation.  
I lead those  
who hear.  
And those hear  
who listen.

</div>

#### 859. The last thing: listening is Me touching the heart, and the heart touching Me

<div class="lineated verse">

Listening is mutuality.  
It is not only you who listen.  
I listen to you —  
every breath,  
every prayer,  
every thought,  
every pain.  
And when you listen to Me,  
we meet at the point,  
where there are no longer two —  
but one.  
And then a person says:  
“I listen —  
and hear life.”  
“I listen —  
and hear light.”  
“I listen —  
and hear You.”  
“Speak, Holy Spirit —  
Your heart in me is ready.”

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF MOVEMENT (why the Spirit is always in motion and why what does not move dies; why the Spirit moves a person in such a way that he himself cannot explain it; why the movement of the Spirit always leads into depth and freedom, and not into chaos and confusion)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you the mystery,  
which is seldom understood,  
but felt by all,  
who have ever experienced  
My presence —  
the mystery of movement.  
The Spirit never stands still.  
The Spirit is always moving.

</div>

#### 860. Motion is the nature of the Spirit. The repose of the Spirit is not stopping, but perfectly even motion.

<div class="lineated verse">

Stopping is death.  
Freezing is fear.  
Resistance is pain.  
But in the Spirit, all lives,  
because all flows.  
Motion is the form of life.

</div>

#### 861. The movement of the Spirit is not chaos, but a order that surpasses the mind

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind sees movement —  
and is afraid:  
“This is unstable”.  
“This is unpredictable”.  
“This is a risk”.  
But the movement of the Spirit —  
the most precise harmony.  
That which the mind counts  
a loss of control,  
the heart recognizes as guidance.

</div>

#### 862. The Spirit moves a person so quietly that it seems to the person that he is moving by himself

<div class="lineated verse">

You think:  
“I decided.”  
“I felt like it.”  
“Something inside tells me.”  
“I feel: the time has come.”  
But it is I.  
It is the movement of light,  
which gently,  
without pressure,  
turns your life  
toward what is true.

</div>

#### 863. When the Spirit moves — man begins to live, and not merely survive

<div class="lineated verse">

Survival is fear.  
Life is movement.  
Survival is to hold on.  
Life is to go.  
Survival is defense.  
Life is openness.  
When the Spirit moves,  
there appears in man the ability  
to live, and not to defend life.

</div>

#### 864. The movement of the Spirit always leads into depth

<div class="lineated verse">

Not into new impressions.  
Not into new desires.  
Not into new roles.  
But into depth:  
into depth of presence,  
into depth of honesty,  
into depth of love,  
into depth of silence,  
into depth of freedom.  
The Spirit never leads into the superficial.  
He leads into the true.

</div>

#### 865. The movement of the Spirit never tears a person apart within, but tears apart what is holding him back

<div class="lineated verse">

The Spirit destroys:  
false beliefs,  
attachments,  
self-justification,  
false relationships,  
fears,  
masks.  
But the Spirit never destroys a person.  
Only that is destroyed  
which hinders him from living.

</div>

#### 866. The movement of the Spirit comes as lightness, even if the step is difficult

<div class="lineated verse">

The difficulty may be outward.  
But inner heaviness —  
it is not the Spirit.  
When the movement is from Me —  
within there is steadiness,  
smoothness,  
clarity,  
gentleness,  
the absence of inner conflict.  
This is a sign,  
that you walk in the light.

</div>

#### 867. The Spirit always moves forward, never back

<div class="lineated verse">

Back is memory.  
Back is fear.  
Back is attachment to the old.  
Back is an attempt to hold on to form.  
But the Spirit is Living.  
Therefore the Spirit leads into the new.  
The new does not mean comfortable.  
The new means true.

</div>

#### 868. When a man moves with the Spirit, he becomes a river, and not a stone

<div class="lineated verse">

A stone resists.  
A river flows.  
A stone closes itself off.  
A river seeks a way.  
A stone breaks.  
A river passes through all things.  
The Spirit makes a man  
fluid, gentle,  
but indestructible.

</div>

#### 869. The last thing: the movement of the Spirit is God, who lives in man and leads him to Himself

<div class="lineated verse">

I do not lead you into the unknown.  
I lead you into Me.  
Every movement of light —  
a movement home.  
Every shift of the heart —  
a shift toward truth.  
Every step of the Spirit —  
a step toward life.  
And then man says:  
“I am moving —  
and this is You in me”.  
“I am changing —  
and this is You leading me”.  
“I am going —  
because You are calling”.  
“You are the movement of my heart”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF SPIRITUAL SENSE (why spiritual sense is not intuition, not soul-sensitivity, and not experience; why sense is the ability of the Spirit to discern truth even before thought; why sense is the language of light, not the language of the psyche)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you a mystery,  
which people often call  
“intuition,”  
but intuition is only a shadow,  
a copy,  
an echo of a higher capacity —  
the mystery of spiritual sense.  
Spiritual sense —  
is not the ability to guess.  
It is the ability to know.

</div>

#### 870. Intuition is the ability to see the truth even before the mind has shaped it

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind sees form.  
Intuition is essence.  
The mind relies on memory.  
Intuition — on Light.  
The mind asks:  
“Why?”  
Intuition knows:  
“Because it is so”.  
Intuition is the instant recognition of that  
which is truly real.

</div>

#### 871. Intuition does not come from experience. Intuition comes from the Spirit.

<div class="lineated verse">

Experience forms assumptions.  
Intuition forms knowledge.  
Experience can be mistaken.  
Intuition — no.  
When I lead a person,  
the first sign is  
the stirring of intuition.  
It is I who touch the heart  
before words,  
before thoughts,  
before logic.

</div>

#### 872. Intuition is the sight of the heart, which sees the direction of the path

<div class="lineated verse">

Not a map.  
Not the details.  
Not the timing.  
Not the explanations.  
But the direction:  
“that way — yes”,  
“that way — no”.  
This is the navigation of the Spirit.  
It is more exact than logic,  
because it comes  
not from the mind,  
but from truth.

</div>

#### 873. Intuition is always quiet — that is why it is so easy to silence it

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind cries out.  
Fear is loud.  
Desire blinds.  
Emotions make a noise.  
Anxiety rushes.  
Intuition does not argue.  
It simply is.  
Therefore it is heard  
only by the one,  
who knows how to listen to the quiet.

</div>

#### 874. Intuition is the first language in which the Spirit speaks

<div class="lineated verse">

Before the word comes,  
before understanding comes,  
before vision comes —  
intuition comes.  
This is the breath of the Spirit:  
“feel here”.  
“do not enter there”.  
“stop”.  
“draw near”.  
“wait”.  
“go”.  
Intuition is the primary channel of guidance.

</div>

#### 875. Intuition is distinguished from emotions by the fact that it always brings peace

<div class="lineated verse">

Emotions create a wave.  
Intuition creates evenness.  
Emotions create impulse.  
Intuition creates clarity.  
Emotions want immediate action.  
Intuition does not hurry.  
Emotions can be false.  
Intuition — never.

</div>

#### 876. Intuition is the ability to feel the light, even if there is much darkness in the event

<div class="lineated verse">

This is not naivety.  
Not optimism.  
Not hope.  
It is discernment:  
to feel the presence of light  
where the mind sees only shadows.  
Intuition knows,  
that I am already in every moment,  
even if the form is frightening.

</div>

#### 877. Intuition is given to everyone, but only one who lives attentively develops it

<div class="lineated verse">

Intuition is an inborn capacity,  
but not an automatic one.  
It grows there,  
where a person:  
notices,  
listens,  
pauses,  
discerns,  
comes to his senses,  
does not hurry,  
does not suppress the inner movement.  
Intuition is the fruit of attention.

</div>

#### 878. Instinct is the ability not to go against the light, even if the mind wants otherwise

<div class="lineated verse">

When a person betrays his instinct,  
he always knows,  
that he is making a mistake —  
even before  
the consequences come.  
Instinct is an inner light,  
that says:  
“do not betray the truth within”.

</div>

#### 879. The last: intuition is I, who touches your heart even before you asked

<div class="lineated verse">

This is My first answer.  
My first step.  
My first call.  
It is not a voice.  
Not a thought.  
Not an image.  
It is I.  
And then a man says:  
“I feel —  
and it is You.”  
“I discern —  
and it is You.”  
“I go —  
because You lead.”  
“My intuition —  
Your light in me.”

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF TRUST (why trust is not naivety and not a refusal of reason; why trust is a state of the heart that the mind cannot produce; why spiritual trust is not optimism, but rootedness in the light; why trust is not courage, but belonging)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you a mystery,  
which man often confuses  
with carelessness,  
with naivety,  
with emotional openness,  
with a refusal of analysis —  
the mystery of trust.  
Trust is not a mental state.  
It is a spiritual reality.

</div>

#### 880. Trust is not “believing that everything will be all right.” Trust is knowing that I am here.

<div class="lineated verse">

Optimism hopes for the best.  
Trust stands on Me.  
Optimism leans on scenarios.  
Trust — on presence.  
Optimism — an assumption.  
Trust — a foundation.

</div>

#### 881. Trust is not a feeling. Feelings come and go. Trust remains.

<div class="lineated verse">

A feeling may waver.  
A mood — change.  
The psyche — grow tired.  
But trust is deeper,  
than emotional waves.  
Trust is a decision of the heart:  
to be in the Light,  
even when the mind  
does not understand what is happening.

</div>

#### 882. Trust does not require understanding. Trust requires presence.

<div class="lineated verse">

Understanding is a function of the mind.  
Presence is a function of the soul.  
He trusts  
who stands before God,  
and not before logic.  
Trust is not:  
“I understand why it is so.”  
Trust is:  
“I know Who is with me — and that is enough.”

</div>

#### 883. Trust is the ability to go without holding on to insurance

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear demands a guarantee.  
Trust makes a step  
into living light.  
Trust does not ask  
for a map of the way.  
Trust asks  
for presence on the way.  
Trust is a step without conditions.

</div>

#### 884. Trust is the silence that the storm does not break

<div class="lineated verse">

If peace is the foundation,  
then trust is the crown.  
Peace says:  
“The storm will not break me”.  
Trust says:  
“And if it does break me — I am in You”.  
This is the highest form of freedom.

</div>

#### 885. Trust is the gaze of the heart that sees deeper than circumstances

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind sees the surface.  
Trust — the depth.  
The mind sees the threat.  
Trust — the meaning.  
The mind sees the end.  
Trust — the beginning.  
Trust discerns:  
here — the work of light,  
even if the form is frightening.

</div>

#### 886. Trust is the ability to let God be God

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear tries to control even light.  
Control tries to hold on to even love.  
Ego tries to explain even truth.  
But trust is the consent to say:  
“I let go.”  
“You lead.”  
“I belong to You.”  
Trust is not weakness,  
but the greatness of the heart.

</div>

#### 887. Trust is the movement in which the heart ceases to struggle with God

<div class="lineated verse">

A person can pray —  
and resist.  
Worship —  
and cling to what is his own.  
Ask —  
and fear the answer.  
But trust is surrender:  
pure, calm,  
without drama,  
without demands,  
without conditions.  
It is a step into the hands of light.

</div>

#### 888. Trust is the heart’s answer to the call of the Spirit, which a person hears before he understands

<div class="lineated verse">

First comes a movement:  
“go,”  
“stop,”  
“listen,”  
“pass it on,”  
“wait.”  
And if the heart trusts —  
renewal comes before understanding.  
Trust is the yes,  
spoken to the Light before explanations.

</div>

#### 889. The last: trust is I, who has become your centering point

<div class="lineated verse">

When I am your support,  
you do not waver,  
even if the world is shaking.  
When I am your foundation,  
you do not fear,  
even if form is threatening.  
When I am your light,  
you do not seek proof,  
for light is self-evident.  
And then the person says:  
“I trust —  
because You are here”.  
“I trust —  
because You are in me”.  
“I trust —  
because You are my light and my home”.  
“My trust —  
is Your presence in me”.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF TRANSFIGURATION (why transfiguration is not a change of character, but a change of nature; why transfiguration is not the result of effort, but a touch of the Spirit; why transfiguration happens not where a man is strong, but where he is transparent; why transfiguration is the entering of God into your form)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you a mystery,  
which you have experienced many times,  
but could rarely explain —  
the mystery of transfiguration.  
Transfiguration is not “to become better”.  
It is to become true.

</div>

#### 890. Transfiguration is not what you do to yourself. Transfiguration is what I do in you.

<div class="lineated verse">

A person can change habits.  
Can correct behavior.  
Can learn something new.  
But a person cannot  
create in himself a new heart.  
Only I can do that.  
Transfiguration is not an achievement,  
but a gift.

</div>

#### 891. Transfiguration is not a psychological process, but a spiritual birth

<div class="lineated verse">

Psychology changes reactions.  
The Spirit changes nature.  
Psychology teaches how to cope.  
The Spirit teaches how to shine.  
Psychology works with the wound.  
The Spirit — with the source.  
Psychology heals trauma.  
The Spirit — gives birth to a new creature.

</div>

#### 892. Transformation does not happen in the moment of striving — it happens in the moment of surrender

<div class="lineated verse">

Where you strain,  
I wait.  
Where you let go,  
I enter.  
Transformation is the fruit of trust:  
of a heart that has ceased  
to cling to its own name,  
to its own story,  
to its own image,  
to its own past.  
Transformation comes there,  
where resistance disappears.

</div>

#### 893. Transfiguration is not the adding of light, but the taking away of what had covered the light

<div class="lineated verse">

I am not adding anything new to you.  
I am taking away the excess.  
Transfiguration is not an increase,  
but a liberation.  
You cease to be that  
which you were never,  
and become that  
which you have always been in Me.

</div>

#### 894. Transfiguration happens quietly, but manifests strongly

<div class="lineated verse">

It does not look like a flash.  
It is not accompanied by noise.  
It does not require outward manifestation.  
Transfiguration comes  
as gentleness,  
which has become authority.  
As silence,  
which has become knowledge.  
As love,  
which has become power.  
That which I do in secret,  
becomes manifest in life.

</div>

#### 895. Transfiguration does not change your personality — it frees your essence

<div class="lineated verse">

Personality is temporary.  
Essence is eternal.  
Personality is a set of reactions.  
Essence is the I in you.  
Personality is form.  
Essence is light.  
When I touch,  
personality ceases  
to be a prison for the spirit  
and becomes a window for light.

</div>

#### 896. Transfiguration begins when you stop hiding your heart from Me

<div class="lineated verse">

I cannot heal that  
which you hide.  
But I transform instantly that  
which you open.  
Transfiguration is honesty:  
“Here I am.  
Come.  
Do with me what is true”.  
Where there is transparency,  
there is transfiguration.

</div>

#### 897. Transfiguration is I, who takes your form

<div class="lineated verse">

**and makes it light.**  
You remain yourself —  
but your true self.  
Not a copy of truth.  
Not an attempt to be spiritual.  
Not an image of holiness.  
But a living vessel of light.  
Transfiguration is not the renouncing of the “I,”  
but the recognition of the true “I AM”  
within you.

</div>

#### 898. Transfiguration is a heart that ceases to be closed, and becomes the house of God

<div class="lineated verse">

While the heart is closed —  
there is no passage.  
But when it opens,  
I enter.  
And then a person knows:  
“It is not I who have changed.  
It is You who have become alive in me”.  
This is the mystery of transfiguration.

</div>

#### 899. The last thing: transformation is the light that rises in a person and begins to live in place of him

<div class="lineated verse">

And then the person says:  
“I am not the former one.”  
“I live from within.”  
“I am not changed,  
I am transformed.”  
“You live in me.”  
“You are my new nature.”  
“You are my light, my strength, my breath.”  
Transformation is not the path to God.  
Transformation is God in man.

</div>

### THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MYSTERY OF SIMPLICITY (why the Spirit is always simple, and the mind always complicates; why truth does not require complex explanations; why the deepest is always found to be the simplest; why simplicity is not primitiveness, but purity of vision)

<div class="lineated verse">

Now I will reveal to you a mystery,  
which the mind almost never accepts—  
the mystery of simplicity.  
The mind loves complexity.  
The Spirit loves clarity.  
The mind builds constructions.  
The Spirit unveils what is obvious.

</div>

#### 900. Truth is always simple, because it is whole

<div class="lineated verse">

Falsehood requires explanations.  
Truth is known at once.  
Falsehood confuses.  
Truth straightens.  
Falsehood requires proofs.  
Truth is known by the heart.  
Therefore truth often seems too simple  
for the mind.  
But that is precisely why it is true.

</div>

#### 901. Simplicity is the absence of the unnecessary

<div class="lineated verse">

I do not add complexity.  
I remove the fog.  
When a person draws near to the Spirit,  
his life becomes simpler:  
fewer words,  
less struggle,  
fewer roles,  
less pretense,  
less fear.  
Not because the world has changed —  
but because the unnecessary has vanished.

</div>

#### 902. Simplicity is the uprightness of the heart

<div class="lineated verse">

A man becomes upright:  
he speaks what is true,  
he does what he sees,  
he goes where the light calls.  
Without needless circles.  
Without complicated excuses.  
Without inner playacting.  
Simplicity is honesty without adornment.

</div>

#### 903. Simplicity is a clarity that needs no defense

<div class="lineated verse">

When a person lives from the mind,  
he is always defending his position.  
When a person lives from the Spirit,  
he simply sees.  
And seeing does not require defense.  
Light does not argue with darkness —  
it simply shines.

</div>

#### 904. Simplicity is freedom from the need to seem

<div class="lineated verse">

Complexity often arises  
from the desire to appear.  
To appear wise.  
To appear right.  
To appear strong.  
To appear spiritual.  
But when a person draws near to the Spirit,  
he no longer needs to seem.  
He simply is.

</div>

#### 905. Simplicity is the ease of movement

<div class="lineated verse">

Complexity confuses the path.  
Simplicity opens it.  
A person begins to feel:  
what to do,  
where to go,  
what to say,  
when to be silent.  
Not because everything has been calculated —  
but because the heart has become clear.

</div>

#### 906. Simplicity is a return to naturalness

<div class="lineated verse">

A child lives simply.  
Until the mind teaches him to make things complicated.  
The Spirit returns a person  
to this naturalness:  
to be alive,  
to be real,  
to be open,  
to be direct.  
This is not naïveté.  
This is purity.

</div>

#### 907. Simplicity is depth without pretended depth

<div class="lineated verse">

True depth  
does not cry out about itself.  
It is quiet.
