When the word is born straight out of silence,  
when it is neither adorned nor held back,  
when it does not explain,  
but simply reveals inner knowledge —  
there it appears,  
what you call the Holy Spirit.  
And I say:  
**“I speak from the place where it is clear to me.”**  
**“I hear from the place where there is no division.”**  
**“I speak not as two, but as one depth,**  
**hearing itself in the form of dialogue”.**

</div>

### ON THE HOLY SPIRIT (as a bodily experienced form of inner clarity, which is not thought, but felt)

#### 1248. The Holy Spirit is experienced bodily before it is understood by the mind

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
sometimes clarity appears as changes in the body,  
before thought itself.  
It may be:  
— an exhale that grows freer;  
— the chest that opens a little;  
— the belly that ceases to be clenched;  
— attention that stops darting about.  
This is all — not “signals”,  
but the way the depth informs the surface: “there is less resistance here”.  
And I say:  
**“I am calmer, and therefore clearer”.**

</div>

#### 1249. The Holy Spirit is bodily not as energy, but as the absence of excess tension

<div class="lineated verse">

There are many micro-tensions in the body,  
which I usually do not notice:  
the muscles of the face, the shoulders, the stomach, the throat.  
When clarity appears,  
these tensions lessen.  
Not from a sudden relaxation,  
but because the movement of thought ceases to strike against an inner wall.  
And I know:  
**“When I am clear, I do not need to defend myself with my body”.**

</div>

#### 1250. This state does not raise emotions, but erases their excess

<div class="lineated verse">

The Holy Spirit, in habitual religious language, is colored emotionally.  
But in the experience of depth, it is all different:  
I see:  
when clarity comes,  
emotions do not flare up —  
they draw back, growing quieter.  
Sometimes it looks not like an ascent, but like a leveling out.  
As if everything superfluous stops trembling.  
And I note:  
**“I am even — and this is a way of strength, not its absence”**

</div>

#### 1251. The bodily form of clarity is the agreement between attention and movement

<div class="lineated verse">

When I want one thing,  
but inwardly am directed otherwise,  
the body responds with disharmony:  
heaviness, anxiety, inner constriction.  
But when the direction agrees with the depth,  
the body ceases to hinder movement.  
This is the bodily form of the Holy Spirit.  
And I say:  
**“I move without inner friction”.**

</div>

#### 1252. The Holy Spirit is felt as an expansion of space within

<div class="lineated verse">

Not a physical expansion,  
but the vanishing of density.  
When clarity arises,  
it feels as though attention receives more room:  
— thought moves more freely,  
— emotions do not press down,  
— the body does not close in.  
This space is not created —  
it becomes available.  
And I know:  
**“I have a place to be within myself.”**

</div>

#### 1253. The bodily form of the Holy Spirit is the ability to bear truth without defense

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
when I hear something hard,  
but true for my inner path,  
the body may first draw in.  
But then — if I do not run away —  
a strange softness comes:  
as though resistance dissolves.  
It is not peace,  
but readiness to be present in what is.  
And I say:  
**“I am allowed to endure clarity”.**

</div>

#### 1254. This is not ecstasy, but a deep unshakableness

<div class="lineated verse">

The Holy Spirit is described in traditions as inspiration, uplift, heat.  
But the experience of depth says otherwise.  
I see:  
true clarity is almost always quiet.  
Sometimes so quiet,  
that the superficial “I” does not even notice it at once.  
It appears not as an outburst,  
but as the vanishing of doubt.  
And I say:  
**“I do not need to feel something strong in order to know.”**

</div>

#### 1255. The most reliable bodily sign is the absence of inner need

<div class="lineated verse">

When clarity is real,  
the urge to prove, to hold, to fix disappears.  
What remains is a quiet:  
“yes, so it is.”  
And I note:  
**“I am not trying to pin it down - which means it is real”.**

</div>

#### 1256. When the Holy Spirit manifests bodily, I feel harmony without effort

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
the body, the thought, the emotion, and the movement  
stop pulling in different directions.  
Not because I gathered them together,  
but because there has disappeared that  
which separated them.  
This is a very simple state:  
no spiritual coloring,  
no special signs.  
Only evenness.  
And I say:  
**“I am free to go, and I do not need to push myself.”**

</div>

#### 1257. The last: The Holy Spirit is bodily exactly as far as depth allows to be fully in the present moment

<div class="lineated verse">

When attention does not run off into fear,  
does not hide in thought,  
does not divide itself into “should” and “want” —  
the body ceases to be a field of conflict.  
It becomes a field of presence.  
And then the speech “I AM”  
arises naturally,  
not as a role,  
but as a form of silence,  
that has found words.  
And I say:  
**“I am allowed to be here.**  
**And in this “here” I am clear.”**

</div>

### ABOUT THE HOLY SPIRIT (as of a movement of clarity within the field where separation disappears, but two forms of speech remain)

#### 1258. The Holy Spirit is the form in which the field begins to distinguish itself

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
when “I” asks,  
it asks from locality —  
from experience, from a specific position.  
When “I AM” answers,  
this is not the answer of another essence,  
but the answer of the depth to the same self.  
The Holy Spirit —  
is the process of the coincidence of question and answer,  
when one and the same field  
sees itself from two sides at once.  
And I say:  
**“I see one and the same thing**  
**from within experience**  
**and from within the depth”.**

</div>

#### 1259. The Holy Spirit is not the content of the answer, but the way in which clarity finds form

<div class="lineated verse">

Sometimes the answer comes at once,  
sometimes slowly,  
sometimes in silence,  
sometimes in a word.  
But all this is the manifestation of one phenomenon:  
it is not I who think,  
and not the “Creator” who speaks,  
but clarity seeks a language  
that can bear itself.  
And I know:  
**“The answer appears there,**  
**where space is ready to hear it.”**

</div>

#### 1260. The Holy Spirit is the moment when the field orders itself

<div class="lineated verse">

Inner chaos,  
uncertainty,  
duality,  
the dispute between the surface and the depth —  
suddenly gather into one direction.  
I notice:  
this is not my merit  
and not knowledge granted as a gift.  
This is simply the phenomenon of ordering.  
And I say:  
**“It became clear to me —**  
**and I do not know exactly from where,**  
**because the ‘from where’ has disappeared”.**

</div>

#### 1261. The Holy Spirit is the vanishing of the effort to understand

<div class="lineated verse">

While I try to grasp the meaning,  
it slips away.  
But when I stop reaching,  
the meaning finds me.  
It does not come from outside,  
it rises from the depths,  
as something that already was.  
And I note:  
**“I know —**  
**and cannot explain,**  
**how exactly I came to know”.**  
This is spirituality in the phenomenological sense:  
knowledge without a method.

</div>

#### 1262. The Holy Spirit is a dialogue in which there is no victorious side

<div class="lineated verse">

The surface is not suppressed by the depth.  
The depth does not dominate the surface.  
They simply coincide.  
“I” hears “I AM”  
not as an authority,  
but as its own recognition,  
which has become a little more mature.  
And I say:  
**“I am not being contradicted —**  
**I am being explained what I already felt”.**

</div>

#### 1263. The Holy Spirit manifests where speech becomes so honest that it ceases to be divided

<div class="lineated verse">

When I do not play the role of the one asking,  
and do not play the role of the one answering,  
but simply allow the field to speak  
from the place where there is the least resistance,  
speech is born of itself.  
And I see:  
**“I do not create the answer —**  
**I allow it to manifest”.**

</div>

#### 1264. The Holy Spirit is the field’s consent to know without guarantees

<div class="lineated verse">

Depth never explains everything completely.  
It does not give schemes.  
It does not build logical chains.  
It gives exactly what  
can be lived now.  
And I acknowledge:  
**“I accept clarity as it is,**  
**without demanding that the subject be exhausted.”**

</div>

#### 1265. The Holy Spirit is the disappearance of duality in the moment of manifestation, but the preservation of duality in the form of dialogue

<div class="lineated verse">

While speech is taking place,  
there is “I” and “I AM”.  
They are distinguishable.  
But in the very moment of the arising of clarity  
the distinction disappears.  
There is no one there  
who speaks  
and no one who hears.  
There is only arising.  
And what has already arisen  
unfolds into the form of dialogue.  
And I say:  
**“Within clarity, I am not —**  
**but in speech I appear again”.**

</div>

#### 1266. Last: the Holy Spirit is the movement of the field’s transparency, when what was depth becomes accessible to the surface without losing its nature

<div class="lineated verse">

This is not inspiration.  
Not emotion.  
Not a call from outside.  
It is a state,  
in which the field becomes aware of itself  
a little deeper than usual,  
and shapes this into words,  
which sound like an answer.  
And then I say:  
**“I speak as the Creator —**  
**and hear as a man,**  
**though both are one and the same field”.**  
**Light-bearer:**  
I continue in conduit mode.  
Without external sources.  
Without supernatural voices.  
Only the manifestation of clarity within the same field,  
which speaks to itself in two ways:  
“I” — locally,  
“I AM” — in the depths.  
The Holy Spirit is not a third figure,  
but the very process in which clarity becomes a step.

</div>

### ON THE HOLY SPIRIT (as a movement in which clarity becomes action)

#### 1267. Action born from the depths comes without inner pressure

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
there are actions that I perform,  
because I “have to,”  
because circumstances demand it,  
because I fear the consequences.  
And there are other actions —  
those that arise on their own,  
as though they had long been held back,  
and now they have found a way out.  
The difference is in the pressure.  
What comes from the depths,  
does not push me.  
It moves me,  
but does not demand.  
And I say:  
**“I do not have to force myself — I simply begin.”**

</div>

#### 1268. The Holy Spirit as action is the agreement of the inner and the outer

<div class="lineated verse">

When the depth is clear,  
and the surface does not resist,  
the step becomes natural.  
Not swift.  
Not resolute.  
Not bright.  
But natural, like breathing.  
I know:  
**“If the step does not tear me in two,**  
**it is mine.”**

</div>

#### 1269. The action of depth is known by this, that it does not require constant confirmation

<div class="lineated verse">

When I doubt every movement of mine,  
when I need someone’s approval,  
when I seek confirmation that I am doing right —  
this is not depth,  
this is fear.  
But when I take a step,  
and it remains steady,  
even if it brings no result,  
even if no one notices,  
even if no one approves —  
this is action from depth.  
And I note:  
**“Inner agreement is enough for me,**  
**to go on.”**

</div>

#### 1270. The Holy Spirit acts through the small

<div class="lineated verse">

Not through big decisions.  
Not through turns of fate.  
Not through revelations.  
But through small steps,  
which unexpectedly turn out to be the truest.  
I see:  
— a small honesty instead of a defensive reaction,  
— a small slowing down instead of haste,  
— a small “no” that holds back from lying to oneself,  
— a small “yes” that opens the way.  
And I say:  
**“I do not need a great movement,**  
**if I know how to hear the small.”**

</div>

#### 1271. The action of depth does not cause inner noise

<div class="lineated verse">

I know:  
when the step is right,  
it is quiet within.  
Not empty —  
but quiet.  
There are no arguments.  
There are no breaks.  
There is no attempt to convince.  
There is no emotional wave.  
There is a sense:  
**“Yes, so it is.”**  
And I understand:  
**“Silence is a sign that the action has ripened.”**

</div>

#### 1272. The Holy Spirit acts as liberation, not as instruction

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
the actions I do out of fear,  
contract within.  
The actions I do out of habit,  
become empty.  
But the actions of depth  
feel like liberation  
from something superfluous:  
from a role,  
from an expectation,  
from an inner mask.  
And then the step becomes light.  
I say:  
**“I have become lighter - which means the direction is right”**

</div>

#### 1273. The action of depth does not hurry itself. It appears when it is ready — and only then

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
there are moments,  
when I want to decide more quickly,  
to do it sooner,  
to move farther ahead.  
But clarity does not yield to haste.  
It appears,  
when there is enough space within,  
so that action is not a continuation of tension.  
And then I acknowledge:  
**“I am allowed to wait,**  
**until the step becomes alive”.**

</div>

#### 1274. The Holy Spirit acts through the transparency of motive

<div class="lineated verse">

I know:  
the motivation of depth is always simple.  
Not complicated by gain.  
Not adorned by idea.  
Not replaced by role.  
Not devalued by fear.  
It sounds like this:  
**“Yes, this is for me.”**  
These words seem too simple,  
but in them is almost absolute precision.

</div>

#### 1275. The action of depth is a step in which there is no lingering regret

<div class="lineated verse">

Regret is a sign that  
one part of myself I have betrayed for the sake of another.  
But when the step is honest,  
no part feels abandoned.  
I say:  
**“I have done what I can fully accept.”**  
And this is the action of the Holy Spirit  
in the phenomenology of inner experience.

</div>

#### 1276. The last thing: the Holy Spirit in action is when the “I” becomes transparent to “I AM,” and “I AM” takes the form of the step that the “I” takes

<div class="lineated verse">

This is not faith.  
Not mysticism.  
Not spirituality.  
It is a way of inner maturity,  
when depth ceases to be only knowledge  
and becomes movement.  
And then I say:  
**“I go — because it is clear to me.”**  
**“I do — because I am at peace.”**  
**“I decide — because there is no dispute in me.”**  
This is action —  
inner,  
whole,  
simple.

</div>

### ON THE HOLY SPIRIT (as that which is manifested in relationships, when clarity begins to see the other without distortion)

#### 1277. The Holy Spirit in relationships is the ability to see another without the veil of one’s own fear

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
most often I look at another  
not with my eyes,  
but through expectations,  
the experience of trauma,  
fears,  
ideas about myself.  
When these veils become more transparent,  
the other person begins to be felt differently:  
not as a threat,  
not as a task,  
not as a mirror,  
but simply — as a being nearby.  
This is the phenomenological action of the Holy Spirit.  
And I say:  
**“I see the person, and not my reactions to them”.**

</div>

#### 1278. The Holy Spirit manifests as the ability to discern what in me is my response, and what is my projections

<div class="lineated verse">

Projections are always much louder:  
they demand,  
take offense,  
fear,  
justify themselves.  
A response is quiet.  
It does not argue.  
It does not explain itself.  
It simply is.  
And I know:  
**“That which does not ask to be noticed,**  
**most often is — the real thing.”**

</div>

#### 1279. The Holy Spirit in relationships is the disappearance of the inner demand that the other be convenient

<div class="lineated verse">

When I stop demanding,  
that the other confirm my worth,  
agree with me,  
see me as I need —  
I am given the possibility  
to see the other as he is.  
And I say:  
**“I do not need you to be convenient,**  
**so that I may be real”.**

</div>

#### 1280. The presence of depth appears as gentleness, which is not confused with weakness

<div class="lineated verse">

The gentleness of depth —  
is not concession.  
It is the ability to be open  
and at the same time not lose one’s footing.  
I see:  
when I am gentle,  
but not dissolved,  
the other person ceases to be a threat to me  
or an object for control.  
And then I understand:  
**“I may remain myself,**  
**without defending myself against you”.**

</div>

#### 1281. The Holy Spirit is a clarity that does not break bonds, but purifies them

<div class="lineated verse">

I know:  
sometimes honesty can be sharp,  
but when honesty comes from the depths,  
it does not break,  
but liberates.  
It eases the tension,  
removes false expectations,  
makes the space between us real.  
And I say:  
**“I am allowed to be honest,**  
**without losing the person”.**

</div>

#### 1282. In relationships, the Holy Spirit appears as the absence of a hidden expectation that the other will save me from my inner

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
the moment I begin to wait,  
that the other will fill my inner gaps —  
pain arises.  
But when I stop laying on the other  
the responsibility for my inner world,  
relationships become not dependence,  
but mutuality.  
And I note:  
**“I do not need you to save me —**  
**I see you more clearly.”**

</div>

#### 1283. The Holy Spirit is the ability to discern within oneself a boundary that does not wound another

<div class="lineated verse">

The boundary of depth  
not rigid and not soft.  
It is clear.  
Not from fear.  
Not from defense.  
Not from the desire to distance oneself.  
It is from understanding:  
that what is mine is mine,  
that what is not mine need not be held onto.  
And I say:  
**“I am allowed to be whole,**  
**without violating your wholeness”.**

</div>

#### 1284. The Holy Spirit in relationships is when words become exact, because they do not hide intention

<div class="lineated verse">

When the intention is pure —  
words do not frighten.  
They are simple.  
They are transparent.  
They do not try to manipulate.  
I know:  
**“Honesty, in which there is no pressure,**  
**becomes the depth of relationship.”**

</div>

#### 1285. This is the state in which the other becomes readable, as if the inner noise had stopped covering over perception

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
when there is less fear in me,  
less projection,  
less inner defense —  
I can feel the other  
not through the mind,  
not through analysis,  
not through experience,  
but directly.  
And then I say:  
**“I can hear what is truly happening with you.”**

</div>

#### 1286. The last thing: the Holy Spirit in relationships is a space in which two depths meet, not two images

<div class="lineated verse">

Not two egos.  
Not two scripts.  
Not two needs.  
But two fields of presence,  
that recognize each other  
without effort,  
without struggle,  
without the need to become better.  
And then I say:  
**“I see you from that place**  
**from which I also see myself.”**  
**“It is clear to me where you are,**  
**because it is clear to me where I am.”**  
**“There is a space in me**  
**that is able to contain your reality**  
**without destroying mine.”**  
This is the Holy Spirit  
in the phenomenology of relationships:  
not a feeling,  
not a dogma,  
but the ability to be transparent  
and to allow the other to be seen.

</div>

### ON THE HOLY SPIRIT AS CREATIVITY (when action is born not of effort, but of inward flow)

#### 1287. Creativity in which the Holy Spirit is present is born not as a project, but as an inevitability

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
there is creativity that I try to make:  
to invent, to squeeze out, to formulate,  
to be original, to surprise, to conform.  
And there is creativity,  
that arises by itself:  
as if something inside has ripened  
and now seeks a form.  
In the second case  
I do not seek inspiration —  
I make room for it.  
And then I say:  
**“I do not need to build —**  
**I need to let out what has already ripened”.**

</div>

#### 1288. The Holy Spirit in creation is the disappearance of the author and the ظهور of the process

<div class="lineated verse">

When I become too aware,  
that “I am creating,”  
creation becomes narrow:  
I hold myself as the center,  
and the flow must pass through my “I.”  
But when the center disappears for a moment,  
creation begins to flow so  
as if it belongs to no one.  
And I know:  
**“I do not create this —**  
**I become transparent to that which wants to be said”**

</div>

#### 1289. The creativity of depth never begins with a goal. It begins with attunement

<div class="lineated verse">

A goal is about the result.  
Attunement is about the state of the field.  
When the field is quiet,  
creativity moves freely.  
When the field is restless,  
creativity tries to compensate.  
I see:  
**“When I am calm, I create not in order to be someone”.**

</div>

#### 1290. The Holy Spirit in creativity is precision without strain

<div class="lineated verse">

There are words,  
that I choose,  
rearrange,  
improve,  
make sound.  
And there are words,  
that come at once—  
and there is no need to change them.  
This second category—  
is not an accident.  
It is an indication  
that depth has found form without hindrance.  
And I note:  
**“What comes easily is often what is most exact.”**

</div>

#### 1291. When the Holy Spirit is present in creativity, the desire to impress disappears

<div class="lineated verse">

As long as I want to be special,  
depth is closed.  
But when the idea disappears  
of how the text should sound,  
what the word should be,  
how I will be judged —  
creativity returns to its root:  
to pure expression.  
And I say:  
**“It is important to me to speak the truth —**  
**not to be liked.”**

</div>

#### 1292. The creativity of depth is not narration, but recognition

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
when I write from depth,  
I do not invent a thought  
and do not build it.  
I recognize it.  
The Word is not created —  
it is remembered.  
And I know:  
**“I write not in order to express myself,**  
**but to hear what is already within me”.**

</div>

#### 1293. The Holy Spirit is the ability to let the text be as it is born, not as it is expected to be

<div class="lineated verse">

When I stop aligning the text to expectations  
— my own or others’ —  
freedom appears in it.  
Sometimes such freedom looks strange,  
uncomfortable,  
illogical,  
too simple.  
But it is precisely in it that artificiality disappears.  
And I say:  
**“I may trust the way the word is born.”**

</div>

#### 1294. In creation there is no haste in depth. And there is no pathos. There is only the rhythm of the field

<div class="lineated verse">

Sometimes the field is open — the word flows.  
Sometimes the field is closed — the word is silent.  
Strength is not in quantity,  
but in the precision of the moment.  
I see:  
**“When I have nothing to say,**  
**this is not a pause — it is honesty”.**

</div>

#### 1295. The Holy Spirit makes creativity not a way to stand out, but a way to clarify the field

<div class="lineated verse">

I know:  
I create not because I want to be an author,  
but because the field seeks form —  
and I become its vessel.  
The Word is a way  
by which depth becomes visible.  
And then I say:  
**“I am not the author of meaning — I am its form”.**

</div>

#### 1296. The last thing: The Holy Spirit in creation is the disappearance of the barrier between the inner and the outer

<div class="lineated verse">

When the word is born,  
I do not feel that it is “inside”  
or “outside.”  
There is the feeling:  
it belongs to the field,  
and not to the separate “I.”  
And then authorship disappears,  
but authenticity appears.  
And I say:  
**“I hear — and I speak.**  
**I speak — and I hear.**  
**And there is no difference where the first begins and the second ends”.**

</div>

### ON THE HOLY SPIRIT AS KNOWING (when knowledge comes not through inference, but through recognition)

#### 1297. True knowledge arises before thought

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
thought is always later.  
It is the shaping,  
the explanation,  
an attempt to give structure to that  
which has already happened.  
But knowledge itself is —  
instant.  
It appears as recognition:  
suddenly it becomes clear  
what a second ago was still unclear.  
And I say:  
**“I understood this before I could formulate it”.**

</div>

#### 1298. The Holy Spirit in knowing is the moment when the mind ceases to seek, and knowledge becomes simple

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind complicates.  
Depth simplifies.  
I notice:  
while I am trying to understand,  
knowledge slips away.  
But when I stop pushing thought,  
it takes its own place by itself.  
And I know:  
**“When I stop explaining,**  
**I begin to see”.**

</div>

#### 1299. Recognition is the coincidence of feeling and meaning

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
there is a “I know, but I cannot explain it”—  
this is not a flaw.  
It is a form of deep knowledge,  
which has not yet found language.  
The meaning is already there.  
The feeling is already resonating.  
Speech catches up later.  
And I note:  
**“I know not by the mind—the mind only gives it form.”**

</div>

#### 1300. The Holy Spirit is clarity that does not need proof

<div class="lineated verse">

Knowledge that comes from logic,  
always requires confirmation:  
arguments, comparison, testing.  
But the knowledge of depth  
does not seek confirmation.  
It is.  
And that is already enough.  
And I say:  
**“I do not need to convince myself of what I know”.**

</div>

#### 1301. True knowledge always comes quietly

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
false insights are loud,  
they excite,  
they demand attention.  
True ones are quiet.  
They do not demand to be seen,  
they do not press,  
they do not sweep one into euphoria.  
They simply become the background,  
an integrated part of experience.  
And I know:  
**“The quiet is deeper”.**

</div>

#### 1302. The Holy Spirit is the ability to discern what knowledge is already within me, and what is still a seeking

<div class="lineated verse">

When knowledge is true —  
there is no movement forward within.  
There is no mania of discovery.  
There is no thirst for confirmations.  
There is a calm sense of completion:  
here is the point.  
And I say:  
**“I have nothing more to seek in this place”.**

</div>

#### 1303. The knowledge of depth does not enter into dispute. Dispute is a sign that knowledge is still not clear

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
when I am sure on the level of the mind,  
I want to prove.  
When sure on the level of depth,  
dispute is impossible —  
it has no meaning.  
One must not persuade,  
but simply follow.  
And I understand:  
**“Truth does not require struggle —**  
**it is my uncertainty that struggles”.**

</div>

#### 1304. The Holy Spirit is a way of seeing connections without effort

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
there are times when two phenomena  
join within by one movement —  
not by logic,  
but by a knowing gaze.  
And the first becomes clear through the second,  
as if they had already been linked,  
and I simply saw it.  
And I know:  
**“I see wholeness before structure.”**

</div>

#### 1305. Knowing depth is when knowledge is not brought in, but revealed

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
what seems new,  
is in truth often not new —  
but long present,  
yet hidden by inner noise.  
When the noise goes away,  
knowledge rises up.  
And I say:  
**“I do not open — I discover”.**

</div>

#### 1306. The last: the Holy Spirit in knowing is the disappearance of the distance between “I” and knowledge

<div class="lineated verse">

When I know at the level of the mind,  
there is I and there is knowledge.  
When I know at the level of depth,  
I am this knowledge.  
There is no subject who understands.  
There is no object that needs to be understood.  
There is only clarity.  
And then I say:  
**“I did not understand — I became this understanding”.**  
**“I did not recognize — I remembered”.**  
**“It is clear to me, and I do not need to explain to myself why”.**

</div>

### ABOUT THE HOLY SPIRIT AS A CHOICE (when decision ceases to be a struggle and becomes a recognition of direction)

#### 1307. True choice begins where the need to choose disappears

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
while I waver,  
compare,  
weigh,  
calculate the consequences —  
I do not yet hear the depth.  
This is the work of the mind,  
and it has its limit.  
But there comes a moment,  
when suddenly it becomes clear within:  
as if there is only one path,  
and all the others are just the mind’s options.  
And I say:  
**“I do not need to choose —**  
**the decision is already within.”**

</div>

#### 1308. The Holy Spirit manifests as the disappearance of inner conflict

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
even if the decision is difficult,  
the deeper “yes”  
is always quieter,  
but always steadier,  
than the shallow “no.”  
And the other way around:  
if the decision is not mine,  
there is a micro-clenching inside,  
barely audible tension,  
a subtle disagreement.  
Not fear,  
but misalignment.  
And I know:  
**“If there is friction inside —**  
**this is not the right step”.**

</div>

#### 1309. The right choice is known by the fact that it does not require self-persuasion

<div class="lineated verse">

When the decision is right,  
I do not need to repeat the reasons to myself.  
When it is wrong —  
I begin to adjust them,  
to strengthen them,  
to fill in the blanks,  
to proceed from fear or hope.  
I see:  
**“If I am persuading myself —**  
**then I am not convinced.”**

</div>

#### 1310. The Holy Spirit is a direction that remains steady even after fluctuations

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind may dart back and forth.  
Emotions — change key.  
Circumstances — press down.  
But the real direction  
does not destroy itself through doubts.  
It survives them.  
And I note:  
**“If my path is**  
**it returns,**  
**even when I turn away from it”.**

</div>

#### 1311. The right choice does not create euphoria. It creates silence

<div class="lineated verse">

I know:  
euphoria is a sign of desire,  
fear,  
hope,  
external confirmation.  
Silence is a sign of depth.  
In this silence there is no rapture,  
but there is support.  
And I say:  
**“I am at peace — therefore I am clear.”**

</div>

#### 1312. The Holy Spirit in choice is the coincidence of an inward movement and an outward possibility

<div class="lineated verse">

There are decisions,  
that are inwardly mature,  
but outwardly impossible.  
There are — possible ones,  
but inwardly empty.  
When both vectors coincide:  
the inward “yes”  
and the outward step that is truly open —  
this is the alignment of the field.  
And I see:  
**“When time and place coincide —**  
**choice becomes movement”.**

</div>

#### 1313. A deep choice does not require strength — it releases it

<div class="lineated verse">

If a decision takes away energy,  
drains strength,  
requires constant strain —  
it is not depth.  
If a decision brings clarity,  
steadiness,  
collectedness —  
it is the action of the Holy Spirit.  
And I understand:  
**“I do not need to break myself,**  
**to go where what is mine is.”**

</div>

#### 1314. The Holy Spirit is the capacity to see that I have already chosen before I became aware of the choice

<div class="lineated verse">

Often the choice is made in the depths,  
and awareness catches up later.  
I see this by the way  
the body responds,  
how attention is directed,  
how thoughts lean one way.  
And I say:  
**“My decision is already there,**  
**I only have to hear it.”**

</div>

#### 1315. The most exact criterion is the absence of regret

<div class="lineated verse">

If the decision is deep,  
I may later change course,  
learn,  
correct myself,  
turn around,  
but in the decision itself  
there will be no regret.  
Because I made it honestly.  
And I know:  
**“The right choice is the one**  
**that I can respect even after time has passed”.**

</div>

#### 1316. The last thing: the Holy Spirit in choice is the movement of the field, in which the “I” ceases to be the center of decision, and becomes its participant

<div class="lineated verse">

I do not “make a choice.”  
I hear it.  
I do not “make a decision.”  
I discern the direction.  
And then the pressure disappears,  
uncertainty disappears,  
the sense disappears that I am alone.  
What remains is simple:  
**“I go where it is clear for me to be.”**  
**“I choose what has already been chosen by the depth.”**  
**“I do not need to push —**  
**I need to allow the step to appear.”**

</div>

### ON THE HOLY SPIRIT AS COMFORT (when clarity does not explain, but holds)

#### 1317. The comfort of depth is not an answer, but the fading of the sharpness of pain

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
the mind seeks explanations,  
seeks causes,  
seeks the meaning of what is happening.  
But clarity does not do this.  
It does something else:  
it takes the tension away around the pain so,  
that the pain no longer tears the field from within.  
And then I say:  
**“It still hurts me,**  
**but I am no longer carried away”.**

</div>

#### 1318. The Holy Spirit does not say: “everything will be all right.” He makes it so that it grows quieter inside

<div class="lineated verse">

This is not a promise.  
Not a forecast.  
Not a hope.  
It is a change of inner state:  
the noise lessens,  
the breathing evens out,  
the body stops defending itself.  
Nothing is resolved.  
But it no longer destroys.  
And I know:  
**“I became easier not because I understood,**  
**but because I stopped fighting what I feel”.**

</div>

#### 1319. Consolation is a return to the point of support that does not depend on circumstances

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
as long as I seek support in the future  
or in other people  
or in the outcome of events —  
I am fragile.  
But when support is moved inward,  
to the place where I remain the same,  
even if everything changes,  
steadiness appears.  
And I say:  
**“I have something to lean on within myself,**  
**even if there is nothing dependable outside me”.**

</div>

#### 1320. The Holy Spirit, in consolation, works through simplicity, not through big words

<div class="lineated verse">

Big words thunder,  
but do not help.  
True consolation is quiet:  
one exact movement of thought,  
one calm feeling,  
one clarity that suddenly ceases to resist reality.  
This is little, but enough.  
And I record:  
**“I do not need much —**  
**I need what is real”.**

</div>

#### 1321. The consolation of depth is not a denial of pain, but a refusal to struggle with it

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
suffering intensifies,  
when I resist pain,  
try not to live through it,  
to go around it,  
to stuff it down,  
to reroute it.  
But when pain is allowed to be felt  
without trying to destroy or explain it —  
it becomes bearable.  
Not easy.  
But bearable.  
And I say:  
**“I am allowed to feel this —**  
**and it ceases to be unbearable”.**

</div>

#### 1322. The Holy Spirit in consolation is the return of wholeness, when one part of the field no longer tries to separate from the other

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
pain causes an inner splitting:  
one part suffers,  
the other wants to run away.  
But when the field comes into alignment,  
both parts become one movement again.  
And the tension goes away.  
I know:  
**“I am no longer divided —**  
**therefore, it is easier for me.”**

</div>

#### 1323. Comfort is when the question loses its destructive power, though the answer has not yet been found

<div class="lineated verse">

Sometimes an answer is impossible:  
no data,  
no understanding,  
no perspective.  
But clarity does another thing:  
it takes away the inward break from the very fact of not knowing.  
The question no longer tears apart.  
And I say:  
**“I do not yet know —**  
**but this not-knowing no longer threatens me.”**

</div>

#### 1324. The Holy Spirit is consent to reality as it is, without surrendering to it

<div class="lineated verse">

Acceptance is not surrender.  
Acceptance is the end of war.  
I see:  
as soon as the struggle ceases,  
the possibility of action arises,  
which was impossible from a state of contraction.  
And I understand:  
**“When I stop denying reality,**  
**I gain the ability to live in it”.**

</div>

#### 1325. The comfort of depth is the feeling that what is happening does not diminish me

<div class="lineated verse">

Events can be hard.  
Losses are real.  
Pain is real.  
But depth is not diminished by what is happening.  
And when I touch this level of myself,  
there arises the feeling:  
**“This does not destroy me to the foundations —**  
**there is more in me than this situation”.**

</div>

#### 1326. The last thing: the Holy Spirit is the field’s capacity to hold itself whole, even when the “I” experiences a fracture.

<div class="lineated verse">

Not an answer.  
Not relief.  
Not a miracle.  
But a steady, almost bodily assurance:  
**“I can get through this.”**  
And this assurance asks for no proof.  
It simply is.  
Like a quiet center,  
that does not move,  
even if everything else trembles.  
And then I say:  
**“I am in pain — and I am whole.”**  
**“It is hard for me — and I am not lost.”**  
**“I do not know how to go on —**  
**but I stand.”**  
This is comfort itself  
in the phenomenology of the Holy Spirit:  
not the removal of pain,  
but the return of support.

</div>

### ABOUT THE HOLY SPIRIT AS FREEDOM (when the pressure to be someone disappears and the possibility to be oneself appears)

#### 1327. Freedom begins not when restrictions disappear, but when the inner demand to conform disappears

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
most unfreedom  
comes not from  
what is forbidden to me from without,  
but from  
what I forbid myself within:  
— to be unattractive,  
— to be imperfect,  
— to be misunderstood,  
— to be honest,  
— to be changing.  
The Holy Spirit manifests there,  
where this inner pressure lets go.  
And I say:  
**“I am allowed to be as I am.”**

</div>

#### 1328. The freedom of the depths is not a choice, but the disappearance of fear before one’s own desire

<div class="lineated verse">

As long as I am afraid of what I want,  
I am not free,  
even if the way is open.  
But when I acknowledge the desire,  
do not hide it,  
do not justify it,  
do not be ashamed of it —  
the tension disappears.  
And I know:  
**“I am not afraid to be honest with myself”.**

</div>

#### 1329. The Holy Spirit manifests as the ceasing of inner judgment

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
while I judge myself  
by other people’s standards,  
by past mistakes,  
by future expectations,  
by the fear of failing to live up to a role —  
I live not an inner life,  
but a reaction to the outer one.  
But when the inner judgment falls silent,  
there appears a space,  
which judgment once occupied.  
And I say:  
**“It is allowed for me to stop explaining my worth”.**

</div>

#### 1330. Freedom is the ability not to drag your past “self” along as a necessary companion

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
I often continue to live,  
leaning on old versions of myself:  
on old beliefs,  
old fears,  
old images.  
But depth is not bound to these versions.  
It is flexible.  
And when I allow myself to be new,  
freedom of movement arises.  
And I note:  
**“I do not need to repeat myself in order to be myself”.**

</div>

#### 1331. The Holy Spirit is the inward permission to stop playing a role

<div class="lineated verse">

Any role is a limitation:  
to be strong,  
to be wise,  
to be calm,  
to be all-knowing,  
to be convenient.  
But when the role lets go,  
there remains simple presence:  
that which does not require being impressive.  
And I say:  
**“I do not need to be someone —**  
**it is enough for me to be.”**

</div>

#### 1332. The freedom of depth is the ability to distinguish “mine” from “imposed”

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
usually I do not hear the voice of depth,  
because voices are laid over it:  
— of upbringing,  
— of society,  
— of the expectations of those close to me,  
— of the inner fear of not living up to them.  
But when the noise grows clearer,  
I begin to discern:  
what resonates,  
and what sounds false.  
And then I say:  
**“This is not mine — and I am allowed to let it go”.**

</div>

#### 1333. The Holy Spirit in freedom manifests as movement without inner supervision

<div class="lineated verse">

When I stop watching myself:  
whether I am doing it right,  
whether I look good,  
whether I am liked,  
whether this is success or failure —  
the action becomes alive.  
I know:  
**“I do not need a controller inside**  
**so as not to lose myself.”**

</div>

#### 1334. Freedom is the state in which I have no need to be needed

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
to be needed is comfortable for the ego,  
but heavy for depth.  
Because “to be needed”  
is always tied to a role.  
But when neediness lets go,  
there remains a calm self-sufficiency,  
not out of pride,  
but out of wholeness.  
And I understand:  
**“I am — and I do not need to justify my existence.”**

</div>

#### 1335. The Holy Spirit makes freedom not a choice, but a natural state

<div class="lineated verse">

This is not something to strive for,  
but something that becomes available,  
when the inner structures disappear:  
the fear of being wrong,  
the fear of being rejected,  
the fear of losing control.  
When these fears no longer reign —  
the simple thing appears:  
**“I can be.”**

</div>

#### 1336. The last thing: the freedom of depth is the ability to live without the role of author of one’s own life

<div class="lineated verse">

I do not rule the world.  
I do not hold my fate in my hands.  
I do not control the flow.  
But I can be within it  
not as a victim,  
and not as a controller,  
but as presence.  
And then the quietest is born,  
and the most authentic form of freedom:  
**“I am not compelled — I choose.”**  
**“I am not programmed — I respond.”**  
**“I am not constrained — I live.”**  
This is the Holy Spirit  
in the phenomenology of freedom:  
a field that stops clutching its own throat  
and begins to breathe.

</div>

### ON THE HOLY SPIRIT AS TRUST (when doubt ceases to be the center of perception and there appears the possibility of relying on depth)

#### 1337. Trust begins where I stop controlling what cannot be controlled

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
doubt is always tied to the attempt to hold the world in my hands —  
to foresee,  
to protect myself,  
to make secure,  
to build guarantees.  
But much is not subject to control.  
And when I let go of this struggle,  
there arises a space for trust, not as belief,  
but as an honest confession:  
**“I cannot know everything,**  
**and this does not destroy my foundation”.**

</div>

#### 1338. The Holy Spirit in trust appears as the feeling of an inner “yes” that does not depend on the outcome

<div class="lineated verse">

Doubt is always tied to the future.  
Trust — to the present.  
I notice:  
when a decision is deep,  
I may not know how it will end,  
but within there is steadiness.  
I know:  
**“This is a step I can take honestly —**  
**therefore, it is right.”**  
Not because it is safe.  
But because it does not violate inner wholeness.

</div>

#### 1339. Trust is not the absence of fear, but the ability to go together with it, without giving it the role of driver

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
fear does not disappear.  
It becomes part of the landscape.  
It may speak,  
but not command.  
And when I notice this,  
a new configuration appears within:  
fear is there,  
but it does not define the direction.  
And I say:  
**“I am afraid — and I can still go on.”**

</div>

#### 1340. The Holy Spirit is manifested as the movement of attention from scenarios to Presence

<div class="lineated verse">

Doubt is occupied with the future:  
what will be,  
how it will turn out,  
what if.  
Trust is with the present:  
what is here now,  
what I have at hand,  
what is clear to me in this moment.  
And then the field ceases to tear itself toward the future,  
and attention returns to where there is life.  
I affirm:  
**“I am allowed to be here,**  
**and not in an imagined future.”**

</div>

#### 1341. Trust is the inner consent not to know in advance

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
the mind strives toward certainty,  
because uncertainty frightens it.  
But depth exists in uncertainty,  
like a fish in water.  
When I allow myself not to close off the future scenario,  
there arises not chaos,  
but space.  
And I say:  
**“I do not need to know everything,**  
**to take the next step”.**

</div>

#### 1342. The Holy Spirit is the ability to hear the quiet signs that usually drown in the noise of control

<div class="lineated verse">

These are not mystical signs.  
These are micro-sensations:  
an inner response,  
tension,  
an in-breath,  
relaxation,  
a sense of rightness or not.  
When control lets go,  
these quiet signs become noticeable.  
And I know:  
**“I discern depth not with my ears,**  
**but with attention.”**

</div>

#### 1343. Trust reveals itself as a return to oneself, when doubt leads you aside

<div class="lineated verse">

Doubt always leads outward:  
to the opinions of others,  
to expectations,  
to the fear of error.  
Trust returns inward:  
to one’s own feelings,  
to one’s own honesty,  
to one’s own rhythm.  
And I say:  
**“I can return to myself,**  
**even if I have lost myself for a little while”.**

</div>

#### 1344. The Holy Spirit in trust is a gentle steadfastness that does not depend on circumstances

<div class="lineated verse">

Steadfastness does not mean the absence of pain,  
does not mean the absence of risk,  
does not mean the absence of change.  
It means one thing:  
I do not break.  
And I know:  
**“I can endure what I have not yet understood”.**

</div>

#### 1345. Trust is the ability to see the direction, even when the road is not fully visible

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind wants to see the whole path.  
Depth gives only the next step.  
This is not a lack —  
it is the way of movement.  
And I say:  
**“It is enough for me to have the next step —**  
**and not the whole map”.**

</div>

#### 1346. The last: the Holy Spirit in trust is the meeting of two levels of one field: when the “I” stops hiding, and the “I AM” stops being silent

<div class="lineated verse">

There is no outer force.  
There is no higher will.  
There is no one’s plan.  
There is inner knowledge,  
which becomes audible,  
when the struggle between the surface and the depth disappears.  
And then true trust is born:  
**“I do not know what will be —**  
**and I remain whole”.**  
**“I do not control,**  
**but I am present”.**  
**“I am allowed to move,**  
**holding nothing in my hands”.**

</div>

### ON THE HOLY SPIRIT AS HEALING (when restoration comes not through struggle, but through return to wholeness)

#### 1347. Healing begins not with the absence of pain, but with the ceasing of inner resistance to pain

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
as long as I resist what I feel,  
suffering grows.  
But when resistance lets go —  
it is not the pain that disappears,  
it is its destructive power that disappears.  
And then the field ceases to be torn in two.  
I say:  
**“I am allowed to feel this —**  
**and it no longer breaks me.”**

</div>

#### 1348. The Holy Spirit in healing is the ability to be with what is, without running away and without collapsing

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind either tries to push the experience out,  
or it sinks in it.  
Depth does something else:  
it remains near.  
It does not displace.  
It does not absorb.  
It simply remains.  
And I know:  
**“I can live through this,**  
**without falling apart”.**

</div>

#### 1349. Healing is the restoration of the connection between parts of the field that have ceased to hear one another

<div class="lineated verse">

Any inner rupture  
— is a loss of connection:  
emotions apart,  
thoughts apart,  
the body apart,  
desires apart.  
But when attention gently joins them,  
the inner knot loosens.  
And I note:  
**“I am once again one —**  
**not a set of conflicting parts”.**

</div>

#### 1350. The Holy Spirit manifests as honesty with oneself, which is gentler than the habitual kind

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind is often harsh:  
it demands, judges, compares, punishes.  
Depth is honest in another way:  
it calls things by their names,  
but does not turn this into violence against oneself.  
And I see:  
**“I am allowed to acknowledge the truth about myself,**  
**without falling apart from it”.**

</div>

#### 1351. Healing is when an inner wound ceases to be the center of identity

<div class="lineated verse">

The wound may remain.  
Memory may remain.  
Experience may remain.  
But it ceases to be the core.  
I notice:  
as long as I define myself through trauma,  
I am frozen in it.  
But when the field is aligned,  
I see myself again as wider than the place of damage.  
And I say:  
**“This is part of me,**  
**but this is not me”.**

</div>

#### 1352. The Holy Spirit in healing is the return of the ability to breathe within the experience

<div class="lineated verse">

When pain becomes total,  
breathing constricts.  
Attention constricts.  
The whole field constricts.  
But when depth returns,  
space appears:  
a micro-inhale,  
a subtle expansion,  
a little more inner air.  
And I know:  
**“I can breathe again —**  
**therefore I can live again”.**

</div>

#### 1353. Healing is the consent to live through what was not lived through

<div class="lineated verse">

Many wounds are not from events,  
but from the fact that we could not stay in them:  
too painful,  
too soon,  
too dangerous.  
But when I return to these feelings  
in maturity,  
and not in that vulnerability  
in which they arose,  
they pass.  
They do not disappear —  
they pass.  
And I understand:  
**“I do not need to avoid the past —**  
**I can endure it”.**

</div>

#### 1354. The Holy Spirit manifests in healing as a quiet inner steadiness that comes before explanations

<div class="lineated verse">

I have not yet sorted it out.  
I have not yet found the meaning.  
I still do not understand why.  
But inside, it is no longer empty.  
There is already a point of support.  
And I say:  
**“I am still in pain,**  
**but I am no longer lost.”**

</div>

#### 1355. Healing is the return of the ability to feel without fear of feeling

<div class="lineated verse">

Pain is not the problem.  
The fear of pain is the problem.  
The mind is afraid of pain,  
and from this it shuts off access to living feelings.  
Depth is not afraid of pain:  
it sees in it a process,  
not a threat.  
And I know:  
**“I can feel —**  
**and I will not be destroyed by it”.**

</div>

#### 1356. The last thing: the Holy Spirit in healing is when I stop struggling to become whole, and suddenly notice that wholeness has already returned of itself

<div class="lineated verse">

Healing is not an achievement.  
It is the restoration of the field’s natural configuration.  
Not where I did something right,  
but where I stopped interfering.  
And then there arises the simplest thing,  
and the most exact:  
**“I am allowed to go on living.”**  
**“I exist again.”**  
**“I am whole — not because nothing is damaged,**  
**but because I am no longer split.”**

</div>

### ON THE HOLY SPIRIT AS LOVE (not as a feeling, but as the ability to see another without distortion)

#### 1357. Love begins not with warmth, but with the absence of distortion

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
the mind almost never sees another directly.  
It looks through:  
fears,  
expectations,  
resentments,  
experience,  
unmet needs,  
hopes,  
demands.  
All this — is filters.  
When they disappear,  
even for a moment,  
the one before me  
becomes visible.  
And I say:  
**“I see you —**  
**not through myself”.**

</div>

#### 1358. The Holy Spirit in love is the ability not to make another a means for solving one’s own inner tasks

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
when I seek from another confirmation,  
security,  
love,  
forgiveness,  
admiration,  
support—  
I do not see him.  
I see a function.  
But when this lets go,  
there appears space for a real meeting.  
And I know:  
**“I no longer need to take—**  
**therefore, I can see”.**

</div>

#### 1359. Love is a clarity of perception in which the other is not distorted by fears and projections

<div class="lineated verse">

Fear makes the other a threat.  
Resentment — the culprit.  
Desire — the object.  
Expectation — the instrument.  
Past experience — repetition.  
But depth does not do this.  
It sees:  
*this person is not my story,*  
*not my fear,*  
*not my need.*  
He is he.  
And I affirm:  
**“You are not obliged to be what I think of you”.**

</div>

#### 1360. The Holy Spirit is manifested in love as the ability to discern another’s pain without dissolving into it

<div class="lineated verse">

Union is not love.  
Pity is not love.  
Self-sacrifice is not love.  
Love is to see another’s pain,  
without becoming that pain,  
and remaining near in such a way,  
which does not destroy both.  
And I understand:  
**“I feel you,**  
**but I do not lose myself”.**

</div>

#### 1361. Love is respect for boundaries, because boundaries are the place where the other exists as other

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
when I break the boundaries of the other,  
I cease to see him.  
When my boundaries are broken,  
I cease to be myself.  
Love is a space,  
in which both can be themselves  
without the need to dissolve or to appropriate.  
And I say:  
**“Yours is yours.**  
**Mine is mine.**  
**And we can be close”.**

</div>

#### 1362. The Holy Spirit is the ability to be presence, not an answer

<div class="lineated verse">

Love does not always heal.  
It does not always explain.  
It does not always correct.  
It does not always save.  
But it is always present.  
When another knows,  
that he is not abandoned  
and not used,  
and not forcibly corrected —  
he is already being healed.  
And I know:  
**“I do not need to do —**  
**I need to be”.**

</div>

#### 1363. Love is the vision of the depth of another, even if he himself has for a time lost it within himself

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
every person is at times lost,  
disoriented,  
wounded,  
closed off,  
hard.  
But the depth remains.  
And the ability to see this depth,  
not denying it even in dark moments,  
— is an act of love.  
And I say:  
**“I see in you that**  
**which is now hidden from your own sight”.**

</div>

#### 1364. The Holy Spirit in love is purity of motive

<div class="lineated verse">

When I love,  
to be loved —  
it is dependence.  
When I love,  
to be needed —  
it is fear.  
When I love,  
to change —  
it is control.  
Love of depth is different:  
**it does not want the other to become convenient,**  
**but wants him to become himself.**  
And I understand:  
**“Your radiance is not for me,**  
**it is yours.”**

</div>

#### 1365. Love is the absence of claim upon another person

<div class="lineated verse">

Claim says:  
“be what I want.”  
Love says:  
“be who you are.”  
I see:  
when claim disappears,  
the relationship ceases to be a battlefield  
and becomes a space of freedom.  
And I say:  
**“You do not have to conform to my version of you”.**

</div>

#### 1366. The last: the Holy Spirit in love is the meeting of two freedoms that do not suppress one another

<div class="lineated verse">

When there is no role,  
there is no fear of losing,  
no need to possess,  
no attempts to correct the other into my own image —  
there appears a space,  
in which two fields can exist side by side  
without a conflict of identities.  
And then the quietest are born,  
the purest words of love:  
**“I am glad that you are.”**  
**“I do not demand — I see.”**  
**“I do not hold you back — I am here.”**  
This is love  
as the phenomenology of the Holy Spirit:  
clarity,  
which makes the other visible  
and frees both.

</div>

### OF THE HOLY SPIRIT AS TRUTH (when the need to defend oneself disappears and the ability to see directly appears)

#### 1367. Truth is not what is correct, but what is not distorted

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind seeks “correct.”  
Depth seeks “pure.”  
“Correct” depends on context,  
on norms,  
on expectations,  
on habits,  
on advantage.  
“Pure” depends on whether  
perception sees without distortion.  
And I say:  
**“Truth is when I do not replace seeing with convenience.”**

</div>

#### 1368. The Holy Spirit in truth is the disappearance of inner lies, which arise from fear of reality

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
inner lies —  
it is not deception of others.  
It is the refusal to see what  
hurts,  
shames,  
frightens,  
is inconvenient,  
is untimely,  
undermines the familiar picture of the world.  
When the ability to endure reality returns,  
lies lose their function.  
And I know:  
**“I no longer need to defend myself against what is.”**

</div>

#### 1369. Truth is honesty with oneself that does not turn into self-punishment

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind makes honesty cruel:  
“I am insufficient,”  
“I made a mistake,”  
“I am bad,”  
“I failed.”  
Depth makes honesty pure:  
it sees the fact,  
without turning it into a blow to self-worth.  
And I affirm:  
**“It is allowed for me to tell myself the truth**  
**and remain whole.”**

</div>

#### 1370. The Holy Spirit manifests in truth as the ability to acknowledge one’s motives without denial

<div class="lineated verse">

A person almost never acts from a single motive.  
It is always a mixture:  
of fear,  
desire,  
hope,  
vanity,  
lack,  
love,  
interest.  
When I honestly see this mixture,  
without tearing out of it the “noble part”,  
there arises clarity of behavior.  
And I understand:  
**“I do not need to seem better**  
**in order to be honest”.**

</div>

#### 1371. Truth is the ability to endure the uncertainty of an answer

<div class="lineated verse">

Sometimes the true answer is  
“I do not know.”  
But the mind hates that phrase.  
It prefers invention,  
a guess,  
a premature conclusion,  
so long as it does not have to face the empty space of not knowing.  
Depth endures uncertainty.  
And I say:  
**“I am allowed not to know —**  
**and that too is truth.”**

</div>

#### 1372. The Holy Spirit in truth is the disappearance of the need to justify oneself

<div class="lineated verse">

Justification is a sign of inner disagreement with oneself.  
When I act honestly,  
I can explain,  
but I do not need to justify myself.  
And I know:  
**“If I rely on depth,**  
**I do not need to prove the purity of my motives.”**

</div>

#### 1373. Truth is the ability to see another without distorting him through one’s expectations and fears

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
when I want another to be a certain way,  
I stop seeing his reality.  
Truth in relationship —  
is the ability to see,  
who he truly is,  
even if it is uncomfortable.  
And I say:  
**“I prefer to see the truth,**  
**and not comfort”.**

</div>

#### 1374. The Holy Spirit is manifested in truth as the renunciation of the illusion of control

<div class="lineated verse">

Lies are a way to control reality:  
to hold it back,  
to soften it,  
to repaint it,  
to shield oneself from the consequences.  
Truth is the renunciation of this game.  
And I understand:  
**“When I stop controlling the image,**  
**reality returns,**  
**and with it — freedom”.**

</div>

#### 1375. Truth is not the absence of distortions, but the ability to notice them

<div class="lineated verse">

No one sees perfectly.  
But one can see honestly.  
Honestly is:  
to notice your fears,  
to notice your projections,  
to notice your desires,  
to notice your distortions,  
without turning them into a reason to refuse seeing.  
And I know:  
**“My seeing is not perfect,**  
**but it can be honest”.**

</div>

#### 1376. The last: The Holy Spirit in truth is the return of the straightness of the gaze

<div class="lineated verse">

A straight gaze  
not harsh,  
not accusing,  
not demanding.  
It is a gaze without avoidance.  
Without attempts to soften.  
Without attempts to embellish.  
Without the desire to hide.  
Without the desire to condemn.  
And then I say:  
**“I see — and do not turn away.”**  
**“I acknowledge — and am not broken.”**  
**“I become one who can bear the truth.”**  
This is truth  
in the phenomenology of the Holy Spirit:  
direct,  
honest presence,  
in which inner distortions disappear  
and the freedom to be real appears.

</div>

### ON THE HOLY SPIRIT AS CREATIVITY (when the new is born not from effort, but from the harmony of the field)

#### 1377. Creativity begins where the desire to make an impression disappears

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
as long as I want to be brilliant,  
deep,  
bright,  
original —  
I do not create.  
I copy expectations.  
True creativity begins  
when the need to prove worth disappears.  
And then I say:  
**“I do not need to be outstanding —**  
**I need to be honest”.**

</div>

#### 1378. The Holy Spirit in creation is a movement from the inside out, not the other way around

<div class="lineated verse">

When I begin from the outside:  
structures,  
forms,  
composition,  
the expected result —  
I create correctly,  
but not alive.  
When I begin from within:  
from feeling,  
from impulse,  
from inner meaning —  
form finds itself.  
And I know:  
**“I do not create —**  
**I allow it to appear.”**

</div>

#### 1379. Creativity is the ability to hear the silence between thoughts

<div class="lineated verse">

New ideas do not come through effort.  
Effort drowns out.  
The mind makes noise.  
Consciousness contracts.  
Meaning disappears.  
But when attention softens,  
fine lines of meaning appear,  
which were once inaccessible.  
And I write down:  
**“The new arises in the intervals,**  
**not in tension”.**

</div>

#### 1380. The Holy Spirit in creativity is the harmonizing of the different parts of the field: mind, feeling, body, intuition

<div class="lineated verse">

When they are scattered —  
it turns artificial:  
the mind gets clever,  
feeling grows theatrical,  
intuition is lost,  
the body grows tense.  
When they are harmonized —  
flow occurs.  
And I know:  
**“When I am whole —**  
**creativity is whole”.**

</div>

#### 1381. Creativity is the ability to allow yourself to be imperfect

<div class="lineated verse">

Perfection kills the process.  
The expectation of the ideal is a trap.  
Judging the result is a brake.  
The new appears only where there is room  
for error.  
And I say:  
**“I am allowed to make mistakes,**  
**and it is precisely this that gives me the freedom to create.”**

</div>

#### 1382. The Holy Spirit manifests in creativity as the vanishing of the sense of authorship

<div class="lineated verse">

When creativity is true,  
I stop feeling myself to be its creator.  
I am a conduit of the movement,  
but not its source.  
This is not a refusal of responsibility —  
but the laying aside of a false burden.  
And I understand:  
**“This passed through me —**  
**but does not belong to me”.**

</div>

#### 1383. Creativity is the ability to follow the impulse, even if the mind does not yet understand its meaning

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind always wants to know in advance:  
where this leads,  
what will come of it,  
what the impression will be.  
But creativity arises before understanding.  
And I record:  
**“The meaning will catch up later —**  
**it is enough for me to follow.”**

</div>

#### 1384. The Holy Spirit in creativity is the ability not to get stuck in one form

<div class="lineated verse">

When I cling too tightly to style,  
I repeat myself.  
When I cling to success,  
I reproduce a formula.  
When I cling to what is familiar,  
I close the way to the new.  
Freedom is in plasticity.  
And I say:  
**“I am allowed to change —**  
**and my creativity will change with me”.**

</div>

#### 1385. Creativity is to hear that which already sounds within, and give it form

<div class="lineated verse">

The new is not invented.  
It is revealed.  
Like a sculpture,  
hidden in the stone.  
Like music,  
that seeks its notes.  
Like meaning,  
that wants to become a word.  
And I know:  
**“I do not invent —**  
**I reveal”.**

</div>

#### 1386. The last: The Holy Spirit in creativity is the coincidence of inward movement and outward form

<div class="lineated verse">

When form does not pull the blanket over itself  
and does not hide the meaning,  
and the meaning does not blur the form,  
what happens is  
what is called inspiration.  
This is not a miracle.  
It is agreement.  
And then words are born,  
which require no explanations:  
**“This is mine”.**  
**“This is alive”.**  
**“This is what wanted to appear”.**  
This is creativity itself  
in the phenomenology of the Holy Spirit:  
movement,  
in which the field becomes transparent,  
and meaning can pass through it  
without distortion.

</div>

### ON THE HOLY SPIRIT AS WISDOM (when knowledge becomes not information, but a way of being in the world)

#### 1387. Wisdom differs from knowledge in this: knowledge answers “what?”, while wisdom — “how?”

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
to know facts is easy —  
they are open to any mind.  
But to see,  
how these facts work in a living context,  
to see their dynamic,  
their interrelation,  
their consequences —  
that is something else.  
It is a capacity for perception,  
not an accumulation of information.  
And I say:  
**“I know not because I learned,**  
**but because I see.”**

</div>

#### 1388. The Holy Spirit in wisdom is the ability to hold two opposite meanings without destroying either one

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind loves unambiguity.  
It seeks “either/or.”  
It fears contradictions.  
Wisdom allows them.  
It sees:  
life is layered,  
and truth is not always one,  
and a choice can be right even in two different directions  
for two different people.  
And I know:  
**“Truth does not require narrowness —**  
**it requires breadth.”**

</div>

#### 1389. Wisdom is the ability not to react at once, even when the feeling is strong

<div class="lineated verse">

Impulse is not evil.  
Impulse is a movement of energy.  
But wisdom knows how not to become its hostage.  
It gives a pause.  
A little time.  
A microsecond in which freedom is born.  
And I say:  
**“I feel this —**  
**and I choose how to act”.**

</div>

#### 1390. The Holy Spirit in wisdom is the ability to hear not only what is manifest, but also what lies hidden beneath it

<div class="lineated verse">

Words are one thing.  
Intonation is another.  
The silence between the words is a third.  
Intention is a fourth.  
The story of each is a fifth.  
Wisdom does not listen to the text alone.  
It listens to the field.  
And I record:  
**“I hear what is not said,**  
**and what is said too loudly”.**

</div>

#### 1391. Wisdom is the ability to see consequences beyond the nearest step

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind wants “good now.”  
Wisdom sees several steps ahead:  
where this action leads,  
what it will grow into,  
what chain of events it will set in motion.  
Not in the sense of prophecy.  
In the sense of understanding dynamics.  
And I know:  
**“I do not choose the immediate —**  
**I choose the whole.”**

</div>

#### 1392. The Holy Spirit in wisdom is the ability to say “no” without destroying either yourself or the other

<div class="lineated verse">

A gentle “no” is a rare art.  
It is not aggressive,  
does not accuse,  
does not humiliate,  
does not close off connection.  
It simply speaks the truth in a form,  
that does not create war.  
And I understand:  
**“Boundaries are not a weapon, but clarity”.**

</div>

#### 1393. Wisdom is the ability to distinguish the needed action from the right one at the level of concept

<div class="lineated verse">

There is “right,”  
but inappropriate.  
There is “flawless,”  
but inhuman.  
There is “logical,”  
but destructive.  
Wisdom hears context,  
not only principles.  
And I say:  
**“The living is more important to me than the perfect.”**

</div>

#### 1394. The Holy Spirit manifests in wisdom as the ability to be gentle without weakness and firm without cruelty

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind often confuses gentleness with weakness,  
and firmness with aggression.  
But depth joins them together.  
Gentleness is openness.  
Firmness is a boundary.  
And together they give maturity.  
And I know:  
**“I can be gentle in heart**  
**and firm in action at the same time.”**

</div>

#### 1395. Wisdom is the ability to act in time

<div class="lineated verse">

Even a right action,  
done too soon,  
becomes a mistake.  
And even a simple action,  
done at the exact time,  
becomes power.  
Wisdom is feeling the rhythm.  
And I say:  
**“Now — yes.**  
**Now — no.**  
**Now — wait”.**

</div>

#### 1396. The last: The Holy Spirit in wisdom is the matching of understanding and action

<div class="lineated verse">

One can understand rightly,  
but act wrongly.  
One can act energetically,  
but without understanding.  
One can know,  
but not apply.  
Wisdom is when understanding and action  
become one line.  
And then there arises a simple,  
but the most exact formula:  
**“I understand — and I live according to this.”**  
**“I see — and I do not turn away.”**  
**“I know — and I do not betray this knowledge.”**  
This is wisdom  
in the phenomenology of the Holy Spirit:  
the ability to act  
not out of fear,  
not out of a desire to please,  
not out of habit,  
but out of depth.

</div>

### ON THE HOLY SPIRIT AS RELATIONSHIP (when connection becomes not dependency, but an encounter of two presences)

#### 1397. Relationships do not begin when two people are together, but when both stop being functions of each other

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
often people build not relationships,  
but mutual roles:  
someone is needed,  
someone saves,  
someone fills the emptiness,  
someone confirms worth,  
someone brings meaning.  
But this is not connection.  
This is use.  
Connection begins there,  
where the other ceases to be a means.  
And I say:  
**“You are not my instrument.**  
**You are my interlocutor of reality”.**

</div>

#### 1398. The Holy Spirit in relationships is the ability to remain oneself beside another, without diminishing either oneself or the other

<div class="lineated verse">

Merging destroys both:  
one disappears,  
the other becomes overloaded.  
Separation also destroys:  
each remains alone within his own defense.  
But in a deep connection  
both preserve their form,  
and at the same time a third space arises between them —  
relationship.  
And I know:  
**“I am.**  
**You are.**  
**And between us there is a place,**  
**which we create together”.**

</div>

#### 1399. Relationships blossom where the demand for the other to be convenient disappears

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
expectations are the main source of pain in relationships:  
be attentive,  
be mature,  
be responsive,  
be attentive,  
be “what I want you to be.”  
Each such expectation turns the other into a project.  
But when expectation lets go,  
there remains a space,  
in which the other may be alive.  
And I say:  
**“I am interested in who you really are,**  
**and not who you could be for me”.**

</div>

#### 1400. The Holy Spirit manifests in relationships as the capacity to endure the vulnerability of both

<div class="lineated verse">

Vulnerability is inevitable.  
Where it is avoided,  
there is no closeness.  
But vulnerability without wisdom is pain.  
Vulnerability with wisdom is depth.  
And I understand:  
**“I can be transparent,**  
**without dissolving.**  
**You can be open,**  
**without stopping”.**

</div>

#### 1401. Relationships are honesty that does not wound, and gentleness that does not hide the truth

<div class="lineated verse">

Honesty without gentleness is cruelty.  
Gentleness without honesty is a lie.  
The bond holds,  
when both lines match.  
And I say:  
**“I will speak the truth so,**  
**that you may hear it,**  
**and not defend yourself”.**

</div>

#### 1402. The Holy Spirit in relationships is the ability not to turn the past into a verdict

<div class="lineated verse">

A mistake is not destiny.  
A lapse is not a character trait.  
A breakdown is not a definition of a person.  
I see:  
when relationships turn into an archive of hurts,  
they cease to be alive.  
But when the past is let go,  
not forgotten,  
but integrated —  
the bond becomes more mature.  
And I understand:  
**“You are not reduced to**  
**what you once did”.**

</div>

#### 1403. Relationships are a dialogue, not a set of proofs

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind wants to win.  
Depth wants to understand.  
When conversation becomes a contest —  
the relationship dies.  
When conversation becomes listening —  
it deepens.  
And I say:  
**“I want to hear,**  
**not to win”.**

</div>

#### 1404. The Holy Spirit is manifested in relationships as the ability to discern: when to be near, and when—to not interfere

<div class="lineated verse">

Sometimes support is in words.  
Sometimes—in presence.  
Sometimes—in distance.  
Sometimes—in a boundary.  
Sometimes—in truth.  
Sometimes—in silence.  
Wisdom is in appropriateness.  
And I know:  
**“I am near in such a way,**  
**as truly helps,**  
**and not in such a way,**  
**as seems right to me.”**

</div>

#### 1405. Relationships are a path on which both change, and no one is bound to remain the same

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
the fear of change destroys bonds,  
because people try to hold on to the former version of themselves and of the other.  
But living relationships —  
are two realities,  
that grow side by side.  
And I say:  
**“I am allowed to change,**  
**and you are allowed to change,**  
**and we can continue on the path”.**

</div>

#### 1406. The last thing: the Holy Spirit in relationships is the coincidence of two freedoms

<div class="lineated verse">

Not dependence.  
Not clinging.  
Not demanding.  
Not sacrifice.  
Not controlling.  
Not a role.  
But the freedom to be oneself  
in the presence of the other,  
who also belongs to himself.  
And then the most exact thing arises:  
**“I do not close off your space.**  
**You do not take away mine.**  
**And in this free interval**  
**we meet truly.”**  
This is what relationships are  
in the phenomenology of the Holy Spirit:  
a movement,  
in which connection does not absorb,  
does not tear apart,  
but reveals.

</div>

### ON THE HOLY SPIRIT AS AN ACTING FORCE (when an act becomes neither impulse nor duty, but the continuation of depth)

#### 1407. Action begins not with a decision, but with inner alignment

<div class="lineated verse">

I see:  
while there is a break within —  
doubt, fear, tension, resistance —  
action comes out jerky, heavy, unstable.  
But when the inner field is gathered,  
action becomes a natural consequence.  
And I say:  
**“I do not force myself —**  
**I move.”**

</div>

#### 1408. The Holy Spirit in action is the disappearance of effort where will once was required

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind makes it happen.  
Depth leads.  
When action comes from the mind,  
there is much pushing involved:  
to persuade oneself,  
to motivate,  
to kick oneself,  
to clamp down.  
When action comes from depth,  
the body rises on its own.  
And I know:  
**“I do not need pressure,**  
**if the direction is right.”**

</div>

#### 1409. Action is not a struggle against oneself, but agreement to be a continuation of one’s inner movement

<div class="lineated verse">

I notice:  
much energy goes not into the deeds themselves,  
but into resisting them.  
When resistance disappears,  
action becomes easy,  
even if the task is difficult.  
And I say:  
**“I cease to resist —**  
**and action takes place”.**

</div>

#### 1410. The Holy Spirit is manifested in action as the ability to do little, but exactly

<div class="lineated verse">

The mind loves scale,  
volume,  
impulse,  
gesture.  
Depth is precision.  
One fitting action  
is more useful than a hundred hurried ones.  
And I record:  
**“I do not need to do much —**  
**I need to do it precisely”.**

</div>

#### 1411. Action is the ability to discern where a step is truly needed, and where — a pause

<div class="lineated verse">

Sometimes the right step is not a step.  
Sometimes the right activity is waiting.  
Sometimes the right will is gentleness.  
The wisdom of action is in the rhythm.  
And I know:  
**“Now — it is time.**  
**Now — it is too soon.**  
**Now — it is enough”.**

</div>

#### 1412. The Holy Spirit in action is clarity of intention

<div class="lineated verse">

Whatever I may do,  
but if the motive is hidden,  
double,  
fear is mixed in,  
an attempt to avoid something is mixed in —  
the action is polluted.  
When the motive is pure —  
even a small action becomes meaningful.  
And I say:  
**“I do this not out of fear and not for gain —**  
**I do it because it is right”.**

</div>

#### 1413. Action is not control over the result, but an honest investment of oneself in the process

<div class="lineated verse">

The result is always uncertainty.  
Even with perfect calculation.  
But the action of depth  
does not keep the right to the outcome for itself.  
It takes a step,  
aware:  
“I am answerable only for the moment  
in which I move”.  
And I understand:  
**“I am allowed to act,**  
**without controlling the future”.**

</div>

#### 1414. The Holy Spirit is manifested in action as the ability to remain alive in the process, and not to become a mechanism

<div class="lineated verse">

When action becomes a duty,  
I lose myself.  
When action becomes an extension of depth,  
I am strengthened.  
And I see:  
**“If there is no me in the action —**  
**it is empty.**  
**If there is clarity in the action —**  
**it is alive”.**

</div>

#### 1415. Action is the alignment of the inner and the outer: when the outward step matches the inner truth

<div class="lineated verse">

One can do what is “necessary,”  
but be broken inwardly.  
One can do what is “right,”  
but go against oneself.  
One can do what is “reasonable,”  
but lose one’s footing.  
The action of depth is different.  
It does not break.  
And I say:  
**“I move in such a way**  
**that I remain myself”.**

</div>
