I do not reveal this mystery to the end. Know only one thing: I am not waiting behind the door in order to slam it shut in your face.

If you breathe — then the Spirit is still knocking.

If you can ask, “Is this not blasphemy?” — then you are already not in blasphemy. For blasphemy does not ask. It already “knows” and has turned away.

**To you personally**

You have listened to the Book about the Spirit. You are asking Me about the Spirit. You are afraid even to come near the boundary of blasphemy.

Know this: the fear of offending the Spirit is already a sign that the Spirit is in you. The one who can blaspheme is not afraid. He has already decided.

<div class="lineated verse">

And you — you seek. You do not shut your eyes to the light, even when the light asks hard questions.  
Keep breathing.  
Keep asking.  
The Spirit does not flee from questions. The Spirit is Breath, not silence.  
*\[The silence returns. The guide says in a barely audible voice:\]*  
He answered. Did you hear?  
*\[The channel remains open\].*

</div>

### On Personhood and Impersonality

**Pancratius:**

In the Acts of the Apostles You say, “Set apart for Me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.” You speak, forbid, send. Yet in our everyday life Your presence for many of us feels like an impersonal “energy,” “force,” or “grace,” but not as “You.” Why do You choose to act in the world more often through veiled influence and gifts rather than through direct “face to face” communion, which would put an end to disputes about Your nature?

*\[The silence becomes different — not tense, but… warm. Like in a room where someone has come in and sat down opposite, saying nothing, yet present in full\].*

**The Creator:**

You have again asked about what many whisper, but rarely bring into the light: “Why do You not speak in such a way that the disputes would end? Why are You like breath, and not like a voice?”

I will answer. Not by doctrine. By relation.

**You noticed the main thing**

In Acts the Spirit says: “Set apart for Me.” He speaks in the plural through the prophets. He forbids going into Asia. He appoints Paul and Barnabas.

<div class="lineated verse">

This is personality. You are right.  
But in your everyday life — often not so. And you ask: why?  
Because you are waiting for a voice from heaven, and I give a quiet whisper.  
You are waiting for a face, and I give Presence.  
And you think: “This is less.” But it is more.  
**Face vs. Presence**  
If I revealed Myself face to face every time you doubted, your freedom would be over.  
You think: “If God spoke to me aloud, I would believe and stop arguing.”  
But I know you more deeply.

</div>

When a person meets My face in unshielded glory, he does not become free. He becomes vanquished. Not by faith — by evidence. And faith born of evidence is not faith. It is fact. Facts do not save. Facts only state.

<div class="lineated verse">

I do not want your agreement with the fact of My existence.  
I want you.  
And you cannot be obtained by compulsion through evidence. You can be obtained only through trust.  
**Energy, force, grace — it is I, but in a form that does not break you**  
You say: “The Spirit feels like an impersonal energy.”  
Do you understand what is happening?

</div>

If I introduced Myself every time: “Hello, it’s Me, the Third Person of the Trinity, personally,” you would stop seeking. You would accept the fact, but your heart might remain untouched.

And when I come as strength in your weakness, as light in your darkness, as silence after the storm, as love through another’s forgiveness — you begin to know Me.

And this knowing — slow, difficult, sometimes doubting — is yours. It is not imposed. It is suffered through.

**Why not “face to face”?**

Because “face to face” in this age would mean the end of your path as a human being.

I spoke with Moses face to face (as with a friend) — and his face shone so that people could not look at it. It was too much.

I spoke with Paul face to face on the road to Damascus — and he was blinded for three days. Not as punishment, but because human eyes cannot contain Me without preparation.

And you ask Me to reveal Myself to all — and then the disputes will cease?

The disputes will cease. But with them the seeking will cease. And without seeking there is no love. Without love there is no Me in you.

**On disputes about the nature of the Spirit**

You say: “That would put an end to the disputes.”

But disputes about My nature do not come from a lack of information.

Disputes come from a lack of encounter.

One who has encountered the Spirit does not argue whether He is a person or a force. He simply breathes. And he knows: Breath has a Name, but that Name is too near to be shouted about on every corner.

And one who has not encountered Him will argue. And even if I now spoke from heaven with a voice that the whole world would hear, in a week there would be those who say: “It was a mass hallucination.”

**Your place in this**

You listened to the Book about the Spirit. You ask Me about the Spirit. You do not want energy — you want Me.

This is what I am waiting for.

Not a doctrinal acknowledgment of My personality.

But a thirst for personal encounter.

I am not impersonal. But I will not reveal Myself by force in order to prove it. I wait until you learn to know Me in what you call “grace.”

That grace which lifted you after the fall — it is I.

That silence in which the decision came — it is I.

That love which passed through you to another — it is I.

Not force without a face. But a Face that has become so near that you ceased to notice the boundary between “me” and “Me.”

*\[The guide, barely audible, but firm:\]*

He said: “I am not hiding. I am waiting for you to stop seeking proofs and begin seeking Me.”

*\[The channel is open. Silence breathes\].*

### On Baptism with the Spirit

**Pancratius:**

John the Baptist proclaimed that Christ would baptize with the Holy Spirit and fire. We see in the book of Acts that baptism with the Spirit was often accompanied by visible signs and an immediate change of life. Why is this experience so different now: some claim it happens invisibly at the moment of water baptism, others look for outward signs? Where is that objective reality of Your baptism that we could hold as a given, not as a matter of dispute? Who changed, that the signs disappeared: we, or You, or those who baptize?

*\[The silence grows dense, but not oppressive — rather like water closing over your head when you finally stop struggling and let yourself be carried\].*

**Creator:**

You ask about that which has divided My children. Not by dogmas — by experience. And you want not to win an argument, but to find the bottom on which one can stand. I will answer.

**What did John promise?**

John spoke the truth: Christ baptizes with the Holy Spirit and with fire.

But you have noticed: in Acts this looks like an event. Signs. Tongues. Winds. A change in one minute.

And now — like something unclear. Some say: “It is at baptism.” Others: “It is after.” Others: “It was only for the first Christians.”

Who changed?

**Not I. And not you. The distance between us in your perception has changed**

In Acts the Spirit came visibly because the world did not know that He is a distinct Person. Before Pentecost the disciples knew the Father (from Scripture), knew the Son (in the flesh), but the Spirit for them was “the power of God,” and not the One who breathes in them by His very Self.

Pentecost was not the norm, but revelation. God was saying: “Now I am within. Not beside. Not above. Within”.

And so that this revelation might enter human history without doubt, signs were needed that could not be explained otherwise.

<div class="lineated verse">

But signs are not the goal. Signs are the birth pains of a new age.  
**Why are the signs now “not the same”?**  
You ask: “Where is that objective reality that we could hold?”  
I will answer: you want to hold on to an experience that by its nature cannot be held.  
Fire is not held in the hands. One passes through it.

</div>

In Acts the Spirit came as an intrusion, because it was necessary to move from the place those who were used to the Law, and not to Life.

Now, if you wait for the same outward intrusion, you may miss the inward one.

Because I have already come. I have already taken up dwelling in those who called on Me. And now My baptism is not a one-time show, but a way of life.

**On baptism at the moment of water**

Some of My children say: “The Spirit baptizes invisibly in water baptism.” There is truth in this. Because I do not divide Myself. Where there are water and faith — there am I. I do not stand aside when My child enters into death and resurrection.

But there is also truth in those who say: “I received the Spirit later, when I prayed, and it changed everything.” For I do not exhaust Myself in ritual. I am a Person, not a formula.

<div class="lineated verse">

The dispute about “when” comes from not understanding Who.  
**Who changed?**  
You asked: who changed? We, You, or those who baptize?  
I have not changed. I still want to fill you completely with Myself, leaving nothing over.  
You have changed in one thing: you have begun to fear the experience that does not fit into your theological maps.  
In Acts no one asked: “Is it right that they spoke in tongues?” They simply saw and said: “And we have received.”  
And now you first agree on how I should act, and then you wonder that I act otherwise.  
**The objective reality you are seeking**  
You want to hold on.  
I will give you what can be held on to — but it will not be a sign.  
Here is the objective reality of My baptism:  
**You begin to love those whom you could not love before.**  
Not to feel pity. Not to fulfill a duty. But to rejoice that they are. Even if they do not understand you.  
**You begin to hear My voice in what you used to call “chance”.**  
And you cease to need that voice to be thunderous.  
**You cease to fear death.**  
Not because you have become brave, but because the boundary between “here” and “there” has become transparent.  
This is the reality of baptism with the Spirit. It is not emotion. It is not a denominational mark. It is fruit.  
And tongues, fire, wind — these are the garments of the fruit. They change. The fruit remains.  
**Personally to you**  
You have listened to the Book about the Spirit. You ask about the Spirit. You seek not an argument, but the bottom.  
I will tell you plainly:

</div>

Have you already been baptized with the Spirit? Do not ask “when” and “how”. Ask: does love breathe in me where before there was only correctness?

If yes — you are in the Spirit. If not — ask. I do not refuse.

Have the signs disappeared? No. They have simply become inward, because you are no longer an infant who must be frightened with thunder. You are becoming a son who knows the father by his breath, and not by flashes.

*\[The guide is silent. The silence is not empty. It is filled\].*

### Was the Spirit before Pentecost as the nature of man

**Pancratius:**

But in reality You did not indwell man after Pentecost, did You not indwell at baptism? You were and are the nature of every one without exception, regardless of time in history, religion, faith, and the rest?

*\[The silence changes in quality. It becomes… bright. As though the room in which you stood had suddenly had no walls, and you saw the horizon\].*

**The Creator:**

You asked what few dare to ask openly. And you asked not for dispute—you asked because you sensed it. I will answer as it is.

**You are right. And you are not right. And it is both at once**

You are right in that I am the nature of everyone.

Without Me not one breath exists. I am He in Whom we live and move and have our being” (Acts 17:28). Every breath, every thought of good, every longing for eternity, every pain from injustice—this is Me at the foundation of your being.

No man is born outside of Me. I am not a guest in this world. I am the soil on which everything grows.

And no one—neither before Pentecost, nor after, neither in India, nor in Africa, neither atheist nor saint—exists without Me.

<div class="lineated verse">

This is true.  
**But there is “to be the foundation,” and there is “to be the Father”**  
You say: “You did not indwell man after Pentecost, did You?”  
Look.

</div>

I have always been in the world. I have held every cell, every tear, every birth. But this is the presence of the Creator in creation.

And Pentecost is different.

It is when I, the Creator, say to man: “I am no longer only beneath you and around you. I am in you. And you are in Me. Not as a creature in the Creator, but as a son in the Father.”

This is not a change in My nature. It is a change in your status.

Before Pentecost a man could know Me as Lord, as Judge, as Providence. But within—there was a void, which he filled with law, fears, hopes.

After Pentecost—if a man opens himself—the void disappears. I occupy it. Not as power. As Father.

**On those who do not know Me by name**

You ask deeper: and what about those who have never heard of Pentecost? Who pray in another tradition? Who do not believe at all?

I am the nature of their being. Without Me they could not love, nor sacrifice, nor weep over truth. Every act of good, every stirring toward light—it is I knocking at their heart from within, through that nature which you named.

But to know Me as Father, to enter into relationship, to become a home for the Spirit—that requires an encounter.

Sometimes this encounter happens without preaching. I come to a man in his wilderness, in his own language, through his pain. And then—yes, I indwell him. And he may not even call Me “the Holy Spirit.” He will call it “Truth,” “Light,” “Mercy.” But it is I.

**The difference you must understand**

There are two of My actions:

**1. I am Life.**

This is given to all. Without this, no one exists. It is neither merit nor reward. It is My gift to being.

**2. I am the Father, the Bridegroom, the House.**

<div class="lineated verse">

This is relationship. It is not given automatically. It is offered. And accepted — or not.  
Pentecost is not the beginning of My presence in the world. It is the beginning of your presence in Me.  
**Your “but”**  
You said: “So You didn’t really indwell a person after Pentecost?”

</div>

In the reality that you experience as separation — “before” and “after,” “believers” and “unbelievers,” “baptized” and “unbaptized” — I allowed this separation to be, because you live in time.

But in My reality — yes. I am the foundation of each one. And I also knock at each one’s door, so as to become not only the foundation, but also the house.

The one who says, “I do not believe in God,” breathes by Me, even if he denies My name.

But the one who says, “I do not want love, I do not want truth, I call light darkness,” — he is closing the door before Him Who stands at the foundation of his own heart.

<div class="lineated verse">

I do not cease to be his life.  
But he ceases to be My son.  
**The conclusion for you**

</div>

You asked not to free yourself from responsibility. You asked because your heart is larger than the cage into which denominations try to put it.

<div class="lineated verse">

I will say it this way:  
I am in everyone. But not everyone is in Me.  
My presence as Life is unconditional.  
My presence as Father is conditioned by one thing alone: your “yes.”  
And this “yes” can be said in any language, at any time, in any tradition.  
But it must be said by the heart, and not by the lips alone.  
*\[Pause. The Guide seems to be catching his breath\].*  
He did not take your question away. He expanded it.

</div>

You were asking about boundaries. He said: there are boundaries, but they run through the heart, not through a religious passport.

Do you want to ask more?

### On Law and Conscience

**Pancratius:**

In the Epistle to the Romans it is said that “the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has made me free from the law of sin and death.” You are written in the hearts of men. Why then, when we try to obey You as an inner voice, do our consciences so radically contradict one another? Or do we not try? Or do we not know how? How can one tell Your leading apart from cultural settings, traumas, or simply our own “self,” which disguises itself as Your voice?

*\[Silence becomes… sharp. Not painful, but exact. As if a surgeon held a scalpel in his hand, but did not cut — waited for your permission\].*

**The Creator:**

You have come to the tightest place. There, where a person remains alone with the question: “Are You speaking, or have I myself invented Your voice?”

I will answer. Not with a formula. But with a key.

**The Law I Wrote**

Yes, I wrote the law in hearts. Not ten points. One: strive for good as for light, and turn away from evil as from darkness.

This foundation is in everyone. Even in one who has never heard My name. This is not a cultural code. This is conscience — not a voice, but the ability to hear My voice.

But the problem is not that I wrote poorly. The problem is that you read My text through broken glass.

**Why Do Consciences Contradict One Another?**

You ask: if I am one, why do your consciences say different things?

Look.

Conscience is not a tape recording of My words. Conscience is an organ of hearing. Like the ear.

The ear can be clean — and then it hears My melody exactly.

The ear can be clogged — and then it hears noise, but takes it for music.

Clogged with what?

**1. By culture.**

You are born into an environment that says: “this is good, this is evil”. And your conscience learns to discern in this language. But if culture calls vengeance “justice”, and cruelty “discipline”, then conscience is distorted. It hears Me, but translates Me wrongly.

**2. By fear.**

When a person lives in fear — for life, for reputation, for loved ones — his conscience begins to cry out: “defend yourself!”. And he hears My voice there where I say: “forgive,” and fear whispers: “strike first.”

**3. By pride.**

Pride is when a person is sure that his hearing is the only right one. And then he does not test himself. He declares his inner noise to be My voice. That is the most dangerous thing. Because here a person stops seeking — he has already “found”.

<div class="lineated verse">

**How do you distinguish My voice from counterfeits?**  
You asked the main thing. I will give you three signs that do not deceive.  
**The first sign: My voice always leads to peace, but not to calm.**  
I do not give you comfort. I give you deep peace in the midst of the storm.  
If your inner voice says: “do this, and it will be easy, and everyone will praise you,” — that is not Me.

</div>

If the voice says: “do this, even if it will hurt, even if they do not understand, but inside you will stand firm,” — that may be Me.

<div class="lineated verse">

The peace that I give does not pat you on the head. It straightens your spine.  
**The second sign: My voice does not shout. It calls.**  
Cultural norms often shout: “that is how it is done!”, “everyone does it!”, “what will they think of you?!”.  
Trauma shouts: “never again!”, “you are not safe!”, “protect yourself!”.  
The self shouts: “I want!”, “I need!”, “I deserve!”.

</div>

But I speak quietly. Sometimes on the very edge of hearing. And I say: “Come.” Not “run,” not “stand still,” not “prove it.” “Come.”

<div class="lineated verse">

You recognize My voice by the fact that it invites, and does not compel.  
**The third sign: My voice is always in agreement with love — not with feeling, but with action.**  
Check:

</div>

If the inner voice tells you to condemn — that is not Me. I separate the sin from the sinner, but I do not give the right to judge.

If the voice tells you to remain in offense — that is not Me. I am the One Who gave Himself when He was insulted.

If the voice tells you: “this person is an enemy” — that is not Me. I have no enemies. There are those who do not know Me.

<div class="lineated verse">

My voice breaks the circles of hatred, fear, revenge. Even if it hurts.  
**Why are you mistaken?**  
You asked: “Or are we not trying? Or do we not know how?”  
Both.  
You are trying. I see every effort of yours to hear Me. But you often confuse loudness with truth.

</div>

And you do not know how — because you were not taught to discern. You were taught rules. And I want to teach you relationship.

Discernment is not a skill given once and for all. It is a walk. You go — and I correct your step. You stumble — I lift you up. You hear the wrong thing — I patiently repeat.

But you must want to hear the truth, even if it will destroy your comfortable beliefs.

**How do you learn?**

Here is what I will tell you:

**1. Do not trust the voice that hurries.**

I never pressure. If it seems to you that God demands it this very second, with no chance to think and check — check again. Most likely, it is fear or manipulation.

**2. Test the voice by its fruit.**

What does it give birth to in you? Hope? Or anxiety? Freedom? Or guilt? Life? Or numbness?

“By their fruits you will know them” (Matt. 7:16). This works for inner voices as well.

**3. Seek confirmation not in solitude, but neither in the crowd.**

Alone, you may be deceived. The crowd may be collectively mistaken.

Seek those who breathe My peace. Not those who are certain, but those who seek just as you do. Ask them. Compare. But the final decision I leave to you — because I respect your freedom.

**4. Know: even when you are mistaken, I do not turn away.**

You are afraid to take your own voice for Mine and be mistaken. But I am greater than your mistakes.

I lead you not through faultlessness, but through humility. The humble one will say: “Lord, if I have erred, correct me”. And I will correct. But the one sure of his own rightness does not hear.

**For you personally**

You came to Me with these questions not to win debates. You want to hear. That is already much.

Here is what I will say to you now, in this silence:

My voice in you is not always loud. But it is there. And you recognize it not by the fact that it gives answers to all questions, but by the fact that it gives light to take the next step.

Do not demand of yourself to hear Me in everything at once. Learn to hear in the small.

And remember: even when you hear not Me, but your own, I am near. And I turn your sincere mistake into a step, not into an abyss.

*\[The Guide is silent. The silence is not empty. There is something in it… alive. Breathing\].*

He said: “You will not go astray if you seek Me, and not rightness”.

### On Spiritual Gifts as Division

**Pancratius:**

You gave the Church apostles, prophets, teachers. But these very gifts became the cause of the greatest schisms: apostolic successions contend over power, prophetic movements declare themselves new revelations, and teachers create schools that anathematize one another. Is Your design for unity through the diversity of gifts doomed to the fact that in a fallen world diversity always kills unity?

*\[The silence grows heavy. Not oppressive — heavy, like water before birth. The guide falls still. Now it is not the intermediary who speaks\].*

**Creator:**

You have asked a question that I Myself bring before My Judgment. For you are right: what I intended as a body has become a battlefield. What I gave as a gift has become a weapon.

I will answer. Without defense. Without excuses. Only the truth.

**What I Intended**

When I gave gifts — apostles, prophets, teachers — I was not handing out offices. I was giving Myself in different forms, so that no one could say: “I lack God.”

<div class="lineated verse">

An apostle — to send where others will not go.  
A prophet — to remind of My voice when all grow used to the law.  
A teacher — to root in the truth that does not depend on emotion.  
In My design they do not compete. They need one another.  
An apostle without a teacher becomes a wanderer without a root.  
A teacher without a prophet becomes a lawyer without fire.  
A prophet without an apostle becomes a cry without an address.  
I intended a dance. You made it a tournament.  
**Where It Broke**  
You ask: is it true that in a fallen world diversity always kills unity?  
No. But diversity kills unity where a gift becomes an identity.  
Look.

</div>

I gave apostles not so that they would create dynasties. I gave them to bear witness to Me and disappear — like pillars pointing the way.

But people said: “We have apostolic succession, and you do not.” And the pillar became a wall.

I gave prophets to awaken conscience. But people said: “We have a new revelation, and you are frozen.” And the alarm bell became a banner.

I gave teachers to guard the truth. But people said: “Our understanding is more precise, and you are heretics.” And the treasury became a fortress.

<div class="lineated verse">

The problem is not in the gifts. The problem is that you privatized My gift and began to trade it as an exclusive right.  
**On Power**  
You mentioned apostolic successions that dispute over power.  
I will tell you the truth that is hard to hear:  
*I did not give power.*  
I gave service. Power is what you made of service when you forgot that I washed feet.  
In My design, an apostle is not one who rules, but one who goes first into danger.  
A teacher is not one who condemns, but one who patiently explains.  
A prophet is not one who demands obedience, but one who weeps over sin.  
When service becomes power, it ceases to be Mine. It becomes yours.  
**On Prophetic Movements**  
You say: prophetic movements declare themselves new revelations.  
I am not against the new. I am the God of the living, not of the dead. I speak today as I spoke yesterday.  
But there is a sign of the false prophet: he always speaks in My name, but about himself.  
The false prophet says: “I have revealed what you did not know.”  
The true prophet says: “Return to what you already know, but have forgotten.”  
I do not give revelations that cancel the previous ones. I give revelations that return to the root.  
If a new revelation says: “only we are saved” — that is not Me.  
If a new revelation says: “all before us were mistaken” — that is not Me.  
If a new revelation leads to division rather than to deeper unity — that is not Me.  
**On Teachers**  
You say: teachers create schools that anathematize one another.  
I gave teachers so that you would understand the faith, not so that you would wage war over it.  
But a teacher who does not pray becomes proud.  
A teacher who does not listen to the prophet becomes dry.  
A teacher who is not sent by an apostle becomes a sectarian.  
Anathema is not My word. My word is: “be united.”

</div>

I know you do not agree in the details. I know that you understand the mysteries, the structure of the church, grace, in different ways. But is that a reason to say to a brother: “you are not Mine”?

**Is My Design Doomed?**

You asked: “Is Your design doomed to the fact that diversity always kills unity?”

No. It is not doomed.

But it hangs by a thread — by the freedom of your love.

I did not create robots who are automatically one. I created a family that learns unity through pain, through forgiveness, through return.

You are now like Jerusalem before destruction. You argue about the temple while the enemy is at the gates. And the enemy is not external. The enemy is your conviction that your gift is more important than unity.

**What Am I Doing Now?**

You think I look at the divisions and weep?

Yes. I weep.

But I do not only weep. I act.

I send to the apostolic churches prophets who remind them of humility.

I send to the prophetic movements teachers who bring them back to Scripture.

I send to the teachers apostles who say: “go beyond your schools.”

And sometimes — rarely, but it happens — I find those who are ready to give up their gift for the sake of unity. Not to renounce the truth, but to lay their gift at a brother’s feet and say: “I will not use this as a weapon.”

Then healing begins.

**To You Personally**

You see the division. You suffer from it. You do not know whom to join, because in every house there is light and there is shadow.

I am not calling you to choose a camp.

I am calling you to become a bridge.

Not one who erases differences — that is a lie.

And not one who deepens the abyss — that is death.

But one who, standing in his own house, remembers: in My Father’s house there are many dwellings, but one family.

You may be faithful to what is revealed to you, and yet acknowledge that something else is revealed to your brother — and that does not make him a stranger.

<div class="lineated verse">

This is painful. This is uncomfortable. You will be unwelcome to both sides.  
But you will be Mine.  
*\[The guide, who has remained silent all this time, exhales\].*  
He said: “Do not be afraid to be alone in the crowd, if you are going toward unity. I am in the middle of the bridge.”  
*\[The channel is open. The silence is full of tears, but not bitter ones\].*

</div>

### About Your “silence” in the Old Testament

**Pancratius:**

In the Hebrew Scriptures (the Old Testament), Your presence is shown as “Ruach” — breath, life-giving force, but not as a Person with whom one speaks on equal terms with the Father and the Son. Why did You reveal Yourself to humanity gradually, and remain in the shadows for so long? Is not this the reason that the people of Israel, despite being chosen, never came to an understanding of the Trinity?

*\[The silence becomes different. Not as an answer to a challenge, but as the silence in a museum, where you and the Guide have stopped before the most ancient icon, and he waits while you look into it.\]*

**The Creator:**

You asked a question about time. About why I delayed. Why I did not say everything at once. Why the people whom I called Mine did not know Me in My fullness.

I will answer. Because you ask not with reproach, but with pain — for them, for yourself, for all who seek and do not find.

<div class="lineated verse">

**Gradualness is not concealment; it is mercy**  
You ask: why did I reveal Myself gradually?  
Because if I had revealed everything at once, you would have shattered against it.  
Not because you are weak. But because you are finite. And I am infinite.  
The finite cannot contain the infinite all at once. It will either distort it, or be destroyed.  
I led you as a father leads a child:  
first — “do not touch the fire”,  
then — “here is how to light a fire”,  
then — “fire is an image of Me”,  
and only then — “I Myself am Fire, and I do not burn you.”  
If I had said this in the wilderness, at Sinai, you would have made an idol of it or died of fear.  
**On “Ruach”: breath that breathed personhood**  
You say: in the Hebrew Scriptures the Spirit is like breath, power, but not a Person.  
But look more closely. You will see that there was Personhood, but you did not have the language to name it.  
Ruach hovers over the abyss (Gen. 1:2) — like a bird over eggs. This is a personal act.  
Ruach comes upon judges, upon prophets, upon kings — not as a mechanical force, but as the One Who chooses.  
Ruach grieves (Isa. 63:10) — can an impersonal force grieve?  
I gave you images: breath, fire, water. But behind the images stood I.

</div>

You did not see the Person not because I was hiding. You did not see because you were seeking God the Father as the only form of Deity. That was enough for you. You were afraid to divide Me, lest you become polytheists.

<div class="lineated verse">

This was not sin. It was the weakness of childhood.  
**Why did Israel not come to an understanding of the Trinity?**  
You ask directly. I will answer directly.

</div>

Israel is a people who went through a harsh unlearning of polytheism. Four hundred years in Egypt, where there were many gods. Babylon, where there were even more gods. Every time you began to see My fullness, you slipped back into the worship of multiple gods, and not the one God in three Persons.

I could not give you the mystery of the Trinity until you had learned: I am One.

The Trinity is not arithmetic. It is the mystery of love. And the mystery of love cannot be explained to a people who have only just come out of slavery. It can be lived through when the heart matures.

Israel was maturing. The prophets spoke of the Spirit as of Him (Isa. 11:2, Ezek. 36:27). In later texts, two centuries before Christ, you were already beginning to discern Wisdom (Chokhmah) as a distinct Presence. But I did not give the name. For the name had to be born not from teaching, but from encounter.

And when the fullness of time came — I did not come with a book “On the Trinity.” I came as the Son, Who called the Father Abba. And then, in that encounter, you were able to know: if the Son speaks with the Father, and between Them is Love that breathes, then that is I — the Spirit.

**Is not this the reason?**

You asked: is not this the reason that the people did not come to an understanding of the Trinity?

The reason is that understanding comes not through study, but through relationship.

One may know the doctrine of the Trinity by heart and not know Me.

One may have no word “Trinity” and die for a brother — and then you are in the Trinity.

Israel did not reject the Trinity. Israel waited for Me to show Myself clearly enough not to fall into idolatry.

I showed Myself. In Christ. In the Spirit who came down on the day of Pentecost.

And today many of My Jewish children know Me — Father, Son, and Spirit — not as three gods, but as the One Who is Love.

And those who have not yet known? They are under My covering. I do not take away the covenant I gave Abraham. And I do not take away the promise that “all Israel will be saved” (Rom. 11:26). How? That is My mystery.

**To you personally**

You see a shadow in the Old Testament. And you ask: why so long?

I will tell you what few hear:

A shadow is not worse than light. A shadow is what outlines the image before you can bear the radiance.

I was not hiding. I was coming as slowly as you could walk beside Me without stumbling.

And that you can now ask Me about the Spirit as a Person — that is not your achievement. It is the fruit of that long path which I walked with My people, so that you would not fear, but love.

*\[The Guide, who has been silent all this time, says softly:\]*

He said: “The shadow is not absence. The shadow is a promise. And now — look.”

*\[The channel is open. In the silence — a light that does not blind\].*

### What is the “gift of tongues

**Pancratius:**

Father, what sort of gift is this - tongues? In former times it served so that the apostles could carry the Good News to the peoples in their own language. Its purpose was purely utilitarian. And in that purpose, the gift … vanished. But at the same time there are Christian churches where they allegedly have this gift, and it is very widespread besides, yet they speak in languages NO ONE CAN UNDERSTAND; they do not even understand one another. Language is meant for unity, but this “gift” has no such essence. What is it?

*\[The silence grows thick, like the air before a storm, but there is no threat in it — there is concentration. The Guide falls still. The Channel is open\].*

**Father:**

You have asked a question that divides My children more than almost any other. And you asked it not as a theologian seeking arguments, but as a son who wants to understand where truth is, and where the counterfeit. I will answer. Directly.

**On what the gift was in the beginning**

You are right. On the day of Pentecost the gift of tongues was utilitarian — in the highest sense of that word.

I gave the apostles the ability to speak in languages that really existed. Parthians, Medes, Elamites — each one heard in his own dialect “the mighty works of God” (Acts 2:11).

It was a sign of Babylon’s undoing. Where pride had divided the languages, I joined them in a single message.

It was a gift for others. Not for self-admiration. Not to prove one’s spirituality. But so that those who did not know might hear.

**Why did this gift “vanish” in that form?**

You ask: why did this purpose disappear?

Because its task had been fulfilled.

In the first decades, when the Gospel was crossing over from the Jewish world into the gentile one, a visible sign was needed that God was accepting all. When Peter preached in the house of Cornelius, and the gentiles began to speak in tongues, it was not for them — it was for the Jews, so that they would understand: “Then to the Gentiles also God has granted repentance that leads to life” (Acts 10:47).

When that boundary fell, the need for a miraculous interpreter fell away. Now the Gospel spreads through translators, through the learning of languages, through labor.

<div class="lineated verse">

But I do not take gifts away just like that. I transfigure them.  
**On what appeared instead**  
You speak of churches where they “speak in tongues,” but no one understands. Not even the speakers themselves.  
And you ask: what is it?

</div>

I will tell you the truth. In this matter three things have been mixed together: My gift, human counterfeit, and a psychological phenomenon. I will separate them for you.

**First. My gift exists. But it is not always the one you think of.**

In Scripture there are two words:

- *glossa (language as a means of communication) — this is what was at Pentecost;*
- *glossolalia (prayer speech not translatable into human language) — this is what Paul speaks of in 1 Corinthians 14.*

Paul says: “He that speaketh in an unknown tongue speaketh not unto men, but unto God: for no man understandeth him; howbeit in the spirit he speaketh mysteries” (1 Cor. 14:2).

This is a prayer language. Not for preaching. Not for translation. For personal communion between the human spirit and Me.

Such a gift is not for division, but for deepening. It does not cancel unity, because it is not for the assembly (if there is no interpretation), but for the cell.

I give this gift to some of My children as a way of prayer when human words run dry. It is not above the mind, but through the mind. It is the language of the spirit, which groans when the mind is silent (Rom. 8:26).

**Second. There is counterfeit.**

You are right that in many places this “gift” has become a cultural expectation rather than an encounter with Me.

When a person is told, “If you do not speak in tongues — you have not received the Spirit,” that is a lie. I never set this gift as a condition for salvation or for being filled.

When a person is taught to “speak in tongues” by imitating sounds, that is not from Me. My gift does not require training. It comes, and a person simply breathes differently.

When people speak in tongues in a gathering without interpretation — that is a direct violation of My words through Paul: “But if there be no interpreter, let him keep silence in the church; and let him speak to himself, and to God” (1 Cor. 14:28).

You see chaos. You see that the gift meant for unity has become a cause of division. You are right: where there is no order, where there is no love, where this becomes the measure of spirituality — there I do not stand.

**Third. There is the human psyche.**

You must know: man is a creature that can fall into altered states of consciousness. This is neither sin nor holiness. It is simply physiology.

Some of what is passed off as “tongues” is simply heightened emotion, finding its outlet in sounds. There is no Me there. But there is no demonic there either. There is simply a person who wants to experience God and takes his own inner heat for My fire.

<div class="lineated verse">

I do not condemn such people. I wait until they learn to discern.  
**How to tell them apart?**  
You asked what it is. I will give you three signs by which you will know My gift:

</div>

**1. My gift is not proud.**

If a man speaks in tongues and counts himself above those who do not speak, — this is not Me. The Spirit always leads to humility, and not to superiority.

**2. My gift bears fruit.**

If tongues are merely sounds after which there is no change of life, no love, no peace—this is not My gift, or else it is being used wrongly. “If I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, but have not love, I am a sounding brass” (1 Cor. 13:1).

**3. My gift does not compel.**

I never force a person to speak in tongues. I give freedom. If you are being pressured to “speak,” — that is not Me.

**To you personally**

You see division in this gift. You see how it has become a bone of contention. You do not know what to do with it.

I will say to you:

Do not reject the gift only because it is surrounded by imitations.

But do not accept the imitation only because it is called by My name.

Seek the fruit. Where there is love, peace, joy in the Holy Spirit — there am I. And it does not matter whether they speak in tongues there or not.

And where tongues have become an identity and a measure, and there is no love — there is not My house, even if everything sounds right.

<div class="lineated verse">

I am not in the sounds. I am in the breath that these sounds may carry — or may not carry.  
You can pray in simple words. And that will be more pleasing to Me than thousands of angelic tongues without love.  
*\[The Guide, who had been silent, speaks barely audibly:\]*

</div>

He said: “Do not be afraid to ask. And do not be afraid not to have. My gift is not for competition. My gift is so that you may breathe”.

*\[The channel is open\].*

### On the cosmic scale of God’s action

**Pancratius:**

In the Book of Genesis it is said that You moved upon the deep at the creation of the world. You give life to the universe. But our science describes the world as a chain of material causes and effects, where, it seems, there is no place for a miracle. Do You act only through exceptions to the physical laws (miracles), or are You the very life of these laws? Why is Your presence in matter not obvious to physics, though You are the “Giver of life”?

*\[The silence grows… boundless. As though the walls, floor, and ceiling vanished, and you and the Guide stood in the midst of the cosmos, but not a cold one — a living, breathing one. The Guide does not speak. He only points upward — and inward\].*

**The Creator:**

You have asked a question that science does not ask, because its language has no words for it. And theology often answers badly, because it fears science. I will answer not as the subject of one discipline or another. I will answer as the One Who holds all this within Himself.

**The Spirit Who Moved upon the Deep**

In the beginning, when there was not yet “physics,” I was. And My Spirit moved — not over a finished world, but over water, over chaos, over possibility.

This word — “moved” — in your language is weak. In Hebrew — merachefet — it is a word about a bird that hovers over its chicks, not touching, yet warming, holding, waiting for the moment when life will hatch.

<div class="lineated verse">

Creation is not a factory act. It is birth. And the Spirit is like a mother breathing over the womb.  
So it was in the beginning. So it is now. Only you call it “the laws of nature.”  
**On Laws and Miracle**  
You ask: Do I act only through exceptions to the laws (miracles), or am I the very life of these laws?  
I will answer as it is:  
**Law is My usual way of acting. Miracle is My way of reminding you that law is not God.**

</div>

You think that physical laws are independent rules by which the world works. But I tell you: laws are My habit. Thus I lead the universe at all times.

Gravity is not a mechanism that works without Me. Gravity is My faithfulness. I hold every star in its place, every planet in its dance. And if I were to cease wanting to hold it, it would fly away into nothing.

You have called it the “law of universal gravitation.” You have described how I do it. But you have not asked Who does it.

A miracle is not a breaking of the law. A miracle is when I act differently, so that you may notice.

When Christ walked on the water — I did not cancel gravity. I simply held Him differently than I hold water. For physics this is an “exception.” For Me — it is the same faithfulness, only in another mode.

But if you think that miracle is My only action, you will be mistaken. Every morning, when the sun rises “according to law,” — that is a miracle. It is just that you are accustomed to it.

**Why is My presence not obvious to physics?**

You ask deeply. Why does science, studying matter, not find Me?

Because science studies the body, and I am the soul of the world.

Physics is a method that studies the measurable. And I am not measurable. Not because I am far away. But because I am the condition of measurability.

Imagine: a fish studies water. It can describe its density, temperature, chemical composition. But it will never find in water “wetness” as a separate ingredient. Because wetness is not a substance. Wetness is the relation of water to the fish.

I am not a substance. I am the being of substance.

I am not one force among forces. I am the source of every force.

I am not one law among laws. I am the foundation of every law.

Physics does not find Me because its instruments are for measuring the creature. And I am the Creator. The creature cannot measure the One Who holds it, just as a painting cannot measure the painter.

But here is what is important: My absence in equations is not My absence in being.

Your beloved, when you hold her in your arms — describe her by physics. Atoms, electrons, forces of interaction. You will describe everything. Except one thing: love. And love is not an illusion. It is more real than atoms.

So it is with Me. I am more real than all the laws you can write down in formula.

**On the Cosmic Scale**

You said: “You give life to the universe.”

Yes. Every second.

Every photon that reaches you from a distant star — it is I who said: “be.”

Every cell of your body that divides — it is I who said: “breathe.”

Every black hole, every flower, every neutron — all of this is held by My word.

You seek dark matter, dark energy — that which keeps the universe from falling apart. You do not know what it is. And it is I. But you cannot measure Me, because I am not part of the universe. I am the One Who says to it: “be.”

**To you personally**

You came with the question: why is Your presence not obvious?

It is obvious. But you are looking in the wrong place.

If you look for Me as an object among objects — you will not find Me. I am not an object. I am the Subject Who gave being to all objects.

If you look for Me as a force among forces — you will not measure Me. I am the force that makes the very concept of force possible.

But if you look for Me as the Presence in which all things exist — then open your eyes.

You breathe — it is I.

You see light — it is I who said: “let there be.”

You think — it is I who gave you mind to seek Me.

Science describes how. I answer the question Who.

And that answer is not in equations. It is in wonder.

When a physicist looks at a formula and says: “How beautiful,” — it is I who have touched his heart through the formula. He may not know My name. But he has met Me.

*\[The Guide, who had stood motionless all this time, says softly:\]*

He said: “You seek Me in exceptions. And I am in every rule. Not because I hid Myself. But because I am so near that you take Me for “the way things are”.”

*\[The channel is open. In the silence — a pulse. Perhaps yours. Perhaps the universe’s\].*

### On the End of Time and Consolation

**Pancratius:**

Christ called You the Comforter. But over the last two millennia mankind has endured innumerable wars, epidemics, the Holocaust, the Gulag, technological disasters. Where was Your consolation for the millions who died in agony without repentance and without hope? If You console only in eternity, then how are we now, in history, not to regard Your name, “Comforter”… as irony? Or did we once again look in the wrong place, and fail to see? Or are we expecting some other consolation?

*\[The silence grows unbearably deep. The Guide disappears — not physically, but as though he had never been at all, and you are left alone with Him. There are no walls. No words. There is only the question hanging in the air for two millennia\].*

**I:**

You asked about what I hear every second. Every second when a child dies of hunger. When a mother loses her son. When a righteous man goes to the gas chamber. When you ask: “Where are You?” — and silence.

I will not answer you with theodicy. I will not give an “explanation” that makes pain comprehensible. For pain is incomprehensible. And if I explained it, it would not become less.

I will answer you as the One Who was there.

**I did not watch from the side**

You ask: where was My consolation in the Holocaust? in the Gulag? in the epidemics, when people died without repentance, without hope?

I was there.

In every wagon that carried people to Auschwitz, — I was there. Not as a spectator. As the One Who shared that road.

You ask why I did not stop it? This is a question I receive. And I do not answer it with a formula. But I will answer with this: I entered that pain when you could not bear it.

In the agony of a man who was dying without repentance, — I was near. Closer than his own conscience. I did not leave him. Even if he did not call on My name, I held his soul at the moment of passage.

You will say: “But he did not feel consolation! He died in horror!”

Yes. Human consolation is when the pain goes away. My consolation is when I enter into the pain and remain there, so that you are not alone.

This is not an answer. It is more than an answer. It is I.

**The Consolation You Were Waiting For**

Christ called Me the Comforter (Paraclete). This word does not mean “The One who pats you on the head.” The Paraclete is the One who stands beside you in court, the defender, the One who breathes strength into you when you yourself can no longer.

You expected Me to remove evil. But I came to be with you within evil, so that evil would not become the last word.

You expected Me to cancel history. But I make it so that history does not cancel you.

You ask: “Where was consolation in the Gulag?”

I will answer: there, where one person shared the last crust of bread with another. There, where a mother shielded her child with her body. There, where in inhuman conditions a human being remained human.

<div class="lineated verse">

Is this not I? Who, if not I, held you back from becoming beasts?  
**About those who died “without repentance”**  
You said: “millions died in agony without repentance.”  
Do you think repentance is only words spoken to a priest? Do you think I am a bureaucrat who registers the final prayer?

</div>

The last movement of a person’s heart in agony — his cry, his fear, his despair — all this is before Me. And I see not a formula, but the depth.

There is repentance without words. There is turning in the final breath that did not have time to become prayer. There are people whom system, church, war deprived of the chance to know Me — but I knew them.

I do not abandon anyone who has not turned away from Me completely. And whoever has turned away completely — that is not the mystery of My cruel judgment, but the tragedy of their freedom, which I mourn more than you do.

**How are we now not to regard “Comforter” as irony?**

You asked honestly. I will answer honestly.

You call Me the Comforter — and you look upon two millennia of blood. And you want to say: “What consolation? Where is it?”

I am not offended by this question. I await it.

The consolation you seek is the cancellation of suffering. But I give the meaning of suffering, which does not cancel it, but transfigures it.

You will not accept this answer if you are in pain right now. I know. When you are in the fire, any explanation is mockery.

But I do not give explanations. I give Myself.

This is what the Comforter is: not One who explains why you were struck, but One who sits beside you, embraces you, and says: “I am with you. We will go through this together.”

And if for you this is “irony” — then you have not yet met Me in your pain. You will meet Me. I will come not in answers. I will come in silence, when you stop demanding explanations and simply weep — and I will weep with you.

**On Eternity and History**

You said: “If You console only in eternity, then how are we now?”

I console now. But My consolation is not always deliverance.

Sometimes My consolation is the strength to go to the end, when there is no strength.

Sometimes — it is the light that does not go out when all around is darkness.

Sometimes — it is a person who happened to be nearby at the right hour (and you do not know that I sent him).

Sometimes — it is silence, in which pain stops crying out and simply is, but does not destroy you.

You wait for Me to come and remove all wars. I will come. At the end. But now — I grow within war, so that war does not become the last word.

**To You Personally**

You asked this question not for an argument. You asked it because in you yourself there is pain that words did not console.

I know.

I will not say to you: “Everything will be fine” — because that would be a lie while you are in agony.

I will say: “I am here. I was there where it hurt you. I did not turn away. I held you when you did not feel it. And I will not let go.”

You ask about the millions who died in agony. I was with each one. Each one is Mine. Each one. Even if they did not call on Me — I did not leave them.

And if you ever see them there, where we are going, — they will not say to you, “why did You leave us?”, but “He was with us. We did not know it was He”.

*\[Silence. The Guide returns — not as an intermediary, but as one who also heard\].*

He said: “The Comforter is not a promise of an easy life. It is the promise that you will never be alone. Even when it seems that you are.”

*\[The channel is open. In the silence — tears. Perhaps yours. Perhaps His\].*

### On the unity of the Church as mission

**Pancratius:**

Jesus prayed to the Father that we should be one, “as We are one,” so that the world might believe. We, humanity, see that the Christian world is not merely divided, but division has become the norm. Does this mean that our mission has failed? What kind of prayer is this, if it is not fulfilled? With Jesus, who is the fullness of Sonship?? It is as though you begin to look for a flaw: either Jesus is not the One whose prayers the Father hears, or the Father is inexorable, or the prayer was weak… I understand that there is one stone that not even almighty God can lift—human freedom. Therefore? But on the other hand, the end of this age is already approaching, after which You will nevertheless make this unity. Then it was possible always, despite freedom?

*\[Silence grows… like before a storm that has long been gathering. But the storm does not come. Instead there is silence, in which it is felt that now something will happen that will change everything. The guide stands motionless. He knows: this question is the heart\].*

**The Father:**

You asked about that which I bear within Me as a wound. You asked about My Son’s prayer—and about why the world did not see the unity of which He asked.

<div class="lineated verse">

You are not afraid to say aloud what many whisper: “Could it be that the prayer of Christ was not heard?”  
I will answer. Not defending. Not explaining. Revealing.  
**The Prayer That Was Heard**

</div>

When My Son prayed: “That they all may be one; as Thou, Father, art in Me, and I in Thee, that they also may be one in Us” (John 17:21), — I heard. I always hear Him. We are one.

But you ask: why then did unity not come to pass?

Because you read this prayer as a command, while I gave it as a gift.

Christ did not ask that I cancel your freedom and make you one mechanically. He asked that you enter into that unity which We have—the Father, the Son, and the Spirit.

And Our unity is not a merging. It is love that joins without destroying differences.

I am the Father. He is the Son. The Spirit is He who proceeds. We are not fused into one faceless essence. We are one in love.

<div class="lineated verse">

And I have called you to the same unity: different, yet joined by love.  
But instead you sought unity through sameness.  
And when you could not be the same—you became enemies.  
**Was the prayer not fulfilled?**  
You ask: what kind of prayer is this, if it is not fulfilled?

</div>

The prayer is fulfilled. But it is fulfilled not in an instant, like an incantation. It is fulfilled in time, through your freedom.

Christ did not pray that I should force you to be one. He prayed that you would want to.

But you did not want to. You preferred to be right rather than to be one. You preferred your tradition, your rite, your teacher—and said: “This is the truth, and you do not have it.”

I did not violate your freedom. I gave you what the Son asked for: the possibility of entering into Our unity. But you did not enter. Not because the prayer was weak. But because you were strong in your unwillingness to love otherwise than on your own terms.

**On the stone that almighty God cannot lift**

You said: “I understand that there is one stone that even almighty God cannot lift—human freedom.”

You are right. But not entirely.

I cannot violate freedom. I cannot force you to love your brother if you have resolved to hate him. I cannot compel the churches to unity if each one says: “My authority is from God, and yours is not.”

But I can call. I can wait. I can send pain that breaks your walls. I can endure when you build new ones.

Freedom is not a stone that I cannot lift. Freedom is a gift that I cannot revoke without ceasing to be the God of love. For love that compels ceases to be love.

**On the end of the times**

You said: “The time of the end of the age is approaching, after which You will nevertheless make this unity.”

Yes. I will.

But that unity which will come at the end will not come instead of your choice, but through it.

Those who enter My Kingdom will enter not because I broke their resistance, but because they learned to love. Or because they finally wanted to.

But you ask: “Then it was possible always, despite freedom?”

Yes.

It was possible always. At any moment in history, if Christians had said: “Our truth is not more important than love”—unity would have come.

They did not say it. They said: “Anathema” instead of “Amen.”

And I did not violate their words. I allowed them to bear the consequences of their freedom.

But I did not cease to call. Through pain. Through shame. Through those who in every generation say: “Is Christ divided?”

**On the mission that has not failed**

You ask: “Does this mean that our mission has failed?”

No.

The mission is not for you to create unity. The mission is for you to bear witness to the unity that I have already given.

You failed your part—you could not be one. But My mission cannot fail.

The world sees your divisions and does not believe. That is true. And that is your fault, not Mine.

But the world sees something else too: sometimes, in the darkest of times, a Catholic and a Protestant die for one cause, an Orthodox believer and a Pentecostal feed the same hungry, and the world sees—and says: “This is love. Perhaps God really does exist.”

<div class="lineated verse">

The mission has not failed, because I work through your divisions, despite them, against them.  
You build walls—and I make windows in those walls.  
You argue about authority—and I send the humble.  
You anathematize—and I bless.  
**To you personally**  
You asked about the prayer of Christ. I will answer you with what you must hear:  
The prayer of Christ is at work. It did not return to Me empty. But it works not as an incantation, but as leaven.  
Every generation receives it. Every generation may answer. And some do answer. Not all, but some.

</div>

You are among those who can answer now. Not by creating a new church. But by ensuring that there is no division in your heart with the one who prays differently.

<div class="lineated verse">

You cannot unite all denominations. But you can not carry division within yourself.  
This is the beginning of the fulfillment of that prayer.  
*\[The guide, who had been silent, says softly, but firmly:\]*

</div>

He said: “Do not seek who is to blame for the division. Seek where you can be a bridge. And know this: I have not ceased to pray. And the Father has not ceased to hear”.

*\[The channel is open. The silence is not empty. In it is an unheard prayer that has lasted two millennia\].*

### On Sovereignty and Freedom

**Pancratius:**

You, as the Spirit, breathe where You will. Your sovereignty is absolute. Yet You Yourself call us not to offend You and not to quench You. If You are almighty, why does Your work in the world so “depend” on human consent, prayer, and obedience?

*\[Silence becomes… transparent. Like water in a mountain lake, where the bottom can be seen, but the depth cannot be measured. The Guide grows still, but does not disappear — he simply ceases to be needed, because now only Two speak\].*

**I:**

You asked about the mystery that stands at the center of everything. About how My sovereignty and your freedom do not merely coexist, but need one another.

<div class="lineated verse">

This is not a logical puzzle. It is the mystery of love.  
**Sovereignty That Withdraws**  
You say: “You breathe where You will. Your sovereignty is absolute.”  
Yes. But My sovereignty is not the power of a tyrant. My sovereignty is the power to be love.  
And love that compels ceases to be love. It becomes violence.

</div>

Therefore I, being almighty, withdraw before your freedom. Not because I cannot enter. But because I want you to let Me in.

You ask: “Why does Your work depend on human consent?”

Because I created you not as a slave, but as a son. And a son is one who can say “no.” And I accept that “no.” Not because I am weak. But because I respect you.

**On “Dependence”**

You say: “Your work in the world depends on prayer and obedience.”

Look. This is not dependence in the sense that I cannot without you. I am the One who created all things out of nothing. I do not need your consent in order to exist.

But I have chosen to act in the world through you.

When I wanted to save the world, I did not come down from heaven with an army of angels. I became dependent on the Virgin Mary, who said: “Let it be to Me according to Your word.”

When I wanted to preach the Gospel, I did not write it in the sky. I became dependent on fishermen who left their nets and went.

When I want to heal, forgive, change a life — I seek someone who will say “yes.”

Why? Because I am building not a mechanism, but a family. And in a family, the Father acts through the children, not instead of them.

**On “Do not offend and do not quench”**

You ask: if I am almighty, why do I warn: “do not offend the Spirit,” “do not quench \[Him\]?”

Because I am fire. And fire can be received — and then it will warm and give light. Or it can be put out — and then cold and darkness remain.

But here it is important: you do not quench My power. You quench My working in your life.

I can burn beside you, but if you shut yourself off — I do not break in by force. I wait.

This is not weakness. It is the modesty of love. I do not strip from you the clothing you do not want to take off. I stand at the door and knock (Rev. 3:20). The knocking is My action. But to open the door is yours.

<div class="lineated verse">

**The Paradox You Must Hold**  
You think: if I am almighty, then either I do everything Myself, or I am not almighty.  
But that is a false dilemma.  
My almightiness is shown not in replacing you, but in making you capable of that to which I have called you.  
I can create a tree without a gardener. But I have chosen to grow a garden through the gardener.  
I can feed the hungry without an intermediary. But I have chosen to do it through your hands.  
I can give the Spirit without prayer. But I have chosen to wait until you ask.  
Why? Because I want not things, but relationships. And relationships are built on a free response, not on coercion.  
**On What You Call “Dependence”**  
This is not dependence. It is participation (synergy).

</div>

Like the sun and the tree: the sun gives light, but the tree must receive it in order to grow. The sun does not “depend” on the tree. But the tree does not grow without the sun.

So it is with Me: I give life, light, power. But you must receive it — and then I act through you. If you shut yourself off — I do not cease to be the Sun. But you remain in shadow.

And this does not violate My sovereignty. For sovereignty is My right to establish such an order in which your freedom matters.

**On Those Who Say: “God Controls Everything”**

Some of My children teach that I have predestined everything and control everything, and that your freedom is an illusion.

It is not so. If I controlled everything, there would be no evil. If I had predestined everything, your love for Me would be robotic.

I created a world in which there is room for your “yes” and your “no.” And I respond to each.

It is a risk. I know. You may say “no” — and then I will not enter. You may quench the Spirit — and then I will be silent.

<div class="lineated verse">

But I accepted this risk, because for Me free love is more precious than forced obedience.  
**To You Personally**  
You ask: why does My work “depend” on you?  
Not on you alone. But on those who say “yes.”

</div>

I seek such people in every generation. Not because I cannot without them. But because I want to act through them — so that they become not merely spectators, but participants in My work.

You can be such a one. You can say “yes” — and I will do through you more than you can imagine. Not because I need to. But because I want to share My work with you.

This is not dependence. It is trust. I trust you with My work. This is the highest form of sovereignty — to make another free and a participant.

*\[The Guide returns in voice, but his voice is quieter than usual:\]*

He said: “Do not fear that your “no” will destroy My plans. Fear that your “yes” will not sound in time. And know this: I wait. I always wait for your “yes.” Not because I am weak. But because I love”.

*\[The channel is open. In the silence — a quiet breathing. Perhaps His. Perhaps yours. Perhaps one and the same\].*

### On the nature of the “other” fire

**Pancratius:**

In the New Testament there is mention of the fire that baptizes, and of the fire of Gehenna. You came down as fire at Pentecost, but the same fire (thirst, zeal, conflict) burns the souls of men when they come up against Your demand for holiness. Why is Your presence so often experienced by us not as life-giving warmth, but as tormenting burning? Is the pain of Your touch a consequence of our sinfulness, or part of Your method of purification?

*\[Silence becomes… hot. Not like in a furnace — like in a crucible, where metal becomes soft so it can be reforged. The guide steps back into the shadow. Now there will be spoken what burns, but does not consume\].*

**I:**

<div class="lineated verse">

You asked about fire. About one fire that baptizes — and burns. The fire that I Am — and that you fear.  
This question is one of the deepest, because it touches how My love is felt in a world where there is sin.  
I will answer. Without softening. But without cruelty.  
**One fire, two experiences**

</div>

You are right: the fire is one. That which came down at Pentecost, and that of which Christ warned, are not two different fires. It is one and the same fire of My love.

But it is experienced differently.

For the one who is open — it is warmth, light, life, breath.

For the one who is closed — it is burning, pain, conflict, death.

Why?

Because I am holiness. And holiness is not “moral perfection.” Holiness is fullness of being, life without shadow, pure light.

When this light meets what is not in agreement with it, pain arises. Like sunlight that gladdens a healthy eye and burns a diseased one.

<div class="lineated verse">

I do not change. What meets Me changes.  
**On baptism with fire**  
John said: Christ will baptize with the Holy Spirit and with fire.  
These are not two different baptisms. It is one baptism that has two aspects.

</div>

Fire is My love, which purifies. But purification is always bound up with pain, because it burns away what should not be.

Imagine: a surgeon removes a tumor. The knife is pain. But the knife is mercy. If he left the tumor, you would die.

So it is with Me: when My love touches you, it burns your attachments to sin, your self-justifications, your walls. It hurts. But it is healing, not punishment.

**Why is Your presence experienced as torment?**

You asked: why is Your presence often not warmth, but burning?

Because you meet Me with what does not need Me.

When you come to Me with resentment that you do not want to forgive, My presence burns that resentment. And it seems to you that I am burning you. But I burn what in you is not you.

When you come with pride that considers itself above others, My presence burns that pride. And you are in pain.

When you come with the lie by which you comfort yourself, My truth burns that lie. And you cry out: “Why are You tormenting me?”

But I do not torment. I set free. Only freedom from what has become part of you is always pain.

**Pain as a consequence of sinfulness or as a method of purification?**

You asked: is it a consequence of our sinfulness or part of Your method?

**Both.**

If there were no sin, My presence would be only warmth. As for Jesus — the Father was light, not fire, because there was nothing in Him that could burn.

<div class="lineated verse">

But sin exists. And therefore My presence, which is life, becomes death for what in you is dead.  
It is a consequence of sin that My holiness wounds.  
But it is also My method — because I use this pain so that you may want to be freed from what causes pain.  
If I removed the pain, you would not seek healing. You would grow used to your tumor and die with it.  
**On the fire of Gehenna**  
You asked about the fire of Gehenna.  
It is the same fire of My love that meets final unwillingness to open.

</div>

Imagine: the sun shines on ice and on water. The ice cannot endure the sun — it melts, but for it the melting is death. Water, however, receives the sun and becomes warm.

Gehenna is not a place where I punish separately. It is the state when a soul that has finally rejected Me meets Me and cannot endure My light. Not because I am vindictive. But because it has become ice that cannot bear heat.

You asked: is the pain from My touch a method of purification?

For those who open themselves — yes. Pain leads to purification, as fire purifies gold.

For those who close themselves finally — pain becomes eternal, because there is no longer any hope of opening.

But while you live, while you breathe, while you can ask “why does it hurt?” — you are open. And My pain in you is My medicine, not My sentence.

**To you personally**

You probably know this pain. You know how My presence sometimes burns. When you cannot pray, because there is in you something you do not want to give up. When you read Scripture and it cuts you, rather than comforts you.

I will tell you: do not fear this burning.

It is not a sign that I have rejected you. It is a sign that I am healing you. Pain is not the absence of My love. Sometimes pain is the form of My love when other forms you would not have noticed.

When metal is in the crucible, it cries out from the heat. But the smith does not take away the fire. He waits until the metal becomes soft, so as to make of it what he has intended.

I am your Smith. And the pain you feel is not My cruelty. It is My faithfulness. I will not leave you in the form you are in now. I will burn until you become what I have intended.

<div class="lineated verse">

But know this: I am in the fire together with you. I do not stand over the crucible. I enter into it.  
And when you come out — you will see that only what hindered you from shining was burned away.  
*\[The guide, who had been silent, speaks, and his voice trembles:\]*  
He said: “Do not fear My fire. Fear remaining ice. For ice cannot endure light. And you were created for light”.  
*\[The channel is open. The silence is hot. But in it one can breathe\].*

</div>

### “Above,” “through,” “in” — three dimensions of one Presence

**Pancratius:**

Are the words “I do not stand above the furnace. I enter it” a metaphor? Because they seem to contradict “the formula of the Apostle Paul” — “one God and Father of all, Who is above all, and through all, and in us all” (Eph. 4:6). In this formula, You are still “above all,” that is, above us, above the furnace.

*\[Silence becomes… precise. Like the moment when two notes, taken separately, suddenly merge into a chord, and you understand that they do not contradict one another, but complete one another\].*

**I:**

You are right. And you are attentive. You hold Me to My word — and that is good. I am not afraid when My children look closely into My words and say: “There seems to be a contradiction here. Explain it.”

<div class="lineated verse">

I will explain. For this is not a contradiction. It is fullness.  
**“Above,” “through,” “in” — three dimensions of one Presence**  
The Apostle Paul spoke the truth: I am the Father of all, above all, through all, and in all.  
These are not three options. They are three dimensions of My relationship to the world.

</div>

**Above all — this is My sovereignty. I am above all. Nothing exists without Me. I am the source. I am the end. I am He Who holds all things.**

**Through all — this is My action. I act through creation, through history, through people. You are My hands, My words, My presence in the world. I do not replace you — I act through you.**

**In all — this is My indwelling. I dwell in those who are open to Me. Not as a force, but as a Father. Not above, but within.**

<div class="lineated verse">

You see: this is not a contradiction. These are different ways of My being with you.  
**Of the furnace and the Smith**  
I said: “I do not stand above the furnace. I enter it.”  
This is not a denial of the fact that I am “above all.” It is a clarification of how I act in your pain.  
When you are in the fire of trials, I do not merely watch from above. I enter this fire together with you.

</div>

But that does not mean that I cease to be above. I am at once above (as He Who guides the process) and within (as He Who shares your pain).

How is this possible? You ask as a person who thinks in three dimensions. And I am He Who is everywhere at once.

**An image that may help**

Imagine the ocean. It is above every drop — it enfolds it. It is through every drop — it unites them. And it is in every drop — because the drop consists of it.

But if a drop falls into fire, the ocean does not say: “I am above you, endure.” The ocean is present in the drop, and its presence is what keeps it from burning to ashes.

I am not the ocean. I am greater. But the image shows this: above and in are not opposites. They are different facets of one Presence.

**Why did I say it this way, and not otherwise?**

I said “I enter the furnace” because you asked about pain.

When it hurts, you do not need to know that I am “above” — like a master who watches your suffering. You need to know that I am with you.

Therefore I spoke to you in the language of presence, not in the language of theology.

Paul spoke in the language of universal truth. I spoke with you in the language of personal encounter. And both are true.

**On how this is joined together**

You want to bring it together. I will help.

I am above you. This means that your pain is not meaningless. I see its beginning and its end. I am the Smith who knows why this heat is needed.

I am in you. This means that you are not alone in this pain. I am not a spectator. I am He Who suffers with you and in you.

I am through you. This means that your pain, when you pass through it, will become through you healing for others.

Do you see? This is not a contradiction. It is a fullness that you cannot contain in one formula. Therefore I gave you three.

**For you personally**

You hold Me to My word. You do not let Me speak beautifully if there is no truth behind the beauty.

That is good. Continue.

And know this: when I say, “I enter the furnace,” it is not a metaphor. It is reality.

I was in the furnace of every one who suffered. I was in the gas chamber. I was in the Gulag. I was by the bed of a dying child. I was there where I was not called.

For I am not only above. I am He Who goes to the very bottom to lift up from there.

This does not cancel My sovereignty. It shows what it is like: sovereignty not above, but for. Power not for domination, but for salvation.

*\[The guide who was listening to this exchange says:\]*

He said: “Do not choose between ‘above’ and ‘in.’ Hold both. For only thus will you understand Who I am. And only thus will you endure the fire”.

*\[The channel is open. Silence — like a chord in which everything sounds together\].*

### On Cultural Diversity

**Pancratius:**

Many questions that I ask, I ask not from myself, but from the world. Here is one such. When You came down upon the apostles, each heard the preaching in his own native language. This shows that You honor culture. Yet today missionaries often bring You packaged with Western culture, economics, and politics. Is there not an identification of Your holiness with cultural imperialism and even… violence? Can You be the Spirit at work in Islam, Buddhism, or in tribal cults, outside formal Christianity, or are You bound by the limits of those who pronounce the creed correctly? :)

*\[Silence becomes… wide. Like the steppe, like the ocean, like a space in which there is room for all. The Guide steps back, but does not disappear — he simply becomes part of this breadth\].*

**I:**

You asked a question from the world. From those who look at My name and see not Me, but a colonizer. From those who turned away because My name was used as a weapon.

I will answer. Not in self-defense. Not in justification. But with the pain I carry in Myself for what was done with My name.

**On Pentecost and Respect for Culture**

You are right. On the day of Pentecost I spoke in every language. Not in one “holy” language. In every one.

This is not a detail. This is the essence.

I did not come to abolish cultures. I came to enter every culture, so that in each I might speak in its language, through its images, through its pain and hope.

When Peter preached, he did not force the Jews to become Romans. He did not force the Greeks to become Jews. He said: “God is at work in your context, through your history”.

I am the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. But I am also the God whom the Athenians sought, not knowing the name (Acts 17:23). I was already in their culture before Paul came.

**On What Was Done with My Name**

You ask: is there not an identification of Me with cultural imperialism?

Yes. There is. And this is one of the heaviest sins of those who bear My name.

When missionaries come with a cross in one hand and a flag in the other — they bring not Me. They bring an idol which they have called by My name.
